ŇBut I think I have to go to the hospital wing.Ó Something was thrust in front of HarryŐs face, and he squinted at it before he realized it was a hand. He accepted it and allowed himself to be helped to his feet. ŇWeŐll take you,Ó another voice said, and Harry identified NottŐs voice. ŇHere.Ó Something was placed in his hands, and Harry realized it was his glasses, thankfully still in one piece. ŇIŐm fine,Ó Harry insisted as he unfolded his glasses. ŇI just fell.Ó ŇThat was a tripping jinx,Ó Nott said. ŇMoreso, that was a tripping jinx while you were on the stairs.Ó Harry placed his glasses on his nose, and ZabiniŐs face came into view. His expression looked grave. ŇPotterÉÓ he said. ŇSomeone just tried to murder you.Ó Chapter End Notes The stuffed animal anteater dream is a repeated nightmare I used to have as a child. >.> I have quite a lot of this story written, however - I have zero scenes from the next chapter written. I don't write longer stories in chronological order; I just write whatever scene tickles my fancy whenever I sit down to write. Therefore, the next one or two chapters will be slower in getting posted. My apologies, and thank you so much for reading. <3 Chapter4: The Lie Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! I have no idea what's going on with me. I normally take 12 days to write 500 words. I guess having an actual outline helps. 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! ŇNobody tried to murder me,Ó Harry said for the fourth time as Zabini and Nott helped him to the hospital wing. In addition to his throbbing shoulder, he felt like he had likely at least sprained his ankle; he wasnŐt able to walk without leaning on Zabini with his good arm. Nott followed close behind them. ŇYes, someone did so,Ó Zabini said. Harry shook his head. ŇIt was just a prank.Ó ŇEven if it was just meant as a prank, that fall could have still resulted in your death,Ó Nott said. ŇI think youŐre both being just a little dramatic,Ó Harry protested. ŇI mean, yeah - somebody has tried to kill me every year, so this is just a little ahead of schedule -Ó Nott let out a choking noise from behind them. ŇYou have zero survival instinct, Potter,Ó Zabini said with a sigh. ŇThatŐs not very Slytherin of you.Ó ŇYeah, well - IŐve only been a Slytherin for a day,Ó Harry said, rolling his eyes. Even so, the sorting hatŐs words to him from the previous night flew into his head: you need to learn how to survive whatŐs thrown at you. The three were silent for a few moments. When they reached the hall down from the hospital wing, Nott asked, ŇIf someone had tripped your friend Hermione Granger on the stairs, what would do?Ó ŇIŐd probably go after whoever did it,Ó Harry said without thinking. Then he paused. ŇAfter I made sure she was okay, I mean.Ó Out of the corner of his eye, Harry saw Zabini smirk. ŇSo it seems youŐll only take something personally when itŐs not you thatŐs been personally hurt,Ó Nott said. Harry blinked, confused. ŇWhat?Ó ŇHarry, we cannot let an attack on one of our Slytherins just slide,Ó Zabini said. ŇEven if they only have been a Slytherin for a day.Ó Harry sighed. ŇRight. The Ôcovenant.ŐÓ ŇYes,Ó Zabini said, his voice firm. ŇBut also if we donŐt address the issue it will surely happen again, and you could be badly hurt.Ó ŇWorse than you are now,Ó Nott added. By that point, theyŐd reached the hospital wing, and Nott went to the back of the room to fetch Madame Pomfrey. ŇSo,Ó Zabini said. ŇDo you have any idea who might have done it?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇNot a clue.Ó ŇWell, we need to come up with some theories to start,Ó Zabini mused. ŇIŐm thinking either a Gryffindor or a Hufflepuff.Ó ŇWhat? Why?Ó ŇWell, Ravenclaws donŐt often go after us. ŇI donŐt really want to say this to you, but someone in Hufflepuff might be blaming you forÉÓ He trailed off. ŇFor Cedric,Ó Harry said in realization. Hermione had just told him that Harry showing up with CedricŐs body had looked incredibly odd; he couldnŐt imagine what the Hufflepuffs must have thought. Zabini nodded. ŇMr. Potter, I think this is a record for you!Ó Madame Pomfrey declared as she marched across the wing. ŇItŐs the first day of classes!Ó ŇSorry,Ó Harry said sheepishly. ŇDonŐt apologize!Ó Nott snapped. ŇItŐs not your fault.Ó ŇMr. Nott said you were attacked?Ó Madame Pomfrey asked. ŇI... Ň Harry sighed. ŇYeah. I guess.Ó ŇSomeone cast a tripping jinx at him while he was on the stairs,Ó Zabini said. ŇWhat?!Ó Pomfrey exclaimed. ŇZabini, help him to the bed over here. Nott, please firecall your head of house.Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry said. He did not want Snape involved; heŐd likely immediately launch into a spiel about how Harry was a lying, attention-seeking brat. ŇWe can leave him out of it.Ó ŇThis is not optional, Potter,Ó Pomfrey said firmly. ŇA head of house must be informed when one of their students is attacked.Ó Pomfrey spent a few minutes casting various diagnostic spells. ŇYour ankle is merely a sprain, but your shoulder is dislocated. WeŐll have to pop it back into place.Ó Harry thought that sounded painful. It was. Even more unfortunate was the fact that Snape walked into the hospital wing just as Harry yelped in pain when Pomfrey put his shoulder back where it was supposed to go. Snape immediately ordered Zabini and Nott to go directly back to the Slytherin common room. ŇSir,Ó Zabini said, ŇPotter really shouldnŐt walk alone in the hallways -Ó ŇIf Madame Pomfrey allows him to return to the dorm tonight, I will escort him back to the dungeons,Ó Snape said in a clear dismissal. Zabini and Nott each shot Harry a glance, but left without further protest. Snape remained silent as Pomfrey continued treating Harry. She gave him two potions that smelled and tasted like death and then began applying a balm to his ankle and his shoulder. She considered his shoulder for a few moments before deciding to place his arm in a sling. ŇThe ankle should be fine by tomorrow, and the shoulder can come out of the sling by Friday,Ó Pomfrey said. ŇYou may still feel some pain in the shoulder but it should be gone by the end of the weekend, provided you continue to apply this balm.Ó She handed him a small jar. ŇOkay,Ó Harry said. ŇSeverus, you may take your student now,Ó Pomfrey said. ŇI trust that you will take the proper steps to ensure that heŐs not here again for the same reason.Ó Snape nodded and then turned to Harry. ŇWith me, Potter.Ó They were both silent until they turned the corner. ŇHave you any clue as to who might have jinxed you, Potter?Ó Snape asked. ŇNo,Ó Harry said. ŇAre you lying to me?Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry snapped. ŇMind your tone, Potter,Ó Snape said. ŇI am well aware of your history of taking matters into your own hands. Know that such actions will not be tolerated in my house.Ó Harry bit back a retort. He hadnŐt wanted to do anything, much less Ôtake the matter into his own hands.Ő He took a deep breath, and something suddenly occurred to him. ŇSir,Ó he said. ŇI mean no offense by this, but IŐm a little surprised that you even believe that I was attacked. ŇI mean, I donŐt even really believe it; Zabini and Nott are the ones saying itÉÓ Snape didnŐt say anything for a few moments, and Harry worried that heŐd pushed the wrong buttons again. ŇSlytherins being attacked in the hallways of this school is nothing new, Potter,Ó Snape finally said. There was an odd tone in his voice. ŇI thought you would have been aware of that.Ó *** When they arrived back at the common room, Snape sent Harry in and called for Parkinson and Malfoy to see him in the hallway. Parkinson walked by Harry with wide eyes and Malfoy looked at Harry with a furious expression on his face. Meanwhile, Greengrass, Nott, and Zabini crowded around Harry. ŇAre you okay?Ó Greengrass said to Harry. ŇTheo and Blaise told us what happened.Ó ŇIŐm fine,Ó Harry said. ŇÔDo unto others as you would have them do unto you?Ő Ringing any bells?Ó ŇYou meanÉÓ Parkinson said with a frown. ŇWhat did I do?Ó Harry had thought for certain that Malfoy would remember insulting Ron and his family, but he appeared clueless enough that he mustŐve truly not known. ŇHonestly?Ó he said. ŇYou reminded me of my Muggle cousin.Ó He was satisfied to see a slight look of horror dawn on MalfoyŐs face. ŇYou came across as a bully. ŇOkay.Ó Warrington departed, and Zabini sighed. ŇOkay... Potter takes Divination, so thatŐs going to be the tricky one since none of us take it. Harry struggled with his essay for Snape; the Potions text hardly had any information on the properties of powdered Moonstone, and he had no idea how to expand what little there was into a full essay. He was also having difficulty writing; although his dominant hand hadnŐt been injured, having his other arm in a sling while trying to write was awkward and annoying. ŇPotter,Ó Malfoy said quietly from behind him. ŇWhat do you want?Ó Harry said coldly. He was still rather crabby with Malfoy; their earlier argument had brought up a lot of bad feelings. ŇButÉÓ Harry sighed. ŇOf course thereŐs a catch.Ó ŇDonŐt tell them,Ó Malfoy said. ŇOr anyone else, for that matter.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, confused. ŇWhy not?Ó ŇSlytherins protect one another, and we keep each otherŐs secrets,Ó Malfoy said, his eyes narrowing. ŇAnd this needs to remain secret.Ó ŇI donŐt -Ó ŇLook, Potter,Ó Malfoy said, running a hand through his hair. ŇThereŐs a lot weŐre going to have to hash out between us. ŇUhÉ thanks.Ó Malfoy nodded once more, then went back to his own homework. Harry stared at his essay, then scratched out his last line, rewriting it with the information Malfoy had given him. He looked up ÔfeldsparŐ in the glossary of his Potions text and realized Malfoy had been right, and thought he might actually be able to come up with enough information to expand his essay to the required length. He worked for another hour or so, and then decided to call it a night. He drew his curtains and lay back on his pillow, staring up at the underside of the green canopy as he contemplated the day. His thoughts mostly swirled around the eveningŐs conversation with the other fifth years. Were children really getting attacked by the other houses, or was it a Slytherin exaggeration? Suddenly, Harry remembered something and he sat up straight in his bed, a feeling of horror overtaking him. ŇSlytherins being attacked in the hallways of this school is nothing new, Potter,Ó Snape had said. ŇI thought you would have been aware of that.Ó And what had Sirius had told him just a few weeks prior? ŇThis is actually one James asked me to teach him. Flips people up in the air and upside down. He liked using it on SnivellusÉÓ A pit yawned open in HarryŐs stomach. Had his own father been one of those students that attacked Slytherins? *** The next morning, HarryŐs thoughts continued to revolve around his father. He considered writing Sirius, but he knew that he would also have to inform his godfather of his new house placement. Harry knew he would have to let him know eventually, but he had no idea how. Harry also suspected that if his father had been attacking Slytherins, Sirius may have done the same. The thought wasnŐt a pleasant one. About halfway through breakfast, Greengrass pulled Harry out of his thoughts when she pointed out that they had Defense first thing. ŇDid you find anything out about the class from Granger?Ó she asked him. Harry nodded. ŇYeah,Ó he said. ŇHermioneŐs furious. Apparently, the class is all theory and nothing but.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó Davis asked. ŇShe wonŐt do any practical work for the entire year,Ó Harry said. ŇMeaning we wonŐt actually be using magic in the class.Ó ŇAt all?Ó Zabini asked, an expression of horror on his face. ŇIn our O.W.L. year? WeŐre not going to actually learn how to use any of the spells that weŐre going to be tested on?Ó Harry laughed. ŇYou sound a bit like Hermione right now, you know.Ó Zabini blinked, looking aghast, and Malfoy snickered. *** Harry took a seat beside Zabini and stared in trepidation at Umbridge. She was dressed in a pink frilly cardigan and had an enormous velvet black bow perched atop her head. Out of the corner of his eye, Harry could see Parkinson sneer in disapproval. Umbridge was clearly strict, demanding that the class answer her as a whole when she asked them a question. She explained that the curriculum was Ministry-approved, and would make up for the appalling teaching theyŐd had in previous years. She instructed them all to copy down the Ôcourse aims,Ő which, naturally, said nothing about using magic. Beside him, Zabini thrust his hand into the air before Umbridge could continue. Umbridge peered at him for a moment. ŇYour have a question, MrÉ?Ó ŇZabini,Ó he said, lowering his arm. ŇBlaise Zabini.Ó ŇWhat is it, then?Ó ŇI just need some clarification, Professor Umbridge,Ó he said. His voice sounded more proper than what Harry had heard from him so far. ŇI was just wondering when our practical lessons in defensive magic would be taking place, as they arenŐt mentioned in the course aims.Ó Umbridge made a ÔtuttingŐ sound. ŇThe Ministry-approved course has been crafted in such a way that practical lessons are deemed unnecessary.Ó ŇAs you mentioned, this is our O.W.L. year, maŐam,Ó Zabini continued in that prim and proper manner of speaking. ŇDoes the Defense O.W.L. not contain a practical exam any longer?Ó ŇThe practical exam still exists,Ó said Umbridge. ŇHowever, if you study the theory correctly, the practical part of the exam will not be a problem for you.Ó Harry saw a few Slytherins shifting in their seats, and then, surprisingly, MalfoyŐs hand shot up. ŇYes?Ó Umbridge said, turning to face him. ŇAnd what is your name?Ó ŇMalfoy. Draco Malfoy.Ó The way he said it was uncomfortably familiar to Harry, bringing up all kinds of bad memories from over the years, but when he saw the flicker of alarm on UmbridgeŐs face at hearing MalfoyŐs name, Harry suddenly became pleased. Umbridge calmed her expression and gave Malfoy a falsely saccharine smile. ŇAnd what is your question, Mr. Malfoy?Ó ŇIŐm concerned that without actual practice, we will not be adequately prepared for the practical exam,Ó he said. ŇEspecially since, as you said, the classes in Defense that weŐve taken prior to this year have not been the Ministry-approved standard. ŇBut what about learning how to defend ourselves?Ó Davis suddenly asked. ŇYou will raise your hand before speaking in my classroom, Miss -Ó ŇDavis. ŇWhat good will all this ÔtheoryŐ be when someone attacks us out there?Ó Zabini swiftly kicked Harry in the shin. Harry ignored him. An unpleasant smile spread across UmbridgeŐs face. ŇWho would possibly be attacking you outside of the school, Mr. Potter?Ó ŇGee, I wonder...Ó Harry said mockingly. ŇHow about Lord Voldemort?Ó They could have heard a pin drop in the classroom. ŇTen points from Slytherin, Mr. Potter, for attempting to incite panic,Ó Umbridge said after a moment. ŇYou will immediately cease spreading such vile lies.Ó ŇItŐs not a lie.Ó HarryŐs lip curled upward. ŇCedric Diggory was killed right in front of me,Ó he said. ŇWho do you think was responsible for that?Ó ŇDetention, Mr. Potter,Ó Umbridge said swiftly. ŇClass, please let me assure you that Cedric DiggoryŐs death was nothing more than a tragic accident -Ó ŇIt was not!Ó Harry shouted, rising from his seat. ŇYouŐre just too afraid to admit that heŐs back!Ó Umbridge stared at Harry. She opened her mouth, and then Zabini suddenly got to his feet, as well. ŇThis isnŐt about fear of the Dark Lord, is it?Ó Zabini said. ŇThis is about what youŐre afraid of, Professor.Ó ŇBlaise, what are you doing? Ó Malfoy hissed. Zabini ignored him. ŇYouŐre frightened of having to actually show us defensive spells.Ó His voice was scathing. ŇMaybe you just donŐt want to have to demonstrate any of the practical work because youŐre actually incapable of casting those spells.Ó UmbridgeŐs mouth fell open, and Harry couldnŐt help it - he laughed. She then straightened herself up to her full height - which wasnŐt much - and said, ŇMr. Zabini. They approached UmbridgeŐs desk, where she was just finishing writing a note. She folded it and handed it to Zabini. ŇYou two will immediately bring this to Professor Snape. I will know if you donŐt.Ó Zabini took the note, and then gave her a bizarre kind of half-salute. He grabbed Harry by his good shoulder and began pushing him out of the classroom. In the hallway, Zabini unfolded the note as they walked. ŇWeŐve both been assigned detention for the rest of the week, starting tomorrow.Ó ŇWell, I already have detention with Snape tomorrow, so bully for me,Ó Harry snapped. ŇYou really shouldnŐt have said those things, Potter,Ó Zabini said. ŇAnd what about you?Ó Harry said. ŇYou actually insulted her, personally.Ó Zabini rolled his eyes. ŇI was trying to get you to shut the hell up, actually,Ó he said. Harry stopped in his tracks. ŇWhat?Ó Zabini walked past him, throwing his arms up in frustration. ŇYou wouldnŐt stop running your mouth, and you obviously donŐt realize how much danger youŐre putting yourself in by saying those things -Ó ŇOh, so you donŐt believe me, either?Ó Harry snarled. Zabini finally stopped and turned around, a furious expression on his face. ŇI donŐt know if youŐve put two and two together yet, Potter,Ó he whispered heatedly, Ňbut you are now in the one house in this school that does believe you.Ó Harry froze. Zabini was almost certainly right - Slytherins would believe that Voldemort had returned because their parents had actually been there the night heŐd been restored. Harry wasnŐt exactly sure what to do with that information yet, so he filed it away and started walking again. ŇI canŐt even begin to imagine what youŐve been going through -Ó ŇNo, you really canŐt,Ó Harry snapped. ŇNext time IŐll just bring Voldemort himself.Ó ŇMerlin, please donŐt do that,Ó Zabini said, horrified. ŇIŐm just tired of being called a liar!Ó Harry shouted. He felt beyond frustrated and wanted to throw something, but he had nothing in his hands. Suddenly overwhelmed, he whipped his wand out of his robes and sent a blast of raw magic at the wall. Harry felt a trickle of satisfaction at seeing tiny bits and pieces of the stone fly into the air. Zabini was silent for a moment. ŇYou have quite a temper, donŐt you?Ó he finally offered. ŇWhat are you two doing out of class?Ó SnapeŐs voice rang out from in front of them. ŇWhy, might I ask?Ó Zabini walked forward and held out the note from Umbridge, and Snape took it from him and unfolded it. As he was reading, his expression became more and more annoyed. ŇIn,Ó he said, holding open the door to his office. Harry and Zabini walked in, Snape behind them. The door slammed forcibly, and both of them jumped. ŇYouŐve both been assigned detention with for the rest of the week. ŇThis note merely says that you, Potter, shouted at her, and that you, Zabini, insulted her.Ó Harry remembered what Zabini had said, and he let out a snicker. Snape shot him a glare and he instantly quieted. ŇWell?Ó ŇSheŐs not including any practical work in this yearŐs course, sir,Ó Zabini said. That overly proper tone had returned. ŇThis is our O.W.L. year. ŇYou need to learn how to deal with her like a Slytherin, not a Gryffindor.Ó His expression then became almost murderous. ŇAnd I will not have you somehow convincing one of the most well-behaved Slytherins in your year to emulate your Gryffindor antics.Ó Harry swallowed. You will serve your detention with me on Saturday afternoon, instead. Be here immediately after lunch.Ó Harry nodded, unhappy at having to serve detention on a weekend, but he felt too drained to argue. He exited SnapeŐs office and found Zabini leaning up against the wall. ŇHe didnŐt murder you,Ó Zabini said. ŇThatŐs justÉ swell.Ó He was clearly irritated, and Harry remembered what Snape had said about Zabini usually being well-behaved. ŇYou donŐtÉ get in trouble very often, do you?Ó Harry asked. ŇWhich, considering at least two of them tried to kill me, is saying something.Ó ŇWhat?!Ó Zabini exclaimed. Harry waved it off. ŇLesson one: being loud and brash isnŐt going to get you what you want. What we need to do is use these detentions to figure out what she wants.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó ŇWe need to get her to reveal what she values,Ó Zabini said. ŇThat way, we can strike at her where it will hurt the most.Ó Chapter5:The Detention Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Thank you! In the Slytherin common room later that night, Malfoy beckoned Harry over to a circle of chairs. ŇFifth year meeting tonight,Ó he said. ŇEveryone else already knows about it. Make sure youŐre back here in half an hour.Ó ŇI thought the fifth years met weekly, not nightly,Ó Harry said. ŇUse the bathroom or whatever you need to do,Ó Parkinson said. ŇItŐs going to be a long one.Ó ŇI have homework,Ó Harry protested. ŇThe rest of us do, too,Ó Malfoy said. ŇDo it now, or do it after.Ó Harry didnŐt want to think about what the meeting could be about - perhaps another intervention about being Ôtoo GryffindorŐ towards Umbridge? HeŐd heard enough of that from Snape and Zabini. He followed MalfoyŐs advice and distracted himself with finishing up his essay for Snape, and he was moderately satisfied with the results. Perhaps, despite the mandatory meeting, Malfoy would still look over it afterward. Harry found himself wishing that Hermione was looking over his essay, not Malfoy. At least Malfoy actually seemed decent at Potions. Despite what Harry had thought over the years, it hadnŐt just been SnapeŐs Slytherin favoritism that made Malfoy shine in that class. At the thirty-minute mark, the fifth years descended on the common room. ŇSo what is this about?Ó Harry said. He could already tell that it was almost certainly about him. If their ÔweeklyŐ meetings were getting moved up so often, it had to be the new factor, which was Harry, himself. Malfoy didnŐt dance around the issue, instead getting straight to the point. ŇWe need to talk about the Dark Lord,Ó he said. Harry stared. ŇThe rest of us know exactly where our families are aligned,Ó Zabini said. ŇYou donŐt know, and you should.Ó ŇWe want to make sure you feel safe in our house,Ó Parkinson said. ŇAnd taking into account... what happened, I canŐt imagine how you could feel safe here,Ó Nott said. ŇI meanÉÓ ŇI know you have no reason to believe us, all things considered,Ó Malfoy offered. ŇBut you are in our house. You are a Slytherin. There is a rule in Slytherin - no matter your alliances or your familyŐs alliances, our covenant still stands.Ó Harry looked at all of them with wide eyes. He could tell Malfoy had been right - this conversation would indeed be a long one. ŇSo, to start off,Ó Malfoy said. ŇI think we need to ask the question that Theo wanted to ask the other night.Ó ŇWeŐre going to start there? Really?Ó Parkinson asked in disbelief. ŇThis question relates toÉ what you were so angry about this afternoon, Harry,Ó Zabini said. Harry hesitated, then nodded at Nott. ŇGo ahead.Ó Malfoy suddenly sat up and pulled out his wand. ŇWait, Theo.Ó He whispered a spell and then re-pocketed his wand. ŇSilencing ward. I donŐt think you want everyone hearing this.Ó Harry tilted his head to the side, studying Malfoy. ŇI thought Slytherins kept each otherŐs secrets,Ó he said carefully. ŇAs a rule, we do,Ó Parkinson said. ŇBut there are certain things that we simply donŐt broadcast to the entire house.Ó Harry stared at Malfoy and Parkinson for another moment, then turned his attention to Nott. ŇWhatŐs your question?Ó ŇIÉÓ Nott sighed. ŇLike I said the other night, the question might actually make you angry.Ó ŇIŐd like to know, too,Ó Malfoy said. ŇI can ask.Ó ŇNo, Draco,Ó Nott said. ŇYou lack tact,Ó Parkinson said. ŇI do not!Ó Malfoy protested. Harry just sat and watched the exchange, feeling a little bewildered. He was quickly learning that Slytherins werenŐt all that different from Gryffindors, at least in how they spoke to one another. ŇOkay,Ó Nott said. ŇI want to ask you something, and I will completely understand if you donŐt want to answer. Being in our house... it must be completely strange for you, and I donŐt want to make you more uncomfortable than you probably already are. So if you donŐt want to say anything, you donŐt have to. Or if you donŐt want to say anything now but would be okay with it later -Ó ŇTheo, youŐre babbling,Ó Greengrass quipped. ŇJust ask whatever it is you want to ask,Ó Harry said tiredly. Nott took a deep breath. ŇWhat actually happened... in the graveyard?Ó Harry was taken aback. HeŐd been expecting a question relating to it, but not one quite so on the nose. ŇLook - you know my father was there,Ó Nott said. Your father, and other peopleŐs fathers, Harry thought, but he was vaguely surprised to hear Nott admit it to him. Although Malfoy very nearly admitted it last night... ŇAnd IŐve heard my fatherŐs side of the story, but after this past summer IŐm starting to think my father might be half-insane,Ó Nott continued. ŇI justÉÓ ŇSomeÉ people... are clearly biased and might not be telling the whole truth,Ó Malfoy said quietly, and Harry turned to look at him, his eyes wide. Malfoy didnŐt meet his eyes; instead, he stared down at his hands. ŇI want to hear your side of the story, Potter,Ó Nott said. ŇHarry.Ó HarryŐs first name was said very deliberately, not at all as an afterthought. ŇBecause certain things in my fatherŐs story donŐt add up.Ó "And his father's story and my father's story don't even align with one another," Malfoy added. "And ours haven't said anything at all," Crabbe said, gesturing to himself and Goyle. Harry took a breath and sat back in his chair. This was bizarre. Harry had been sorted into Slytherin - VoldemortŐs house - and now he was being questioned about what had actually happened by the children of VoldemortŐs followers. After finding out that wizarding world had been painting him as an unhinged, pathological liar, the fact that the children of Death Eaters were willing to even listen to what he had to say seemed unbelievable. And it wasnŐt just their willingness to hear him that was throwing Harry for a loop - it was the fact that they actually seemed to want to know. That was far more than Harry could say for the rest of the wizarding world. But as Harry started picturing that night again in his mind, he could hear VoldemortŐs high pitched command -kill the spare - and he could see that flash of green light. ŇUm,Ó Harry said. ŇIŐm not angry. IŐm actually kind ofÉ glad that you want to listen to me. Or seem to.Ó Then he sighed. ŇI do want to, but IŐm just not sure if IÉ can. I meanÉÓ He closed his eyes. ŇCedric was killed right in front of me,Ó he said quietly. He expected heckling, or someone to call him weak. Instead, Bulstrode finally spoke up. ŇI canŐt imagine what that must have been like,Ó she said, her voice quiet. ŇPotter,Ó Malfoy said, and Harry opened his eyes. ŇIŐd be lying if I said I didnŐt want to hear it, but you donŐt have to speak of this if you donŐt want to.Ó Harry was almost more startled at that than nearly anything else that had happened so far in his time in Slytherin. Malfoy almost sounded kind. ŇI want to,Ó Harry said again. And, with difficulty, he did. He told them the whole grizzly tale - the end of the Triwizard Tournament, CedricŐs death, and the ritual. He struggled through a few parts, but he managed to get through it. The Slytherins simply sat and listened, until one thing seemed to grab Malfoy by the throat. ŇThe Dark Lord had you unarmed and surrounded, and he gave you back your wand?Ó Malfoy said, aghast. Harry nodded. Malfoy let out a noise of disgust. ŇThatŐs an immensely stupid move on his part.Ó Harry stared at Malfoy, and Parkinson kicked Malfoy in the shin. He apparently realized how his words sounded, and he shook his head. ŇIŐm notÉ ungrateful that you managed to make it out,Ó Malfoy said. ŇI just find it odd that my father reveres someone who would be so stupid as to arm his enemy.Ó Harry was a bit bewildered at that statement. HeŐd always thought Malfoy worshipped his father; heŐd never heard him criticize Lucius before. He continued. He included the part about their wands connecting, but he didnŐt explain that he knew that it had happened due to their wandsŐ twin cores; that wasnŐt information he wanted to share with anyone. He finished up by explaining that he summoned the portkey back into his hands, reappearing at Hogwarts. The Slytherins stared at him for a few moments after he finished the tale. ŇYeah,Ó Nott said finally. ŇThatŐs not at all what my father said.Ó ŇAmong other things, my father essentially implied that you fell on that portkey, and that was how you got away,Ó Malfoy said. ŇAnd who do you believe?Ó Harry asked, narrowing his eyes. ŇHonestly? You,Ó Nott said. Malfoy agreed with a quick nod. ŇYour story makes much more sense than my fatherŐs.Ó ŇYou can tell someone is lying when there are contradictions in their story,Ó Malfoy said. He had a contemplative look on his face, and he leaned back on the sofa, evidently deep in thought. An enormous swell of gratitude suddenly came over Harry, and he had to look down at his feet. ŇThank you,Ó he said. ŇI donŐt think I can explain how good it feels to actuallyÉÓ ŇHave someone believe you?Ó Zabini finished for him. ŇYeah.Ó ŇWell, I did tell you that youÉÓ Harry chuckled. ŇThat I was now in the only house that actually would,Ó he said. ŇYeah. ItŐs still appreciated.Ó ŇIŐm sorry you went through all that, Harry,Ó Parkinson said quietly. ŇNo one should have to endure that, much less someone who was only fourteen years old.Ó Harry stared at her in shock and amazement. ŇRight,Ó Malfoy suddenly said. ŇPotter, itŐs time we cover some of the basics.Ó Harry blinked. ŇWhat?Ó ŇYou know my father is a Death Eater,Ó Malfoy said. ŇAs are TheoŐs, VinceŐs, and GregŐs.Ó Harry nodded, and the Slytherins launched into explaining where their families laid on the ÔDark Lord spectrum,Ő as Zabini called it. They laid out that ParkinsonŐs family were definite Voldemort sympathizers, but there werenŐt any actual Death Eaters in her line aside from her motherŐs cousin - who happened to be NottŐs father. Bulstrode, Greengrass, and ZabiniŐs families were all neutral and wouldnŐt do anything to help either side, though Zabini said his mother had expressed hope that Voldemort would win. Davis was the only one aside from Harry who came from a family that firmly stood against Voldemort. She revealed that she was a half-blood like Harry, and that she worried what would happen to her and her family should Voldemort win. They talked far into the night, and Harry found that he genuinely enjoyed the conversation even though none of the Slytherins revealed where their own loyalties lay. Harry supposed he was actually okay with that; after all, they all seemed to agree that Harry himself had been pulled into the conflict far sooner than he should have. Throughout their talk, Harry began to find himself growing more comfortable with them. He realized he enjoyed making them almost comically uncomfortable with jokes about VoldemortŐs lack of a nose, and in turn, they teased him about his Gryffindor luck at being mortally threatened every year heŐd been at Hogwarts. The night broke down one barrier between them. HermioneŐs mouth dropped open; she obviously couldnŐt imagine being that disrespectful to a teacher, even if it was Umbridge. Harry also noticed that Draco and Pansy were fairly reserved for most of the class. Care of Magical Creatures tended to be one of their favorite classes in which to mock Gryffindors, so their silence was deafening. As he promised, he didnŐt mention it, and he wondered if Hermione had even noticed. One other person was oddly silent through the whole class: Ron. He didnŐt mention RonŐs reserved nature, and neither did Hermione. But towards the end of class, Harry saw Hermione look back and forth between himself and Ron with an unhappy expression on her face. *** They stood outside the door to UmbridgeŐs office, and Blaise gave Harry a sideways glance. ŇYouŐre going to say exactly what you told me you would say when we were at dinner, right?Ó he asked. ŇNo surprises?Ó Harry gave him a jerky nod. ŇYouŐll keep that impressive temper of yours in check?Ó ŇAs long as she does,Ó Harry spit out. Blaise sighed. ŇI suppose thatŐs the best IŐm going to get, isnŐt it?Ó He raised his hand and knocked on the door. They heard Umbridge tell them to come in, and they both took a breath and entered. ŇGood evening Mr. Potter, Mr. Zabini,Ó she said as they stood in front of her desk. ŇGood evening, Professor Umbridge,Ó they both said obediently. She gave them a sickeningly sweet smile, and gestured to the two small tables beside her desk, each with a single piece of parchment. ŇTake a seat, please.Ó ŇProfessor?Ó Blaise said. ŇI was hoping we might both be able to say something to you before we begin tonightŐs detention.Ó His voice had, as Harry expected, regained the proper tone. ŇIŐve never had a detention before, so IŐm not sure if thatŐsÉ done, but -Ó UmbridgeŐs eyes narrowed. ŇWhat is it that you want to say?Ó ŇI want to offer my sincerest apologies,Ó Blaise said with a slight incline of his head. ŇI realize this is no excuse, but I believe that the stress of my O.W.L. year was affecting me far more than I expected it to. I fear that I may have taken that stress out on you.Ó He shook his head sadly. ŇI never should have said those appalling things to you. IŐm truly ashamed of myself.Ó Umbridge stared at him for a moment, and then gave him one of those disgusting, sugary smiles. ŇApology accepted, Mr. Zabini.Ó She turned to Harry. ŇAnd you?Ó ŇI also want to apologize,Ó Harry said. ŇThe events of last spring were... confusing, to say the least. Cedric died right in front of me, and itŐs been haunting me ever since. I may have beenÉ mistaken about what I saw happen.Ó He hated every word coming out of his mouth, but heŐd made a promise to Blaise. ŇI only shared what I thought I saw.Ó He swallowed. He didnŐt want to say the next part. He saw Blaise shift his weight as he clearly waited for Harry to continue. ŇI think Harry may have beenÉ traumatized by what he witnessed, maŐam,Ó he said, obviously trying to fill the empty space that Harry had left. Harry gave a jerky nod, despite the guilt at using CedricŐs death as an excuse. ŇAnd I fear thatÉ certain peopleÉÓ He took a breath to steady himself. ŇCertain people might have taken advantage of that.Ó It wasnŐt quite what Blaise had coached him to say, but Umbridge still got his meaning. ŇYou mean your headmaster,Ó she said slowly. Harry hesitated, then nodded. There. HeŐd done it. HeŐd thrown Dumbledore under the bus to Umbridge. Despite his recent anger towards Dumbledore, it still made Harry feel ill. UmbridgeŐs smile became even more sickening. ŇWell, Mr. Potter, I must say - this is most unexpected,Ó she said. ŇIŐm pleased, but itŐs unexpected.Ó Even with her smile, she didnŐt sound entirely convinced. ŇNo one actually bothered to talk to me about what happened afterward,Ó Harry continued, and in the corner of his vision, he saw BlaiseŐs eyes widen slightly. It was understandable; Harry hadnŐt planned on saying more, but he wanted to make sure Umbridge believed the lie. ŇI had the whole summer to go over what I thought I saw, with no one telling me otherwise, so I think I must have justÉ convinced myself it was true.Ó He swallowed nervously. ŇDid you not read the reports in the Daily Prophet, Mr. Potter?Ó Umbridge asked. ŇSurely reading that the Dark Lord has indeed not returned should have given you someÉ peace of mind.Ó ŇI have to admit that I stopped reading the Prophet when I saw how it was speaking about me, Professor Umbridge.Ó Umbridge smiled. ŇI suppose thatŐs understandable.Ó ŇI know now that it isnŐt true. The... Dark Lord -Ó Harry imperceptibly clenched his teeth at that, "- is dead." And that apparently worked, because Umbridge let out a titter. ŇI knew you would come to see it our way eventually, Mr. Potter,Ó she said. She sighed happily. ŇI do still need both of you to serve your detention, of course.Ó ŇOf course, maŐam,Ó Blaise said. ŇWe werenŐt expecting to get out of it. We just wanted to make sure you knew how much we regret our actions in class today.Ó ŇNaturally!Ó Umbridge said. ŇTake your seats, please.Ó Harry and Blaise sat down, and Harry glanced at Blaise nervously. ŇMr. Potter,Ó Umbridge said, moving in between their two tables and looking down at Harry. ŇI must admit that I was quite surprised at seeing you move to Slytherin house. As I mentioned in your class, that was my house when I attended Hogwarts.Ó Harry nodded. ŇIt seems we have more in common than either of us may have initially realized,Ó she continued, and HarryŐs stomach twisted up at that. ŇI think that we will come to understand one another sooner than I thought we would have.Ó Despite his ill feeling at having anything in common with the horrible woman, Harry said, ŇI wholeheartedly agree, Professor. I think we will.Ó Behind her, Harry saw Blaise flash him a now-familiar smirk. Apparently, Harry was being a very good Slytherin. Umbridge smiled widely at both of them, then turned back to her desk. ŇYouŐll be writing lines today, children.Ó She retrieved two quills and inkwells and placed them in front of Harry and Blaise. ŇI admit that I was not expecting such admirable behavior from the two of you in this detention, so your lines will be as I initially plannedÉ but despite your apologies, I think itŐs all the better that you both walk out of here truly knowing what you did wrong. DonŐt you?Ó ŇYes, maŐam,Ó Blaise and Harry said. ŇMr. Zabini, you will write ÔI must not insult a professorŐs abilities.Ő Mr. Potter, your line is ÔI must not tell lies.ŐÓ A wave of fury swept through Harry, and he tried to tamp it down as he stared down at his parchment. ŇHow many times?Ó he asked. ŇAs many as necessary,Ó she said. ŇI shall know when itŐs enough.Ó And so they set to writing their lines. About twenty minutes into the detention, they both requested another piece of parchment, as they had been filled. Umbridge smiled and she handed them two more. ŇProfessor Umbridge?Ó Blaise asked as he dipped his quill in his inkwell to start on his new sheet. ŇYes, Mr. Zabini?Ó ŇI was just wondering... IŐm not sure if itŐs appropriate to ask you this at detention, butÉÓ Blaise sat up straighter in his seat. ŇIŐve heard that you work for the Ministry for Magic.Ó ŇI do.Ó ŇThatŐs very impressive,Ó Blaise said, putting his quill to paper again. ŇI know you must have things that you need to accomplish tonight, but I was wondering if you had any advice to a young O.W.L. student who aspires to work for the Ministry one day.Ó He scribbled down another line. ŇI mean - IŐll have to decide what N.E.W.T. classes to take at the end of this school year, and I was wondering which classes might give me anÉ advantage over other potential candidates. Or might you have any suggestions as to which Ministry departments might be willing to take on a recent Hogwarts graduate?Ó Umbridge smiled broadly and seemed to puff up in her seat. ŇYou seem like an intelligent, hard-working, and well-spoken young man, Mr. Zabini,Ó she said. ŇAnd I will dare to say that you would be an excellent fit for the Ministry.Ó ŇThank you, maŐam.Ó ŇNow, then, as for your N.E.W.T.sÉ what are your electives again, my dear?Ó ŇI take Arithmancy and Ancient Runes.Ó ŇThose would be excellent N.E.W.T.s to have,Ó Umbridge said. ŇSince the Ministry has such a wide array of responsibilities, having one or both of those will open many doors for you. Of courseÉÓ She tittered quietly. ŇWhile having the proper education does matter, something else that matters is who you know.Ó ŇNaturally, Professor,Ó Blaise said smoothly. ŇAs the Senior Undersecretary, I have countless connections in the Ministry, and the more intelligent Ministry employees know who holds sway over many of their careers,Ó she said. ŇIf you keep up your good behavior, Mr. Zabini, I may be able to arrange some introductions for you.Ó ŇReally?Ó Blaise said, his eyes wide. ŇI would be extremely grateful if you could.Ó As planned, Harry remained silent for the rest of the detention. He continued writing lines and watching Blaise be sickeningly sweet to Umbridge, and Umbridge just soaked it up. Towards the end, she took their pieces of parchment and looked them over. ŇI think we can all agree that this night has gone far better than expected, wouldnŐt you say?Ó ŇYes, maŐam,Ó they both said. ŇI know detention was assigned for the rest of the week,Ó she said. ŇBut with your earlier apologies, I think one night was enough for both of you.Ó She peered at both at them and gave them that awful sugary smile. ŇLetŐs not repeat this again, shall we?Ó ŇI can assure you, Professor,Ó Blaise said. ŇIt wonŐt happen again.Ó *** ŇSo?Ó Harry asked once he and Blaise had returned to the common room. ŇShe clearly values her power,Ó Blaise said. ŇHer position at the Ministry. The problem is that the only person I have any kind of connection to that would be able to actually strike at her power isÉÓ ŇLemme guess: Lucius Malfoy,Ó Harry said. ŇRight,Ő Blaise said. ŇAnd IŐm not certain if Lucius has said anything to Draco about what UmbridgeŐs role is here; if itŐs a plot that Lucius is involved in or approves of, weŐd be out of luck.Ó He considered that for a moment. ŇI suppose it wouldnŐt hurt to ask him, though.Ó Harry wondered exactly when the thought of Draco writing his father became one that he could actually appreciate, and then the guilt that overtook him at the idea of using an enemy against another enemy overwhelmed that thought. ŇIf that doesnŐt work, weŐll just have to come up with something else,Ó Blaise said. ŇWe know where to hit her; weŐll just have to figure out how.Ó Harry returned to their dorm to use what little he had left of the night to work on homework. Draco was the only other person in the room, and he handed him his corrected Potions essay as soon as Harry walked in. Harry quickly rewrote his Potions essay, and then decided it was finally time he wrote to Sirius. Harry ran into a problem at that, though: he still had no idea what to say. He also knew that it was likely that Ron or Ginny or the twins had already written to Mrs. Weasley, or that Dumbledore had visited the Order headquarters. Sirius may have already known what had happened, and Harry felt guilty for not writing to his godfather sooner. He scrapped one attempt after another. Some boldly asked if his father had made a habit of attacking Slytherins in the halls when they were at school. Others explained HarryŐs re.sorting, and how being in Slytherin had been going far better than Harry could have ever expected. Still others begged Sirius to remember that, despite his hatred of Slytherin, Harry was still the same person heŐd always been. But are you? a nasty voice inside his head whispered. At detention earlier that night, Harry had finally become what everyone in the wizarding world was accusing him of being: a liar. Even worse, heŐd used Cedric DiggoryŐs death as an excuse to get out of trouble. Harry knew that it was for the best; he needed UmbridgeŐs eyes off of him. That didnŐt change the twisted up feeling in HarryŐs stomach. He sighed and crumbled up yet another note, and walked over to chuck the parchment in the fire. ŇHaving trouble with your homework, Harry?Ó Draco asked, evidently amused at how many pieces of parchment Harry had burnt in such a short amount of time. Harry froze. Despite the recent inroads heŐd made with the Slytherins, he didnŐt want Draco to know who he was writing to. Harry thought about it, and realized that he didnŐt have to discuss the recipient of the letter. ŇNo,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇThereŐs just someone that I need to tell about myÉ my re-sorting, and I have no idea what to say.Ó ŇWhatŐs so hard about it?Ó Draco asked. ŇÔDear so-and-so, IŐve been re-sorted to Slytherin. Hope youŐre doing well. Ever yours, Scarhead.ŐÓ Harry glared, but there wasnŐt any true mirth behind it. He shook his head again. ŇItŐs not just thatÉ itŐsÉÓ He sighed, turning to face Draco. ŇThis person knew my father when he was in school,Ó he said carefully. ŇSo?Ó Draco raised an eyebrow. ŇMy suggestion still stands.Ó ŇNo, itŐs justÉÓ Harry clenched a fist. ŇIŐve been... hearing things, and putting the pieces together.Ó He let his eyes drift towards the picture of his parents on his nightstand. ŇI had no idea about these ongoing attacks on Slytherins, and IŐm beginning to suspectÉ thatÉÓ He trailed off. Draco waited patiently for him to continue. ŇI think my father might have been one of those attackers,Ó Harry said quietly. ŇBack when he was in school here.Ó DracoŐs eyes widened. ŇAre you sure?Ó ŇNo,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd I feel like even asking about it isÉÓ He paused. ŇI donŐt know. Shitting all over my fatherŐs memory?Ó He let out a frustrated sigh and walked over to his bed. He flopped onto his back and pinched the bridge of his nose. ŇThisÉ person,Ó Draco said thoughtfully. ŇIs he the only one you could ask?Ó ŇYes,Ó Harry said. Then he realized that wasnŐt exactly true, and he sat up. ŇActually, it might not be, but the only other person I could askÉ completely and utterly hates my guts.Ó ŇLet me guess: Professor Snape?Ó Harry stared at Draco in amazement. Apparently, he was far more intuitive than Harry had thought. Draco shrugged. ŇI know they had to be about the same age, and IŐve heard Professor Snape make more than one uncomplimentary statement about your father. They clearly knew each other, and they likely attended Hogwarts at the same time.Ó Harry nodded. ŇThey did,Ó he admitted. ŇI think you should ask him,Ó Draco said, and Harry blanched at the thought. ŇYou havenŐt missed the fact that Snape despises me, right?Ó he asked. Draco smirked. ŇHe also obviously despised your father. ÔShitting on your fatherŐs memory,Ő as you say, might score you some approval points with him.Ó ŇThatŐsÉ kind of gross, Draco,Ó Harry said, wrinkling his nose. ŇThatŐs Slytherin,Ó Draco said. ŇAnd it may also get you the information youŐre seeking.Ó He turned back to his desk. ŇBesides, Professor Snape is also your head of house,Ó he said reasonably. ŇYouŐre supposed to be able to go to him with problems you might have. I would say that being haunted by what your father may or may not have done constitutes a Ôproblem.ŐÓ Harry flopped back on his bed again, thinking Draco had just brought their conversation to a close. ŇHarry, even if this thing about your father turns out to be true,Ó Draco suddenly said, Ňit could be worse.Ó He half-turned his head, just enough for Harry to see a melancholy expression on his face. ŇLook at my father.Ó Harry could only stare in shock as his old rival turned back to his homework. He watched him for a full minute and wondered exactly what kind of relationship Draco actually had with Lucius Malfoy. Whatever it was, Harry supposed it was nothing like heŐd imagined. He finally returned to his desk and stared at yet another piece of blank parchment. He sighed, scribbled down a few lines, and blew on the ink to dry. Dear Snuffles, You may have already heard by now, but I was placed in Slytherin. Please write me back. Hope youŐre doing well. Love, Harry Chapter 6: TheDecision Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Thank you! ŇProfessor Umbridge just informed me that she let you out of detention for the remainder of the week,Ó Snape told Harry at breakfast. Harry nodded. If Snape was surprised or pleased by HarryŐs newfound Slytherin ability to slither out of UmbridgeŐs grasp, he didnŐt show it. ŇEven so, the rescheduling of your detention with me on Saturday still stands,Ó he said. ŇIŐve already arranged for it. Immediately after lunch, Potter. Do not be late.Ó ŇYes, sir.Ó As Snape walked down the length of the great hall, Harry began to wonder how he could possibly ask him about what HarryŐs father had been like. Snape had made it clear to Harry over the years that he was biased against HarryŐs father to an absurd level. Would he actually tell the truth if Harry asked him? Harry doubted it. Defense Against the Dark Arts was, once again, nearly unbearable, but the death glares he kept receiving from Blaise somehow managed to keep Harry from exploding in her class again. Umbridge seemed pleased as punch at how well-mannered her fifth year Slytherin class was, and she awarded the class as a whole 50 points for their exemplary behavior. Blaise had heard from some of the other Slytherin years that she was doing the same in their classes. Apparently, she was just as biased as Snape in SlytherinŐs favor. That still didnŐt endear many of the Slytherins to her, especially the fifth years and seventh years, who both had to complete tests at the end of the year that would potentially decide their futures. Harry overheard one of the seventh years complaining about her sabotaging their N.E.W.T.s, saying that she was currently plotting UmbridgeŐs murder and covering it up by making it seem like a horrific accident. Harry wasnŐt entirely sure if the student was joking or not. As they worked on their seemingly endless stream of homework in the library that night, Harry asked Daphne and Blaise about what heŐd overheard the seventh year say, wondering if it could have possibly been true. Daphne shrugged, saying that she honestly didnŐt care. Harry stared. He may have despised Umbridge, and he desperately wanted her out of the school, but he couldnŐt imagine actually wanting someone dead. Except Voldemort, he thought suddenly. Pretty sure I want him dead. ŇButÉ that does remind me,Ó Blaise said, tapping his quill on his chin. ŇDraco said he sent his father an owl about Umbridge this morning. ŇYou need to stay out of her way. He still wasnŐt sure where the twins stood on his re-sorting, and he knew them well enough that he definitely didnŐt want to be on the receiving end of their bad side if they werenŐt happy with him. ŇWeŐve come to notice that our dear brother is being a right twit about your new house placement,Ó George said. ŇWe felt that we should inform you that wonŐt have to worry about the same from us.Ó Harry smiled in relief. ŇThank you,Ó he said genuinely. He felt as if the number of friends he had in Gryffindor had just doubled. Which, Harry supposed, was technically true. ŇIn fact, weŐll let you in on a secret that no one in our family knows aside from the two of us,Ó Fred said. George looked around them for possible eavesdroppers, clearly being just a touch overdramatic. ŇThe sorting hat told both Fred and I that we could have gone to Slytherin if we wanted,Ó he whispered conspiratorially. ŇCunning and ambition coming out of our bottoms, it said,Ó added Fred. ŇBut weŐre a bit too reckless for your house, I think.Ó ŇWhat?!Ó Harry exclaimed, his mouth falling open. ŇWeasleys in Slytherin,Ó Daphne considered. ŇThat would have beenÉÓ Ň... a complete nightmare!Ó Blaise finished for her, a horrified look on his face. He then gave Harry a sideways glance and seemed to self-correct himself. ŇNot due to your name, of course, but because your propensity for pranks isÉ well-known.Ó Both twins grinned at Blaise. ŇWhich is why weŐre here, Mr. Slytherin,Ó Fred said. ŇZabini,Ó Blaise immediately corrected. ŇMr. Slytherin Zabini,Ó George said with a slight incline of his head. Blaise rolled his eyes in response. ŇWe have a proposal for you, dear Harry,Ó Fred said. ŇOne that may interest you as our investor,Ó George continued. ŇInvestor?Ó Daphne repeated, her eyebrows shooting upward in interest. ŇHave you neglected to inform us that you are an aspiring businessman, Harry?Ó ŇUhÉÓ Harry said, somewhat stupidly. ŇYoung Harry here saw a prime financial opportunity and he took it,Ó George said. ŇWe should have seen the Slytherin in him then,Ó Fred added with a wink. Harry sighed. They werenŐt being entirely truthful, of course; Harry had only given them his Triwizard winnings because he hadnŐt wanted them. ŇSo whatŐs your proposal?Ó he finally asked. ŇYour recent re-sorting, dear Harry, has opened a previously closed line of business,Ó George said. ŇYou can get our products into the one house in this school that we once had no connections in,Ó Fred continued. ŇItŐs currently like a roaring waterfall and weŐre still trying to get it down to a leaky faucet.Ó ŇCharming,Ó Daphne said sardonically. ŇNot many Slytherins will be interested in purposely making themselves sick. Do you have any other products?Ó Fred and George looked at each other in deliberation. Harry considered Fred and GeorgeŐs other inventions, scouring his brain to try and remember if they had come up with that Slytherins might find tempting, and then it struck him. ŇExtendable Ears.Ó Blaise raised an eyebrow at that. ŇÔExtendableÉ Ears?ŐÓ ŇExtendable Ears!Ó Fred and George said together, pumping their fists in the air, and they grinned at Daphne. Daphne rolled her eyes. ŇDonŐt look at me; IŐm not saying it, too.Ó ŇYes, those would appeal to Slytherins,Ó George agreed. ŇDo you have any more?Ó Harry asked. ŇOr did your mother manage to get them all?Ó He remembered Mrs. Weasley had found a huge swath of them and destroyed them in her usual fury. A smirk worthy of a Slytherin appeared on FredŐs face. ŇShe did not get them all, dear Harry.Ó ŇWhat are Extendable Ears, exactly?Ó Daphne asked, curious. George opened his shoulder bag and started digging. ŇAn invention of ours that allow the user to - ah!Ó He found what he was looking for and pulled out a few long strings, with ears attached to the end of each one. He plopped them on the table and one of the ears bounced over towards Daphne. ŇEww!Ó Daphne slid her chair back noisily, disgusted. ŇThose look appalling!Ó Madame Pince glared at them from across the room. ŇBut also oh-so appealing,Ó Fred said, lowering his voice. ŇThese little delights will allow the user to eavesdrop on conversations in other rooms, provided the door to the room hasnŐt had the Imperturbable charm cast on it.Ó Daphne looked intrigued at that. ŇWeŐve also improved on the model you tried over the summer, Harry,Ó George said. ŇThese will stretch up to 100 meters.Ó Harry raised an eyebrow. ŇImpressive.Ó ŇOne on the house, dear financer.Ó George untangled one string and handed it to Harry. ŇAnyone else?Ó ŇIŐll take two,Ó Daphne said, already opening her bag. ŇHow much?Ó Fred grinned. ŇFor you, young lady? Five galleons for both.Ó Harry thought this seemed a little steep, but Daphne dug out the galleons and handed them over without protest, and George unwound two strings for her in exchange. ŇIf these work as advertised,Ó Daphne said, tucking them into the front pocket of her bag, ŇIŐll be back for more.Ó ŇPleasure doing business with you, Miss Slytherin.Ó ŇGreengrass.Ó ŇMiss Slytherin Greengrass, then.Ó *** Harry felt out of sorts the following morning. He hadnŐt gotten a good nightŐs sleep. For once, he didnŐt dream of the graveyard, but of wandering through endless halls in search of something. He didnŐt even know what heŐd been looking for, but it felt like the thought was just out of his grasp every time he awoke. It left an unsettled feeling in his stomach, and he picked at his food at breakfast. ŇAre you okay, Harry?Ó Tracey asked. Harry just grunted. ŇYou sound like Theo,Ó commented Daphne. ŇI didnŐt sleep well,Ó he admitted and continued swirling his food around on his plate. ŇHowŐs your shoulder?Ó Blaise asked. The sling had come off that morning. ŇFine.Ó Pansy and Draco took their usual seats across from Harry just in time for the mail to arrive. Harry looked up at the ceiling anxiously, hoping to see an owl winging his way. He hadnŐt heard from Sirius yet. HeŐd only sent his letter the day prior, but he hoped that Sirius wouldnŐt delay in getting back to him. An owl did not arrive for him, and Harry tried to hide his disappointment. Fortunately, the other Slytherins were distracted by the eagle owl that came for Draco. ŇThatŐs your fatherŐs, isnŐt it?Ó Blaise asked quietly, and HarryŐs attention was instantly on Draco, as well. Draco took the scroll and unfurled it. Draco merely passed Blaise the parchment, and Harry read over BlaiseŐs shoulder. Draco, Dolores Umbridge has been posted at Hogwarts to do some much-needed house.cleaning, which is far more important than her abilities as a professor. Leave her be and allow her to do her job. I have confidence that you will be prepared for your O.W.L.s, regardless of the structure of her course. Sincerely, Your Father ŇThatŐs pretty much what I expected,Ó Blaise said with a sigh. ŇLooks like itŐs on to plan B.Ó ŇWhatŐs plan B?Ó Harry asked. ŇIŐll let you know when I figure it out,Ó Blaise said. ŇI can always go back to shouting at her,Ó Harry offered. ŇItŐd be easy.Ó He'd barely been able to reign himself during their last class. Blaise rolled his eyes. ŇI will shout at her, if necessary,Ó he said. ŇYou will stay quiet as a mouse and smile sweetly whenever she so much as looks at you.Ó Harry made a face. ŇGross.Ó ŇSpeaking of Ôgross,ŐÓ Daphne said. ŇHarry, let those Weasley twins know that IŐm impressed with their work.Ó She wrinkled her nose. ŇEven if their inventions are gross. ŇYou were consorting with Weasleys?Ó Pansy asked Daphne, clearly dismayed. ŇOh!Ó Harry said. ŇDraco, you know the list of Ňoff-limitsÓ students?Ó Draco nodded, and based off of his expression of consternation it looked like he knew exactly what Harry was about to ask. ŇCan you add Fred and George Weasley to that list?Ó Draco let out an incredibly dramatic, long-suffering sigh. ŇVery well.Ó ŇAlthough we donŐt usually mess with those two, anyway,Ó Pansy admitted. ŇTheir revenge tactics are a bitÉÓ ŇSlytherin?Ó Blaise said, smirking. ŇSometimes, yes.Ó ŇMaybe thatŐs because they could have been sorted to our house, if they wanted,Ó Blaise said. ŇAnd the more I think about that claim of theirs, the more that makes sense.Ó A look of dread appeared on DracoŐs face. ŇThe thought of the Weasley twins in SlytherinÉÓ he shuddered. ŇIŐm actually a bit disappointed we didnŐt get them,Ó Daphne said. ŇI find them highly entertaining.Ó *** At dinner that night, Draco presented Harry with an invitation. ŇWould you like to accompany the Slytherin Quidditch team on a trip to the pitch?Ó Harry paused in the middle of chewing. ŇYou want to spy.Ó ŇLetŐs call it Ôsurveillance.ŐÓ Harry rolled his eyes. ŇWhich is another word for Ôspying,ŐÓ he said. ŇNo.Ó ŇThen why are you coming with us?Ó Montague, the Slytherin team captain, grumbled from the front of the pack. ŇWell, IŐm still deciding, arenŐt I?Ó Harry said. Montague stopped in his tracks and turned to face Harry. ŇI havenŐt ruled it out.Ó Montague considered Harry for a moment, then began stalking forward again, looking like he exuded anger in every step. HeŐd again dreamt of searching hallway after hallway for something that he needed. The more he searched the more irritated he became. When he awoke, the knowledge of whatever heŐd been looking for flew out of his mind in an instant. He sighed and cast a quick Tempus, discovering that it was barely past 4 in the morning. He flung himself back on his pillow in frustration. He didnŐt want to go back to sleep because he didnŐt want to have that dream yet again, but he still had hours to go until Saturday morningŐs breakfast. After a few minutes of consideration, Harry got up. He didnŐt put much stock into Divination and he despised the class, but he wondered if he could find something about his dreams in the Divination text. They were covering dream interpretation that term, after all. He finally found a reference towards the back in a chapter called Rites and Rituals. The rite of clarity will give the caster insight into their dreams. It will make nearly all of their dreams lucid, meaning they will be able to take control of their dreams. They will find themselves able to turn corners they hadnŐt been able to turn before, open doors that had formerly been locked, and see through fog that might have clouded their vision. Additionally, if they find that an idea in the dream is fizzling away with the rising of the sun, this rite will allow the caster to grab hold and remember it in their waking hours. This rite must be performed on one of the eight cyclical celebratory days as part of a dark circle. It will only be successful if the caster is a fully declared dark witch or wizard .Harry stopped reading and sighed. This ritual looked like it was exactly what he needed, but as he wasnŐt a dark wizard, he couldnŐt use it. He remembered learning over the summer that a wizard had to complete something called Ôdeclaration ritesŐ before they could be considered dark. Harry still didnŐt know what that meant, and besides - despite developing a recent interest in the dark arts, he wasnŐt sure he would ever want to actually be a dark wizard. And even if he wanted it, he honestly wasnŐt sure if he could be one. Even so, the text had been interesting, so Harry flipped back to the beginning of the book and read until his roommates began to wake up. *** Harry, again, did not receive a letter from Sirius the following morning. ŇYou can stop yelling at me.Ó ŇYou donŐt belong over here, anyway!Ó Ron shouted at his retreating back. Harry made his way away from the Gryffindor table, and on his way back to the Slytherins his grief began to turn into outrage. Sirius. Ó He reached the Slytherin table, and he heard a distant, ŇGood job on riling up the lions, Potter!Ó from one of the sixth years. He ignored it. Montague looked up at Harry, a hint of a smirk appearing on his face. ŇYeah.Ó ŇI want to try for Seeker.Ó Chapter 7: The Frame Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! This chapter was beta-read by the lovely mishaleh. Thank you! <3 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! Despite his sour mood over his earlier confrontation with the Gryffindor Quidditch team, Harry reported for his detention with Snape on time. Snape didnŐt even greet him - not that Harry expected him to - he simply launched into his instructions. ŇYou will be scrubbing cauldrons today. You may not use magic.Ó Harry nodded. ŇYes, sir.Ó ŇOnce you have completed scrubbing them, you will coat them with this.Ó He handed Harry a bottle that read ÔNewtonŐs Neutralizing Oil - Eliminate All Traces Of Old Potions!Ő Harry nodded once more, although he was feeling more and more dismayed as Snape continued to explain. ŇYes, sir.Ó ŇOnce you have applied a coating to all of the cauldrons, you will wait for them to dry, and I will inspect your work. If I have deemed your work to be satisfactory, you will then be dismissed.Ó ŇYes, sir.Ó Snape pointed to the side storeroom. ŇProceed.Ó Harry entered the storeroom and was unhappy to find a heap of cauldrons - at least twenty .all with thick coats of something that he could only identify as crispy and blue. He found a bucket, a scrubber, and a towel waiting for him. He sighed and set to work. He quickly discovered that underneath the crispy outer layer, the crust became gooey and sticky and nearly impossible to remove. As he scrubbed, he ran through possible ways he could ask Snape about his father. He rejected all of his initial ideas. ÔProfessor Snape, did my father used to bully and beat you up?Ő he thought sardonically. ThatŐd go over well, IŐm sure. He continued working, mulling over how to open the topic. When he was about halfway through with the cauldrons and his muscles had begun to ache, he finally realized that he could just ask what he was truly most curious about - if his father had attacked any Slytherins while heŐd been in school. He could leave Snape out of it entirely. He had nearly scrubbed all of the cauldrons before he worked up the courage to finally ask the question. ŇUmÉ Professor Snape?Ó Snape looked up from his desk, not bothering to hide his annoyance. ŇWhat is it, Potter?Ó ŇIÉ uhÉÓ Harry stammered. Holy shit, what was he even thinking? How could he have ever thought having an actual conversation with Snape was even possible - especially a conversation about a man that Snape had made abundantly clear he despised? ŇHow thick of a coat of oil should I put on the cauldrons?Ó Harry finally asked. SnapeŐs irritation grew more pronounced. ŇRead the directions on the bottle, Potter.Ó He looked back down at his work. ŇIsnŐt not reading directions exactly why you are here in the first place?Ó Harry picked up the bottle of oil to find the directions on the back, and started silently cursing. The oil had to dry for two hours. He should have been coating the cauldrons as he went along. Harry dropped his head back and thunked it against the wall of the storeroom. Detention was, overall, mind-numbingly boring. *** Harry, We need to talk. Try to get yourself alone in the Slytherin common room late tonight. Harry recognized the handwriting as SiriusŐs. He read the note over and over as if he expected more lines would somehow magically appear. He couldnŐt believe how little Sirius had actually said. And throughout all of Sunday, as he tried to concentrate on his homework, he found himself becoming more and more irritated. At the very least Sirius could have said what kind of conversation to expect. He found it hard to not to take everything about the note personally. It lacked emotion and didnŐt even include any basic terms of endearment - not even a ÔDear Harry.Ő Harry became more and more convinced that not only had he lost his best friend; he was about to lose his godfather, as well. He had a remarkably short fuse throughout the entire day. He snarled at Theo for borrowing his quill without asking. He yanked his Charms book away from Daphne when she tried to point out a line for their homework. He snapped at Vince for chewing too loud at dinner. ŇI realize you must be out of sorts over the GryffindorsŐ behavior towards you, Harry, but really!Ó Pansy sniped at him that evening when he lost his temper at her for laughing too obnoxiously. Harry stormed out of the common room, up to the dorm, and slammed the door with as much force as he could muster. It didnŐt make him feel any better. He stalked over to his bed and threw himself down on the covers, burying his face in his pillow. His godfather hated him. He knew it. HarryŐs face hurt, and he realized that he was smashing his glasses into his nose. He rolled over and glared at nothing. Then his eyes focused, and he realized he was actually glaring at the photograph of his parents, who were still smiling at him. ŇYou hate me, too, donŐt you?Ó Harry whispered. He stared at his mother and father in accusation, and then he abruptly reached out and turned their photo face-down on the nightstand. *** When all of his roommates came upstairs to get ready for bed, Harry departed for the common room. There were, thankfully, only a handful of older years left. Harry took a spot on one of the sofas and stared into the fire. The other Slytherins eventually left, and Harry was alone. Harry continued watching the fire and waited for Sirius to appear. The minutes seemed to drag, and his irritation bubbled more and more the longer he waited. Finally, SiriusŐs head materialized in the fireplace, and Harry immediately jumped to his feet. ŇSirius!Ó ŇHarry, IŐm -Ó ŇWhat the hell kind of letter was that?Ó Harry demanded, a pinched scowl on his face. ŇYou couldnŐt be bothered to give me just a little bit more information? What your thoughts were? Or even a ÔLove, Snuffles?ŐÓ Sirius glowered right back. ŇYours wasnŐt much better, now was it?Ó he said nastily. Harry shut his mouth and glared. ŇThree sentences, none of which said if you were okay or not, or if you were even safe!Ó Sirius continued, the volume of his voice quickly escalating. ŇOh, IŐm so sorry,Ó Harry said mockingly. They glowered at each other for a long moment. ŇCould we start this conversation over?Ó Sirius asked, still looking irate. ŇYes,Ó Harry said, his tone surly. Sirius closed his eyes and took a deep breath, then let it out, evidently in an effort to calm himself down. Finally, he asked, ŇHarry, are you safe? The Slytherins arenŐtÉÓ Harry sighed and his eyes rolled upward. HeŐd already covered this far too many times. ŇYes. IŐm safe. IŐm fine. The Slytherins are not trying to murder me.Ó Sirius nodded. ŇGood.Ó He paused, then said, ŇHarry, IŐm so sorry.Ó Harry blinked. ŇI should have contacted you sooner. ŇWhichever one of you did do itÉÓ he said finally. ŇThank you.Ó Chapter8:The Maneuver Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes Dear Mr. Potter, Although we have never had the pleasure of meeting one another, I felt the need to send you this missive after a mutual friend of ours brought your unique circumstances to my attention. I understand that you are in your fifth year at Hogwarts, and despite having spent your previous four years in House Gryffindor you have now found yourself in the great house of Slytherin. I am quite certain this must be confusing for you. As our mutual friend may have informed you, I was a Slytherin. It has been many years since I was a student at Hogwarts, but I have always had a great amount of pride for having been Slytherin. I hope that you will eventually come to understand that pride, and that one day you will also hold that pride for yourself. As you were once a Gryffindor, I am certain that you are aware of the despicable reputation that Slytherin has attained over the years. I will admit that although many aspects of that reputation are unfair and biased, some of that reputation is unfortunately well-deserved. Slytherins tend to make names for themselves through deeds both wonderful and terrible. While the Dark Lord was a Slytherin, I must remind you that Merlin, the greatest, kindest, and most powerful wizard that ever lived, was also a Slytherin. Slytherins can indeed be wicked, but they can also be honorable, virtuous, and admirable. Slytherins are not inherently evil, just as none of the other houses are inherently good. Courage may lead to recklessness. Loyalty may lead to blind devotion. Intelligence may lead to arrogance. Ambition may lead to crookedness. All of the houses have the capacity to be good or evil. Have you heard that the four houses are not only represented by their animals, but also by the four elements? As the older wizarding traditions have begun to fall into distant memory, the elements are not spoken of as often as they once were. Gryffindor is fire, Hufflepuff is earth, and Ravenclaw is air. The element of Slytherin is water, and water is the element of change. This means that we are adaptable. It also means that we can make the environment around us adapt to the path that we are forging. We have the ability to carve the world into a new image, and we ourselves can adapt to whatever new situations we may find ourselves in. Being Slytherin means we are cunning, resourceful, and ambitious. We have the ability to find clever ways to overcome obstacles which lay in front of us, and we have an unmatched desire to succeed. We are patient and we are determined. These traits mean that we are capable of changing the world, whether for good or bad. Something that I chose not to mention to our mutual friend is that I do truly believe that Slytherin is the most excellent of the four houses. Being Slytherin means we often need to call on the traits of the other three houses in order to achieve our ends. We can be brave enough to face whatever fear stands between us and our goals. We use our intellect in order to figure out how to accomplish those goals. And, of course, we work hard to achieve our goals. Each of the Hogwarts houses are great in their own way, and I believe that we are the true culmination of all four. We have an inherent drive to accomplish whatever it is we wish to accomplish. From what I have heard of you from our mutual friend, I truly believe that you are going to accomplish great and wonderful things in this world. I look forward to seeing you grow into your new home in Slytherin, the greatest house of them all. My kindest regards, Andromeda Tonks ŇYouŐve been staring at that letter for over twenty minutes, Harry,Ó Pansy said. ŇItŐs only a page long. You canŐt possibly be that slow of a reader.Ó Harry finally looked up, blinking, after having read and reread the letter multiples times. ŇIŐm not. ItŐs justÉ a very nice letter.Ó ŇWhoŐs it from?Ó Blaise peered over HarryŐs shoulder. ŇHey!Ó Harry snapped, abruptly folding up the parchment to hide it from BlaiseŐs prying eyes. ŇPrivate!Ó Although SiriusŐs name hadnŐt been used, Harry didnŐt want anyone asking about the Ômutual friend.Ő ŇRelax. All I saw was the signature. ŇAndromeda Tonks wrote you a letter?Ó he said to Harry, his eyebrows rising in surprise. ŇUh, yeah,Ó Harry said. ŇWhy is my estranged aunt writing to you?Ó ŇAunt?Ó Harry asked, then remembered the family tree that Sirius had shown him. ŇI completely forgot - sheŐs your motherŐs sister, isnŐt she?Ó ŇYes,Ó Draco said. ŇMother doesnŐt really speak of her. She wanted toÉ reassure me.Ó ŇAre you?Ó Pansy asked, frowning. ŇUnhappy about being in Slytherin, I mean?Ó ŇIf I was,Ó Harry said, smiling widely, ŇI donŐt think I am anymore.Ó *** Harry had managed to get through most of Defense Against the Dark Arts without a peep.
