He had a now-familiar ritual of reading on his bed with the curtains drawn, and he settled in with the book, preparing to read until he fell asleep. He was halfway through the first chapter when he came across a passage that made him sit up. Once celebrated among wizards both dark and light, the eight traditional cyclical days of the year are still held as sacred in the dark community. They represent the cycle of light leading to dark leading to light, as well as the cycle of death and rebirth. Four of the eight cyclical days are determined by the cycles of the sun, and the exact dates fluctuate from year to year. The other four represent the approximate midpoint between the days determined by the sun. The most important day for dark witches and wizards is obviously Yule, or the Winter Solstice. This is the longest night, and it is the night when dark magic is at its most powerful. When light wizards still celebrated the cyclical days, they treated Litha, the Summer Solstice, with the same high regard, as that is the day when light magic is strongest. The declaration rite for beginning to walk the dark path is almost always performed on Yule, just as beginning light declaration rites were once performed on Litha. Out of a sign of respect for all magic, there are two cyclical days where neither dark wizards nor light wizards perform declaration rites. Although dark witches and wizards may still participate in dark circles or festivals, no one dares to declare on the days when the light and dark are perfectly balanced. These two days of the year are Ostara, the Spring Equinox, as well as Mabon, the Autumnal Equinox. Harry gaped. The autumnal equinox was that very night. Were all of the missing Slytherins at some kind of dark circle or festival, whatever those were? Harry threw his curtains open and began to pace beside his bed. HeŐd been keeping his ear low to the ground for any whispers of dark arts from his housemates, but he still hadnŐt heard a peep. HeŐd been starting to think that what heŐd read about Slytherin being full of dark witches and wizards was an exaggeration, or that perhaps the text heŐd read was out of date. Most of the books in the Black library were old, and none seemed newer than 30 years. But to have nearly the entire house disappear on one of these cyclical daysÉ it was more than a little suspicious. Harry still didnŐt want to mention his involvement or interest in the dark arts until he had confirmation of the same from one of them. Although they all seemed very keen on keeping each otherŐs secrets, Harry had still only been in Slytherin for three weeks, whereas they had been housed together for years. He didnŐt want to test the Slytherin loyalty towards him with something that could potentially get him in a heap of trouble. After all, if word got back to Snape, and if Snape, in turn, told DumbledoreÉ He shook his head. It was a terrible idea to just approach them and ask them, even though he was now almost certain that most of the students in his year were at least involved in the dark arts, if not Ôfully declaredŐ dark witches and wizards. Harry sighed. The Gryffindor in him wanted to just brashly ask one of them, but the Slytherin in him told him to be patient. He still only had circumstantial evidence, not proof. He wanted hard, indisputable proof before he asked them anything. So basically - I need blackmail, Harry thought. He shook his head. Perhaps he really did belong in Slytherin. *** Greg and Vince tried HarryŐs strategy at the next few Quidditch practices, and it almost immediately seemed quite promising. The two Beaters quickly developed a method of diving down from up high, aiming for a Bludger while they were still in motion, and then using the inertia to climb back up again. It was a little unorthodox and looked incredibly bizarre, but Montague was thrilled. He mentioned that heŐd gotten the idea from watching a Muggle documentary on World War II fighter planes, and he was met with blank, somewhat uneasy stares. Harry didnŐt care, and he promptly nicknamed the maneuver the Ôboom and zoom.Ő The method still needed fine-tuning and practice, and there were flaws, but Harry was beginning to feel incredibly confident in the Slytherin Quidditch team. They were actually quite good. It would take a while for Harry to actually admit that they might have been better than his old Gryffindor team. Harry, meanwhile, had also begun an odd kind of pen friendship with Andromeda Tonks. They hadnŐt exchanged many letters, but Harry enjoyed them. He also enjoyed how bewildered Draco became every time Harry received another letter from Andromeda, wondering how in the world Harry had become pen pals with his estranged aunt. True to his word, Blaise continued to get detentions from Umbridge, thankfully not by continuing to punch Harry in the face. He earned these detentions by more and more creative methods, though usually through insults. One day he compared Umbridge to a fuzzy pink pig, and on another day he brazenly asked if she had any gnome blood that would explain her squat face. Likewise, Harry continued his sickeningly polite act with Umbridge, and surprisingly, it started to become easier for him. Although he occasionally had to allow his rage bubble and simmer in class, he would often allow himself to blow his top in one way or another when he left her classroom. Sometimes he simply shouted at one of his housemates, who usually tended to just let him vent, but other times he would blast raw magic into the stone walls of the castle. Draco found those explosions peculiarly amusing. Harry also continued listening and watching his housemates for any signs of their involvement with the dark arts, but it was to no avail. Aside from the suspicious disappearance on the Equinox, there wasnŐt a hint. Not a single one of them made even an oblique reference to the dark arts. And at the end of September, Harry finally agreed to spend his day in Hogsmeade with Hermione and Ron. *** Harry felt awkward, even though it was still oddly familiar. It was as if he was wearing old shoes that no longer fit him. Fortunately, Harry felt some small satisfaction at seeing Ron appear just as uncomfortable. Ron had never apologized to Harry for his earlier behavior, and it seemed abundantly clear to Harry that the only reason they were walking down the path to Hogsmeade together was to appease Hermione. At least Harry and Ron still had that in common - they both would do anything for Hermione Granger. ŇThis is nice,Ó Hermione said as they trudged down the gentle incline. ŇIŐve missed this.Ó Both Ron and Harry grunted noncommittally, and Hermione rolled her eyes. ŇYou two are both suchÉ boys,Ó she said. ŇLetting a silly game get in the way of your -Ó ŇPretty sure itŐs more than just Quidditch,Ó Harry reminded her, giving Ron the stink eye. Ron, to his credit, simply frowned. Hermione suddenly stopped in her tracks and rounded on both of them. ŇOh! You are being suchÉ blockheads!Ó she snapped. ŇWhat did I do?Ó Harry complained. ŇYouÉ oh, nothing, I suppose,Ó Hermione said, exasperated. ŇThis is just so frustrating! You two are friends! Ron, why canŐt you -Ó ŇIŐm sorry.Ó Harry jerked his head towards Ron, his eyes wide. Ron stared at the ground, fists clenched, and swallowed hard. ŇHarry, IŐmÉ IŐm really sorry.Ó Harry didnŐt say anything, and Ron finally looked up to meet his eyes. ŇHermione tore into me, and then Mum wrote me a really nasty letter, then -Ó ŇIn other words, youŐre only apologizing just so they wonŐt be mad at you anymore?Ó Harry snapped. ŇNo!Ó Ron shook his head emphatically. ŇI mean, having Hermione and my mumÉ and then Fred, George, and Ginny on top of itÉÓ He let out a shudder. ŇI donŐt know if you know this, mate, but GinnyŐs really, really scary when sheŐs mad.Ó Despite himself, Harry cracked a tiny smile at that. ŇBut then the other day, Neville just straight up asked me why we werenŐt friends anymore,Ó Ron said. His face seemed to crumble. ŇAnd I realized thatÉ IÉÓ He paused, reaching up to scratch at the back of his head. ŇWell, I miss you.Ó Harry swallowed. ŇI hate that you got re-sorted,Ó Ron continued, Ňand into Slytherin, no less. And then you seemed to beÉ perfectly okay with it.Ó Harry gave a half-shrug. ŇI guess my being able to actuallyÉ grow accustomed to my new house is a sign that I really am supposed to be there,Ó he said. ŇSlytherins are supposed to be adaptable.Ó He paused and then stared at Ron. ŇI canŐt just beÉ miserable and alone for my last three years at Hogwarts, Ron.Ó ŇYeah, I know,Ó Ron said, nodding. ŇThatŐs whatÉ well, thatŐs what pretty much everyone said.Ó ŇLook, RonÉÓ Harry started, then he sighed and ran a hand through his hair. ŇYou donŐt have toÉ understand why IŐm in Slytherin. SiriÉ Snuffles told me that he didnŐt understand it, and that he might have questions, but heŐs still going to be there for me no matter what.Ó Harry narrowed his eyes. ŇAnd IŐm sorry, but heŐs got way more reason to hate Slytherin than you do.Ó ŇI guess,Ó Ron mumbled. ŇBut -Ó Hermione thwacked him on the arm. ŇYouŐre still being stupid, Ronald.Ó Ron gave Hermione the barest of glares, and then he sighed. ŇSnuffles is right. I donŐt understand it. I still donŐt like the house. After everything theyŐve... Ň He trailed off. ŇBut IŐmÉ I still want us to be friends, Harry,Ó he finally said. ŇItŐs just not right if weŐre not.Ó Harry stared at Ron for a long moment. It wasnŐt the best apology, but Ron also wasnŐt the best at apologizing. He also clearly hadnŐt come to terms with HarryŐs new house placement, either. Even so - Harry was beginning to realize just how much heŐd missed the first friend heŐd ever had. ŇYeah,Ó Harry finally said. ŇIt feels weird.Ó Hermione smiled brilliantly. ŇI told both of you that you would work this out, didnŐt I? You two have been friends for too long to let something like this stand in the way.Ó Harry shrugged. He still wasnŐt convinced that he and Ron were in the best spot, but it was certainly better than it had been since the beginning of term. Hermione, still facing the path behind them, squinted into the sun. ŇIsnŐt thatÉ Greengrass up there?Ó Harry glanced over his shoulder and sure enough, Daphne and Tracey were waiting farther up the path, and he had to repress a smile. Apparently, the Slytherins still werenŐt letting him travel anywhere without the watchful eye of at least one other Slytherin. ŇStrange. LetŐs go to ZonkoŐs, shall we?Ó Hermione said. She spun on her heel and led the way. Despite his uneasiness, Harry genuinely appreciated the time with his old friends, and he had to admit that it was a good day. Chapter End Notes 1. This chapter was really fun to write. 2. I learned about "boom and zoom" from playing video games, not cuz I'm a history freak. But it is an actual move. It's not the first inspiration from various video games I've used for this fic, and it won't be the last. 3. The next two chapters are super exciting and I can't wait to share them with you all. <3 Chapter 9: The Snake Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! Yep, two chapters in one day. I was originally gonna save it for tomorrow, but I'm gonna spend the day writing instead. ;) After seeing the comments on the last chapter, I hope nobody murders me for this one. This chapter was beta-read by the lovely mishaleh. Thank you! <3 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes ŇDo you hear that?Ó Harry asked Daphne and Pansy, frowning. TheyŐd been on their way to dinner, and he slowed his pace when something that sounded like angry bellowing hit his ears. Daphne gazed down the hallway. ŇYes.Ó They listened for a moment, and although they couldnŐt make out much of anything that was being said, the word ŇsnakeÓ echoed down the hall. Pansy narrowed her eyes. ŇIt doesnŐt sound good,Ó she said, and the three of them took off down the hallway. As they reached the end of the corridor the shouting grew louder and the words became more distinct. ŇI asked you a question, you nasty, thieving Slytherin! Where did you get this? Answer me!Ó A rush of alarm and adrenaline suddenly ran down HarryŐs spine, and he doubled his speed, pulling ahead of the girls. He rounded the corner and spotted a sixth year Gryffindor hovering over a young girl in Slytherin robes. ŇWhere did you get this?Ó the Gryffindor demanded again, holding something long and stringy over the girlŐs head. ŇThis is a Gryffindor product! ŇWhat the hell are you doing?Ó he shouted. Harry looked at his face and finally remembered that the boyŐs name was Cormac McLaggen. ŇAstoria!Ó He heard Daphne cry from behind him. ŇHer sister bought them. And theyŐre not Gryffindor products; theyŐre the Weasley twinsŐ invention.Ó ŇWho are both Gryffindors,Ó McLaggen spat. ŇWhatŐs that got to do with me?Ó ŇEverything!Ó Seamus roared, throwing his arms up. ŇCanŐt catch any of my ÔnutterŐ cooties now.Ó ŇI know IŐm relieved youŐre not there anymore,Ó Seamus said. ŇYou got resorted to Slytherin because youŐre a liar, Potter.Ó HarryŐs wand was in his hand and the tip shoved upwards into McLaggenŐs chin in an instant. ŇSay that again,Ó Harry said, seething. ŇI dare you.Ó Fear flashed in McLaggenŐs eyes, but after a moment he sneered in defiance. ŇYouŐre nothing but a dirty, lying snake.Ó Harry was so enraged that his ears seemed to pop, and his scar blazed with a burning hot fury. He whipped his wand out from under McLaggenŐs chin and shouted, ŇEngorgio Skullus!Ó and threw the hex at McLaggen without a second thought. Before their very eyes, McLaggenŐs head started inflating, and Harry was reminded of his Aunt Marge in third year. But just his head started growing while the rest of the body remained the same, and it was much more disturbing to watch. McLaggen pitched forward as his head continued to grow, the weight starting to become too much for him to hold up. ŇTo use it on a fellow studentÉ thatŐs -Ó ŇProfessor, that boy pushed my sister and accused her of -Ó Daphne started saying. ŇThat is still no reason to use dangerous hexes on your classmates!Ó McGonagall snapped. ŇYou are all old enough to know that such behavior is strictly forbidden, and it will not be tolerated!Ó ŇHeŐs not my classmate,Ó Harry mumbled. ŇMake that thirty points, as well as a detention, Potter!Ó McGonagall said firmly. She cast the counterspell to HarryŐs hex, and McLaggenŐs head began to shrink back to a normal size. Harry sighed. Seamus and McLaggen had never even drawn their wands, and Harry wasnŐt even sure where that level of fury had actually come from. Umbridge had called him a liar and it hadnŐt been like that. ŇProfessor, it really wasnŐt HarryŐs fault -Ó ŇI am not in the mood to hear any rationalizations, Miss Parkinson!Ó McGonagall immediately retorted, and an expression of outrage appeared on PansyŐs face. ŇI donŐt think thereŐs much I can do about them.Ó He faintly heard the Slytherins around him gasp. The snake in the painting stilled and stared at Harry. ŇYou being a Parselmouth.Ó Harry shrugged, uncomfortable with the attention they were giving him. HeŐd gone from feeling like he was being quizzed to feeling like he was being subjected to a medical exam. ŇHoly shit,Ó Draco said, and Harry blinked. He hadnŐt lived with them for long, but he was fairly sure heŐd never heard Draco swear before. ŇThe bloody Boy-Who-Lived isnŐt just dabbling in the dark arts; heŐs almost certainly got a dark affinity.Ó A wave of annoyance passed through Harry. ŇYouŐre not helping.Ó Blaise, who had been sitting back to observe the exchange, crossed his arms. ŇI think itŐs obvious that none of us want to fight, especially about this,Ó he said. ŇLetŐs figure this out.Ó They wound up talking far into the night. Crabbe and Goyle came in just after curfew and joined the conversation. Harry was surprised to learn that although their fathers were both Death Eaters, they were like Tracey Davis - they didnŐt have an ounce of dark affinity in them. He also learned that Draco, Theo, and Blaise were all dark wizards, having already completed their declaration rites. As they spoke, they decided to set boundaries with one another, deciding what was acceptable to talk about with one another and what was not. ŇI get the feeling that heŐs completely mad. But IŐm not sure IŐll have a choice.Ó He looked Harry straight in the eye. ŇThatŐs why IŐm so excited about you, Harry.Ó ŇI really donŐt want to be a third side - or a fourth side, if you include the ministry - in this war,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇI just want to find a way to defeat him. I thought the dark arts could help me with that; thatŐs why I started looking into them in the first place.Ó ŇThey likely can help you,Ó Theo said. ŇAnd whatŐs more - you might be able to show dark witches and wizards that the Dark Lord isnŐt the only option.Ó ŇWhich could weaken him,Ó Blaise added, Ňand add to the likelihood of his defeat.Ó Their conversation drifted from topic to topic, often circling back to the dark arts or VoldemortŐs followers or blood purity. Through it all, in the back of HarryŐs mind, his thoughts kept swirling around the fact that it almost seemed like the Slytherins wanted him to be their new symbol. And that was terrifying. Chapter End Notes The obligatory video game reference in this chapter is: Engorgio Skullus. The only place in the HP universe I've seen that spell appear is in the LEGO Harry Potter games. I just made it a little less slapstick and a bit more scary. ;) Chapter 10:The Circle Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! See the end of the chapter for more notes HeŐd stayed up talking with his roommates until an ungodly hour, but Harry still couldnŐt fall asleep. His eyes stayed glued to the underside of the canopy of his bed, and his mind kept racing and revolving around everything that had been revealed over the course of the evening. Three of his roommates were dark wizards. Sure, Harry had suspected it, but having actual confirmation of his suspicions was something else entirely. HeŐd been reading about the dark arts for months, and now he could actually have conversations with someone about what heŐd been learning. The biggest shocker of the night, though, was HarryŐs own supposed Ôdark affinity.Ő Harry had never come across the term in any of his reading, although Draco had mentioned that magical affinities were simply common knowledge in the dark community. Actually explaining magical affinities in a book about the dark arts would be akin to a book about the ocean stating that water was wet. And so Draco, Blaise, and Theo had explained affinities as best they could. Witches and wizards all had magical affinities that led them to be inclined towards certain types of magic. On one end of the spectrum were those with strong dark affinities - people who could access the dark arts with ease. The other end of the spectrum was apparently occupied by people with strong light affinities, or those that could more easily cast spells drawn from light magic. However, as old light traditions had fallen into obscurity, it was suspected that many of the spells had disappeared along with the light rituals. The vast majority of witches and wizards fell somewhere in the middle - having just a minor affinity for one or the other. Someone with a tiny bit of affinity for the dark would be able to use the dark arts without feeling ill, but it would be more difficult for them and they would have to work harder to master a dark spell. The opposite was true for those with a small amount of affinity for the light. His roommates also revealed that the vast majority of the magic taught at Hogwarts was neutral - the spells simply drew on magic as a whole, rather than dark or light. In fact, most spells in general use in the wizarding world were neutral. Overall, Harry had left the conversation feeling like he had so much more to learn, and the thought of learning more was certainly enticing. One thing worried him, though: the way his roommates had spoken about how the dark magic must have ÔcalledŐ to Harry. Something had drawn Harry to the Black Library, and according to his roommates, it was dark magic. Dark magic had taken him up the stairs, guided him to the door, and guided his hand to a specific book on the shelf. He hadnŐt even noticed. It was as if the dark magic had actually been controlling him like he was a puppet on a string, and Harry didnŐt like the sound of that at all. Even so, Harry still didnŐt feel dissuaded from learning more about dark magic, and he found himself randomly smiling up at the canopy. Perhaps McGonagall had been right - he really was in a better place in Slytherin. His roommates had already taught him so much more than he already knew in the span of one long conversation. He wouldnŐt have learned any of that on his own in Gryffindor. Besides, if Ron hadnŐt been able to accept HarryŐs newfound cunning, he certainly wouldnŐt have been able to accept HarryŐs study of the dark arts. HarryŐs chest tightened at the thought of Ron and what heŐd said to Harry the previous day. The more he thought about it, the more he realized that he couldnŐt really argue with anything Ron had to say. After all, Harry had been deceiving Umbridge, and he had shot a spell at McLaggen that could have seriously hurt him or even killed him. Ron hadnŐt assumed anything, and he hadnŐt been mistaken about what Harry had been doing. Ron had simply started to see HarryŐs Slytherin side and he hadnŐt been able to stomach it, and that was what hurt the most. *** The fifth year boys all woke up extremely late, and there was something of a mad scramble in the dorm to make sure they could get to the hall before the end of breakfast. Draco even skipped his usual morning bathroom routine and his hair stuck up in a few places, his bangs swept to one side. Harry thought it oddly looked better like that, but he didnŐt say so. ŇAll of you look positively exhausted,Ó Pansy said when the boys finally got to the Slytherin table. Harry felt exactly as tired as Pansy said they looked. He was fairly sure he wound up getting less than an hour of actual sleep. Draco shot her a sleepy grin. ŇWe were all up quite late,Ó he said. ŇWe were having a long overdue conversation,Ó Blaise added, an identical grin plastered across his face. PansyŐs fork paused halfway to her mouth. ŇConcerningÉ ?Ó ŇA bit of everything, really,Ó Blaise said. ŇBut all you need to be concerned with is that I think weŐve found a replacement for Tracey.Ó Harry pointedly didnŐt look at any of them, instead studiously concentrating on scooping a few slices of bacon onto his plate. Pansy set her fork down and clapped once, smiling. ŇThatŐs wonderful! Tracey really doesnŐt want to, and she was only doing it as a favor to me, butÉ oh, who is it, Blaise?Ó A slow smile appeared on BlaiseŐs face. ŇHarry Potter.Ó Anger flashed across PansyŐs face. ŇThatŐs not funny, Blaise!Ó she snapped. ŇWeŐre not joking,Ó Draco said. Pansy blinked, and then looked straight at Harry. ŇReally?Ó she said, sounding intrigued. Harry swallowed a bite of bacon and smiled sheepishly at her before shrugging. ŇHang on,Ó Blaise said, pulling out his wand but keeping it low to the table. ŇMuffliato.Ó Harry was grateful for the foresight, but he still didnŐt feel comfortable discussing it in the hall. Draco and Blaise seemed unconcerned, though, so he just let them continue. ŇThis is part of why we were up so late, Pansy,Ó Draco said, still grinning. If Draco hadnŐt been so damn proper, Harry would have bet he would be bouncing up and down in his seat. ŇIt looks pretty likely that Harry has a dark affinity,Ó Blaise said. ŇA strong affinity.Ó Pansy, Daphne, and Tracey all seemed to mirror one another as their mouths fell open, and Millicent suddenly snapped her gaze over to Harry. ŇH-howÉÓ Pansy stuttered. ŇHarry, youŐre essentially the symbol of the light!Ó Harry frowned and looked down at his plate, shuffling his food around with his fork. ŇI never asked to be,Ó he said quietly. Pansy did something unexpected at that - she reached across the table and placed her hand over HarryŐs. Harry looked up at her, startled. ŇI know you didnŐt, Harry,Ó she said sympathetically. ŇHeŐs essentially a textbook example.Ó ŇAnd you think he should do an affinity rite,Ó Pansy concluded. She sat back, considering their words, and then she grinned. ŇThatŐs actually perfect for the circle! ŇCan I ask something?Ó he said. ŇBlaise said you had to clear me joining thisÉ circle, because itŐs your night. What does that actually mean?Ó Pansy rolled her eyes. ŇFigures that they wouldnŐt tell you,Ó she said. ŇDunderheads, the lot of them.Ó ŇHey!Ó Blaise protested. ŇI think we just forgot,Ó Theo said sheepishly. ŇWe had a lot to talk aboutÉÓ Pansy ignored them both. ŇSamhain is the night I do my final declaration rite,Ó she said with a brilliant smile. ŇAfter that, IŐll officially be a dark witch.Ó *** Over the next few days, the other Slytherin fifth years kept giving Harry more and more reading material on the dark arts. Pansy tracked down a book on dark circles and insisted that he read it prior to Samhain since he would actually be taking part in one. ŇYouŐve finally given me my Ôout.ŐÓ Harry barely kept up with his homework as he consumed every book the other Slytherins threw at him. On Samhain itŐll be myself, Blaise, Theo, Pansy, Daphne, Millicent... and you.Ó Harry thought Draco sounded positively delighted at that. Pansy explained that her family had always been stringent traditionalists when it came to the dark, and that HarryŐs presence in the circle when she declared fell perfectly in line with the rituals of old. ŇHaving someone do an affinity rite in the same circle as me completing my declaration rites - itŐs wonderful,Ó she said. ŇYou represent the first step in the journey on the dark path.Ó ŇIŐm not sure if IŐm taking a journey on any... path,Ó Harry said hesitantly, but Pansy waved her hand at that. ŇItŐs just the first step. ŇI never imagined I would witness Harry Potter doing a dark affinity rite.Ó Harry was beginning to think that Draco and Pansy were more excited about his affinity rite than he was. What surprised Harry the most was that Hogwarts apparently allowed students to go out to the grounds on the cyclical days. They had to stay on the schoolŐs property, of course, but they were allowed out past curfew in order to observe one of the oldest wizarding traditions. Harry was blown away; he thought for certain that the practice would have been banned. ŇThere are a lot of wizarding families that still observe the old ways,Ó Blaise explained. ŇEven if theyŐre not dark, the cyclical days are still widely upheld. ItŐs when we celebrate what makes us wizards. ŇThere are also misinformed idiots that think the cyclical days are only about dark arts, but itŐs really about all magic - light and dark.Ó Harry found the affinity rite in one of the books Tracey had given him. It looked simpler than heŐd been expecting; simply saying a few words, which would call down the dark magic. Depending on how the dark magic reacted and how he felt during and after the rite would determine if he had a dark affinity or not. He was also surprised to learn that all dark witches and wizards had to complete an affinity rite prior to beginning any kind of declaration rites, and that the affinity rite wasnŐt done until the age of thirteen. Even though it was simple, Harry just hoped he could do the rite correctly. *** The night before Samhain, Harry walked into the dorm to just as Draco shot some kind of curse at the wall. ŇUh, are you angry about something?Ó Harry asked. ŇShould I leave?Ó Draco was clearly startled, and he spun on his heel to face Harry. ŇIŐm just practicing something,Ó he said after a moment. ŇWeŐd been so cautious what with you moving into our house that I feel IŐve fallen behind.Ó ŇSo that was somethingÉ dark?Ó ŇSecani,Ó Draco said. ŇItŐs a basic dark cutting curse.Ó ŇSo it is possible to actually practice things like that in the dorm?Ó Harry asked, surprised. DracoŐs eyes widened. ŇYou said you tried some dark hexes over the summer. Have you not done any dark arts since then?Ó ŇUh, just one thing,Ó Harry said. ŇHe has quite a lot of knowledge about what goes on in this school, but he doesnŐt have eyes everywhere.Ó HarryŐs eyes lit up at that. DracoŐs lips twitched at the sides; he was apparently trying not to smile. ŇWas there something in particular you wanted to try?Ó ŇYes.Ó Harry didn't even hesitate. He went straight to his trunk and pulled out a book titled Dark Dueling. ŇThere are a bunch in here that I wanted to try my hand at.Ó He already had several spells bookmarked, and he flipped it open to one of the markers. ŇThis one - and one other - in particular.Ó Draco peered down at the page. ŇSeems simple. This looks like it conjures a dark wall,Ó he said. ŇYeah, itŐs supposed to,Ó Harry said. ŇItŐs supposed to be like a ÔprotegoŐ but larger, and you can use it to cover a group of people. But whatŐs even cooler is that you can layer spells on top of the wall, like adding lightning, or setting it on fire.Ó ŇLetŐs not set it on fire,Ó Draco said. ŇI rather like my belongings.Ó Harry laughed and then set the book down on his desk. He was fairly sure heŐd already gotten the wand movement down; after all, just as they were expected to do in Defense class, in the absence of actually being able to cast the spells heŐd been reading about, heŐd been studying the practical. He doubted heŐd be able to get it right away. Even the spells Sirius had shown him had taken a few tries. ŇHere goes nothing.Ó He inhaled deeply, steadying himself. ŇMuros!Ó He pointed his wand at the floor and slashed it in a line, and then he thrust both hands up in front of him. A shimmering, translucent curtain seemed to materialize from the ground up, stretching all the way to the ceiling. Harry grinned and pumped his fist in the air. ŇFirst try!Ó he cried, and turned to Draco, who was staring at the magic wall with the faintest expression of shock. ŇIŐll admit IŐm impressed,Ó Draco said after a moment. ŇThat spell doesnŐt seem like it would be easy for someone who hasnŐt declared dark.Ó Harry grinned even wider before dispelling the wall. ŇWhatŐs the other one you wanted to try?Ó Draco asked. Harry flipped the book to another page. ŇBut this one looks far more difficult. It requires a great deal of mental concentration and connection with the dark in order to control it, and like I said - I would like my belongings to remain unburnt.Ó Harry pondered for a moment. He really wanted to try the spell. ŇWhat if I cast the wall around it so itŐs contained?Ó Draco seemed to consider HarryŐs proposal and then nodded, but he brandished his own wand. ŇMaintaining two spells at once is difficult, at best, so IŐm going to disarm you if it looks like the sprite isnŐt contained.Ó Harry quickly agreed, then wondered when in the world he got to the point where he would allow Draco Fucking Malfoy to disarm him willingly. Harry conjured the wall again, and then braced himself. Draco was right, after all - this would be far more difficult than any kind of dark arts heŐd tried so far. ŇHange,Ó he said, flourishing his wand in front of him. Behind the wall, a flame springs into being. At first it was formless, and then it gained a shape until it looked like a small, genderless person made out of fire. It was only about a meter tall, but the body was distinct, nonetheless. Dance, Harry thought, and the figure began to dance. It spun on one foot like a ballet dancer, and then leapt into the air, leaving a small puff of smoke in its wake. ŇItŐs kinda pretty,Ó Harry murmured. ŇYou are a pyromaniac, Harry,Ó Draco said. Harry grinned and ended both spells. ŇWell, the element of Gryffindor is supposed to be fire. Maybe IŐm just attached to my roots.Ó He then turned to Draco, who stared back at him with wide eyes. ŇThere is absolutely no way you donŐt have a dark affinity,Ó Draco said. Harry actually found himself hoping that Draco was right, but then something occurred to him. ŇSomethingŐs been bothering me about that, actually.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇWhat you guys were sayingÉ about how the dark magic guided me to that one book,Ó Harry said. ŇSnapeŐs coming over, probably to see why youŐre losing your mind.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened and he glanced down the table, and sure enough - Snape was headed their way. ŇSay nothing about the ward,Ó he hissed at Blaise. Blaise managed to get himself under control and he peered at Harry. ŇWhat?Ó he asked. ŇNothing!Ó Harry hissed again just before Snape reached the fifth years. ŇHello, Professor Snape,Ó Draco said. ŇIs that what I think it is?Ó Harry turned in his seat to spy a small potion in SnapeŐs hands. ŇIt is what you think it is,Ó Snape said. ŇHere, Potter.Ó He held the bottle out towards Harry, who blinked. ŇUhÉ what is it?Ó Harry asked, tentatively taking the bottle from SnapeŐs hand. He held it up to inspect, observing that it was a gentle, milky orange color. Snape sighed. ŇDid you not tell him, Mr. Malfoy?Ó ŇWell, I wasnŐt certain that it would be approved,Ó Draco said, and Harry thought he sounded slightly more posh than usual. ŇIt was. Madames Pomfrey and Hooch both just finished their inspection,Ó Snape said. ŇMadame Hooch?Ó Harry asked curiously. ŇWhat is this, and what does it have to do with Quidditch?Ó ŇThat, Potter, is a basic vision potion,Ó Snape said. ŇIt will improve your eyesight to 20/20 for approximately twelve hours.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇWhat? Really?Ó He looked back down at the bottle in amazement. ŇIt was suggested to me after you made the Quidditch team,Ó Snape said, his eyes flickering over to Draco. Draco grinned. ŇPansyŐs the one who gave me the idea.Ó ŇI brewed it last week,Ó Snape added. ŇMadame Pomfrey and Madame Hooch had to test it to make sure it was what I said it was, and theyŐve cleared it for use in tomorrowŐs match.Ó Harry was completely unable to hide his shock. Snape had brewed a potion - a good potion .for him. Severus Snape, renowned hater of Harry Potter, had actually given him something incredibly beneficial, and it wasnŐt a minor gift, either. From what he remembered Draco saying at the beginning of the year, fixing someoneŐs vision was incredibly expensive, and he was sure even a ÔbasicŐ vision potion couldnŐt have been easy to concoct. ŇUhÉ wow,Ó he said. ŇThank you, Professor Snape.Ó He felt immensely strange saying the words. Snape looked just as uncomfortable to be accepting a Ôthank youŐ from Harry Potter as Harry was in saying it. ŇIndeed,Ó he said, sneering. ŇNow you wonŐt have to worry about losing or breaking your glasses,Ó Draco said. ŇWhen you played for Gryffindor IŐd always secretly hoped a Bludger would smash into your face.Ó He smirked. ŇWhen you started to play for Slytherin, I instead became extremely concerned for your well-being.Ó Harry laughed. ŇThank you, too.Ó He was still concerned about one thing, though. ŇI know Hooch approved it, but arenŐt the Gryffindors still going to accuse me ofÉ Ó He frowned. Even Fred and George wouldnŐt approve of him if they suspected he was using an illegal potion. ŇJohnson has already been informed by Madame Hooch. She should pass on the news to the team,Ó Snape said. ŇI expect a victory for Slytherin tomorrow, Potter.Ó Then he turned back to the head table, robes billowing behind him. Harry was left feeling like he would get the sole blame from Snape if Slytherin wound up losing. Harry immediately snuck a look over towards the Gryffindor table. Sure enough, Angelina did not look very happy at all. She glowered at Harry from across the room, having watched the exchange with Snape. Harry sighed and tucked the bottle away in his bag. ŇWhat was that about not talking to Snape about the ward?Ó Blaise whispered. ŇYou know heŐs a dark wizard, right?Ó ŇNo, but I suspected,Ó Harry whispered back. ŇThanks for the confirmation. ŇHeŐs not going to discipline you for doing dark magic, Harry,Ó Blaise said. ŇHeŐs a Slytherin, and -Ó ŇIŐm pretty sure that above all else, he still completely hates me and he loves getting me in trouble,Ó Harry hissed back. ŇIf he has the opportunity to make me look bad to Dumbledore, he will.Ó Blaise still looked doubtful, but he agreed not to say a word. *** Transfiguration filled the last block of the day, and everyone was eager for the end of class and the start of the weekend. ŇYou assignment for next week is an essay on Switching Spells,Ó McGonagall said. ŇThe essay must be no shorter than three feet and no longer than five feet. If you tend to write in letters that are unusually large, you will write ten feet.Ó She paused and gave Greg a pointed look before continuing. ŇConsidering the variety of applications for Switching Spells, it should not be a problem to complete the essay within the required length.Ó Harry saw Draco roll his eyes. ŇI bet you didnŐt assign Gryffindor an essay on Quidditch weekend,Ó he muttered darkly. ŇFive points from Slytherin, Mr. Malfoy,Ó McGonagall said. ŇGryffindor has the same assignment. Switching Spells will almost certainly be on your O.W.L. no matter what house you are from.Ó She peered around the classroom with her customary stern demeanor. ŇAny questions?Ó ŇNo, maŐam,Ó the class murmured. ŇVery good. Dismissed.Ó They were starting to gather up their things when McGonagall suddenly called out, ŇOh, Mr. Zabini?Ó Blaise paused in adjusting the strap on his bag and looked up. ŇYes, Professor?Ó ŇTen points to Slytherin for showing up on time with both shoes tied.Ó Blaise gawked at McGonagall and Harry laughed. ŇHey, can you wait for me outside?Ó Harry quickly asked Blaise as they turned to leave. ŇI wanted to talk to McGonagall right quick.Ó Blaise raised an eyebrow, but nodded. ŇIŐll grab Theo. WeŐll meet you in the hall.Ó Harry waited for the rest of the classroom to completely empty out before approaching McGonagallŐs desk. ŇProfessor?Ó McGonagall didnŐt even look up from the stack of papers sheŐd been shuffling through. ŇYes, Potter?Ó ŇI wanted to ask you something,Ó Harry said. He took a breath. ŇAbout my father.Ó McGonagall put the papers down and turned around, giving Harry her full attention. ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇWhen he was a student here... Ň Harry paused, then sighed. ŇLook, IŐll justÉ cut right to it. Slytherins tend to get attacked. We get cornered. We get hexed. ItŐs apparently been going on for years.Ó McGonagall frowned. ŇI have heard of a few isolated incidents, but I hadnŐt heard it wasÉ widespread.Ó ŇIt is,Ó Harry said. ŇPeople hate us. I suppose I can even understand the reason for that hate, butÉÓ He shook his head. ŇThe younger kids, getting bullied and attacked by older students. Hexes and jinxes that never get solved. Someone cast a tripping jinx on me on my second day in Slytherin, and thatŐs not even the worst of it.Ó McGonagall looked alarmed. ŇThe tripping jinx was brought up in one of our staff meetings. I believe it is still under investigation. But PotterÉÓ she said, full of concern, Ňare you saying that this is a regular pattern?Ó ŇIt is.Ó McGonagall studied him for a moment. ŇAre you asking me about this as Deputy Headmistress or as someone who knew your father?Ó ŇSomeone who knew my father,Ó he said. ŇAlthough if we could somehow put a stop to the attacksÉÓ ŇI can assure you that I will be investigating these claims quite thoroughly, Potter,Ó she said. ŇNow, then - what does this have to do with your father?Ó Harry sighed. If McGonagall wasnŐt aware of the attacks on Slytherins, she likely wouldnŐt have been aware if her father had been a perpetrator of those attacks. ŇIŐve justÉ put together comments about my father. Some things that Snape said, and some thatÉ friends of my father said.Ó Harry paused, swallowing. ŇI heard that he likedÉ using a curse on my father that flipped Snape up in the air and upside down. And then there was the ÔprankŐ that SiriÉ Snuffles played on Snape involving Professor LupinÉÓ McGonagallŐs mouth was set in a grim line. ŇYouŐre wondering if your father may have been behind some of these attacks on Slytherins.Ó ŇYes, maŐam.Ó McGonagall studied Harry for a moment, and then she walked over to one of the student desks and pulled out a chair, sitting down. ŇHave a seat, Potter.Ó The invitation only served to give Harry a sinking feeling in his stomach, but he still took the seat across the aisle from her. ŇYour father and Professor Snape never did see eye to eye. As IŐm sure you are aware, the rivalry between Gryffindor and Slytherin is storied and rough, and those two took the rivalry to extreme measures. In fact, their relationship was not unlike you and Mr. Malfoy once were, at least up until this year,Ó she said. ŇAlthough I do suspect that in their case, it may have had something to do with your mother.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked. A chill ran down his spine. He felt like he could deal with finding out one of his parents was a bully, but not both. ŇShe wasnŐtÉ involved, was she? In the attacks?Ó McGonagall shook her head. ŇNo. She was once friends with Professor Snape,Ó she said. ŇI would often see them talking or studying together in their younger years.Ó ŇWhat?!Ó Harry was completely blindsided. His mother and Professor Snape had actually been friends? How had no one ever mentioned that to him? ŇI believe that James may not haveÉ well, to put it bluntly, I believe he was jealous,Ó McGonagall said. HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇMy mom and SnapeÉ they didnŐtÉÓ He didnŐt even want to picture it, much less say it. McGonagall let out a laugh. ŇI donŐt believe Lily and Severus were everÉ romantically involved,Ó she said. ŇAnd whatever friendship they had, it must have dissolved when they were around your age. I did not see them speaking for the last few years they were at school. ŇNow, as for your father and Snape,Ó she continued, Ňthey did indeed have an incredibly turbulent rivalry. They were quite often hexing or cursing one another.Ó She paused, and then sighed. ŇBut I suspect that James may have taken it too far more than once.Ó Harry swallowed hard. It wasnŐt as clear of an answer as he wanted, but now that he had a partial answer, he was unsure if he actually wanted to know more. Even so, he looked down at his toes and asked quietly, ŇWas heÉ was he cruel?Ó McGonagall didnŐt answer for a moment, and Harry glanced up at her to see her looking at him with a somber expression. ŇChildren are often cruel, Harry,Ó she said gently. That was the only answer Harry needed. He nodded once and got to his feet, an icy cold winding its way through his heart. ŇIŐm not done, Potter,Ó McGonagall said. ŇPlease stay.Ó Harry stared at her for a moment. The more they spoke the worse he felt, and he wasnŐt sure if he wanted to hear anything else. ŇYour father may have been a nitwitted child,Ó McGonagall said, Ňbut he grew up into a decent man.Ó She smiled sadly. ŇMuch more than decent, in fact. He was one of the kindest, bravest souls I have ever had the privilege of knowing. The man he grew up to be was your father, not the child he was in the past.Ó That only made Harry feel marginally better, but he still appreciated McGonagall saying it. ŇThank you.Ó He turned to leave, but McGonagall stopped him once more. ŇIf you have managed to find a way to forgive Mr. Malfoy for all of his cruelness and misdeeds towards you and your friends,Ó she said, ŇI am certain you will eventually find it within yourself to forgive your father for his own misdeeds.Ó *** Harry found Blaise and Theo just outside the door. ŇAh, Harry!Ó Blaise said. ŇI was just telling Theo that itŐs likely time I reassess my approach with Umbridge. The detentions arenŐt getting me results. But itŐs clear that sheŐs hiding something. IŐm beginning to wonder if her obvious Slytherin bias is affecting howÉÓ Harry unintentionally started tuning Blaise out. After his rather depressing conversation with McGonagall, he was finding it difficult to switch tracks in his head. ŇUmÉ okay,Ó he mumbled. ŇAre you all right, Harry?Ó Theo asked. ŇYeah.Ó ŇNo, youŐre not,Ó Blaise said, studying HarryŐs face. ŇWhatŐs wrong?Ó ŇIÉÓ Harry sighed. ŇI donŐt really want to talk about it.Ó Blaise studied Harry for a moment, then shrugged. ŇOkay. Let us know if we can do anything to help, though.Ó Harry looked over at Blaise suddenly found himself overcome with gratitude. Blaise didnŐt pressure him or try prying into HarryŐs mood or even try to make him feel better; it was as if he had just accepted HarryŐs melancholy and offered help for when Harry was ready. Blaise was a good friend to have, Harry realized. ŇShall we drop our things off and go to dinner?Ó Theo asked after a moment. Harry nodded, and they set off down the Transfiguration corridor. ŇUmbridge was a Slytherin, too,Ó Blaise said, continuing his earlier train of thought. ŇPerhaps she knows that IŐm looking for dirt, so sheŐs being extra cautious with me.Ó ŇIf she knew that you were looking for dirt, do you really think she would be so calm about it?Ó Theo asked. ŇYes, I do,Ó Blaise said. ŇEspecially if she thinks itŐs well-hidden. She also knows how we Slytherins think, and she knows not to underestimate us. Harry knew that they were likely only headed for Gryffindor tower, but he instinctively tensed up. ŇWhat are you doing up here?Ó Dean asked rather darkly when they spotted the Slytherins at the top of the stairs. ŇLeaving class, obviously,Ó Blaise said dryly. ŇClass has been over for a while,Ó Ron said. ŇI had to talk to Professor McGonagall about something,Ó Harry said. He felt drained. ŇDonŐt worry, weŐre leaving now,Ó Blaise said. ŇIŐll do anything to get away from the horrific stench of Gryffindors.Ó Harry didnŐt want to do this. He didnŐt want to take any part in the ancient Gryffindor-Slytherin rivalry. HeŐd played at it for years, but now that he was on the Slytherin side of it, he just felt tired. More than anything, he just wanted to keep walking, and so he did. He took a few steps to the side and then forward, trying to avoid the Gryffindors in front of him. Seamus stepped directly into his path, and Harry met SeamusŐs hard gaze with a fatigued eye. ŇHeard something interesting about you, Potter,Ó Seamus said nastily. Harry sighed. ŇWhat did you hear, Seamus?Ó he asked. ŇThat you went out with a bunch of dark wizards for Samhain last night,Ó Seamus said, his eyes flicking over to Blaise and Theo and then back to Harry. Harry, meanwhile, looked directly at Ron. Obviously, Ron had been talking about him in Gryffindor tower. ŇYou sacrifice anyone to Crom Cruoch?Ó Seamus demanded. ŇOr maybe the Fomorians?Ó HarryŐs face screwed up in confusion. ŇWhat?Ó ŇOr maybe you just called up the dead,Ó Seamus spat. ŇSamhain is when the veil between the living and the dead is at its thinnest. ŇWhere IŐm from, we donŐt like dark wizards, Potter. TheyŐve done too many awful things oŐer the years.Ó HeŐd known Seamus was already angry with him, but apparently hearing even a hint that Harry was involved in something dark had set off something that was rooted much deeper than the lies being printed in the Prophet. Seamus never used to get involved in the Gryffindor-Slytherin fights if he could help it before, but apparently something about Harry was making Seamus take it personally. ŇIŐm not a da-Ó ŇYouŐre hanginŐ out with them now, arenŐt you?Ó Seamus shouted, nodding towards Blaise and Theo. As his voice got louder his accent became thicker. ŇSo you admit that youŐre dark?Ó Seamus spat. ŇYou dark wizards have done so much damage -Ó ŇI admitted nothing,Ó Blaise said, sounding bored. ŇIt was a joke. YouŐve heard of those, right?Ó ŇCanŐt help but notice that youŐre not denying it, either,Ó Dean said, and BlaiseŐs disinterested expression immediately turned irritated as he moved his gaze from Seamus to Dean. Harry sighed. ŇOh, yes,Ó Blaise said, rolling his eyes dramatically. ŇWeŐre so evil. We have wild, evil, dark orgies every night of the week.Ó He smirked. ŇExcept Sundays. ThatŐs the day of recovery.Ó ŇYouŐre Zabini, arenŐt you?Ó Seamus asked. ŇAt your service,Ó Blaise said, sneering. ŇWe heard about you, too,Ó Dean said. ŇSome of the upper years were talking about you.Ó Blaise smiled sardonically, and Harry thought he looked a bit like a predator. ŇAnd what did they have to say about me, hmm?Ó ŇWhat in the world is going on here?!Ó Hermione came marching up the stairs behind the other Gryffindors with a pinched, stern expression, and Harry was forcibly reminded of McGonagall. ŇI heard shouting. YouŐre not fighting, are you?Ó ŇJust having a conversation, Granger,Ó Blaise said, never taking his eyes off Dean. ŇThomas here was just about to inform me of a rumor heŐd heard. So what did you hear?Ó ŇThat if anyone in Slytherin is a dark wizard, itŐs definitely you,Ó Dean spat. ŇYour mumŐs been married seven times, and every single one of her husbands mysteriously died. YouŐre apparently only rich because youŐre living off all of her dead husbandsŐ fortunes. Not much of a 'mystery' when it comes to what's going on in your family." BlaiseŐs expression immediately lit up with fury. ŇThis is outrageous!Ó Hermione snapped. ŇStop this immediately! Dean, how dare you say such terrible things!Ó She rounded on Ron. ŇAnd you! You are a prefect! Why werenŐt you putting a stop to all this?Ó Neither boy said anything back to her, so Hermione turned back to Blaise with a sigh. ŇIŐm quite sorry, Zabini. Rest assured it will be report-Ó ŇShut up,Ó Blaise snapped, and HarryŐs eyes snapped back to his friend. Blaise looked angrier than Harry had ever seen him. Hermione looked taken aback. ŇWell, I -Ó ŇI said shut your filthy mouth,Ó Blaise spat. ŇI donŐt need a Mudblood to protect me from a Mudblood!Ó HarryŐs breath caught in his throat and his entire body went cold. Hermione drew back, gasping, and DeanŐs face twisted up in rage. Out of the corner of his eye, Harry saw Theo put his palm to his face and let out a sigh. ŇYou nasty little snake!Ó Ron shouted, and his wand was in his hand and pointed at Blaise in an instant. Harry's scar blazed hot. Harry reached out and grabbed RonŐs wand right out of his hand. He was not in the mood for curses. ŇWhat the fuck, Blaise?Ó Harry hissed, tossing the wand up against the wall where it clattered to the ground. BlaiseŐs gaze swiveled towards Harry, and his furious expression into morphed into something that resembled embarrassment. ŇHarry, IŐm sorry -Ó ŇIŐm not the one you should be apologizing to!Ó Harry shouted. HeŐd begun to think of Blaise as a friend - a potentially great friend, in fact. He really had. Blaise had never even mentioned his thoughts on blood purity to Harry, even though he and Draco had been openly discussing it just a few nights ago. Hearing Blaise spit out such a hateful word without a second thought made HarryŐs stomach do somersaults. HeŐd insulted not only a former friend, but also one of the only friends Harry had left in Gryffindor. HeŐd honestly expected it from Draco, but Draco hadnŐt even said anything to Hermione all year. He also had at least offered to engage in discourse about such a sticky topic. Blaise had made no such offer. Not only that, but Harry knew to be on guard around Draco. Blaise, on the other hand, had almost started to feel safe. Harry had obviously grossly misjudged who Blaise actually was, and Harry wasnŐt sure if he could stomach the person Blaise turned out to be. Harry didnŐt want to be there anymore. He needed to get away. A wave of anger rushing through him, he pushed past Seamus and started marching down the stairs. ŇHarry, wait,Ó Blaise said, jogging down after him. He reached out and grabbed Harry by the arm. ŇDonŐt touch me, Zabini,Ó Harry spat viciously. He yanked his arm out of BlaiseŐs grasp and took off running across the castle. Chapter End Notes The very large elephant in the room just started stomping. Chapter12: The Hate Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! This chapter was beta-read by the lovely mishaleh. Thank you! <3 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes Harry stared down at his Quidditch uniform with a frown. ÔPotterŐ was emblazoned in silver across green shoulders. Silver accents ran down the sleeves and a snake was coiled on the back. The robes looked sharp and crisp and new. They were apparently a gift from Lucius Malfoy. Harry had overheard Warrington mentioning them when they were at breakfast that morning; Lucius Malfoy gifted the Slytherin team with new uniforms every season. In Gryffindor, Harry had worn used Quidditch robes every year. Harry didnŐt necessarily mind the fact that the Slytherin robes were new, but he certainly minded the provider of those robes. Harry sighed and pulled his sweater over his head. He supposed that wearing a gift from Lucius Malfoy was the least of his worries. Harry hadnŐt spoken to anyone since the previous day. After the confrontation with the Gryffindors on the stairs, heŐd stormed straight back to the dungeons and gone right up to the dorm. In a fit of rage he replaced the blood ward on his bed, drawn the curtains, and had seethed the rest of the night. HeŐd even skipped dinner. At breakfast, heŐd sat at the far end of the table. He had noticed that seemingly all of the fifth years looked unusually downtrodden, and he felt viciously satisfied at seeing BlaiseŐs miserable expression. He refused to engage anyone in conversation. That had left him to be alone with his thoughts for hours on end. Harry supposed that, in retrospect, he should have known that Blaise was a staunch blood purist. Just because heŐd never said anything overtly didnŐt mean he didnŐt hold that ideology. After all, he had made a few snide comments about Muggles, and Harry also remembered the day heŐd been in the library with Hermione - Blaise had seemed uncomfortable. Harry had brushed it off and ignored it at the time. Just like heŐd been somewhat ignoring the blood purity issue that was so deeply rooted in his new house. Harry had initially thought heŐd been dealing with it rather well; after all, heŐd known their views before heŐd been sorted into Slytherin, and aside from a few minor arguments with Draco, their purist political views hadnŐt come in the way of his being in the house. They accepted his dissension and theyŐd still accepted him. HeŐd become comfortable with it being discussed in a controlled debate, and was grateful that Draco had at least been willing to speak about it; it had given him the illusion that he might have been able to change their minds. But it turned out that they were merely hiding their prejudice for HarryŐs benefit, and that just because they didnŐt say anything didnŐt mean they didnŐt still hold such hateful ideology. Actually having to see that hatred, first-hand, was forcing Harry into a hard reality check. He was in a house full of prejudiced blood purists. There was Daphne, he supposed, as well as Tracey, who seemed to be the only other half-blood in the entire house besides himself, but Daphne and Tracey were obviously the exception and not the rule. It also hadnŐt escaped HarryŐs notice that Slytherin was the only house in the entire school without a single Muggleborn. Slytherin had been tolerant of HarryŐs views, and seemed tolerant of DaphneŐs and TraceyŐs as well, but Harry wasnŐt sure he could return that tolerance. He didnŐt think he could be tolerant of blood purity - the opinion that witches and wizards born from Muggles were somehow lesser human beings. Now he was in a house full of blood purists and was about to play a Quidditch game on a team full of blood purists while wearing a gift from a blood purist. Harry scrubbed his face with his hand in frustration. When his hand hit his glasses, he suddenly remembered the potion Snape had given him. He pulled it out of his bag and set it on the bench in the Slytherin changing room. He still wanted to win the game. He was furious with Blaise and furious with the views that much of his housemates held, but he would still play to the best of his ability. Harry dressed and strapped on his gear, and then picked up the potion. He studied it for a moment, then uncapped it and swallowed it down. Amazingly, it was the best-tasting potion heŐd ever had; it tasted like an orange creamsicle. Slowly, his vision began to grow blurry, but when Harry removed his glasses it began to grow sharper, instead. Finally, everything around him came into focus and he stared around the changing room in amazement. Everything was so clear. He blinked a few times. He could see far better with the potion than he could with his glasses. He actually had peripheral vision, and everything was incredibly crisp. He idly wondered if that was how most people actually saw the world around them. He placed his glasses in the locker on top of his clothes and turned around. Draco stood in front of him with his broom in hand, dressed in a matching uniform. Draco looked Harry up and down and his lips parted slightly in surprise. ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, somewhat surly. ŇPansy was right about your eyes,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇYouŐve never looked so Slytherin.Ó Harry shifted uncomfortably, then started adjusting his wrist guard just so he didnŐt have to meet DracoŐs gaze. ŇI wanted to check on you,Ó Draco said. ŇYou skipped the fifth year meeting last night.Ó ŇWasnŐt in the mood,Ó Harry said shortly. ŇI know,Ó Draco said. ŇBut you missed what was probably the biggest argument our year has ever had. You really should have been there.Ó Harry just grunted and turned around to slam his locker shut. ŇYou should talk to Tracey,Ó Draco continued, Ňafter the game.Ó Harry glanced back at Draco in surprise. ŇWhy?Ó Draco sighed. ŇI think itŐs a conversation youŐll appreciate,Ó he said, Ňbut we donŐt have time to get into it right now. I know you had a terrible day yesterday, and I know you must be in a right mood. But we have a game to play, and I wanted to make sure your head is in the right spot.Ó ŇIt is,Ó Harry bit out. ŇGood,Ó Draco said. ŇThereŐs one other thing I need to warn you about.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇMy father is here.Ó Harry spun on his heel to fully face Draco again, his eyes widening in alarm. ŇWhat?Ó ŇHe always comes to at least one game every year,Ó Draco explained. ŇI hadnŐt known it was going to be this one; he didnŐt warn me. He usually comes down to see the team before the game - probably so we can thank him for the uniforms in person - but I suggested to him that it wouldnŐt be a good idea this year. He wasnŐt happy about it, but he agreed.Ó Harry let out a sigh of relief. ŇThank you,Ó he said genuinely. ŇI canŐt guarantee that he wonŐt come see us after, though,Ó Draco said. ŇMy father very much tends to do as he pleases.Ó Harry nodded once, then went to the broom stand to grab his Firebolt. ŇLetŐs go.Ó *** ŇFolks, this game certainly promises to be an exciting one!Ó Lee Jordan belted out through the loudspeaker. ŇGryffindor versus Slytherin is always the roughest game of the year, full of intrigue and drama - and this time around we have the added tragedy of one Harry Potter!Ó Harry rolled his eyes as he rose into the air on his broom. ŇBut at the beginning of the year, an unusual re-sorting took place and, to everyoneŐs great surprise, Potter was placed in Slytherin instead of his old house of Gryffindor. Below, Slytherin already had possession of the Quaffle and was heading towards the Gryffindor side of the pitch. A wild cheer erupted from the Slytherin section of the stands. Vince and GregŐs boom and zoom maneuver actually seemed to be working, and Vince sent a Bludger flying away from Montague and towards Katie Bell. Harry could tell he was letting his nerves get to him, but Harry was still somewhat infuriated with Ron, and he didnŐt care. It was all the better for the Slytherin team, after all. Just as Montague scored his first point of the game, Harry started to hear singing from below. He looked down, confused, and realized it was coming from the Slytherin section. Ron seemed to be getting even worse, if that was possible, and Montague and Warrington promptly scored goals back to back. ŇWeasley is our kingÉÓ The song continued, and Harry noticed Fred and George shooting furious looks towards the Slytherin stands and team - Harry included. By now the score was now already 90-30, and Ron kept missing saves. ŇWeasley is our king, He always lets the Quaffle in, Weasley is our king!Ó Harry took a handful of his hair and gripped it in frustration, feeling absolutely awful. HeŐd already been in a terrible mood before the game even started, and now he felt even worse. Harry may have still been furious with Ron, but he still didnŐt deserve such treatment. Even worse was that Harry felt responsible. HeŐd told Pansy that Ron was no longer off-limits, and he should have remembered how vicious Pansy could be. ŇWeasley cannot save a thingÉÓ With a split-second decision, he sped off towards the Slytherin stands in a fury. ŇKnock it off!Ó he roared at Pansy. ŇThere are three other Weasleys still on that list, and youŐre still using their name, arenŐt you?Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇItŐs cruel and itŐs unnecessary,Ó Harry hissed. WeŐll stop.Ó ŇThank you,Ó Harry snapped, and he turned back to the playing field. He immediately stopped his broom in mid-air. Ginny was right behind him. ŇYou guys will get it back in no time,Ó he said with a sigh. ŇOur housemates were being incredibly distracting. The Snitch darted down the side of the goalpost and he followed right behind, Ginny trailing. The Slytherins started singing again, and Harry felt a wave of irritation. HeŐd already told Pansy not to sing it, but she was apparently not going to listen. He blocked it out and concentrated on the Snitch. It dashed to the left, and then straight up again, and Harry pulled up on his broom hard in order to change direction. The Slytherins in the stands exploded, and then the song theyŐd started grew impossibly loud. Harry turned towards them in annoyance, and then he noticed the words. ŇPotter is a Slytherin So Gryffindors are witherinŐ They lost their star; now heŐs ours And weŐll surely win!Ó Harry released his broom with his other hand and put his palm to his face, shaking his head. ŇThat was very well flown, Mr. Potter,Ó said an uncomfortably familiar voice from behind them. Draco immediately released Harry, and Harry spun around to come face to face with Lucius Malfoy. A swell of loathing rose in HarryŐs chest and he narrowed his eyes. ŇVery well flown, indeed.Ó LuciusŐs eyes flickered over to Draco. ŇYou also did rather impressively, Draco,Ó he said. ŇYou make a fine Chaser.Ó Draco seemed to puff up at that. ŇThis design is my favorite by far.Ó Movement from behind Lucius caught HarryŐs eye, and he realized that Dumbledore was approaching them. A sudden spike of extreme hatred the likes of which Harry had never felt suddenly overcame him, and found himself sneering. ŇI have to go,Ó he spat viciously. He spun on his heel and marched off towards the changing room. As he walked the hatred started to fade, leaving Harry feeling incredibly unsettled. He was entirely unsure who the hatred heŐd felt had been directed towards - Lucius Malfoy or Dumbledore. *** Harry spent a long while in the Slytherin changing room trying to get his head back in order. He still didnŐt understand where that sudden surge of hatred had come from. He supposed he would have been able to understand hating Lucius Malfoy, especially with all the raw feelings heŐd had concerning blood purity, and Malfoy was one of the biggest blood purists heŐd ever met. But even if it had been about Malfoy, Harry had never known he was even capable of feeling hatred like that. It left him feeling out of sorts and confused, and he was completely unsure as to what was going on inside his own head. The celebration was in full swing by the time Harry got back to the Slytherin common room, but he didnŐt feel like joining in. ŇIn fact, I wouldnŐt be surprised if you actually enjoyed it.Ó Harry shrugged. ŇCome back here.Ó Harry glanced back and shook his head. ŇSheŐs not cheating to be better at me in Arithmancy or Ancient Runes; she just actually is better. ŇI know you care about her deeply, Harry, and IÉ donŐt like feeling like I said something so insulting to her.Ó ŇYou did say something insulting to her,Ó Tracey said pointedly. ŇI did,Ó Blaise acknowledged. ŇAnd I feel badly for it.Ó He paused. ŇAnd not for your sake, Harry - but for hers.Ó Harry felt completely floored. Blaise obviously still a firm hold on his blood status prejudices, but he was also obviously feeling incredibly conflicted. ŇIÉ you should tell her that, though.Ó He sighed. ŇAnd Dean, too. He may have been out of line, but -Ó Blaise sighed. ŇAnd therein lies the problem,Ó he said. ŇWhatŐs the problem?Ó Harry asked. ŇIs it Dean, because of what he said?Ó Blaise shook his head and snuck a look at Tracey. ŇIŐm acknowledging that IŐmÉ willing to look at this blood status issue more closely. ŇI believe IŐm an intelligent person, and it wouldnŐt be very smart of me to accept it all at face value,Ó he continued. ŇBut I canŐt apologize to Granger.Ó ŇWhy not?Ó Harry demanded. Blaise sighed. ŇYou know what Thomas was saying about my mother?Ó ŇThe seven dead husbands thing? Yeah. That doesnŐt mean you shouldnŐt -Ó ŇItŐs almost certainly true,Ó Blaise said, and he shook his head. ŇI may be her only son, but I canŐt even imagine what sheŐd do if she heard a rumor that I wasÉ having doubts.Ó Harry stared at Blaise. ŇBlaise said this all so much better earlier, Harry. His point is that heŐs going to be looking at his own prejudice very, very closely.Ó Harry nodded. It wasnŐt perfect, but it was far more than he expected. ŇYou know Hermione wouldnŐt say anything to anyone, right?Ó he said to Blaise. ŇI know you trust her, Harry,Ó Blaise said. ŇBut I donŐt.Ó *** The Slytherins went to dinner late, having celebrated the victory for the rest of the afternoon. Harry took his usual seat next to Blaise, feeling like his soul had been sucked out by a Dementor but then put back wet. He was exhausted, but he felt far more at peace than he had since the previous day. His housemates may have been blood purists, but it apparently wasnŐt all that hard to sow some seeds of doubt in their beliefs. Harry smiled to himself. Slytherins were, after all, supposed to be quite adaptable. Maybe they could change. ŇYou should apologize to Pansy, by the way,Ó Blaise murmured to Harry as he began filling up his plate. ŇShe really did work hard on the song.Ó Harry sighed dramatically and looked across the table. ŇPansy, IŐm sorry for putting a stop to yourÉ rousing hymn.Ó Pansy turned his nose up at him. ŇVery funny.Ó ŇYou said the weasel was no longer off-limits,Ó Draco pointed out. ŇAnd he hurt you,Ó Pansy said. ŇI thought I was doing you a favor.Ó ŇWe wanted to make badges, too, but there wasnŐt enough time,Ó Draco added. HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇI am so glad you didnŐt.Ó ŇThe badges would have been about the weasel, not you,Ó Blaise said. ŇNo worry about embarrassing your humble sensibilities.Ó Harry punched Blaise in the arm, and Blaise laughed. Harry grinned back at him. And then, out of the corner of his eye, he noticed Hermione standing up from the Gryffindor table and begin stalking out of the hall, clearly upset. HarryŐs eyes widened, and he immediately clambered out of his seat, jogging down the side of the hall. ŇHermione!Ó Hermione ignored him, and he picked up his pace. ŇHermione, wait!Ó he called after her. He couldnŐt catch up to her; sheŐd had too much of a head start. He didnŐt see her anywhere outside of the hall, and he deflated in disappointment. Hermione had obviously seen he and Blaise laughing together, just one day after Blaise had called her a ÔMudblood.Ő That wasnŐt going to go over well, and Harry knew it. ŇHello, Harry-kins.Ó Harry looked up to see Fred and George coming his way. ŇVery nice look, the lack of glasses,Ó George said. ŇQuite dashing, if I must say,Ó Fred added. ŇUhÉ thank you?Ó Harry said, hesitant. ŇWe just wanted to thank you for what you did at the match,Ó George said. ŇNot for catching the Snitch - that was a terrible, awful thing for you to do,Ó Fred said, shaking his head. ŇBut for putting a stop to the Slytherin peanut gallery,Ó George said, all traces of humor uncharacteristically gone. ŇGinny told us,Ó Fred said, nodding. ŇSo - thank you.Ó Harry swallowed, then nodded at them. ŇIÉ IŐm sorry that it happened at all. They didnŐt tell me.Ó Fred shrugged. ŇSlytherins are little sneaky snakes, and they snuck it right past you.Ó Then George grinned. ŇBut youŐre turning out all rightÉ for a Slytherin.Ó *** The next day, Harry spotted Hermione in the courtyard and he made a beeline for her. He waved at Draco and Pansy to go on without him. ŇGo. IŐll be fine,Ó he said quietly. ŇI need to do this.Ó Draco looked from him to Hermione, and then, astonishingly, gave Harry a nod that looked more understanding than not. To his great surprise, Hermione spied Harry making his way over to her, and she headed straight for him. They met in the middle of the yard, and at the exact same time they both asked, ŇCan we talk?Ó Harry raised his eyebrows. ŇGo ahead.Ó ŇNo, you first,Ó Hermione said. Harry swallowed nervously. ŇIŐm really sorry about what Blaise said the other day. ItÉ it wonŐt happen again.Ó Harry desperately wished he could say more. Hermione pursed her lips. ŇItŐs nice to hear you say that, especially since it looked like you were quick to forgive him.Ó ŇWe hadÉ a very long conversation,Ó Harry said hesitantly. ŇThatŐs all I can really say.Ó Then he sighed. ŇI knew when I was sorted into Slytherin that IŐd definitely be in the minority when it came toÉ all that. SheŐs a pureblood, but youŐve spoken with her and you know sheŐs all right.Ó He paused, sighing. ŇAnd I know you probably want to talk to me aboutÉ still talking to Blaise, after what he said, butÉÓ Hermione didnŐt look at all happy, but she shook her head. ŇIÉ appreciate it, but thatŐs not why I wanted to speak with you.Ó ŇOh,Ó Harry said. ŇWell, then, whatŐs up?Ó Hermione took a deep breath, and she seemed to be looking very closely at HarryŐs face. ŇHarry, are you okay?Ó ŇIŐm fine,Ó he said truthfully. ŇBit of a rough go for the last few days, but I think IŐmÉ actually better than IŐve been in a while.Ó ŇIŐm worried about you.Ó Harry froze. McGonagall had said the same thing to him, as had Ron - right before Ron had abruptly ended their friendship. ŇWhy?Ó Harry asked suspiciously. Hermione sighed, and then glanced around the yard. She grabbed his arm and pulled him over to an alcove. ŇRonald is convinced that you may have participated in some sort ofÉ dark arts ritual,Ó she whispered heatedly. Harry let out an exasperated sigh. ŇHe caught me and the other Slytherins coming back from Samhain.Ó ŇWhich Ron insists is a holiday to celebrate the dark arts, when dark wizards do awful spells and rituals, and worse,Ó Hermione said. ŇSeamus, Lavender, and Parvati all said the same. ŇI can probably recommend a different one for you.Ó ŇHarry, donŐt you dare -Ó ŇHermione,Ó Harry said, feeling odd. It wasnŐt often that he felt more well-read than Hermione on a topic - never, in fact. ŇAll wizards once celebrated all of the eight holidays. ItŐs just that certain traditional days were more important to certain wizards. Holidays like Beltane and Litha were more important to light wizards, but dark wizards still celebrated them. Samhain is a holiday thatŐs important to dark wizards, and light wizards used to celebrate it, too. She was just assuming those things for the wrong reasons, and besides - he couldnŐt ever admit to her that heŐd had an unyielding interest in the dark arts since the summer. She wouldnŐt understand. Hermione consistently got all of her information from the Hogwarts library, which, according to Theo, had apparently been completely cleansed of anything that was even remotely positive about the dark arts. ŇI have been, and itŐs fascinating. ThereŐs so much I didnŐt know.Ó Hermione let out a weak laugh. ŇYou sound like me.Ó Harry laughed back. ŇYeah, I guess I do.Ó ŇThereŐs something else, thoughÉÓ she continued. Harry nodded at her to proceed. ŇAre youÉ are you dating Pansy Parkinson?Ó Harry spluttered. ŇWhat?Ó he asked, his mouth falling open. ŇRon said you two wereÉ holding hands.Ó Harry scrubbed his hand down his face. ŇOh, geez...Ó ŇSo are you?Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry stared up at the sky, sighing. ŇNo. Pansy is just super touchy-feely with her friends.Ó His comment was met with silence, so he looked back at Hermione to see her staring at him with a frown. ŇWhat?Ó Hermione appeared to brace herself, as if waiting for bad news. ŇSo sheŐs yourÉ friend, now.Ó Harry nodded. ŇYeah. ŇYou seemed toÉ be understanding earlier this year - I wasnŐt just going to be alone and miserable for the rest of my years at Hogwarts. ŇAgain?Ó He shook his head. ŇI get that what Blaise said wasÉÓ He let out a sound of frustration. ŇJust because IŐm friends with them doesnŐt mean I share their beliefs!Ó Hermione didnŐt respond at first, and then she too, grew angry. ŇHarry, you never used to worry about things like that!Ó Harry put his palm to his face and let out a groan, realizing how what heŐd said earlier must have sounded. ŇYouŐre certainly talking about blood status in a way you never have before!Ó ŇBecause you know me, Hermione!Ó Harry snapped. ŇYouŐve known me for five years!Ó ŇAnd you are also telling me that you are now friends with people that feel I am worth less than the dirt underneath their fingernails,Ó Hermione snarled. ŇIt just hurts so much, Harry.Ó Harry stopped and looked at Hermione - really looked at her - and finally realized that he felt absolutely awful. Hermione felt awful about him being friends with blood purists, and he felt awful for making her feel awful. He abruptly reached out and pulled her into a hug. ŇOh, HermioneÉ IÔm so sorry,Ó he said regretfully. ŇI honestly donŐt know if that makes it any better.Ó HermioneŐs voice was muffled; sheŐd buried her face in HarryŐs shoulder. Harry again found himself desperately wishing he could tell her about the discussion heŐd had with Tracey and Blaise the day before. ŇWhich I am absolutely certain is for your benefit and not mine.Ó Harry shrugged, then nodded. She wasnŐt wrong. ŇBut I canŐt begin to forgive them,Ó she said. ŇNot even for you.Ó ŇI can understand that,Ó Harry said. ŇI do just want to be clear that my interest in Ôwizarding traditionŐ has nothing to do with any of the other archaic views they might hold. ŇIŐm not entirely satisfied, butÉ I do believe you, Harry.Ó And Harry believed her completely - including her not being satisfied with his answers. He could tell she wasnŐt happy, but Harry had absolutely no idea what to do about it. Hermione glanced around the courtyard cautiously; it was surprisingly empty - just a few students sat on the other side, out of earshot. ŇThereŐs one more thing.Ó Seeing as how the other topics theyŐd covered hadnŐt exactly been pleasant ones, Harry braced himself. ŇWe really need to do something about Umbridge.Ó Harry raised an eyebrow. ŇWeŐre never going to learn proper Defense in her class, and weŐre not going to pass our O.W.L.s, and weŐll be completely defenseless againstÉ V-Voldemort.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened at that; sheŐd never said his name before. ŇI could write Lupin, but I think heŐs too busy withÉ Order things.Ó ŇIŐm not talking about Lupin, Harry,Ó Hermione said. ŇIŐm talking about you.Ó Harry froze and stared at Hermione. ŇI really wanted to ask you earlier this year, but I figured you had enough going on with your re-sorting and everything, butÉÓ She huffed. ŇYou are the best in our year in defense - oh, donŐt look at me like that, you know youŐve beat me in class more than once - and youŐve actually come face to face with life-and.death situations and survived. ŇIf you remember, I was literally expelled this summer. ThatŐs how I wound up in Slytherin in the first place.Ó He shook his head. ŇHogwarts is my home, Hermione - no matter what house IŐm in. ŇThatÉ that doesnŐt sound like you, Harry.Ó ŇHow does it not sound like me?Ó ŇYouŐre the boy that risked his life to get the PhilosopherŐs Stone, and against the basilisk, and to save Sirius,Ó she said. ŇYou put yourself at far more risk than expulsion. And now youŐre justÉ ignoring this?Ó ŇIŐm not ignoring it,Ó Harry said. ŇThis just isnŐt how I want to deal with it. ItŐs tooÉÓ He cut himself off. ItŐs too Gryffindor, he thought. ŇI just wish you would at least consider it,Ó Hermione said again. ŇIŐll think of something else,Ó he said, not mentioning that Blaise had already been thinking about it since the beginning of the year. An awkward silence fell between them. ŇSoÉ howŐs Gryffindor?Ó Harry asked in a desperate attempt to change the subject. Hermione threw her head back and sighed. ŇI miss having you in the tower so much, Harry. ItŐs really not the same without you.Ó ŇI miss you, too,Ó he said, and it was the truth. Despite the fact that heŐd been growing more comfortable in Slytherin, he genuinely did miss the tower. ŇI feel like IŐm the only fifth year prefect, with how seriously Ron takes it,Ó she said. ŇAnd sometimes itŐs hard to be an example to the others; I nearly hexed McLaggen and Seamus the other night for what they were saying about you.Ó ŇWhat were they saying?Ó Hermione huffed. ŇSeamus is really upset, Harry. I mean - he was already upset before, but with the stories going around the tower about you being involved in the dark arts, heŐs absolutely furious now. ŇItŐs really not that important, Harry; you know that everything that comes out of his mouth is complete hogwash.Ó ŇWhat did he say, Hermione?Ó She sighed again. ŇHe was saying that your tale aboutÉ VÉ Voldemort returning was just to distract everyone from youÉÓ She paused, pursing her lips. Ň... from you working on becoming the next Dark Lord, and that your being sorted into Slytherin just showed how similar you were to him...Ó Harry was first shocked, and then enraged. ŇWhat?Ó ŇItŐs utter tripe, and most of us know thatÉ oh, I shouldnŐt have even told youÉÓ Despite HermioneŐs words, underneath the bubbling anger there was a nasty thought whispering to him.
