You know it doesnŐt mean anything.Ó *** Harry dragged his feet as he walked back to the Slytherin common room. He stopped outside the door and stared at it. How many times did Tom Riddle walk through this door? He knew it was an absolutely absurd thought to have; Voldemort was a Slytherin, too. Of course they would walk through the same door, and into the same common room. But IŐm still becoming more and more like him, Harry thought. Within a few months, I started researching the dark arts, and then was resorted into his houseÉ Then a horrible thought suddenly occurred to him. What if IŐm only interested in the dark arts because Voldemort is interested in the dark arts? He knew it was entirely possible. After all, Dumbledore had told him that he was likely a Parselmouth because Voldemort was a Parselmouth: that the ability had been transferred to Harry the night Voldemort tried to kill him. What if Voldemort had transferred more than just parseltongue? He sighed and desperately wished he had someone to talk to about this, but there wasnŐt a soul in the castle that he could possibly speak to about it. He definitely wasnŐt ready to discuss his connection to Voldemort with the Slytherins - that was more than heŐd even told Ron and Hermione. On the other side, he couldnŐt mention his interest in the dark arts to Hermione, and definitely not to Dumbledore. Not that Dumbledore would even speak to me, he though somewhat bitterly. ŇYou promised to come speak with me, and you have not.Ó Harry turned his head and looked up at the snake painting and saw that the snake was staring at him. ŇIŐm sorry. IŐve been distracted,Ó he replied. ŇYou do seem troubled.Ó Harry nodded, and then something suddenly occurred to him: anything he said to the snake likely wouldnŐt make it anywhere else. After all, the only other person that could speak with the painting was Voldemort, and he wasnŐt likely to appear at Hogwarts anytime soon. ŇIŐm worried about something,Ó Harry said, and he found himself unloading all of his worries onto the snake - the similarities between himself and Tom Riddle, that HarryŐs new obsession with the dark arts wasnŐt coming from himself, and that he really was becoming more and more like Voldemort. ŇThe one youŐre referring to - he was the last speaker here, wasnŐt he?Ó the snake said when Harry finished ranting. ŇYes,Ó Harry said. ŇYou do look quite a bit alike,Ó the snake said. ŇAnd your voices sound the same.Ó Harry immediately absolutely regretted talking to the snake, because now he just felt even worse. *** Alone in the dorm, Harry lay on his four-poster and pulled out his wand. He held it up by both ends, balancing it between his fingertips, and studied it with a deep frown set on his face. His wand shared a core with VoldemortŐs wand. The insides were the same. Yet another thing to add to the list of the ways he and Riddle were similar. He heard the door open. ŇDid I just walk into a moment of teenage angst?Ó he heard Draco ask. Harry let out a weak laugh. ŇPretty much.Ó Draco came into view, leaning over HarryŐs bed. ŇIs this still aboutÉ what Blaise said the other day?Ó he asked quietly. ŇTheÉ blood statusÉ conundrum?Ó For a moment Harry considered saying Ôyes,Ő knowing that would function as a perfectly valid excuse for his melancholy. But before he could say anything, though, Draco continued. ŇNo, thatŐs not it - I can tell just from looking at you,Ó Draco said, narrowing his eyes, and Harry glanced up at him in surprise. ŇWhatŐs wrong?Ó Once again, Harry was astounded by DracoŐs insight. He sighed and sat up. ŇIÉ I donŐt think I can tell you.Ó Draco narrowed his eyes. ŇHarry, I keep telling you - Slytherins wonŐt turn each other in -Ó ŇItŐs not that simple, Draco,Ó Harry said, cutting him off. ŇItŐsÉÓ He looks down and slowly rotated his wand in his hands, studying each mark in the wood. ŇItŐs something about me, and itŐsÉÓ ŇHarry, if I must say itÉ you look terrified,Ó Draco said. ŇI donŐt remember ever seeing you be scared of anything.Ó Harry blinked, and then realized that Draco was right - he was terrified. Some Gryffindor he was. ŇWhat if I swear?Ó Draco asked suddenly. ŇWhat?Ó Draco pulled out his wand and held it upright in front of him. ŇI, Draco Lucius Malfoy, hereby swear to the dark and by my own magic, that I will not reveal to another soul whatever secret Harry Potter is about to tell me.Ó Harry narrowed his eyes. ŇHey, I didnŐt say I was going to -Ó Draco ignored him. ŇIf I dare to reveal his secret without his permission, the dark may strike me down and strip me of my magic. So mote it be.Ó The tip of his wand flashed, and Harry could actually feel the magic of DracoŐs oath drop into place. Draco pocketed his wand. ŇNow tell me,Ó he said demandingly. Harry thought he sounded rather spoiled, and he just stared at Draco. ŇOh, donŐt look at me like that,Ó Draco said, rolling his eyes. ŇYouŐre obviously eating yourself alive from whatever angst is haunting you. ItŐs not like I can tell anyone, including another Slytherin.Ó Harry sighed. ŇHonestly, IŐm not sure if you want to hear it,Ó he said. ŇThatŐs a poor excuse.Ó Draco sat on the edge of HarryŐs bed, leaning up against one of the posters. Harry looked at Draco for a few long moments, and he suddenly realized that Draco was the only person that he felt heŐd really, truly confided in that year. Draco was the one that had talked him through his issues with his father and gave him advice on who to talk to. Draco was the one that had explained that dark magic wasnŐt controlling him. Draco, more than anyone else in the house, had given him the information on the dark arts that Harry had been looking for. Quite abruptly, Harry realized that he felt comfortable talking to Draco. How in the world had his relationship with Draco had changed so rapidly? ŇOkay,Ó Harry finally said, and then wondered where he should start. ŇIŐm waiting.Ó ŇShut up,Ó Harry said, somewhat kindly, then he let out another sigh. ŇOkay. ThereŐs this wizard. HeŐs an orphan, and a half-blood, was raised by Muggles. HeŐs a Slytherin. HeŐs a Parselmouth.Ó Harry paused and took in a deep breath. ŇHis wand has a phoenix feather core. And heŐs got this weird... fascination with the dark arts.Ó Draco looked confused. ŇI know you said this was about you, but...Ó Harry let his eyes fall shut. ŇYep, thatŐs me. But all of that also applies to someone else.Ó ŇWho?Ó ŇA wizard named Tom Riddle.Ó DracoŐs blank look indicated that heŐd never heard the name. ŇYou know him as Voldemort.Ó Draco didnŐt say anything at first. When he finally did, his voice was quiet. ŇThe Dark Lord is a half-blood?Ó Harry felt incredibly annoyed. ŇOf course thatŐs the first thing you jump on,Ó he muttered. The sticky issue of blood purity would always be in the undercurrent of any conversation he had with Draco, no matter how comfortable heŐd become with speaking with him. ŇNo, I justÉÓ Draco paused. ŇI wonder if Father knows,Ó he said, seemingly more to himself than to Harry. Draco suddenly leapt up from his spot on the bed and began to pace in front of Harry. Harry felt his heart sink. Draco wouldnŐt even look at him. ŇI knew I shouldnŐt have told you,Ó Harry said with a sigh. Draco stopped in his tracks. ŇWhat? Why?Ó Understanding then seemed to dawn on his face. ŇYou think youŐre similar to the Dark Lord.Ó ŇWe are similar,Ó Harry said. ŇIŐve even been told more than once that I look a little like he did when he was our age.Ó He didnŐt mention that a young Tom Riddle had been the first to make that comparison. Draco seemed to consider HarryŐs words. ŇMost of theseÉ similaritiesÉ seem rather superficial, Harry.Ó ŇOur wands have the same core,Ó Harry said. ŇPhoenix feather is a very common core -Ó ŇNo, I mean they are literally the same,Ó Harry said insistently. ŇThe tail feathers came from the same phoenix, and that phoenix apparently only gave two feathers.Ó Draco raised an eyebrow. ŇOkay, IŐll admit that part is strange. But youŐre not the same person, Harry.Ó He let out a slight scoff. ŇNot even close.Ó ŇI know weŐre not. ItŐs justÉÓ Harry stopped and sighed. ŇDumbledore has a theory that I can speak parseltongue because Voldemort may have transferred the ability to me when heÉÓ He trailed off and merely pointed to the scar on his forehead. ŇWhat if that wasnŐt the only thing he transferred?Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó ŇI came from a light family,Ó Harry said. ŇI know sometimes it happens, but what if I only have a dark affinity becauseÉ because of him?Ó Draco frowned at that. ŇWhat if IŐm becoming more and more like him? This dark arts thing is ratherÉ new. And then I was sorted into Slytherin...Ó Draco studied Harry for a long moment, to the point that Harry started to become uncomfortable. ŇIt sounds like the situation with you and the Dark Lord is ratherÉ unique, but IŐve never heard of someoneŐs affinity being affected like that. Affinity is either born orÉ manifested, not transferred.Ó Harry could tell from the look on DracoŐs face that there was something else he wanted to say. ŇButÉ?Ó Draco sighed. ŇI have heard a theory that if extremely dark magic is used on a child when theyŐre very young, it can affect their affinity. ThereŐs no proof of it, but -Ó Harry felt his heart sink. ŇSo it is true, then.Ó He looked away from Draco and began studying his wand again. ŇMy affinity is only dark because of him.Ó ŇWould itÉ donŐt take this the wrong way, butÉÓ Draco took a deep breath. ŇWould it really matter if was because of him?Ó ŇOf course it would matter!Ó Harry snapped. ŇWhy?Ó Draco asked. ŇIŐm notÉ trying to be dismissive of your concern. IŐm just trying to understand why it should matter.Ó ŇBecause that means itŐs not me!Ó Harry said, jumping up to his feet. ŇParselmouth is his ability. His affinity is dark. All of it came from him!Ó Draco suddenly reached out and grasped one of HarryŐs hands with both of his. ŇI really donŐt think affinity works like that. Your affinity might be dark because of what he did to you, not because he somehowÉ transferred his own affinity to you.Ó He huffed. ŇAnd even if it did happen like that, though, itŐs yours now. ItŐs your scar. Parselmouth is your ability. Your affinity is dark. Even if the Dark Lord caused all of those things, they all add up to who you are today.Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇYou canŐt help what was done to you. Nobody can,Ó Draco continued. ŇAll you can do is accept those things as part of who you are now.Ó He sighed. ŇYou canŐt change the past, Harry. All you can do is adapt, so that youŐre able to change the future to what you want it.Ó Harry blinked, realizing that Draco had nearly exactly echoed what Andromeda had told him. ŇThatŐsÉ astoundingly level-headed.Ó ŇAre you saying IŐm not level-headed?Ó Harry just raised an eyebrow in response, and then smirked. Draco rolled his eyes. ŇIŐll understand if thisÉ influences your decision to declare dark or not,Ó he said. ŇI hope it doesnŐt, though.Ó Harry nodded, then glanced down to where DracoŐs hands were grasping his own. ŇSoÉ are you telling me youŐre just as touchy as Pansy?Ó he said with a smirk. Draco released HarryŐs hand, and then, to HarryŐs astonishment, he winked. Chapter End Notes The issue of prejudice in the wizarding world mirrors a lot of what happens in our own world. It will be an ongoing issue in this fic, and I'm making every effort to address it in a realistic yet tasteful fashion (and without being preachy). Because it is a sensitive topic I welcome feedback on how I'll be handling this... just please make sure we have a grown-up conversation in the comments rather than yelling. ;) Another note: we're reaching the tail end of the scenes that I had written prior to beginning to post this fic. This means that you're going to see my pace slow down while I write (most) of what's left from scratch. I hope y'all stick with it anyway, and thanks for sticking with me so far. :D Chapter13:The Instinct Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. ŇWeŐll see where it goes from here.Ó *** Harry soon left with Daphne, Blaise, and Draco, and almost immediately after they turned the corner Daphne grabbed HarryŐs arm and began dragging him to an empty classroom. ŇHey!Ó Harry protested. ŇWe need to move your books before we go to lunch.Ó They exited the classroom and began making their way to the dungeons. Harry remained silent on the way there, mulling over their conversation with a frown. While he hadnŐt directly lied to Hermione the day before, heŐd still manipulated her. HeŐd recommended that she try to research the cyclical calendar herself, hoping that she would throw herself into her books like she always did. And heŐd done it with the sole intent of distracting her from the rumors about him and the dark arts. Even more disturbing, he hadnŐt even noticed what heŐd done until Daphne had pointed it out to him. In addition to that disconcerting thought, he realized that although he hadnŐt been willing to teach a secret defense class due to potentially getting expelled by Umbridge, he was more than willing to continue his research into the dark arts at the risk of getting arrested. Six months ago, he more than likely would have been disgusted at the thought of manipulating Hermione or researching dark arts to the point of risking arrest. HeŐd insisted to Ron and Hermione that he was the same person heŐd always been, but both Ron and Hermione had both told him that he wasnŐt sounding like himself. Maybe they were right. *** Dear Andromeda, I honestly have no idea how to even ask this, or what IŐm even asking. IŐve tried to write this letter at least ten times and itŐs still coming out wrong. You said that the element of Slytherin is water, and water is the element of change. I feel like IŐve been changing, and IŐm not sure I like whatever IŐm changing into. IŐm worried about certain things that IŐve been doing. ŇYes, Professor Umbridge.Ó ŇWhatŐs going on that all the teachers need to talk to you?Ó Draco whispered as they packed up their books. Harry shook his head. ŇIt canŐt be about the same thing,Ó he said back. Blaise shot Harry a cautious look on his way out the door, but didnŐt say anything. ŇBlow up afterwards if you need to, but don't do it while you're around her.Ó Harry nodded and gave Pansy a weak smile, and then he and Umbridge were alone. Umbridge was pulling up a chair in front of her desk. ŇHave a seat, Mr. Potter.Ó Harry took in a deep breath and then sat, looking at Umbridge expectantly. He clenched his hand around the strap of his bag so tightly that his knuckles turned white. He took a deep breath and out and tried his hardest to keep his voice steady. ŇIŐm sorry, Professor,Ó he said, Ňbut I donŐt think I will.Ó UmbridgeŐs eyes bulged. ŇWhyever not? ŇIŐve had enough of being in that paper.Ó Umbridge frowned. ŇSurely you know that telling the truth would clear your name?Ó Harry shook his head and searched for the right words; he had to find a way out of this situation. ŇItŐs not just this past summer, Professor Umbridge,Ó he said slowly. ŇThe Prophet has never been favorable towards me.Ó He narrowed his eyes. ŇIŐm sure you recall Rita SkeeterŐs work last year? ŇI must say that I am most disappointed, Mr. -Ó ŇIf your name had been repeatedly and unfairly dragged through the mud before you were even old enough to take your O.W.L.s, what would you have done?Ó Harry asked, interrupting her. He narrowed his eyes in thought; he had to appeal to her somehow. ŇWhat would you have done, as a Slytherin?Ó Umbridge shut her mouth and appeared to actually consider the question. Suddenly, it hit Harry. She valued her power, Blaise had said, and she liked it when people pointed out how much power she actually held. He tried to channel Blaise as he spoke. ŇI will be frank with you, Professor,Ó Harry said, leaning forward in his chair and clasping his hands on her desk. ŇIt is clear that despite protestations from both sides, the Ministry for Magic does hold some sway over what is printed in the Prophet. Would you say that is true?Ó Umbridge let out a short, unpleasant laugh. ŇIt may or may not be true.Ó ŇI see,Ó Harry said. He raised his arm and placed his chin in his hand, imitating Draco when he appeared to be deep in thought. ŇIf you could somehow guarantee that the Prophet would never slander my name again, I may be willing toÉ consider your proposal.Ó UmbridgeŐs eyes lit up at that. ŇI can certainly see what I can do, Mr. Potter, butÉÓ ŇI want to be clear, Professor,Ó he said. ŇAfter how theyŐve treated me, I will not accept a mere handshake from them. I want something... binding.Ó Umbridge frowned. ŇThat may be difficult. You know how reporters are -Ó ŇI certainly do,Ó Harry said. ŇThatŐs exactly why IŐm asking for this in the first place.Ó Umbridge didnŐt look pleased at all, but she nodded. ŇYour terms areÉ understandable. I will speak to a few people to see what we can do for you.Ó Then she smiled, her lips curling disgustingly. ŇYou certainly seem to be adapting to your new house rather well, Mr. PotterÉ Slytherin to Slytherin.Ó Harry swallowed, but nodded. ŇI think I am, maŐam.Ó He picked up his bag and put the strap across his shoulder. ŇIs there anything else?Ó Umbridge waved a dismissive hand. ŇNo, Mr. Potter,Ó she said. ŇYou may go.Ó Harry got to his feet and inclined his head towards Umbridge just slightly. ŇThank you, Professor. Have a good day.Ó ŇYou as well, Mr. Potter.Ó Harry exited the room, and almost immediately began sprinting down the hallway. His wand was already in his hand and as soon as he turned the corner his frustration came barrelling out of him, and he sent blast after blast of raw magic into the wall of the castle. He hated her. She expected him to contribute to the lie that the Ministry continued to spread, and he couldnŐt even give her a straight denial out of fear of being expelled from Hogwarts again. ŇHarry!Ó He sent one more furious blast of magic into the wall before spinning on his heel to see Daphne, Pansy, and Draco jogging towards him. ŇI know you need to let off some steam after that conversation,Ó Pansy said, Ňbut youŐre going to attract attention.Ó ŇThatÉ fuckingÉ toad!Ó Harry let out a cry of frustration. ŇDo you know what she wants me to do?Ó ŇYes,Ó Pansy said. An arrow of confusion pierced through HarryŐs fury. ŇWhat?Ó Daphne held up a long string with an ear attached to the end, and Pansy and Draco followed suit. All three of them had obviously used Extendable Ears to eavesdrop on the conversation. Harry stared at them in disbelief before shaking his head. ŇSlytherins,Ó he muttered. Draco grinned wolfishly. ŇAnd you were a very, very good Slytherin in there, Harry,Ó he said, almost purring. ŇYou played her perfectly.Ó Harry shook his head, feeling frustrated. ŇWhat if she actually manages to come through with that deal from the Prophet? What do I do then?Ó ŇShe probably wonŐt,Ó Daphne said. ŇThe Prophet is under the MinistryŐs thumb, but sheŐs right - they are still reporters. They wonŐt agree to anything thatŐsÉ how did you put it?Ó ŇÔBinding,ŐÓ Draco said, laughing. ŇSo even if by some miracle she does manage to secure a deal, whatever statement you give to the Prophet would be on your terms.Ó ŇMeaning we can help you think of something to say,Ó Pansy said. ŇBut even if it does happen, a deal like that would take a lot of time,Ó Daphne said. ŇThe truth might already be out by the time you both came to an agreement.Ó ŇAnd once the truth comes out the Prophet will be licking your boots,Ó Draco said, cackling. ŇJust like Umbridge was just doing.Ó Harry blinked. ŇWhat?Ó Draco reached out and placed a hand on each of HarryŐs shoulders. ŇThe way she was speaking to youÉ sheŐs clearly preparing for the time when your name is cleared. She wants to be in your good graces.Ó Harry screwed his face up in confusion. ŇI donŐt understand.Ó Rolling his eyes in exasperation, Draco released Harry. ŇWhat I donŐt understand is how you manage to be such a good Slytherin without even knowing your resources.Ó ŇYou are just confusing me more and more, you know,Ó Harry complained. Draco stared at Harry for a moment, then shook his head. Pansy and Daphne started laughing. Harry was left feeling completely bewildered, his earlier anger forgotten. *** Dear Harry, IŐm sorry that this is troubling you so. This issue may be harder for me to explain, as I was always a Slytherin while you were not. As a Gryffindor IŐm sure you saw this as black and white, while as a Slytherin I donŐt believe it is ever black and white. Deceit is not always a bad action to take. I deceived my parents for years when I began to fall in love with Ted, and I feel that I was justified in doing so. You mentioned that you felt that you were doing these things for the right reasons. I am unsure what those reasons are, or what your end goal is, but I think the general rule is that in order to avoid the crookedness we spoke of, your deceit must not cause harm to those around you. This is, admittedly, a difficult rule to pin down. My parents would have said that I harmed the Black family by choosing to marry a Muggleborn, but their reactions were driven by their prejudice. It is hard to say whether or not I feel your actions are justified without knowing the specifics of what happened, but I will say this: just from the short time that we have been communicating with one another, I can already tell that you are not a crooked person, Harry. You have a good heart and I believe that even if your deceit may have been for selfish reasons, it was likely still just. I am confident that you will be able to work through this. You seem to be becoming an impressive young Slytherin, and I cannot wait to see what you have in store for the world. Please continue to reach out to me should you need anything. Sincerely, Andromeda Tonks Harry folded up the letter and found that he felt much better - better than he expected to, really. Andromeda had become an odd source of comfort for him, but he wasnŐt expecting her to be able to say much that would reassure him. Once again, sheŐd surprised him. He had deceived Hermione, but it had been due to the fact that she had the same prejudice against the dark arts that much of the wizarding world held. Andromeda had deceived her parents due to their own prejudice, although that prejudice had been against Muggleborns. It wasnŐt HermioneŐs fault that she held that prejudice, Harry reasoned. She got all of her knowledge from books, and Harry had learned that the Hogwarts library was supremely biased against the dark arts. The content the library held actually contributed to the bias of the wizarding world. And he wasnŐt harming Hermione personally; her feelings would likely be hurt if she found out Harry was purposely trying to lead her away from the truth, but he wasnŐt actively causing her pain. With that settled, he felt he could move on to something that heŐd been putting off, and so, at the next fifth year meeting, Harry announced to his housemates that he wanted to learn more about declaring dark. Naturally, Draco was absolutely delighted. ŇIŐve not made a decision,Ó Harry clarified. ŇI just want to know more. One of the books I read over the summer said it couldÉ change my personality, or my temperament. I want to know why some people declare on certain cyclical days. I want to know whatŐs involved in the declaration itself. I want to make sure I know everything before I make a decision either way.Ó And so they set to educating Harry on the ins and outs of dark declaration, and Harry soaked up the knowledge. Some of the information they gave him Harry had already learned on his own, but he was finding it easier to understand with more context. Nearly all dark witches and wizards would start the path to declaring for the dark on the same day - Yule, or the Winter Solstice. Different families had different traditions and rituals for each step of the path, but the basic steps were always the same. After performing what was called the Ôrite of intentŐ on the Solstice, the witch or wizard had to complete three other rites - the rite of blood, the rite of magic, and the rite of self. The personal rites could be completed whenever the witch or wizard chose. After the three personal rites were completed, the final declaration would then take place on a cyclical day .one that tended to speak to the witch or wizard. Pansy explained that the reason she declared so much later than Blaise, Draco, Theo, and Millicent was because she wanted to perform the personal rites on specific dark cyclical days and then complete her final declaration on Samhain. ŇItŐs the way my family has always done it,Ó she said. Nearly all dark wizards did their final declaration on Samhain, Yule, or Imbolc - the three days that Daphne had mentioned to Hermione as being traditionally dark. Likewise, light wizards once tended to declare on Beltane, Litha, or Lammas. No wizard - light or dark - would ever declare on either the spring or autumnal equinox, because equinoxes were the days when magic as a whole was respected. ŇHowever,Ó Millicent mentioned, Ňit doesnŐt really happen anymore, but it might suit you. Witches and wizards that came from traditionally light or neutral families and wind up declaring dark have declared on Beltane in the spring. Beltane is traditionally a light day, so declaring dark on that day shows that you donŐt intend to forget the light roots that you came from.Ó ŇSome people would say that dark witches or wizards that declare on Beltane arenŐt worth trusting, though,Ó Theo said quietly. ŇMy father says they have split loyalties.Ó ŇMy mother says thatŐs a recent development, though,Ó Draco said. ŇIn reality, even dark wizards that are from dark families have declared on Beltane. ItŐs supposed to represent wanting to protect wizarding kind of all walks of magic - light and dark. Light wizards used to declare for the light on Samhain for all the same reasons.Ó Blaise waved a dismissive hand. ŇThe day on which you declare is more symbolic than anything else. ItŐs not going to change what kind of dark wizard you are.Ó Harry shrugged. ŇThat might be true, but having an option that would show I still respect my parents no matter what path IŐm takingÉ thatŐs certainly appealing.Ó He didnŐt fail to notice Draco trying to hide a smile. ŇAnd what aboutÉ the potential of youÉ changing after you declare?Ó Harry asked. That was what he was truly concerned about. HeŐd already been insanely temperamental this year, and he didnŐt want to start regularly blasting the castle walls every time someone made him angry. ŇItŐs rare,Ó Theo said. ŇBut it can happen. You are opening yourself to the dark, which is raw and wild, and sometimes that can make you question your conscience less, orÉÓ ŇTurn someone like Blaise into a giggling loon,Ó Draco quipped. Blaise laughed. ŇOr make Draco even more of a pointy, sarcastic git than he was before.Ó ŇAss.Ó Harry grinned at the exchange, and Theo continued. ŇIf anything does happen, itŐs not like it truly changes you. ItŐs more like it might amplify parts of you that are already there.Ó Harry raised an eyebrow. ŇSo whatŐs changed about Pansy?Ó he asked, glancing over at the witch in question. ŇWell, sheŐs starting to beat me in time spent in the bathroom every morning,Ó Draco said, and a cushion from one of the couches promptly flew into his face. *** November rolled into December. The air grew colder and the nights longer, and their homework load seemed to get heavier and heavier with each passing day. One the last Hogsmeade weekend of the year, Pansy and Draco finally convinced Harry to allow them to give his wardrobe an overhaul. ŇMuggle clothing is appalling as it is, but yours is especially awful,Ó Pansy quipped as they walked down the path. Harry sighed. ŇI should grab a fashion magazine next time IŐm in the Muggle world,Ó he said, stuffing his hands in his cloak to protect them from the cold air. ŇI think youŐd actually like some of the dresses they tend to wear to red carpet events and the like.Ó ŇOh, could you?Ó Pansy asked. ŇDaphne and Tracey have insisted the same thing - that Muggle glamor actually puts wizarding glamor to shame.Ó ŇWhatŐs a red carpet event?Ó Draco asked. Harry shrugged. Harry rammed into him with his shoulder, and Draco's snicker turned into a laugh. They entered Gladrags, which was unusually crowded, and the blast of sudden warmth made Harry let out a sigh of relief. He pulled his hands out of his cloak and rubbed them together, blowing on them. ŇOh, for pityŐs sake,Ó Draco muttered. He grabbed HarryŐs hands and cast a warming charm on them. ŇI swear, sometimes I think youŐre barely a wizard. ThatŐs a first year spell, Potter.Ó Harry grinned sheepishly, flexing his fingers. ŇI always forget.Ó ŇHow youŐve managed to get to fifth year, I shall never know.Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇWell, good thing I have you for the next three, right?Ó Draco rolled his eyes. His cheeks seemed pink from the cold, and Harry wondered why Draco wouldnŐt just use the warming charm on his face, as well. Harry was quickly distracted by PansyŐs flurry of movement, already pulling item after item off of the racks. She occasionally held up an item next to HarryŐs face, considered it, and then either handed it to Draco or discarded it by throwing it back on top of the rack. ŇShouldnŐt you at least put them back properly?Ó Harry said, simultaneously amused and mildly irritated. ŇItŐs what they get paid for,Ó Pansy said, paying no mind to HarryŐs comment as she moved on to the next rack. With cloaks, slacks, and shirts draped over each of DracoŐs arms, Pansy flagged down an employee. ŇIt may be a bit of a wait, maŐam,Ó he said. ŇItŐs the holiday season, so weŐre a bit -Ó ŇYou do realize that this is Harry Potter, correct?Ó Draco said. ŇAnd this is Miss Pansy Parkinson, and I am Draco Malfoy. Even with whatever lies the Prophet said about him, HarryŐs name still struck witches and wizards with awe. Harry let out a silent sigh. ŇOne moment, sir,Ó the employee said. ŇIŐm certain I can find someoneÉÓ he trailed off and jogged over to the counter, where two other employees were ringing out customers. ŇWhy did you do that?Ó Harry hissed at Draco. ŇWe have plenty of time. I donŐt mind waiting.Ó ŇWell, I do mind,Ó Draco said, his familiar snootiness turned on to full effect. ŇYou should learn how to use your name to your advantage, Harry.Ó ŇTo get clothes five minutes faster than we would have otherwise?Ó ŇYou have an advantage and you should use it,Ó Draco said. ŇMuch like Umbridge wants to.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, confused. Draco rolled his eyes. ŇYou mean you still havenŐt figured out why Umbridge wants to strike that deal with you, and isnŐt punishing you for not immediately agreeing?Ó Harry let out a frustrated noise. ŇI donŐt think I really care -Ó ŇItŐs because your name has power,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇLasting power. More than my father, more than the idiot Fudge. ŇI donŐt want to do anything for her - ever!Ó ŇYou should let her, Harry,Ó Pansy said reasonably. ŇBut this is enough for a start.Ó ŇThis is already likeÉ a month's worth of clothing!Ó Harry moaned as he pulled yet another turtleneck over his head. ŇOoh, I like that one,Ó Daphne cooed. ŇThe green-gray is perfect for his coloring, Pansy.Ó ŇIsnŐt it?Ó Pansy grinned. Harry sighed mournfully and turned to look at himself in the wardrobe mirror. Once again, he was struck by how much he actually looked like a Slytherin. This time, however, he didnŐt have the school uniform or the Quidditch robes to bring it out; it was the fine slacks pressed perfectly along the fronts of his legs, and the soft turtleneck that somehow made him look taller. He felt like he was dressed much like how Draco, Blaise, and Theo tended to dress. ŇThe hair will be taken care of soon,Ó Pansy was explaining. ŇI already have his Yule present ordered.Ó ŇI thought this was my Yule present,Ó Harry said, turning back towards her. Pansy and Draco had insisted on paying for most of his new apparel. Harry wouldnŐt let them pay for all of it, but they still had forked over an impressive amount of galleons at Gladrags. ŇThis was simply to get you to start dressing like a Slytherin instead of a house elf,Ó Draco said. He looked Harry up and down and nodded in approval. ŇTry the sweater vest next,Ó he said. *** A few nights later, just before the end of term, Harry and Draco went through some of the hexes from Nearly Undetectable Curses and Hexes in the dorm. Trying out new dark arts spells at least a few times a week had become something of a regular practice for them; they liked to reward themselves after completing a particularly gruelling homework assignment. As their homework had continued piling up, these nights became more and more frequent. Draco flipped through the book, and Harry was trying to perfect a temporary blinding hex when Draco started laughing. ŇRetorsus!Ó he exclaimed. ŇI never knew a favorite of the Death Eaters was from such aÉ basic book.Ó Harry glanced over his shoulder. ŇWhat do you mean?Ó he asked. Draco turned the book around to point out a page. ŇThis one. The Death Eaters use it to make Muggles float in the air, and then it flips them upside down.Ó He let out another laugh. ŇI think you may have seen it in action at the World Cup last summer.Ó Harry scowled. ŇYeah, I saw it,Ó he said in recognition. ŇIt was apparently also a favorite ofÉÓ He trailed off, his eyes widening in realization when something finally occurred to him. ŇFavorite of what?Ó This is actually one James asked me to teach him. Flips people up in the air and upside down. He liked using it on SnivellusÉ ŇHoly shit,Ó Harry said, staring vacantly at the wall. How had he not put two and two together before? Draco walked around Harry to peer at his face. ŇWhatŐs wrong?Ó Harry swallowed, and he met DracoŐs eyes. ŇSiriusÉ he told meÉÓ He shook his head. ŇWhy didnŐt I realize this sooner?Ó he muttered, mostly to himself. ŇTold you what? Draco asked. ŇMy father liked using this spell on Snape,Ó Harry said. ŇWith all the worry about how my father treated Slytherins, I guess I just completely forgot thatÉÓ Draco seemed to have caught on much faster than Harry had, because his eyes widened in surprise. ŇYour father used a dark arts hex?Ó ŇSirius said that my dad didnŐt even know that it was dark, butÉ yeah, he did,Ó Harry said. ŇIt means that my affinity might not be dark because of Voldemort, after all.Ó He finally allowed himself to smile. ŇMaybe - just maybe - itŐs because of my father.Ó *** Harry found himself in the endless sea of hallways, but something had changed since the last time he was there. His body was different; he was smooth and powerful, and his belly was flat on the ground as he slid into crevices he never would have normally been able to fit into. Even though he wasnŐt in his usual form, it felt normal, like he was familiar with this new body, as if heŐd lived in it every day. He glided across the floor without a sound. There was hardly any light in the corridor, but all he had to do was stick out his tongue to know where he had to go. He scented the air and knew that at the end of this hallway there was a man. The man sat in front of something he wanted desperately. It was whatever heŐd been searching for. The man was sleeping, and Harry let out a hiss of frustration. His instincts screamed out at him to bite the man, but he knew he could slip past the sleeping man without incident. Harry had to do as his master commanded; that was far more important than HarryŐs desires. But just as Harry moved to slip past the man, he stirred, and then he spotted Harry on the floor, the long body nearly surrounding him. The man jumped to his feet, brandishing a wand, and Harry, satisfied that he would get to do as he wanted after all, moved to defend himself. Harry rose off the ground, baring his fangs, and then he struck before the man could even get off one spell. HarryŐs teeth sank into the manŐs flesh, and he felt a sense of satisfaction as he felt blood spurting from the wound. He reared back and struck again and felt bones splinter between his jaws. He struck a third time, and then the man let out a cry of pain and then fell back against the wall and was still. HarryŐs forehead exploded in pain. ŇHarry!Ó Harry heard screaming, and he felt like there were firecrackers in his stomach. He rolled over and was vaguely aware of being sick over the side of his bed, and it was only when the screaming stopped that he realized that the screams had been coming from himself. His scar flared in pain and he felt like his head was being split in two. ŇHarry? Harry!Ó A hand grasped his shoulder and Harry struggled to open his eyes. Draco hovered over him, his expression grave and full of concern. ŇGreg and Vince are getting Professor Snape. Are you okay?Ó ŇArthur Weasley,Ó Harry groaned. ŇHeŐs been attacked!Ó ŇIsnŐtÉ isnŐt that the Father Weasel?Ó Harry wasnŐt really in the mood for DracoŐs jokes about the Weasley family right now, but he was also in no shape to tell Draco off. ŇA snake - a big snake - attacked him,Ó Harry groaned. Snape appeared in a sweep of billowing robes, and a bottle was forced into his hands. ŇDrink this, Potter,Ó Snape demanded. ŇPain relieving potion.Ó Harry obeyed and it felt as if a vice was released from his brain. It felt glorious. ŇPlease, sir, I saw it - Arthur Weasley has been attacked -Ó ŇStop,Ó Snape snapped, his voice harsh. ŇGet up.Ó He grabbed HarryŐs arm and forced him to his feet. He swept Harry out of the dorm and through the Slytherin common room. Harry felt like he was being half-dragged through the halls of Hogwarts, and he realized he had absolutely no idea where they were going. ŇSir, Arthur Weasley was attacked by a snake -Ó Harry tried saying again. ŇWeŐre on our way to Dumbledore, Potter,Ó Snape said in a hissing whisper. ŇTell him when we get there.Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇYou do realize that your being in my house is beginning to put myÉ position at risk?Ó ŇYour position?Ó Harry asked, confused. ŇYouŐre a professorÉÓ He trailed off, realizing that Snape was referring not to his job at Hogwarts, but to his position as a spy within VoldemortŐs followers. ŇIŐm sorry,Ó he said. Even though Harry had found himself beginning to trust his roommates, Snape had still been forced to help him, right in front of the children of Death Eaters. Harry abruptly realized that he was also immensely grateful to Snape - he hadnŐt questioned what Harry had seen, nor had he wasted any time. Harry hadnŐt even asked to see Dumbledore; Snape had simply understood what needed to be done before Harry even really had. His roommates hadn't cut corners, either - Draco had said that they had sent for Snape before Harry even woke up. Snape said nothing more, and they were both silent until they reached the door to DumbledoreŐs office. ŇFizzing Whizbee,Ó Snape hissed at the door, and the gargoyle slid aside and a staircase sprang into being, sliding up through the floor. Snape dragged Harry onto the staircase as it ascended. At the top of the stairs Snape reached up and rapped the door with the griffin knocker. The door opened and Snape pushed Harry inside. The room was dark, but they werenŐt alone. ŇProfessor Snape? AndÉÓ DumbledoreŐs voice rang out, and dim light suddenly illuminated the office. Dumbledore sat at his desk, dressed in a brightly colored dressing gown, and his gaze was firmly planted on Snape. ŇI see,Ó he said quietly. Harry narrowed his eyes. Dumbledore still wouldn't even directly acknowledge him. ŇWhat brings you two here tonight, Severus?Ó ŇProfessor Dumbledore,Ó Snape said, still grasping HarryŐs arm, Ňtwo of my Slytherins came to retrieve me tonight. They reported that in his sleep, Harry was hissing and thrashing, and then he began screamingÉ and clutching at his scar.Ó HarryŐs eyes immediately snapped over to Snape, and furious understanding suddenly washed over him. The way Snape spoke of what had happened, and the fact that Harry had barely gotten to tell him anything heŐd seen, he could tell why Snape had brought him here .Dumbledore had obviously asked Snape to report to him if anything unusual had happened with Harry. Dumbledore couldnŐt be bothered to speak with Harry himself, but he could tell his head of house to essentially spy on him. ŇPotter, now you can tell the headmaster what happened,Ó Snape said. He finally released Harry, and Harry took a step away from him. ŇIÉ I was asleep,Ó Harry said quietly. He glanced up at Dumbledore, and felt another pop of irritation at seeing that Dumbledore still wasnŐt even looking at him. ŇBut it wasnŐtÉ a dream. It was real.Ó ŇWhat did you see, Potter?Ó Snape asked. ŇA huge snake - it attacked Arthur Weasley,Ó Harry said. He needed Dumbledore to believe him. He needed Snape to believe him. HeŐd had enough of not being believed. ŇHeŐs in a hallway. He's badly hurt, and he needs help.Ó Dumbledore leaned back and rested his head on the back of his chair, staring up at the ceiling. ŇHow did you see this?Ó he asked quietly. ŇIÉÓ Harry was confused, and angry that neither of them seemed to understand how badly Mr. Weasley had been injured. ŇIn my head.Ó ŇThatŐs not what I mean, Harry,Ó Dumbledore said. ŇI meant to ask - where were you when you saw this happen? Were youÉ down the hallway, or perhaps watching the scene unfold from above?Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. Dumbledore knew - he had to know. He wouldnŐt be asking, otherwise. ŇI wasÉ I was the snake,Ó Harry said. ŇI saw everything through its eyes.Ó He heard SnapeŐs sharp intake of breath, and Dumbledore immediately looked over to his potions professor. ŇIs Arthur very badly injured?Ó ŇI already said he was!Ó Harry snapped. ŇI see,Ó Dumbledore said, nodding, and he immediately got up from his chair and faced the paintings that surrounded his desk. He quickly commanded a few paintings to check their other portraits and to make sure Arthur was found, which confused Harry. ŇButÉ Mr. Weasley could be anywhere,Ó Harry said. ŇI just know heŐs in a hall.Ó Dumbledore ignored him before turning to another portrait. ŇWould you please wake Professor McGonagall?Ó he asked the painting. ŇPlease have her retrieve the Weasley children and bring them to my office.Ó Apprehension rose in Harry. He didnŐt want to have to face the Weasleys to let them know heŐd seen their father be killed. Even worse, heŐd actually been the snake that had done it. Dumbledore then went to his shelf full of trinkets and toys and pulled down something silver and fragile looking. He placed it on his desk before tapping it with his wand. A puff of smoke emerged from the tube at the top and the smoke swirled before taking the form of a snake, its jaws opening wide. Harry looked from the tiny instrument to Dumbledore, hoping the headmaster would confirm what Harry had seen, but Dumbledore still would not even glance at him. ŇI understand,Ó Dumbledore murmured quietly. ŇBut in essence divided?Ó The smoke snake then split into two, and the snakes swirled and coiled around each other. Dumbledore looked grave, and he tapped the instrument once again with his wand. The snakes dissipated into the air. They waited for several minutes in awkward silence, and all the while Harry grew more anxious and irritated. Finally, one of the portraits that Dumbledore had commanded to find Arthur returned. ŇWe found him,Ó the portrait said. ŇHe is alive. I ensured that one of your aurors found him. He is being transported to St. Mungos as we speak.Ó ŇExcellent, excellent,Ó Dumbledore said, turning to another painting. ŇPhineas?Ó Harry tuned it out, the relief of Arthur Weasley being found alive washing over him. But as he became more and more relieved, a trickling fear started in the back of HarryŐs mind. HeŐd been the one that had attacked Arthur Weasley. Just as Harry had found some relief that perhaps his dark affinity hadnŐt been from Voldemort, heŐd found himself attacking one of the kindest adults that heŐd ever encountered in the wizarding world. And that snake had seemed so familiar... The door to DumbledoreŐs office opened, and Ron, Ginny, and the twins came pouring in, McGonagall behind them. ŇThere are several concerned Slytherin boys at the bottom of your stairs, Professor Dumbledore,Ó McGonagall said. She peered at Harry curiously, and the WeasleysŐ eyes followed hers. Harry looked down at the floor. Snape let out a sigh. ŇI will escort them back to their beds,Ó he said. ŇLet them know that Harry likely wonŐt be returning tonight,Ó Dumbledore said as Snape turned to leave. Once Snape was gone, Dumbledore faced the Weasleys. ŇI am sending you four, along with Harry, to Grimmauld Place immediately.Ó He grasped an old kettle from his desk and he tapped it with his wand. ŇPortus.Ó ŇWhat? Why?Ó George asked. ŇWhat happened?Ó Ginny added. ŇI regret to inform you that your father was attacked,Ó Dumbledore said, his voice and expression grave. ŇIt was only due to HarryŐs quick thinking that his life was saved.Ó Harry swallowed nervously. Dumbledore hadnŐt mentioned how or why Harry had been able to do anything at all, which would likely leave him to explain. And Harry certainly did not want to explain anything. ŇYou have all used a portkey before?Ó Dumbledore asked. ŇGood. Now then - gather round.Ó Harry crowded in between Ginny and Fred, not wanting to be anywhere near Ron. Harry reached out to grasp the kettle and he chanced one more look at Dumbledore. ŇOneÉ twoÉÓ Dumbledore, amazingly, finally looked up from the portkey and he met HarryŐs eyes for the first time in months. Severe pain suddenly sliced through HarryŐs scar. A burning hot hatred suddenly flared up inside of Harry, and it felt frighteningly familiar - heŐd felt it on the pitch when heŐd been with Lucius Malfoy. But now Harry not only felt that awful hatred, but something else was rising within him. He instinctively wanted to bite Dumbledore. He wanted to strike at him and make him bleed. He wanted to hurt him - to kill himÉ ŇThree.Ó The portkey yanked at them and DumbledoreŐs office swirled off into the distance, and Harry felt like his prey had been taken away. Chapter End Notes Again, as a reminder: this is an AU of OotP. I am trying my best to not copy anything directly since it's not the same story, but you will see some similarities here and there. The aftermath is going to be a tad different from the original, though. ;) Chapter14: TheChoice Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! This chapter was beta-read by the lovely mishaleh. Thank you! <3 11/17/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes They landed in the kitchen of Grimmauld Place with a clatter. The odd, foreign feeling of wanting to bite and tear into something dissipated as soon as Harry hit the floor. Harry gasped as he sat up. He hadnŐt been back to Grimmauld Place since the summer, and he felt almost comforted just by being in the house. He could actually sense dark magic running through it, and it seemed as if the magic in the floorboards was reaching up to reassure him. Harry felt simultaneously soothed and uncertain about what that could mean. Sirius came barrelling into the room. He looked haggard; he was unshaven and had clearly slept in his clothes. ŇPhineas said ArthurŐs been hurt,Ó Sirius said as he helped Harry to his feet, and Harry could smell a faint whiff of alcohol on his breath. Sirius made sure Harry was steady, and then he looked to the Weasleys. ŇWhat happened?Ó ŇNot sure,Ó Fred said, frowning. ŇHarry?Ó ŇDumbledore said his life had only been saved because of you,Ó Ginny said. ŇWhat happened?Ó Harry swallowed and suddenly felt like he was surrounded. He moved to the side of the kitchen. ŇIÉ well, I guess I kind of hadÉ a vision,Ó he said quietly. He felt sick to his stomach again, though it wasnŐt from the pain in his scar this time; he just didnŐt feel comfortable telling the Weasleys how he had seen their father attacked. There had been so much blood... But he explained, as best as he could, what heŐd seen, though he changed the story just a bit; he made it sound like he had just observed what had happened, instead of actually being the snake that had attacked Arthur Weasley. ŇWhereŐs Mum?Ó George asked Sirius. ŇIs she here?Ó Sirius shook his head. ŇWe need to go to St. MungoŐs!Ó Ginny insisted. ŇYou canŐt,Ó Sirius said, and then proceeded to argue with all of the Weasley children about why they needed to stay put. Harry tuned it out. He leaned up against the counter and worried about what exactly was happening to him. His affinity was dark. HeŐd had the briefest of hopes that it could have come from his father, but now he was also having visions through the eyes of a snake - VoldemortŐs snake, to be exact. Harry knew sheŐd felt familiar; it had just taken him a while to place where he knew her from. Coupled with that strange, foreign feeling of wanting to kill Dumbledore that had struck him in the office, Harry felt unsettled and ill. What the hell is happening to me? he thought desperately. That rage heŐd felt in DumbledoreŐs office hadnŐt been his, and neither had the hatred heŐd felt on the Quidditch pitch a couple of months prior. Harry now knew that for certain; he wasnŐt capable of feeling hatred like that. Harry could admit to himself that heŐd been angry with Dumbledore for much of the year, but he certainly didnŐt wish death on the headmaster. And Harry himself had been feeling so angry for months now, and now Harry was wondering how much of that anger had actually been his own. He suddenly found himself wishing that Draco was there with him. DracoŐs earlier reassurances about his connection with Voldemort would have been invaluable at that moment. The worries just kept piling on and on, and Draco was the only one Harry had told about any of them. "You can't keep us from him!" Ginny shouted furiously, shaking Harry out of his thoughts. Ron had been right - she was a bit scary when she was mad. ŇWe canŐt let the Ministry know that Harry is having visions of events happening hundreds of miles away!Ó Sirius insisted. ŇWe can say we heard it from someone else!Ó Ron snapped. ŇOur father might be dying!Ó As the shouting continued to rise in volume around him, HarryŐs feeling of nausea grew, and he walked out of the kitchen and straight to the nearest bathroom. He emptied whatever contents were left in his stomach - which wasnŐt much - and sank down to the floor next to the toilet. He stayed there for a long while, letting the magic of the house wrap around him like a warm embrace, and he worried about what exactly was going on inside his head. *** He eventually joined the others back in the kitchen, where Sirius had provided everyone with a Butterbeer. Sirius pulled Harry to the side. The quietness of the room was a stark contrast to the screaming argument heŐd left earlier, but somehow it felt more intense. ŇMolly sent word that Arthur is still alive,Ó Sirius said softly. ŇBut it doesnŐt sound good, Harry.Ó Harry nodded, and he sat down with the others. He didnŐt feel like he belonged with them. He was not only intruding in this familyŐs grief and worry, but he was also the lone snake among the lions. He was the snake that had possibly taken their father away. Hours went by and it seemed like days. Finally, Mrs. Weasley swung the door to the kitchen open. She looked pale and tired. Her children looked at her expectantly. ŇHeŐs going to be okay,Ó she said, her voice barely above a whisper. ŇHeŐs asleep. You all can go see him later.Ó A collective sigh of relief swept through the kitchen. As the Weasley children rose to hug their mother, Harry again felt like he was intruding, and so he decided to help Sirius with breakfast. Mrs. Weasley stopped Harry in the middle of him setting the table, sweeping him into a hug. ŇIf it wasnŐt for you, HarryÉÓ she said, choking back a sob, Ňwe could have lost him tonight. No one would have found him for hours, and by then it would have been far too late. Oh, HarryÉÓ She released him and cupped her hands around his face. ŇThank you so much, my dear. WeŐll never be able to repay you.Ó Harry only nodded, unable to say anything else. He couldnŐt. The guilt was too great, and it caught in his throat. His stomach twisted unpleasantly. Mrs. Weasley and Sirius quickly began talking about staying for Christmas, and Harry pulled Sirius aside as soon as he was free. ŇCan I talk to you?Ó Harry asked quietly. Sirius studied Harry for a moment, and then nodded and led him into the parlor. Sirius waved his hand and the candles flared to life, illuminating the dim room. Harry didnŐt waste a moment. ŇSomethingŐs happening to me, Sirius,Ó Harry said, and he explained his vision again, but this time he included the fact that he had actually been the snake that had attacked Arthur Weasley. Sirius was quiet for a moment. ŇDid you tell Dumbledore all this?Ó Harry nodded. ŇYeah, but he didnŐt tell me what he thought.Ó He shook his head. ŇOf course, Dumbledore doesnŐt really tell me anything these daysÉÓ ŇIf heŐs not worried about it, then IŐm not,Ó Sirius said firmly. ŇHe would have said something if -Ó ŇBut thatŐs not all, Sirius,Ó Harry insisted. ŇRight before we took the portkey here, I wantedÉ I felt like I wanted to attack Dumbledore. I felt like I was a snake again - like I wanted to bite him.Ó ŇAn aftermath of your vision, maybe?Ó Sirius said, frowning. ŇI donŐt think so,Ó Harry said, shaking his head emphatically. ŇIt felt like there was a snake inside of meÉÓ ŇYouŐre tired,Ó Sirius said. ŇYou should -Ó ŇSirius, donŐt,Ó Harry said pleadingly. ŇIŐm really worried. This thing with the snakeÉ itŐs just the mostÉ recent thing. ThereŐs more.Ó SiriusŐs eyes widened. ŇIÉ letŐs sit down.Ó He pulled Harry over to one of the armchairs, taking the one across from him. Harry wasnŐt sure where to begin. ŇIÉÓ He sighed, flopping back in the chair and running a hand through his hair. ŇI haveÉ kind of a weird question, I guess.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇMy fatherÉÓ Harry sighed. ŇYou said he liked using Retorsus on SnapeÉ before he knew it was dark arts.Ó SiriusŐs face took on an unreadable expression. Harry thought it looked quite similar to when heŐd found Harry in the Black Library over the summer. ŇWhen he used it, did itÉ did it make him feel ill at all?Ó Harry asked hesitantly. ŇLikeÉ sick to his stomach, orÉÓ Sirius stared at Harry for a few long moments, and then he let out a half-laugh and shook his head. ŇI had a feeling this would happen.Ó ŇWhat?Ó ŇWith you being sorted into Slytherin... I knew there was a good chance youŐd come back knowing more about the dark arts than you did when you left.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇSoÉ thenÉÓ He swallowed. ŇIs it possible thatÉ my father had a dark affinity?Ó he asked, his voice barely above a whisper. Sirius sighed. ŇIf he did, he was just over the edge of dark,Ó he said softly. ŇThat hex is barely dark, and James struggled for days to get it to work correctly. He never mentioned if it made him feel sick or not. He wasnŐt the type to complain of things like that.Ó He let out a weak, wry smile. ŇHe was also stubborn enough to keep trying it even if it did make him feel rotten.Ó Harry felt the barest of hope that heŐd been clinging onto crumble into nothing. He should have known - after all, the Slytherins had explained to him that Harry was attracted to the dark arts because of his affinity, and Sirius had already told him that his father had apparently hated the dark arts with a passion. Harry sighed and pulled his knees up to his chest, wrapping his arms around his legs. He was finally starting to admit to himself that he might want to declare for the dark, but he was right back to wondering if his affinity was only dark due to Voldemort. HeŐd never felt so conflicted. He again found himself desperately wishing that he could talk to Draco. ŇHarryÉÓ Sirius said. ŇI need to ask you something, and I want you to be completely honest with me.Ó Harry looked at Sirius through bleary eyes and nodded. ŇDo you have a dark affinity?Ó Harry squeezed his eyes shut. ŇI do,Ó he admitted. ŇI did an affinity rite, and it...Ó He sighed. ŇI have a pretty strong affinity for the dark, apparently.Ó ŇI knew it,Ó Sirius said, almost more to himself than to Harry. Harry looked over to Sirius in surprise. He knew heŐd picked up on the hexes fairly quickly, but was that really enough for Sirius to tell that he had a dark affinity? ŇAnd IŐm assuming your new housemates guided you through how to perform an affinity rite?Ó Harry nodded, frowning. ŇIŐm not sure how much you know, butÉ having a dark affinity is nothing to be ashamed of, Harry,Ó Sirius said. ŇI know itŐs not. But what ifÉ what if itŐs not mine?Ó Harry asked. ŇI know that it isnŐt always hereditary, but if I didnŐt get it from my father, what if it came fromÉ from Voldemort?Ó SiriusŐs eyebrows knitted together in confusion. ŇHow do you mean?Ó Harry quickly began explaining his parseltongue, and his wand, and all of his other fears of the things that tied him to Voldemort. Sirius just frowned as Harry spoke. When Harry was finished, Sirius shook his head. ŇI donŐt think itŐs possible, Harry. Affinities for types of magic are inherent. You canŐt Imperius someone into having a dark affinity, after all - youŐve either got it or you donŐt.Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇDo you want to know exactly why I had a gut feeling about your affinity?Ó Harry shrugged. ŇBecause the hexes you showed me were easy?Ó Sirius grinned. ŇPartly, but before that - it was because the Black Library actually opened itself to you.Ó Harry blinked. ŇWhat does that have to do with my affinity?Ó ŇThat library only opens itself to those that it trusts,Ó Sirius said. ŇItŐs usually just members of the Black family, or anyone with an extremely dark affinity that doesnŐt have ill intent.Ó Sirius smiled. ŇThat library looks at everything about you, too. It looks at your heart, your mind, and your soul. No matter whatŐs happened to you, I doubt that Voldemort could influence all of those parts of you.Ó Harry let out a shaky breath. ŇSoÉ IŐm notÉ becoming more and more like him? Even with being a Slytherin, and having this affinityÉÓ Sirius shook his head. ŇYou are still you, Harry. YouŐre not him. And whateverŐs happened to you isnŐt your fault - all you can do is move forward from whateverŐs been done to you.Ó Harry felt an immense wave of relief rushing through him, but he still couldnŐt help but let out a bark of a laugh. ŇThatŐs almost exactly what Draco told me.Ó ŇDracoÉ Draco Malfoy?Ó Sirius looked and sounded appalled. ŇUhÉ yeah,Ó Harry said. ŇHeŐs one of my roommates.Ó He remembered how Sirius had spoken about Lucius earlier in the year, and he suddenly felt defensive. ŇDraco might be Lucius MalfoyŐs son, but heŐs beenÉ heŐs not like his dad. HeŐsÉ kind, in his own weird way.Ó ŇA kind Malfoy, Harry?Ó Sirius said doubtfully. ŇItŐs almost certainly an act.Ó ŇItŐs not,Ó Harry insisted, growing irritated. ŇHarryÉÓ Sirius said, looking gravely concerned, Ňhow much of all this have you told to the littlest Malfoy?Ó Harry took in a breath. ŇPretty much all of it, actually,Ó he admitted. ŇBut not until after Draco swore an oath on his magic to not mention a word of it to anyone else.Ó SiriusŐs eyes widened at that. ŇA MalfoyÉ swore on his magic?Ó He shook his head. ŇThat doesnŐt sound like something Lucius would do.Ó ŇItŐs like I said, Sirius,Ó Harry said, ŇDraco isnŐt his father.Ó *** HarryŐs talk with Sirius left him with barely a shred of doubt about his affinity. Sirius and Draco were in perfect agreement - HarryŐs affinity was all him, and Harry found that he feared the possibility of walking the dark path less and less the more he thought about it. Harry was actually starting to find himself excited at the possibility of declaring dark. He thought back to his affinity rite, and how much the dark magic had welcomed him. Even just being present in Grimmauld Place, where dark magic seemed to have seeped into the house itself, was a comfort. He found himself more and more certain that the dark was where he belonged. He desperately needed to speak with his housemates. Harry finally exited the parlor only to immediately run into Ron. Harry narrowed his eyes and stepped to the side, going around him without a word. ŇHarry,Ó Ron said quietly. Harry stopped in his tracks. ŇWhat?Ó Harry said shortly, not bothering to turn around. ŇThank you,Ó Ron said. ŇFor whatever you did - seeing what had happened to DadÉÓ RonŐs breath caught in his throat. Harry closed his eyes. Despite SiriusŐs reassurances about his affinity, he still had no idea what was happening with him seeing through VoldemortŐs snakeŐs eyes. ŇAnd IŐm sorry,Ó Ron continued, ŇforÉÓ ŇEnding our friendship?Ó Harry said in a cold voice, finally turning to face Ron. It came out harsher than he intended, but he found that he didnŐt regret it. ŇIÉ yeah,Ó Ron said, sighing. He looked absolutely miserable. ŇIŐm justÉ IŐm serious, Harry. I really am worried about you.Ó Harry felt worried about himself, as well, but he was certain it was for completely different reasons than Ron. ŇWhat for?Ó Harry asked. ŇFor beating you in Quidditch?Ó It was a low blow, but Harry wasnŐt really in the mood to have any kind of conversation with Ron. Ron frowned. ŇBecause youŐre saying things like that. It just doesnŐt sound like you, Harry.Ó ŇIf you think back over the years, I think youŐll find it sounds exactly like me,Ó Harry said, thinking of all of the fights heŐd gotten into with Draco in the past. The difference was that now his ire was directed towards Ron, instead. Ron shook his head. ŇYouÉ it just seems like youŐve gotten so close with all of those Slytherins, and -Ó ŇI am Slytherin, Ron,Ó Harry spat. ŇThey are my housemates, and yes, we have gotten close.Ó He narrowed his eyes, and then he took a step into RonŐs personal space. ŇAnd you should be thanking them, as well,Ó he hissed. RonŐs eyes widened. ŇWhat do you mean?Ó ŇBy the time I woke up from having that vision, theyŐd already sent someone to get help,Ó Harry said. ŇWithout them, I never would have gotten to Dumbledore in time for him to do anything.Ó He shook his head. ŇWithout them, your father couldnŐt have been saved.Ó Ron stared at Harry, his lips parted in surprise, but said nothing. Harry let out a huff and turned around, stalking away from Ron. Harry knew he probably should have been kinder, considering what Ron had just been through, but he could tell - his friendship with Ron really was over. After all, even if they somehow managed to repair whatever was left, Harry knew that Ron would never accept the path that Harry was considering. *** ŇWeŐll be able to visit St. MungoŐs this afternoon,Ó Mrs. Weasley was saying. ŇDumbledore is having all of your trunks sent here, and youŐll be able to stay through the holidays.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. After all, Yule was only a few days away, and Harry desperately wanted to speak to them one more time. If he actually was going to make a decision about declaring dark, he didnŐt want to put it off for another year. ŇCan IÉÓ He cleared his throat. ŇI mean, IŐm really glad Mr. Weasley is going to be okay, butÉÓ ŇBut what, dear?Ó Mrs. Weasley asked. ŇIs there any way I could go back to Hogwarts for the rest of the day?Ó he asked quietly. "I'll come back right after." ŇYou actually wanna go back to class, Harry?Ó Fred said, letting out a laugh. Harry shook his head. ŇIŐd like toÉ to say goodbye to my housemates.Ó Mrs. WeasleyŐs eyes widened. ŇYouŐve becomeÉ very comfortable in Slytherin, then?Ó she asked, frowning. Harry looked away, sighing. ŇAnd heŐs turning out like Dromeda.Ó Harry ducked away from him, flashing Sirius a grin. ŇEh, that Greengrass girl is okay, too, I suppose,Ó Fred said, shrugging. ŇI guess you snakes arenŐt all bad.Ó ŇIŐll send Dumbledore a message, then,Ó Mrs. Weasley said. ŇYou be sure to thank yourÉ housemates for us.Ó *** Amazingly, they were able to arrange for a portkey back to Hogwarts, though not until the afternoon. Dumbledore hadnŐt even questioned it, and for once Harry found himself somewhat grateful for DumbledoreŐs new apathy towards him. The portkey surprisingly took him to SnapeŐs office rather than DumbledoreŐs, and his head of house greeted him with a deep frown. ŇIŐm not entirely sure why you wanted to come back, Potter,Ó Snape said, looking quite irritated at HarryŐs presence, Ňbut youŐve already missed the last of the classes for the day, and your housemates are finishing up dinner.Ó Harry nodded, deciding he would meet them in the dorms. Snape barely said two words to him as he escorted him to the common room. He waited on one of the couches, and he stared into the fire and contemplated what he needed to tell them. He wanted to know what he would have to do on the Solstice should he decide to take the first step on the dark path. He also wanted to tell them exactly what had happened in his vision. Harry still didnŐt know what it meant - seeing through NaginiŐs eyes and feeling emotions that he was sure werenŐt his own. If Dumbledore couldnŐt - or wouldnŐt - tell him anything, perhaps his roommates, intimately familiar with the dark arts, would be able to shed some light on what was happening to him. The common room door slid open and Harry looked up eagerly. ŇPotter!Ó Yaxley exclaimed as she entered the common room. ŇI thought youŐd gone for the holidays already.Ó Warrington and Montague came in behind her, and they peered at Harry curiously. ŇIÉ needed to come back for something,Ó Harry said, deflating in disappointment. ŇI think Daphne and Millicent mentioned that they were headed to the library, but the others should be here any minute.Ó Harry nodded, and then Yaxley came over to the back of the couch and wrapped her arms around HarryŐs shoulders. ŇYou, too, Freya.Ó The door slid open again, and Harry was flooded with relief as he saw Draco, Blaise, Theo, and Pansy enter. Harry immediately got to his feet. ŇHarry!Ó Pansy shrieked when she spotted him. She launched herself at Harry and enveloped him in a hug. ŇIÉÓ Harry sighed. ŇIŐm not sure.Ó He swallowed nervously. ŇI really need to talk to you guys.Ó ŇLetŐs go to our room,Ó Blaise said. Harry paced in front of them, trying to figure out what to say. ŇJustÉ start somewhere, Harry,Ó Draco said softly. ŇAnywhere you want.Ó Harry paused in his pacing and shot a look of gratitude towards Draco before turning to the others. ŇSomethingŐs happening to me,Ó he said nervously, Ňand even though IŐm fairly sure Dumbledore has an idea of what it is, he isnŐt giving me a clue as to what it might be.Ó His eyes, almost unwillingly, drifted back over to Draco, who simply nodded at him to continue. ŇLast night - that wasnŐt a dream I had,Ó he said softly. ŇIt was a vision. It actually happened.Ó ŇSo the elder Weasley really was attacked by a giant snake?Ó Draco asked, raising an eyebrow. ŇIs heÉÓ ŇHeŐs going to be okay,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd itŐs likely because you guys acted so quickly in getting Snape. SoÉ thank you.Ó He gave Draco a wry smile. ŇMrs. Weasley even wanted me to thank you all.Ó Theo shifted uncomfortably. ŇI hope that doesnŐtÉ make it back to my father,Ó he said softly. Harry shook his head. ŇI donŐt think it will,Ó he said. ŇThey donŐt want anyone knowing that IŐm having visions ofÉÓ He took a shaky breath. ŇIn the visionÉ I was the snake.Ó A moment of silence fell, then Blaise finally spoke up. ŇLast nightÉ before you started screaming, you were hissing. I thought it might have been parseltongue.Ó Snape had mentioned him hissing, as well. ŇItÉ it might have been,Ó Harry admitted. ŇI have no idea what was coming out of my mouth before I woke up.Ó ŇSo youÉ you saw this all through the snakeŐs eyes?Ó Draco said. ŇLike you wereÉ trapped inside the snake?Ó ŇNotÉ not really,Ó Harry said, swallowing nervously. ŇI mean I actually was the snake. I could feel everything she felt. I wanted toÉ to bite him, to attack him, andÉÓ ŇÔShe?ŐÓ Draco said, his eyes widening. Harry nodded, and then looked down at the floor. ŇThe snake was Nagini.Ó Theo let out a choking sound. ŇThatŐs the Dark LordŐs snake.Ó ŇYeah,Ó Harry said. He raised his hand to run it through his hair, and he saw that he was shaking almost uncontrollably. Pale fingers suddenly reached out and wrapped around his own, and he looked up to see Draco had moved to his side. ŇHarry, sit down,Ó Draco said, pulling Harry down to the floor, never releasing HarryŐs hand. Harry sank to the ground in front of the sofa, folding his legs up under him. ŇSo youŐreÉ seeing things from inside the Dark LordŐs snake,Ó Blaise said quietly. Harry nodded. ŇAndÉ IŐmÉÓ He shook his head, and Draco gave his hand a gentle squeeze. ŇLater, when I was in DumbledoreŐs office, I wantedÉ I felt like I was a snake again. I wanted to attack him.Ó Harry was starting to feel ill again, and he buried his head in his free hand. ŇIt wasnŐt me. I know IŐve been pretty pissed at Dumbledore lately, but I would neverÉÓ ŇMerlinŐs fucking tits,Ó Blaise whispered. Harry looked up at Blaise in alarm. ŇDo you know what any of it means?Ó ŇNotÉ not really,Ó Blaise said, but Harry didnŐt think he sounded like he was being entirely truthful. ŇBut itŐs awfully... suspicious,Ó Theo said, narrowing his eyes. ŇBeing inside the Dark LordŐs snake, and then wanting to attack DumbledoreÉÓ Blaise mused. ŇYou know who hates Dumbledore more than anything?Ó DracoŐs hand suddenly grew tighter around HarryŐs. ŇThe Dark Lord,Ó he said softly. Silence fell on the group again, and then Pansy, who had remained quiet during Harry's explanation, leaned forward from her seat on the sofa. ŇHarryÉ I think itŐs more than just you being in his snakeŐs head,Ó she said. ŇIt sounds likeÉÓ ŇIt sounds like the Dark Lord is in yours,Ó Theo continued. Harry took in a sharp breath, and his insides felt cold. ŇAnd if heŐs aware of itÉÓ HarryŐs eyes widened, and his breath turned quick and unsteady. ŇYouÉ you all shouldnŐt be anywhere near me,Ó he choked out. Considering the allegiances of his housematesŐ families, he was risking their safety. He shouldn't have come back. His selfishness was putting his housemates in danger. DracoŐs grip on his hand became almost painful, but Harry was still glad for the contact. He felt like he was on the verge of completely crumbling, but the touch helped Harry feel calmer, and he took a deep breath, trying to get himself under control. ŇI hate saying this,Ó Theo said, his voice still full of terror, Ňbut I think youŐre right.Ó He abruptly stood up from the couch. ŇIŐm about to go home, and if the Dark Lord has talked to my father about anything heŐs seen through youÉÓ He shook his head and suddenly stalked out of the dorm, leaving the door open behind him. Blaise and Pansy looked at each other in alarm. ŇIŐll go talk to him,Ó Blaise said. ŇConsidering some of the conversations weŐve had, I get why heŐs scared, butÉÓ He shook his head, and he quickly followed Theo. Pansy, too, got to her feet. ŇHarry, donŐt worry,Ó she said. ŇI donŐt think itŐs nearly as bad as TheoŐs making it out to be. He crossed the room and pulled out a parchment and quill from his desk. He started scribbling across the parchment, occasionally looking up at Harry to explain what he was writing. ŇThereŐs an incantation you say at a certain time, and, provided the dark magic trusts you .which itŐs already shown that it does - it will take you directly to the Solstice celebration.Ó Harry blinked. ŇThat many? Draco, I donŐt know...Ó He sighed. ŇIŐd imagine there will probably be Death Eaters there, then?Ó Draco laughed. ŇThere will likely be at least one or two, but thatŐs the magic of Solstice, Harry,Ó he said. ŇYou wonŐt be in any danger. ItŐs a time of celebration, so the magic prevents anyone from harming anyone else whoŐs there.Ó He grinned. ŇIt will also prevent anyone from revealing the identities of anyone else in attendance to anyone who wasnŐt actually there.Ó HarryŐs mouth dropped open. He knew that dark magic was powerful, but he had no idea that it was capable of protecting wizards to that extent. ŇAre you serious?Ó he exclaimed. Draco nodded. ŇThe Solstice is actually the only dark celebration where this happens, because itŐs the night when the dark is strongest,Ó he continued. ŇNo will be able to hurt you, and no one will be able to reveal that you were there.Ó ŇAnd what ifÉ what if Voldemort shows up?Ó ŇMy mother mentioned that the Dark Lord never used to attend prior to his fall,Ó Draco said. ŇI canŐt imagine heŐd start now.Ó ŇWould I be able to leave whenever I wanted?Ó Harry asked. ŇIf he did show up, I mean?Ó ŇYes.Ó Draco scribbled down a few more sentences, and then passed the parchment to Harry. ŇThis is the incantation you say to take you to the celebration. No matter where you are, it wonŐt set off the Ministry trace,Ó he said, pointing to the paragraph heŐd written at the top, and then he moved his finger to the bottom of the parchment. ŇThis is what you say when you need to leave. You can use it at any point.Ó Harry felt more and more certain the more he read through DracoŐs instructions. ŇI want to do this,Ó he said softly. ŇIÉÓ He looked up at Draco, meeting his eyes. ŇIŐm going to declare dark.Ó Draco looked more elated than Harry had ever seen him. He let out an enormous grin, and Harry couldnŐt help but grin back. It was infectious, and Harry felt an excitement heŐd never really felt before. He realized that despite all of the doubts that had been plaguing him for months, he felt like he was meant to declare for the dark. ŇOkay,Ó Draco said, his expression becoming contemplative. ŇSince you donŐt come from a dark family you donŐt have any traditions to follow, so IŐve been thinking about what you could do instead...Ó Harry laughed. ŇWhy am I not surprised that youŐve already got a plan?Ó ŇI wanted to be prepared,Ó Draco said. ŇItŐs not often that someone from outside the dark community declares, you know.Ó Harry shook his head, amused. ŇOkay,Ó he said, Ňso what do you think I should do?Ó ŇYou could follow the Black traditions,Ó Draco said, Ňsince your godfather is a Black.Ó Harry raised an eyebrow. ŇI donŐt know what those areÉÓ ŇI do,Ó Draco said, grinning. ŇMy mother is a Black, and I followed the Black traditions for my declaration, rather than the standard Malfoy rites.Ó He pulled out another parchment, this time sitting down. ŇEven better, youŐll be able to follow the Black traditions more properly than I could when I declared.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó ŇBlack tradition requires a mentor and a peer - fully declared dark witches or wizards that you can reach out to for guidance as you walk the dark path. I didnŐt have a Black peer, but for youÉÓ Draco smirked. ŇI can be your peer, as I am the obvious choice.Ó Harry laughed. ŇAnd IŐm certain my mother would agree to serve as your mentor,Ó Draco continued. ŇHonestly, I think sheŐd be thrilled. SheŐs always complaining that the dark needs new blood.Ó Harry paused at that. HeŐd never directly spoken to Narcissa Malfoy, but the woman was married to Lucius. He wasnŐt sure heŐd enjoy having to go to her with his questions. But Narcissa isnŐt necessarily my only choice, Harry thought, already formulating a plan. ŇIŐllÉ take you up on your mother, if necessary,Ó Harry said slowly. ŇBut I think IŐve got someone in mind.Ó Chapter End Notes Next up: The Solstice Chapter 15: TheSolstice Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. He actually managed to land on his feet, although he stumbled, nearly knocking Hermione over. He caught her by the elbow just as Mrs. Weasley came into the kitchen. ŇThere you two are!Ó she exclaimed. ŇEveryone is in the family room having a nice dessert.Ó ŇHow is Mr. Weasley?Ó Harry asked as Mrs. Weasley bustled them out of the kitchen. ŇHeŐs awake and he should be coming home in a day or two,Ó Mrs. Weasley responded with a smile. ŇHe wanted me to thank you.Ó Harry immediately looked down to the floor. HeŐd been feeling so much better after telling Draco of his plan to declare dark, but the reminder of his vision brought the underlying feeling of fear rushing back. Voldemort could be in his head. He abruptly stopped in his tracks. ŇWhat is it, dear?Ó Mrs. Weasley asked. ŇShould IÉ be here?Ó he asked quietly. If Voldemort really could see into his head, he could be looking into the Order headquarters right at that very moment. Hermione paused, as well, turning to look at Harry curiously. ŇWhat do you mean, Harry?Ó Mrs. Weasley asked. Harry looked at Mrs. Weasley, wondering what he should say. ŇWhatŐs going on?Ó Hermione asked. Harry took a breath, trying to grasp onto the reassurances that Draco and Pansy had given him. ŇI... Ň He sighed. ŇNothing.Ó He continued on into the family room. As soon as he entered, he could instantly tell that something had changed since heŐd left. ŇDid something else happen?Ó ŇNo,Ó Ginny said, climbing out of the armchair sheŐd been curled up in. ŇTheyŐre just being stupid. Can I talk to you, Harry?Ó Harry raised an eyebrow and nodded. ŇItŐsÉ about me, isnŐt?Ó Ginny nodded. ŇYeah,Ó she said. ŇBut considering what was said, I wanted to be the one to talk to you about it.Ó She then put her hands on her hips and set her mouth in a tight line, and Harry was struck by how much she looked like Mrs. Weasley at that moment. ŇWas there something youÉ neglected to mention when you told us about your vision?Ó she asked. Harry swallowed nervously. ŇVision?Ó Hermione asked. ŇIs that what happened? ŇBut that wasnŐt the whole storyÉ right, Harry?Ó Harry sank down into one of the chairs and buried his fingers in his hair, his elbows resting on his knees. ŇYeah.Ó ŇHarry, what happened?Ó Hermione asked. Harry sighed. Ginny was making it abundantly obvious that she already knew what he had left out. ŇIn the vision, I was the snake,Ó he said slowly. ŇAnd I know that it wasÉ VoldemortŐs snake.Ó Ginny nodded. ŇThatŐs what we heard,Ó she said. ŇBut thatŐs not all, HarryÉÓ Harry looked up at her, alarmed. ŇMoody said that it meantÉ that it could meanÉÓ Ginny took a deep breath. Ň... that You.Know-Who could be possessing you.Ó Hermione gasped. Harry closed his eyes. ŇI know,Ó he said quietly. ŇYouÉ know?Ó Ginny asked, her eyes widening. ŇI meanÉÓ Harry sighed. ŇI never really landed on the word Ôpossession,Ő butÉÓ He stared down at his hands, unwilling to meet their eyes. ŇI was in his snakeŐs head, and it seems likely that heŐsÉ in my head.Ó He took in a shaky breath. ŇThat was kinda why I wanted to go back to HogwartsÉ Dumbledore wasnŐt telling me anything about what he thinks might be happening to me, and I needed to try and figure it out.Ó ŇYouÉ you asked your housematesÉ ?Ó Hermione said quietly. Harry nodded. Hermione paused for a moment before responding. "Do youÉ often talk aboutÉ Voldemort with the other Slytherins?" Harry picked at one of his fingernails in a pathetic effort to keep his hands busy.
