ŇThat was something else that Moody mentioned, Harry.Ó Harry finally looked up at her. ŇWhat?Ó ŇHe thinks thatÉ your being in SlytherinÉÓ Ginny trailed off, sounding uncertain. ŇIŐm justÉ letting you know what was said.Ó ŇHarry, are you okay?Ó Hermione asked, looking at him in concern. ŇThis seems like a lot to be dealing with.Ó ŇIŐm nervous about being here if VoldemortŐs actually in my head,Ó Harry said. ŇBut IÉÓ He sighed. ŇMy housemates also had reason to be nervous, consideringÉÓ He trailed off. He couldnŐt get into the Slytherin family alliances - not with them. ŇConsidering what?Ó Hermione asked, her eyes narrowing. Harry shook his head. ŇI canŐt really talk about it.Ó Hermione looked decidedly unhappy about that, but she didnŐt respond. ŇThe diary.Ó ŇI kept telling them - considering IŐm the only one we know of whoŐs actually been possessed by himÉÓ ŇYou would know,Ó Harry finished for her, and he smiled at her. *** Harry pulled Sirius aside as soon as he could, although he almost regretted interrupting SiriusŐs jovial mood. Sirius had been singing Christmas carols and dancing in the halls; he was clearly happy with having company. If heŐd really been mostly alone for months, Harry supposed he couldnŐt really blame him. They went into the parlor, and Sirius shut the door and turned to Harry. ŇWhatŐs up, pup?Ó Harry swallowed. HeŐd practiced his questions in his head a thousand times over, but he still felt nervous. ŇI need to ask you something,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd I honestly have no idea how youŐre going to react. I meanÉ I think youŐll be okay with my asking, considering some of the things weŐve talked about, butÉÓ He trailed off when he realized he was babbling. Sirius had grown up in a dark family, he wasnŐt against the dark arts, and he clearly had at least some level of affinity for the dark. Only one question really remained. Harry had never asked, and Sirius had never offered the information. Harry figured there was a good chance of it going either way; after all, it seemed like Sirius had rebelled against his family in every way he possibly could have. Sirius raised an eyebrow. ŇWhat is it?Ó Harry steeled himself, and then finally asked the question that had been burning brighter and brighter ever since the summer, even though he hadn't really put words to it until that very day. ŇAre you a dark wizard?Ó Sirius didnŐt say anything for a few moments, and HarryŐs nervousness grew. Perhaps Sirius was like Daphne - he could have a dark affinity, and not have any problem with the dark arts, but he might have never declared. HeŐd been a Gryffindor, after all, and he seemed to hate most dark wizards quite vehemently. ŇAre youÉ are you thinking of declaring for the dark, Harry?Ó Sirius finally asked, his voice quiet. Harry hesitated, then nodded. ŇI feel likeÉ like IŐm meant to,Ó he said, his voice unintentionally coming out in a whisper. "Like I'm supposed to." ŇI was only a bit younger than you when I declared.Ó Relief flooded through every inch of HarryŐs body. HeŐd had his suspicions, but hearing Sirius actually say it made the tension drain out of his shoulders. Sirius apparently noticed how on edge Harry had been about asking, and he pulled Harry into his arms. Harry almost felt like crowing in satisfaction. ŇI was fourteen,Ó Sirius continued. ŇAnd it caused such a massive blow-up between your father and me. He didnŐt speak to me for a month.Ó Harry drew back from Sirius and looked up at him in surprise. ŇMy father knew?Ó he asked. ŇAbout you?Ó Sirius nodded. ŇI told him the night before my final declaration,Ó he said, and he frowned. ŇI had hoped my best friend would understand that it was what was best for me, butÉÓ He sighed. ŇEven though James was furious with me, I could never find it within myself to regret it.Ó He let out a lopsided grin. ŇIŐm the black sheep no matter where I go. ŇI still attended celebrations until I wasÉ well, about your age - when the war really started getting dangerousÉ but most of them tried to ignore the fact that I was there.Ó He grinned. ŇThatŐs another part of why Andromeda was my favorite. She never pretended I didnŐt exist.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇAndromeda is a dark witch, then?Ó HeŐd wondered - and he knew it had been likely considering she was both a Black and a Slytherin - but it had never come up in any of their letters, and Harry had no idea if it was even safe to ask something like that in a letter. Sirius nodded. ŇAlthough as far as I know, she also stopped attending after falling out with her sistersÉÓ Harry filed that information away. ŇYule is tomorrowÉÓ Sirius let out a sigh. His eyes drifted up to the ceiling and he gave a shake of his head. ŇYou want to go, donŐt you?Ó ŇUh, wellÉ yes,Ó Harry said. Sirius looked back down and studied HarryŐs face for a long moment. ŇYou know that a good chunk of the dark community will be there, right?Ó His eyes narrowed. ŇIncluding Death Eaters?Ó ŇYeah, butÉ doesnŐt the magic prevent anyone fromÉ attacking me?Ó Harry asked. ŇOr revealing to anyone that I was there?Ó He found that he trusted Draco, but getting confirmation from his godfather would be safest. Sirius nodded. ŇItŐs really powerful stuff. ŇPissed James off again when I told him I couldnŐt reveal anyone else.Ó Harry had no idea what Legilimency was, but he didnŐt want to distract Sirius from the question. ŇSoÉ youŐd be okay if I go?Ó Harry asked hopefully. Sirius sighed again, more exasperated this time. ŇBut thatŐs not the only thing I wanted to ask.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇSince IŐm declaringÉ I want to do the rite of intent,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd Draco suggested that -Ó ŇDraco Malfoy again,Ó Sirius huffed, rolling his eyes upward. Harry ignored him and plowed on. ŇDraco suggested that since I donŐt come from a dark family, I could follow the Black ritesÉ because you are my godfather.Ó SiriusŐs eyebrows quirked upward, and his lips parted slightly in surprise. ŇDraco said he followed the Black traditions when he declared, and that he could serve as the peer. He suggested his mother for the mentor, butÉÓ Harry took a breath. ŇIŐd rather it be you.Ó Sirius let out a bark of a laugh. ŇMalfoyŐs only son choosing to end the Malfoy rites with himÉ oh, thatŐs priceless.Ó He gave Harry a wry grin. ŇPerhaps heŐs not a giant git, after all.Ó Harry glared at Sirius, annoyed. Sirius had never even met Draco. ŇAnd of course IŐll serve as your mentor, Harry,Ó Sirius said, the humor slowly dropping from his face. ŇIf you are completely sure - if this is what you really want to doÉ I wouldnŐt have it any other way.Ó HarryŐs irritation immediately faded into elation, and he grinned. He officially had everything he needed in order to start walking the dark path. He threw his arms around Sirius. ŇThank you,Ó he whispered. Sirius hugged him back, and they stayed like that for a long while. ŇI canŐt believe IŐm going back to a Yule celebration,Ó Sirius muttered in disbelief. ŇItŐs beenÉ well, literally decades.Ó He shook his head. ŇThank you,Ó Harry whispered, and then he finally drew back from his godfather. ŇSiriusÉ about my fatherÉÓ ŇYeah?Ó ŇDid he everÉ I mean, you guys were clearly friends at the end, butÉ did he...Ó Harry trailed off. HeŐd already given up on Ron, but he couldnŐt help but wonder if he could ever be completely honest with Hermione again. Sirius sighed. ŇWeÉ got over it, eventually,Ó he said. ŇBut I donŐt think James ever really looked at me the same after that.Ó He let out a bitter laugh. ŇI've always wondered if that was why..." He trailed off. "What?" "Nothing." Sirius gave him a weak smile. "But thatŐs why I was so surprised when he asked me to be your godfather. I meanÉ godfathers are supposed to spoil kids rotten and give them great presents, but theyŐre also supposed toÉ provide spiritual guidance.Ó He shook his head. ŇI guess James decided he was okay with having a dark wizard provide spiritual guidance to his son, so maybe he really didÉ finally accept it.Ó Harry found himself grinning. ŇOr maybe he knew that IŐd be like you.Ó Sirius laughed. ŇI doubt it, Harry.Ó ŇEither way, SiriusÉ IŐm glad youŐre my godfather.Ó "Me, too, pup." Sirius grinned wryly at him. "Andromeda and I could use the company, too." "What do you mean?" Harry asked. "Me, her, and soon, you - I think we're the only dark wizards who aren't aligned with Voldemort." Harry nodded, but a thought sprang up, almost unbidden, in the back of his mind. Not if I can help it. *** Harry had a hard time sleeping that night, and he could barely contain his excitement all throughout the next day. More than once he found his knee bouncing up and down whenever he took a seat. Sirius had to keep standing on his foot, hissing at him to settle down. Fortunately - or unfortunately - Fred and George served as a great distraction, apparently having gotten over their earlier apprehension. Unfortunately, that also meant they kept jumping up and screaming dramatically every time Harry moved - more to scare Ron than Harry. Harry did not appreciate it, especially since it didnŐt look like Ron had truly believed GinnyŐs assessment that Harry was not, in fact, being possessed by Voldemort. Finally, it was five minutes from midnight, and most of the residents of Grimmauld Place were in bed. Harry and Sirius were the only ones still awake, and they went to the top floor together. Harry tugged the hood of his cloak over his head and Sirius eyed it warily. ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked. Sirius was the one who had asked Harry to hide his face, at least for when they first arrived at the gathering. Although Sirius acknowledged that the magic of the night would keep Harry safe, he still wanted to assess who was in attendance before they revealed HarryŐs presence. ŇYouŐre dressed veryÉ Slytherin.Ó Harry laughed. ŇItŐs a black cloak, Sirius,Ó he said. ŇNot that unusual.Ó ŇItŐs veryÉ nice.Ó Sirius didnŐt make it sound like it was very nice at all. ŇWell-made, I suppose.Ó Harry stuck his tongue out at his godfather in a fit of immaturity. ŇPansy and Draco took me shopping a few weeks ago, so I guess it is kinda Slytherin.Ó He shrugged. ŇSlytherins picked it out, anyway.Ó Sirius let out a sigh. ŇItŐs fine. You justÉ remind me of someone.Ó Harry blinked. ŇWho?Ó he asked softly. ŇRegulus,Ó Sirius said, and the briefest flash of grief ran across his face. ŇI donŐt think I ever mentionedÉ he was Seeker for Slytherin, just like you.Ó Then he smirked. ŇNot as good as you, though. Slytherin didnŐt win the cup the entire time he was on the team.Ó Harry grinned. ŇWell, we fully intend to get the cup this year.Ó ŇSpoken like a true Slytherin,Ó Sirius said, rolling his eyes. ŇI know you already walloped Gryffindor.Ó Then he sighed. ŇIŐm just checking one more time, HarryÉ are you sure youŐve thought this through?Ó ŇThoroughly,Ó Harry responded. ŇEven though the magic of Solstice will prevent anyone from revealing you were there, it wonŐt stop those who were there from knowing,Ó Sirius said. ŇYouŐre ready for that?Ó Harry nodded, but truthfully, he wasnŐt entirely sure if he was ready for Death Eaters to know that he was preparing to declare for the dark. However, the urgency he felt about declaring trumped whatever fear he felt. Sirius let out another sigh, and then he suddenly stood up straight, as if sensing something in the air. ŇItŐs time.Ó He glanced at Harry. ŇYou ready, pup?Ó Harry nodded and pulled out his wand. ŇYou remember the words?Ó Sirius asked softly, drawing his own wand. ŇYes,Ó Harry said. HeŐd been repeating them in his mind all day, along with the words he needed to say for the rite of intent. The two of them faced one another, holding their wands straight up in front of their faces. Harry took a breath, and they began. ŇObsecro tenebris,Ó they said together. ŇAperta porta, ut cultui nocte...Ó Harry felt something rising around them. It licked at his skin and his heart hammered in his chest. ŇUbi fidem et reverentiam lava per pia.Ó Grimmauld Place disappeared and Harry saw nothing but beautiful purple light, and when it faded he and Sirius stood in a beautiful, starlit clearing. It was surrounded by tall trees and the wind rushed through the bare branches in a gentle, percussive whisper. Harry could feel dark magic thrumming in the air, which felt warm and comfortable despite the winter night, and Harry let out a sigh of wonder. In the distance, he could see a few silhouettes, while still others continued winking into existence. In the middle of the field, a giant bonfire suddenly flared to life. He heard Sirius let out a breath, and he glanced at his godfather. SiriusŐs eyes were wide, his lips parted. Finally, Sirius shook his head as if to clear it and he turned to Harry. ŇCome on,Ó he said, putting his arm around HarryŐs shoulder. They began walking towards the fire, and as they drew closer the more Harry could feel the magic swirling around him. It seemed to be whispering wordlessly at him -welcome, youŐre safe, you belong. Harry found himself looking up into the sky and smiling in delight, and then Sirius tugged his hood farther down his face, forcing his head back down. ŇSorry,Ó Harry whispered. Sirius just flashed him a lopsided grin. As they got closer, Harry started to realize that the fire was the biggest heŐd ever seen - nearly the size of the Burrow - and it cast beautiful dancing shadows all over the witches and wizards as they approached. One witch looked to be heading straight for them. He and Sirius noticed her at the same time, and Sirius centered himself in front of Harry. Harry ducked his head down under his cloak but dared to take a peek at her face, and his eyes widened in recognition. HeŐd only seen her once before, but he still knew her instantly. It was Narcissa Malfoy. ŇHello, cousin,Ó she greeted Sirius. ŇCissy,Ó Sirius replied, his lips curling in distaste. ŇItŐs been an age since IŐve seen you at a Yule gathering - since before Draco was born, IŐm certain.Ó ŇWell, twelve years in Azkaban tends to keep one away,Ó Sirius said, not bothering to hide his sarcasm. Mrs. Malfoy clasped her hands in front of her elegantly. ŇItŐs dreadful that anyone imprisoned there canŐt use the incantation to come here. I would like to see BellaÉÓ Sirius let out a bitter laugh. ŇBellatrix actually deserves to be there, Cissy.Ó Mrs. Malfoy frowned and then sighed. ŇSiriusÉ please,Ó she said quietly. ŇLetŐs not argue or snipe at one another. ItŐs a night of celebration.Ó She swallowed, and Harry saw her face become softer. ŇThere are so few of us left.Ó Her voice was nearly a whisper. Harry wasnŐt sure if she was referring to dark wizards or Blacks - or both. Sirius seemed to study Mrs. Malfoy for a few moments. ŇFine,Ó he finally said with a huff. Mrs. Malfoy smiled. ŇWhat brings you here tonight, then? YouŐve beenÉ outÉ for a few years now. Is there a rite you needed to complete?Ó Sirius paused. ŇOf sorts,Ó he said slowly. ŇIŐm going to be performing the Black dedication rite tonight.Ó Mrs. MalfoyŐs eyes widened in surprise. ŇYouŐre serving as mentor to someone?Ó she asked. ŇWho?Ó Harry watched SiriusŐs shoulders heave up and down once in trepidation, and then Sirius stepped to the side. ŇMy godson,Ó Sirius said quietly, putting an arm around HarryŐs shoulders. Harry took that as an invitation and he raised his chin just a bit, but Narcissa still had to tilt her head to the side to peer underneath the hood of his cloak. When she saw HarryŐs face, her mouth fell open in shock and she let out a gasp, and Harry suddenly realized that Draco hadnŐt said a word about him to his own mother. Slytherins keep each otherŐs secrets. Not knowing what else to do, Harry smiled sheepishly at her. He had a feeling he was going to be getting a lot of similar reactions throughout the night. Mrs. Malfoy quickly schooled her expression - proper pureblood lady that she was - but her sheer astonishment still shone in her eyes. She was just opening her mouth to say something when someone suddenly flew past her. ŇYou made it!Ó Draco cried, launching himself at Harry in a manner that was reminiscent of Pansy. His hands lingered on HarryŐs back. Over DracoŐs shoulder, Harry saw a gamut of emotions run across Mrs. MalfoyŐs face .shock, amusement, then understanding - before she finally regained her composure. ŇDraco, please show some decorum,Ó she said in a scolding voice. Draco stepped back from Harry and stood up straight, clasping his hands behind him. ŇIŐm sorry, Mother.Ó ŇCissy, itŐs the Solstice,Ó Sirius said. ŇLet them be teenagers.Ó Surprisingly, Mrs. Malfoy let out a laugh. ŇYou have always been a teenager, Sirius.Ó She smiled down at Draco and Harry. ŇDraco informed me that you had moved to Slytherin house, Mr. Potter,Ó she said, Ňbut he didnŐt tell me much more.Ó Her smile grew broader. Harry felt Sirius roughly tugging his hood back over his head; it must have flown off when Draco had tackled him. ŇIf Sirius is performing the dedication rite for you, Mr. Potter, am I right in assuming that my son is serving as your peer?Ó Mrs. Malfoy asked. Harry nodded, and Draco grinned. Mrs. Malfoy was silent for a moment, and then she gave another small smile. ŇWhy donŐt you twoÉ go see what friends of yours are here?Ó she said, and then she glanced up at Sirius. ŇIf itŐs permissible to your godfather, of courseÉÓ Harry could tell from her demeanor that she wanted to speak with Sirius in private, and it looked like Sirius knew it, too. Sirius sighed. ŇJust stay where I can see you, okay?Ó he said quietly to Harry. Harry quickly nodded in agreement, eager to see some of his other housemates. ŇBe back before we begin,Ó Narcissa added. Draco and Harry agreed and began to saunter across the clearing, and Draco leaned towards Harry to whisper at him. ŇSirius Black was my second guess,Ó he said. ŇYou said you had someone in mind, and I knew it had to be either him or my Aunt Andromeda.Ó Harry laughed. ŇI honestly hadn't even really realized that Andromeda was a dark witch until yesterday,Ó he said. ŇThen what are you two always writing about?Ó Draco asked demandingly. Harry shook his head. ŇItŐs private.Ó Draco let out a melodramatic sigh, and Harry smiled. At some point DracoŐs theatrics had become somewhat endearing rather than annoying, and Harry found he quite enjoyed them. Something was nagging at Harry, though. ŇDracoÉÓ he said. ŇIs yourÉ father here?Ó Draco shook his head. ŇMy father seems to have less and less time for tradition these days,Ó he said. ŇBit hypocritical of him, if you ask me.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó Draco didnŐt say anything, but he pursed his lips together and frowned. Harry peered at him for a moment, and then decided not to press. Draco clearly didnŐt want to talk about it. ŇDraco!Ó PansyŐs voice rang out from behind them, and they both turned to see Pansy grinning at them widely. Her dress was even more beautiful than the one sheŐd worn on Samhain, Harry thought. It looked to be entirely made of a crushed velvet in deep purple. The neck was high and the sleeves had a long slit running from shoulder to elbow, showing off just a hint of her skin. ŇHi, Pans,Ó Draco said as Pansy enveloped him in a hug. ŇHappy Yule.Ó Pansy drew back from Draco and peered at Harry curiously. ŇWhoŐs yourÉÓ She peered into HarryŐs hood and let out a shriek when she saw his face. ŇHarry!Ó ŇQuiet,Ó Harry said with a shushing noise. ŇMy godfather wants to make sure he knows everyone whoŐs here before they know IŐm here.Ó ŇSorry,Ó Pansy said in a staged whisper, not sounding sorry at all. ŇI justÉ IŐm quite excited youŐre here. I wasnŐt expecting you.Ó ŇDid you not tell anyone, Draco?Ó Harry asked curiously. Draco laughed. ŇI didnŐt want to disappoint if you couldnŐt make it, after all.Ó Then he smirked. ŇOr maybe I just like having a secret.Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇAre youÉÓ Pansy paused and bit her lip before continuing. ŇAre you justÉ checking out Solstice, or are youÉÓ Harry grinned. ŇIŐm planning on doing the rite of intent, yeah.Ó Pansy jumped up and down, clapping, and then tightly wrapped her arms around Harry. ŇYouŐre going to love being a dark wizard,Ó she whispered. ŇThis is my first Solstice as a dark witch, and itŐs amazing. I can actually feel the dark magic all around us, protecting us.Ó ŇYeah,Ó Harry agreed. ŇIt was a bitÉ intense when I first got here.Ó Pansy pulled back from Harry and stared at him in shock. ŇYou can feel it now?Ó she asked. ŇBefore youŐve even really started walking the dark path?Ó Harry blinked. ŇI meanÉ yeah,Ó he said. ŇIs that notÉÓ Normal, heŐd almost said, but heŐd had quite enough of not being Ônormal.Ő HeŐd been sensing dark magic the more and more he practiced the dark arts; heŐd assumed that was what happened to everyone with a dark affinity. ŇItŐs notÉ unheard of,Ó Draco said, and then his mouth curled into a wolfish grin. ŇBut it does mean that Harry Bloody Potter is going to be an immensely powerful dark wizard.Ó Harry rolled his eyes. ŇSometimes I think you only like me for my name, Draco,Ó he quipped. ŇNonsense,Ó Draco replied, waving a hand dismissively. ŇI like you for your power.Ó He laughed. Pansy let out a giggle. ŇDraco has always been attracted to powerful things,Ó she said. Harry found himself feeling flush, and he wasnŐt entirely sure why. Before he could really process the comment, though, he saw movement over PansyŐs shoulder and spotted Daphne approaching them. Daphne recognized Harry instantly, which made Harry tug on the hood of his cloak to pull it farther over his face. Daphne smirked at him. ŇItŐs your glasses,Ó she said. ŇThe fire is reflecting off of them, and theyŐre quite distinctive.Ó ŇYou mean theyŐre quite hideous,Ó Pansy said, huffing. ŇI still think we should either fix your eyesight, or find a pair that donŐt look like someone cut off the bottom of Butterbeer bottles and stuck them to your face.Ó Harry snorted once, and then burst out laughing. ŇI am so glad IŐm here,Ó he said. ŇI donŐt know what I would have done without your daily criticisms of my appearance.Ó ŇIŐm surprised to see you here, Daphne,Ó Draco drawled. ŇYou didn't come last year." ŇAstoria is doing the rite of intent tonight,Ó Daphne replied. ŇI wanted to be here for my sister.Ó Harry peered at Daphne curiously. ŇCan I ask you something, Daphne?Ó Daphne nodded. ŇOf course.Ó ŇYou have a dark affinity...Ó he said. Daphne smiled. ŇI do. And youŐre wondering why IŐve never declared,Ó she deduced. Harry nodded, and she shrugged. ŇIŐve just never felt the need to. You know if itŐs right for you or not. IŐve never felt like it would be aÉ bad thing, I just feel like IŐm already... complete without it.Ó She let out a full-blown grin. ŇI know there are advantages, but I just like keeping my optionsÉ open.Ó Harry puzzlement at that response must have shown on his face, because Daphne continued on. ŇIf you feel like declaring dark is right for you, you should do it.Ó ŇDaphne just enjoys being strange,Ó Pansy said. ŇObsessed with Muggle science, refuses to declare darkÉÓ Daphne rolled her eyes, but then grinned at Pansy. Harry couldnŐt help but smile. He loved the feeling of being surrounded by protective dark magic, but he loved the fact that he was with his friends even more. ŇWe should get back,Ó Draco said. ŇTheyŐll be starting soon.Ó Harry nodded. HeŐd been hoping to see Blaise or Theo, but he wasnŐt even sure if they were there. Harry and Draco rejoined Mrs. Malfoy and Sirius, and Harry noticed that the witches and wizards were surrounding the fire in the center. Draco had been right - there were several dozen, and Harry felt a kind of kinship that heŐd never felt before. Even if they werenŐt declared, every single witch and wizard there had a dark affinity. Sirius pulled Harry to a spot a healthy distance away from the others, but still helping to complete the circle. Harry decided they were far enough away from the Malfoys to inquire about what Mrs. Malfoy had wanted. ŇShe was asking about you,Ó Sirius whispered back. ŇShe knows that you clearly have to have a dark affinity in order for you to even be here, but she was wondering if you had some kind of ulterior motive for wanting to declare.Ó Harry blinked. ŇIÉÓ He paused. ŇI guess itŐs understandable that sheŐs asking that, actually. But I donŐt.Ó ŇI explained that to her,Ó Sirius said. ŇShe seems quite interested in you, Harry, and IŐmÉ not sure thatŐs a good thing. Keep an eye on her.Ó Harry nodded, and then silence fell over the crowd. Four silhouettes stepped towards the fire in the middle. They spread out around the fire and each one took a point on each side of the circle. From what Sirius and Draco had explained to him, these witches and wizards were taking the cardinal directions. Harry felt like he was practically vibrating with excitement. HeŐd never seen the elements called before. The red-headed witch that stood up and to the right of him - the east corner - raised her arms, her wand clasped in her right hand. ŇEurus,Ó she said in a melodic voice. ŇDe aere et vento. Coniungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis.Ó ŇConiungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis,Ó Harry repeated, along with the others in the circle. Join us on this longest night. A sudden wind swept through the circle, and it took HarryŐs breath away. The air had answered. The wizard to his left - the south - repeated the witchŐs motions, raising his arms. ŇAuster,Ó he said, his voice low and rumbling. ŇDe flamma et ignis. Coniungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis.Ó Harry again repeated the line with the others, and the fire in the middle suddenly roared twice as large, before fading back to its original size. Harry couldnŐt restrain the smile that spread itself across his face. Draco was right - he did like fire. The next witch, a woman with beautiful dark hair and wearing a long blue dress, began. ŇFavonius. De aqua et pluvia. Coniungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis.Ó Misty rain suddenly began cooling HarryŐs warm face. He tilted his head up into it, closing his eyes. Then it was gone as fast as it had appeared, but Harry could still feel its energy surrounding him. Finally, the wizard at the north corner raised his arms ŇSeptentrio. De terra et lutum. Coniungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis.Ó ŇConiungere nobis in hoc maxima noctis,Ó Harry said, one last time, and the ground beneath his feet trembled ever so slightly, like a tiny earthquake. At his back, Harry felt the dark magic move around them. He could see over the heads of the other witches and wizards that a faint purple haze formed a kind of wall, surrounding the circle. Harry suddenly felt almost overwhelmed with emotion. HeŐd understood DracoŐs explanations that magic was sentient, in a way, but it was the first time Harry had truly witnessed it and understood what that meant. The dark magic surrounding them wasnŐt just protecting them; it was watching them. Dark magic had been guiding Harry every step of the way to get him to the clearing on the Solstice, and Harry was immensely glad that he had let it do so. The same wizard who had called down earth began to speak once more. ŇA circle has no beginning and it never ends. This is our magic, and this is our strength,Ó he said. ŇThe wheel has turned once more, and we come together for this, the longest night. This is when the dark is strongest, and thus it is our night. It is a time for the gathering of strength, personal renewal, and reflection, as well as the beginnings of new journeys. ŇYou may now begin any personal rites that you wish to complete,Ó the man continued. ŇThe elements have been called, so you may ask them for assistance should you need them. I just ask that everyone return when the bell sounds so we can bear witness to those taking their first steps on the dark path.Ó Sirius suddenly reached out and squeezed HarryŐs shoulder, and Harry smiled at him before glancing over to see that Draco and Mrs. Malfoy were making their way over to them. ŇI canŐt believe IŐm about to complete a dedication rite with a Malfoy,Ó Sirius muttered unhappily. Harry glared at him. ŇMy son is also a Black,Ó Mrs. Malfoy said, obviously having heard him. Sirius had the sense to look slightly abashed, but Harry could still hear him muttering, "Blacks aren't much betterÉ" Harry gently kicked Sirius in his ankle. ŇMother would like to know if you mind if she witnesses the dedication,Ó Draco said softly. ŇI told her I would ask.Ó Keeping SiriusŐs earlier warning in mind, Harry looked over to Mrs. Malfoy cautiously, who smiled at him. ŇIn these times, it is so rare that someone born outside of the dark community declares, Mr. Potter,Ó she explained. ŇIt used to be a few every year, and now itŐs one every few years. As of late, even that small number has dwindled. The dark needs new blood.Ó Harry blinked. Draco had mentioned his mother complaining of exactly that, so it must have been a phrase that she used often. He glanced at Sirius. ŇItŐs up to you, Harry,Ó he said quietly. ŇUmÉÓ Harry said hesitantly. ŇI guess itŐs fine.Ó Mrs. MalfoyŐs delicate smile blossomed into a full-blown grin, which looked a bit odd on a woman who held herself so properly. Harry was struck by how much she looked like Draco. His face may have resembled his fatherŐs, but his smile was all his mother. Sirius nodded. ŇOkay, Harry. MalÉ Draco and I will swear our dedication with the element that speaks to you the most. Did you decide which one?Ó ŇI can probably guess,Ó Draco said, laughing, and Harry grinned. ŇFire,Ó Harry said, and Sirius smiled in response. ŇThat was mine, too,Ó he said, putting his arm around Harry and guiding him to the southern corner of the circle. ŇItŐs the Gryffindor in us,Ó Harry said with a laugh. ŇGryffindor is supposed to be fire.Ó ŇAnd Slytherin, water,Ó Mrs. Malfoy said, a note of surprise in her voice. ŇNot many people know how the elements correspond to the houses anymore. Who did you hear that from?Ó ŇAÉ a friend,Ó Harry said. He wasnŐt sure he should mention Andromeda to her sister, not with how fractured their relationship seemed to be. And if Sirius wanted Harry to be cautious around Mrs. Malfoy, Harry certainly didnŐt want to offer her any information about who heŐd been communicating with. The closer they drew to the south corner, the warmer it became. Harry wondered if the other corners had similar sensations - if the east would be windier, or the west damp and humid. Sirius moved Harry between himself and Draco, Sirius on the left and Draco on the right, with Harry facing outward. He and Draco turned inward to face Harry. ŇYou ready?Ó Draco asked softly. Harry nodded. Sirius raised his wand. ŇDevotio vestri itineris,Ó he said. Draco repeated his actions. ŇDevotio vestri itineris.Ó ŇAuster,Ó Sirius said. ŇIgnis. We beg you to help us protect this young wizard as he begins to walk the dark path.Ó ŇWe beg you to help us guide him as he takes each step forward,Ó Draco continued. ŇI am the archer.Ó ŇI am the bow.Ó ŇAnd Harry James Potter is our arrow,Ó said Sirius, Ňthat we will send blazing forward.Ó Sirius then pointed his wand at the ground and with a twist of his hand, he conjured a beautiful, pristine rose. He bent down and picked it up. HarryŐs eyes widened as Sirius gripped the stem tight and dragged his hand down it. When he pulled his hand away, it was covered in cuts and SiriusŐs blood shone in the firelight. Sirius passed the rose to Draco, who repeated the action, and then Draco moved to stand in front of Harry. ŇThe dark, like a rose, is beautiful,Ó Draco said as he held the rose out to Harry with a bleeding hand. ŇBut it has thorns.Ó Harry, breathless, reached out and took the rose. ŇWe will not protect you from the thorns of the dark,Ó Sirius said as Draco moved back to HarryŐs side. ŇBut we will give you the tools you need to see that the thorns hold beauty, even if they might cause pain.Ó Sirius then moved to HarryŐs front and an affectionate smile appeared on his face. ŇHarry, when I first realized that you likely had a dark affinity, IŐll admit I wasÉ scared,Ó he said. ŇFor you, I mean.Ó He shook his head. ŇBeing a dark wizard in this world is not easy. I wanted nothing more than for you to have an easier life, especially with how difficult yours already is. ŇBut now that weŐre hereÉÓ Sirius let out something that wasnŐt quite a sigh. ŇI look at you now, and I can tell that you truly do belong here.Ó He bent down and enveloped Harry in an embrace. ŇI will stand with the dark, and the dark and I will protect you and guide you as you walk the dark path.Ó ŇThank you, Sirius,Ó Harry murmured. He swallowed hard; something of a lump had formed in his throat. Sirius stepped back to the side, and Draco took his place. They just stared at each other for a few moments. The silence stretched out, and Harry raised his eyebrow at Draco. Draco smirked. ŇHi, Scarhead.Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇHarry.Ó Draco sighed, and his smile faded into something more serious. ŇWhen you were sorted into Slytherin, I wasÉ completely baffled. But IŐve come to realize that your Slytherin qualities and Gryffindor qualities actually compliment each other quite well.Ó He paused for a moment and tilted his head to the side in consideration. Whether he was considering what to say next or consider Harry himself, Harry couldnŐt tell. ŇYou are a Slytherin with no fear,Ó he continued, and he gave Harry a wicked smile. ŇAnd I am so very excited to watch as you walk the dark path. Even though your lack of fear scares me, I think itŐs going to lead you to discover things the rest of us have not.Ó He stepped forward and wrapped his arms around HarryŐs shoulders, and Harry lifted his arms to return the embrace. ŇI will stand with the dark,Ó Draco said, Ňand the dark and I will protect you and guide you as you walk the dark path.Ó When Draco stepped back, they both had identical grins on their faces, and then Draco took his place back to HarryŐs right. ŇDraco Malfoy,Ó Sirius said, Ňdo you accept the privilege of being this wizardŐs peer?Ó ŇI accept,Ó Draco responded. ŇSirius Black, do you accept the privilege of being this wizardŐs mentor?Ó ŇI accept.Ó As one, Draco and Sirius raised their wands, and they spoke together. ŇDevotio vestri itineris.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened as the now familiar purple light of dark magic swirled around and weaved in and out between all three of them. The rose in HarryŐs hands started to glow with the same iridescence, and then, amazingly, the rose began to break apart and dissolve. Each piece shone with the same purple light and they danced up into the air, joining the dark magic already surrounding them. The magic became focused and something of a twister formed around Harry, and then it disappeared into the night. Harry glanced at Draco, who grinned at him. ŇMr. Potter,Ó said Mrs. Malfoy. Harry turned around to see her with a look of elation on her face. ŇYou are going to give the dark community quite a surprise tonight.Ó ŇThatŐs what IŐm worried about,Ó Sirius muttered. Harry had to admit that he agreed, but he wasnŐt going to let his worry stop him. ŇI saw his affinity rite, Mother,Ó Draco said. ŇAnd I think theyŐre going to be far more surprised at how much the dark magic likes him.Ó *** The four of them wandered aimlessly for the next twenty minutes or so, sometimes stopping to witness a wizardŐs rite or to watch as someone performed some impressive bits of dark magic. One witch in a sleeveless dress was covered in deep scratches all down her arms, and blood was dripping onto the ground around her. She was crying, but she was also smiling. Harry thought the sight probably should have disturbed him, but she looked so happy that instead he was just curious as to what she had done. ŇShe performed a blood rite for fertility,Ó Mrs. Malfoy explained quietly. ŇSheŐs likely had problems conceiving, so sheŐs asked the dark magic for help.Ó She reached out and put a hand on DracoŐs shoulder and she gave him a soft smile. ŇThe scratches must not be healed by a spell; she needs to allow them to heal naturally. Once they are healed, she will try for a child again, and she will almost certainly be successful.Ó She placed a kiss on the top of DracoŐs head. ŇI was, at least.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Draco asked in disbelief. ŇYou wouldnŐt have been born if not for the blessing of the dark,Ó Mrs. Malfoy said. Draco stared at her. ŇI never knew that,Ó he said quietly. A deep bell chimed and rang out across the clearing. ŇItŐs time,Ó Sirius said. Harry nodded, smiling. ŇIŐm ready.Ó The entire crowd gathered at the west side of the circle, where the woman who had called water stood waiting. ŇI ask that any who are performing the rite of intent to please gather behind me,Ó she announced. Sirius gave HarryŐs shoulder a squeeze, and Harry joined the other children. There werenŐt as many as Harry had been expecting - there was Astoria Greengrass, of course, and four other Slytherins from third year, as well as one other student that Harry recognized as a fourth-year Ravenclaw. There were seven in all, including Harry. ŇGather round, now,Ó the woman said. ŇI want to make sure you all can hear me.Ó She waited for the seven of them to surround her in a half-circle, and she smiled at all of them. ŇMy name is Violetta. Witnessing the next generation perform their rites of intent has always been my favorite part of every Solstice. This is your time. We are gathered here to celebrate you, as you take your first steps on the dark path.Ó She paused. ŇI need to speak with each of you to ensure that you have performed the necessary steps prior to the rite of intent, and that you are all at least thirteen years of age. I may also ask you a few questions. Your answers will not prevent or hinder you from performing the rite, but they are questions that you must ask yourself before you take this step.Ó Violetta pulled Astoria aside first, and she quickly waved her wand over their heads. Although Harry could see their mouths moving, Harry couldnŐt even hear a whisper of their voices; Violetta must have cast a silencing charm. One by one she moved through the others, and then she finally came to Harry. She waved her wand and smiled. She didnŐt waste a moment. ŇYou are certainly older than thirteen, but how old are you, dear?Ó ŇFifteen,Ó Harry responded. Violetta nodded. ŇAnd what is your name?Ó Harry hesitated. Violetta frowned at HarryŐs silence. ŇStating your name is a part of the rite, you realize...Ó ŇI know,Ó Harry said, sighing. ŇIŐve just kinda beenÉ putting it off.Ó ŇPutting what off?Ó Harry swallowed nervously, and then he reached up and drew the hood of his cloak back. Violetta blinked, and then her eyes widened in recognition. Her gaze automatically drifted to HarryŐs scar. ŇIs this a trick?Ó she whispered. She didnŐt sound angry, just disbelieving. ŇNo,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇI swear itŐs not.Ó ŇYouŐre notÉ under polyjuice or anything of the sort?Ó Violetta asked. ŇYou really areÉ Harry Potter?Ó Harry nodded. Violetta stared for another few moments before neutralizing her expression, but she wasnŐt smiling like she had been before. ŇYou must perform the rite of affinity before performing the rite of intent. You could become gravely ill if you and the dark have not spoken yet.Ó ŇI did,Ó Harry responded. ŇI did it on Samhain.Ó Violetta narrowed her eyes. ŇAnd why do you wish to declare for the dark?Ó Harry took in a shaky breath. ŇItŐs beenÉ pushing me hereÉ or guiding me, maybe?Ó he said quietly. ŇIt took me a while to realize what was even happening, butÉ the dark wants me here. And I want to be here. When I did my affinity rite, itÉ I felt like the dark was home.Ó He sighed. ŇItŐs hard to explain. I just know that the dark is where IŐm meant to be.Ó ViolettaŐs expression changed to one of surprise. ŇI understand how difficult it is to explain, and it must be even harder for you,Ó she said, a hint of sympathy in her voice. ŇWith not having been raised in this communityÉÓ The smile reappeared on her face. ŇYou are going to give these witches and wizards the shock of a lifetime.Ó ŇI know,Ó Harry said. ŇI wishÉÓ He trailed off, not entirely sure what he wanted to say. Violetta grinned at that and Harry was reminded of someone, though he couldnŐt quite place who. ŇThe rites are performed from youngest to oldest. You are the oldest, so youŐre going to be quite the finale.Ó Harry sighed, then nodded. ŇGood luck, Harry Potter,Ó Violetta said, and then she ended the silencing spell and looked to the other children. ŇPlease line up.Ó As the silencing spell ended, Harry could hear faint whispers from the people standing closest to him. He dared to sneak a glance, and he could see a few them staring at him. Only a few seemed to have recognized him so far, and even those who did seemed unsure who they were actually looking at. He knew it wouldnŐt last for long, and he took his place at the rear of the line. ŇWe are here to bear witness to these young witches and wizards as they begin walking the dark path,Ó Violetta announced to the crowd. ŇWe are also here to pledge ourselves to help them as they make their way to their final declaration. The stronger and deeper the roots, the taller the tree will grow.Ó Violetta was handed a pitcher by the wizard who had opened the circle. She held it up to the crowd. ŇThey perform their first rites in front of Favonius, the water. Water is the element of the journey, and it will also allow them to put down strong roots.Ó She then tilted the pitcher and water streamed out of it. She poured the water in a small circle around her, and then stepped out of it. The circle lit up with that gorgeous purple light that Harry was now becoming accustomed to. Violetta handed the empty pitcher back, and turned to Astoria. ŇCome, child.Ó Astoria stepped into the circle and crossed her chest with her wand. She took a breath and began to speak. ŇI, Astoria Nova Greengrass, intend to start walking the dark path. I ask for the dark to protect me and guide me, as I will protect and guide the dark.Ó Her voice shook as she continued speaking the words of the rite, and then she raised her wand. ŇTenebris ambulant mecum!Ó Light surrounded her, and she stepped out of the circle on shaking legs. One by one, the children stepped into the circle and repeated AstoriaŐs actions. Some had light that was a bit brighter than the others, and some seemed more unsteady on their feet as they finished. The Ravenclaw boy stumbled as he finished his rite, and Harry took in a deep breath and held it before stepping into the circle. He gripped his wand and raised it to cross his chest, his hand just below his sternum, and the tip of his wand up by his ear. He let out his breath, and he heard that the whispers were starting up again. He blocked them out and steeled himself. If this was anything like his affinity rite, he didnŐt want to fall to his knees like he had on Samhain. ŇI, Harry James Potter, intend to start walking the dark path,Ó he said, and he ignored the gasp that ran through the crowd. ŇI ask for the dark to protect me and guide me, as I will protect and guide the dark. I will dedicate my blood, magic, and self, in the hopes that the dark will accept me as its child.Ó His hair stood on end as he lifted his wand and pointed it to the stars. ŇTenebris ambulant mecum!Ó The light was so bright that the crowd faded into nothing, and Harry gasped. It wasnŐt like his affinity rite - it was far more intense. Harry felt as if every part of his body .inside and out - was being caressed by dark magic, and it sent tingles running all over his skin. The wordless whispers started up in his mind again -welcome, you are safe, you are protected, you belong - but this time there was another feeling that seemed louder than all the rest combined. Love. And it was unconditional. The dark wanted Harry, even with all of his flaws and questions. Harry felt almost overcome; even his dearest friends always had questions, but he knew that the dark would never question him. It didnŐt last as long as his affinity rite, but with how strong it felt, Harry was grateful. He wasnŐt entirely sure if he could even stay conscious if the magic remained any longer. The light faded, as did the magic, but that feeling of being loved stayed with him, and Harry felt like crying. He nearly fell out of the circle, but he somehow managed to stay on his feet. He leaned over and rested his hands on his knees. He heard a shriek of joy and arms suddenly wrapped around him. Another pair tackled him from the other side, and he came back to himself to suddenly realize that Pansy and Draco were surrounding him. He grinned and then sagged in their arms, just letting them hold him upright. To his side, he heard one person start to clap. He glanced over DracoŐs shoulder and saw Mrs. Malfoy, front and center in the crowd, applauding. One by one, others around her joined in. It didnŐt spread through the entire crowd - most of them seemed much too shocked to even move. But he did see a few familiar faces begin to make their way over to him. Blaise got to them first, and he started laughing when he saw Harry peeking out over Pansy and DracoŐs arms. ŇYour face is beet red, Harry!Ó he said. ŇItŐs a little embarrassing,Ó Harry muttered. ŇI wish this wasnŐt a big deal.Ó ŇIt is, whether you like it or not,Ó Draco whispered in his ear. It tickled. ŇIŐm sorry.Ó "You've been keeping secrets, Pansy," a voice said from their side. Harry glanced over and saw Violetta with an amused expression on her face. Pansy laughed. "You know how it is, Mother." Harry blinked and looked from Pansy to Violetta; there certainly was a strong resemblance. ŇAre you okay to stand up yet?Ó Pansy said. Harry put his weight on his feet again and felt steadier, and he nodded. Almost immediately after Pansy and Draco released him, Harry was nearly tackled by someone taller than him, who lifted him off of his feet as they squeezed him tighter. He felt alarmed until he looked up to see Freya Yaxley grinning down at him. She was apparently stronger than she looked. ŇHarry, you have no idea how huge this is,Ó she said quietly. ŇYou made my father completely speechless, and he always has something to say.Ó She set Harry down and whispered to him. ŇAnd heŐs a Death Eater.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry exclaimed. He supposed he shouldnŐt have been surprised; his housemates couldnŐt be the only children of Death Eaters in his house, after all. ŇHeŐs not the only one here, either,Ó Freya continued whispering, ignoring HarryŐs shock. ŇWhat youŐve doneÉ what youŐre going to doÉÓ She smirked. ŇIt just might change everything. *** Harry had to deal with a few random witches and wizards introducing themselves. He seemed to get a mix of everything in their reactions - disbelief, gratitude, wonder, and more. A few even offered their assistance with his personal rites, and he thanked them, informing them that he already had the help he needed. Sirius never strayed far, and it seemed that Mrs. Malfoy didnŐt either. One person, though, made him grit his teeth. Something about his demeanor and the way he carried himself set Harry on edge. He was an older gentleman, dressed completely in black, and he gave Harry a long look before beginning to speak. ŇMr. Potter, I am Rasmus Nott,Ó he said. ŇYou are now housemates with my son, I believe?Ó He didnŐt offer his hand, and neither did Harry. ŇWeŐve met,Ó Harry bit out. ŇSort of.Ó ŇI know,Ó Nott said. ŇI find myself wishing that we hadnŐt met under suchÉ strenuous circumstances.Ó Sirius was immediately at HarryŐs side, and NottŐs gaze drifted to him. ŇSirius Black,Ó Nott said. ŇI spotted you earlier and wondered what could have possibly brought you back after so many years.Ó He smirked, looking back at Harry. ŇNow I understand.Ó ŇI think you need to leave,Ó Sirius said, sneering. ŇI simply wanted to congratulate Mr. Potter on beginning his journey,Ó Nott said. ŇI look forward to seeing what sort of dark wizard you will becomeÉ should you finish walking the dark path.Ó ŇIs that a threat?Ó Sirius growled, taking a step towards Nott. Nott shook his head. ŇItŐs not intended to be,Ó he said. ŇIŐm just bearing in mind that people from outside the community who decide to declare often donŐt finish all of the necessary rites.Ó His smirk widened disgustingly. ŇThey donŐt seem to have the stomach for it.Ó ŇI have every intention of completing the declaration,Ó Harry said, narrowing his eyes. ŇI hope that you do,Ó Nott said. ŇAnd I do genuinely mean that.Ó He turned to leave, revealing Theo right behind him. Theo quickly moved into his fatherŐs place. ŇIŐm sorry about that,Ó Theo said quietly. ŇI canŐt even begin to tell what he must be thinking right now.Ó ŇWhoŐs this?Ó Sirius asked, frowning. Theo looked at Sirius, his eyes widening. ŇThis is Theo,Ó Harry offered hesitantly. ŇOne of my housemates.Ó Theo swallowed, clearly nervous. Harry supposed Sirius was slightly intimidating. ŇIŐm Theodore Nott. And thatÉÓ He glanced over at Mr. NottŐs retreating form. Ň... is my father.Ó Sirius scowled. ŇHarry, how many of your roommates in Slytherin have Death Eaters for parents?Ó Harry elbowed Sirius. ŇStop it,Ó he hissed. Theo sighed. ŇThere are four of us, sir,Ó he said quietly. ŇTheyŐre my friends, Sirius,Ó Harry whispered. ŇPlease donŐtÉ donŐt be cruel.Ó ŇIŐm not my father, sir,Ó Theo said. Sirius looked like he desperately wanted to say more, but he simply pursed his lips and glared at Theo. ŇCan I talk to you, Harry?Ó Theo asked. Harry nodded, and they stepped away from the crowd. ŇHarryÉÓ Theo sighed, and his shoulders slumped. ŇIŐm sorry forÉÓ He instantly knew what Theo was talking about. ŇItŐs okay, Theo. I donŐt blame you for being worried.Ó He sighed. ŇHell, IŐm worried, and itŐs my head.Ó Theo gave Harry a wry grin at that, and he opened his mouth to say something but was interrupted by another familiar voice, one that usually set HarryŐs teeth on edge. ŇPotter.Ó Harry turned, and sure enough - Professor Snape stood behind him. His eyes widened, and he swallowed. ŇProfessor,Ó he said hesitantly. SnapeŐs expression was oddly more neutral than Harry had ever seen it - at least when it was directed towards him, anyway. Snape simply stared at him for a long moment, and Harry began to grow uncomfortable. It reminded him of when Snape had studied him right after being sorted into Slytherin, but there was something almost deeper about his scrutiny this time. ŇSir?Ó Harry finally asked, wanting to break the silence. Snape seemed to snap himself out of whatever spell heŐd fallen into, and a familiar scowl appeared on his face. ŇWhen do you intend to complete your final declaration?Ó he asked. Harry blinked. ŇUmÉÓ he said. ŇI was hoping to do it on Beltane.Ó Snape scoffed. ŇYes, that would suit you,Ó he said, seemingly more to himself than to Harry. ŇIn more ways than one.Ó His eyes pierced into Harry. ŇI feel I must inform you, Potter - if you are truly serious about walking the dark path, you must take every caution to not allow the headmaster to find out what you are doing,Ó he said. Harry swallowed. ŇI am serious. And I wasnŐt intending on letting him find out.Ó ŇAfter your final declaration, thoughÉÓ Snape actually appeared somewhat thoughtful. ŇGet away from my godson, Snivellus,Ó Sirius said, suddenly swooping in, stationing himself firmly between Harry and Snape. Harry sighed. ŇSiriusÉÓ ŇBlack,Ó Snape sneered. ŇI understand that you must want to feelÉ useful, but your protection of Potter is completely unnecessary and useless here.Ó He paused. ŇYou should know that.Ó ŇI donŐt want you even speaking to him,Ó Sirius snapped. Snape looked at Sirius for a moment, and then a particularly nasty smile appeared on his face. ŇSpeaking to him is inevitable. I am his head of house, Black,Ó he said. ŇYour godson is now a Slytherin. Did you forget? Or perhapsÉ force it out of your mind?Ó Sirius moved as if to snatch Snape, or hit him, and Harry reached out and grabbed SiriusŐs arm. ŇSirius, donŐt,Ó he pleaded. ŇHarry!Ó DracoŐs voice rang out from behind him, but Harry didnŐt dare turn around. He had no idea what would happen if Sirius tried to attack Snape in a place where dark magic supposedly prevented it, but Harry was certain that he didnŐt want to find out. Suddenly, cutting through the tense silence, Harry heard music. It was eerie, ethereal, and haunting, but also somehow beautiful. Harry blinked. ŇWhat is that?Ó he asked. The tension slowly ebbed out of SiriusŐs body, and he glanced down at Harry. ŇItŐs the start of my favorite part of Yule,Ó he said, smirking. He shot one more dirty look at Snape before turning away from him, and Harry finally let go of his arm, looking back to see Draco and his mother. Draco was grinning at him. ŇYou ready to dance?Ó Harry felt completely appalled, remembering the Yule Ball of the previous year. He shook his head emphatically. ŇIŐm complete shite at dancing, Draco.Ó ŇDoesnŐt matter,Ó Draco said, and he sidled up to Harry, looping their arms together. ŇWith how much the dark magic likes you, all you have to do is listen,Ó he whispered. "You'll find that you want to move with it." Sirius snorted. ŇCheers,Ó he said, and then he pulled out a flask from his pocket. He uncapped it, saluted Draco and Harry, and took a deep swallow. He moved to put the cap back on, but then paused in consideration. After a moment, he held it out to Mrs. Malfoy. Mrs. Malfoy eyed the flask warily. ŇThat is mostÉÓ ŇItŐs the fuckinŐ Solstice, Cissy,Ó Sirius said, rolling his eyes. ŇCut loose.Ó There was a beat of silence from both of them, and then, astonishingly, Mrs. Malfoy took the flask. She sipped it delicately at first, and then tipped it back fully. Sirius laughed, and DracoŐs eyes went wide as saucers before he started laughing as well. ŇCome on, Harry,Ó Draco said, tugging on HarryŐs arm. All around the fire, witches and wizards were gathering, swaying in time to the melody that seemed to be coming from nowhere. Harry stood and watched them for a moment, before Draco leaned over and whispered in his ear. ŇLet go.Ó Harry closed his eyes and let the music wash over him. The music started soft and slow, but began picking up the pace and soon became lively and almost frantic. Harry found himself dancing with Pansy, then Daphne, and even Astoria, who looked just as pleased as he felt. Witches and wizards all around him danced wildly, and a select few even started losing their clothes. Harry didnŐt mind. The night was frenzied and free; they all could do exactly as they pleased. Then Draco took HarryŐs hands and they started whirling around recklessly, not caring if they ran into anyone or if they fell down. Anytime one of them lost their footing the other would stop and help the other, and then they would resume their wild dance. Harry couldnŐt stop laughing. As the night went on, Harry and Draco danced with each other. Sometimes Pansy or Blaise or another would join them, but it always went back to just the two of them, and they let the wild, dark magic of the night spin them together, one constantly orbiting the other. Harry didnŐt think it was possible to feel more certain in his choice to declare dark, but with every passing moment he found himself more and more sure. He was where he belonged. Chapter End Notes Confession time: right before I was about to post the last chapter, I deleted two paragraphs that revealed who Harry was going to ask to be his mentor. SORRY IF THIS CAUSED STRIFE, but it was really fun to see all of you guessing. Lots of people guessed Andromeda (and I got some other guesses I wasn't expecting, haha), but I needed Sirius. Thank you! <3 11/18/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes No matter how much she tried to distract herself, HermioneŐs thoughts kept returning to Harry. She tried everything to get her mind off of him. She revised endlessly, she read, and she had inane conversations with Ginny, but she would always inevitably start thinking about one of her dearest friends again the moment her thoughts werenŐt occupied. Her problem became even worse when they arrived at Grimmauld Place, where she got a chance to spend time with him and observe him in a way she hadnŐt been able to since he was still in Gryffindor. Although there wasnŐt anything terribly obvious - aside from the possibility of Voldemort being able to get into HarryŐs head - a lot of little things had made Hermione realize that something was definitely going on with him. Despite RonŐs assessment of HarryŐs recent Ôchanges,Ő Hermione couldnŐt see that Harry was truly acting any differently. The sharp new clothes heŐd recently acquired were quite the change from his old, baggy flannels and oversized hoodies, but she quickly decided those were a non-issue. Clothes werenŐt the problem, and she had to admit that he had apparently also picked up some new habits and quirks that he definitely hadnŐt exhibited before. He smirked far more often than he smiled, for one. HeŐd always been a little bit mischievous -although usually with good reason - but his brilliant smile had given way to an odd twitch of his lips that made him look like he knew something that others did not. Harry was also starting to talk a bit differently than he used to. His phrasing was often a bit more proper than how he used to talk, and at times he even sounded just a little posh and old-fashioned. It wasnŐt a constant thing; for the most part, he sounded exactly like how he always had. But Hermione had noticed his manner of speaking changing more and more often, and it startled her every time he did it. Hermione knew she could attribute those little, almost superficial changes to his placement in Slytherin. It wasnŐt a bad thing, she supposed, and she knew that the company one kept could affect how one dressed and spoke; it was why people who moved to a new region would start unconsciously mimicking an accent that they heard every day. Hermione just hadnŐt realized how fast someone could really pick up on it, or that it would be noticeable when Harry had just moved to a different house within the same school. If Hermione had to admit it, Harry was starting to speak more like Daphne Greengrass, Blaise Zabini, and Draco Malfoy, and she wasnŐt entirely certain how she felt about Harry picking up on their mannerisms. Admittedly, Greengrass had been surprisingly pleasant to Hermione; she seemed to be just as fascinated with Muggle academics as Hermione was with Wizarding academics. Even so, Greengrass had never bothered to give Hermione the time of day until Harry had joined Slytherin, and it was hard not to suspect that Greengrass was only reaching out to her as a favor to Harry. Zabini, on the other hand, was somewhat of an enigma. HeŐd never been awful to Hermione in the past; heŐd mostly seemed like a well-behaved, applied student. SheŐd had no idea that he subscribed to the blood purity prejudice until heŐd called her a Mudblood in the middle of the hallway. His apology had been incredibly unexpected, but Hermione truthfully wasnŐt sure which bridge he was trying to mend - with Hermione, herself, or with Harry. And then there was Draco Malfoy. Malfoy had notoriously been the most unpleasant person in the entire school by far, and then as soon as Harry had joined Slytherin heŐd been completely silent towards Hermione. But what had shocked Hermione even more than that was that the animosity that had been so palpable between Harry and Malfoy for years seemed to dissipate almost instantly after Harry joined Slytherin. Even more surprising was that as the months went on, Harry Potter and Draco Malfoy appeared to actually become friends. SheŐd often seen them laughing or talking with one another at meals, and they were regularly seen in the halls together. They always sat at the same table in Potions, and sheŐd seen Malfoy help Harry day after day in that class. He achieved such improved results that Professor Snape had actually given Harry points more than once. Hermione certainly couldnŐt fault Harry for improving his schoolwork, but she couldnŐt help but be alarmed that he was spending so much time with Malfoy. It wasnŐt simply because Malfoy had bullied Hermione relentlessly for years - although Hermione had to admit that seeing Harry get along with him so easily made her stomach twist up in a knot - it was because Malfoy was the son of a Death Eater. Even more alarmingly, Harry told Hermione and Ginny that heŐd sometimes spoken with his housemates about Voldemort, and it was apparently his housemates that had informed him that Voldemort could actually be in HarryŐs mind. Hermione had to keep reassuring herself that Harry would never reveal any information that could hurt their allies, but considering what Sirius and Mrs. Weasley had said about not wanting the information about HarryŐs vision getting out, the fact that Harry had gone to his housemates with that information made Hermione feel incredibly uneasy. The children of Death Eaters knew that Voldemort could insert himself into HarryŐs head, and Hermione didnŐt like to think about what could be done with that information. On the other hand, Ron had reluctantly told her what Harry had said about his housemates .that if they hadnŐt gone for help when Harry had the vision, Mr. Weasley likely wouldnŐt have survived the attack. So Harry was starting to dress differently, talk differently, and he was confiding in people that were once his enemies, as well as unconsciously emulating their facial expressions. Hermione knew that all of those things shouldnŐt be any cause for alarm, but she just couldnŐt get her mind off of her friend. Her gut kept telling her that she was missing something, and it was frustrating her more than answering a question on a test incorrectly. With everything that had happened in the past few months, Hermione honestly couldn't pin down exactly what it was about Harry that was making her feel like something more was going on with him. Ginny was fairly certain that Harry wasnŐt actually being possessed by Voldemort, and Hermione supposed that she should trust HarryŐs judgment about what to tell his housemates and what not to tell them. Whatever it was, Hermione was grateful to have the holiday break to get the chance to finally spend some time with her friend - if only to reassure herself that Harry was actually okay. But then, just a day after their arrival at Grimmauld Place, she observed him acting incredibly odd; he seemed on edge and impatient for the entire day. He didnŐt seem to be able to stay still. Even when he sat down, he seemed to bounce up and down in his seat. She saw him check the time more than once, as if he had somewhere more important he had to be. Even more bizarre was the fact that whenever he seemed to be behaving incredibly anxious, Sirius would swoop down on Harry. They would exchange a few words that Hermione couldnŐt hear, and then Harry would smile sheepishly at Sirius and seem to calm down for a short while. It wasnŐt until the evening that it suddenly occurred to Hermione that it was one of the cyclical days that Harry and Daphne had spoken about earlier - it was the Winter Solstice, otherwise known as Yule. Hermione had taken DaphneŐs advice and spoken to Terry Boot about the cyclical days, and heŐd confirmed DaphneŐs words - many witches and wizards still observed the old wizarding traditions. In fact, Boot almost seemed offended when sheŐd asked about their association with the dark arts. He confirmed that his family observed all eight of the days throughout the year, and that they would celebrate Samhain instead of Halloween, as well as Yule instead of Christmas. Could HarryŐs impatience have had something to do with Yule? He had said heŐd been reading about the cyclical days, but he hadnŐt given her any indication that he wanted to start observing them. She had assumed that he only went with the Slytherins on Samhain to see a tradition heŐd never seen before. And if Harry really did want to start celebrating the cyclical days, wouldnŐt he have spoken with her about it? TheyŐd already discussed them before; Harry wouldnŐt have a reason to keep it to himself. Even coupled with SiriusŐs odd behavior, Hermione thought she could likely rule Yule out as a reason for HarryŐs unusual hyperactivity. Sirius had made it clear what heŐd thought about his family and their observations of wizarding tradition. Unless SiriusŐs attitude towards wizarding tradition was the reason why he kept standing on HarryŐs footÉ Hermione had to admit that she knew far too little about what was going on with Harry to actually determine if she was right in feeling perturbed. However, the fact that she knew so little to begin with was troubling in and of itself. Harry had been her best friend since their first year, and she used to know everything that was happening with him. Now, it felt as if she was only on the outskirts of his life. She supposed that it wasnŐt just his moving to a different house to cause them to start drifting apart; it could also be that they were getting older. After all, she hadnŐt told him everything going on in her own life, either. The day after HarryŐs day of constant anxiety, though, just added to the puzzle. He seemed to be on the opposite end of the spectrum in terms of energy - he looked and acted so exhausted that it appeared as if he had played a Quidditch match, fought a basilisk, and outrun a dragon all in one night. She kept seeing him nearly nodding off while he, Hermione, and Ginny were hanging out in the sitting room. He finally just appeared to just give in, laying down on the sofa and almost immediately fell asleep. Only a few minutes later, Sirius nearly stumbled into the room, took one look at Harry, and then collapsed onto the adjacent sofa and started snoring away, as well. She and Ginny quickly left so as not to disturb them, and Hermione settled into the dining room to revise for Charms. A few hours passed before Mrs. Weasley marched into the sitting room and Hermione could hear her irritated tones. ŇWhy in the world are you both so knackered?Ó she asked. Hermione could hear Sirius mumble something, but she couldnŐt make out what the words were. ŇAnd what were you two doing that caused you up so late?Ó Mrs. Weasley demanded. There was a moment of silence, and then Sirius responded. ŇTalking.Ó ŇYou are a terrible influence!Ó Mrs. Weasley snapped. ŇYour godson is fifteen; he shouldnŐt be up until all hours of the night!Ó She clucked her tongue. ŇIŐve told you before, Sirius - he isnŐt James.Ó ŇI know he isnŐt!Ó Sirius snapped, and Hermione froze. She hated hearing Sirius and Mrs. Weasley argue, but they seemed to rub each other the wrong way more often than not. SiriusŐs next statement was so quiet that Hermione could barely hear it. ŇI was actually starting to think heŐs more like me than James,Ó he said. ŇBut now IŐm finally learning that Harry is completely his own person.Ó There was another pause. ŇThatŐs part of why we were up so late, Molly. We havenŐt gotten a good chance to really get to know each other.Ó Hermione thought she heard Mrs. Weasley let out a sigh. ŇWell, make sure he wakes up soon,Ó she said. ŇWe donŐt want him up all night again tonight. HeŐll ruin his sleep.Ó ŇIŐll get him up,Ó Sirius said, and then whatever he said next Hermione couldnŐt make out. Mrs. Weasley soon exited the sitting room, closing the door behind her, and Hermione put an extra effort into looking like she was concentrating on revising. A few minutes later, Harry came out of the sitting room, his glasses in one hand and rubbing his eyes with the other. He placed his glasses back on his nose and then blinked owlishly at Hermione. ŇWhat are you studying?Ó he asked. ŇCharms,Ó Hermione responded. ŇOkay,Ó he said, and then he turned and left the room. Hermione frowned at his retreating back, but she quickly turned her attention back to revising. Within a minute or two, though, Harry pulled out the chair across from her and plopped his own Charms text down on the table. Hermione stared in shock as he flipped open his text. ŇAre youÉ are you willingly going to revise with me?Ó she asked. ŇDuring the holidays?Ó HarryŐs lips twitched - and there it was again, that new smirk of his - and he shrugged. ŇIŐve been doing fairly well in Charms this year, so I may as well not lose it.Ó Hermione blinked at him. ŇYouŐve been doing better in Potions, too,Ó she said carefully. Harry nodded. ŇAll of my classes, really,Ó he said, and then he grinned. ŇMcGonagall even pointed it out.Ó Hermione stared at him for another few moments, and then she let out a laugh. ŇI feel like I should be offended that you ignored my advice for years, and then as soon as you leave Gryffindor you start taking it,Ó she joked. Harry laughed, and then they settled in to study. Hermione found that she actually quite enjoyed revising with Harry. They quickly came up with a rhythm: one quizzing the other with a few questions and then switching. They kept it up for just over an hour before they heard some shuffling out in the front hallway, and then Mrs. Weasley appeared in the doorway. ŇHarry, Professor Snape is here to see you,Ó she said. Harry blinked. ŇWhat?Ó he asked. ŇWhy?Ó ŇHe didnŐt say,Ó Mrs. Weasley responded. ŇHeŐs waiting in the kitchen.Ó A flicker of something Hermione couldnŐt pinpoint ran across HarryŐs face before he got to his feet, looking a bit grim. Hermione gave him a sympathetic look before Harry appeared to steel himself, and then he went through the door into the kitchen, closing it behind him. Hermione strained her ears to see if she could hear anything, pushing down the guilt at eavesdropping for the second time that day. Unfortunately, she could only hear the timbre of their voices, and wasnŐt able to make out a single word. And then, quite abruptly, she heard nothing at all. Hermione blinked, staring at the door. Someone had obviously cast a silencing spell. What in the world could Harry and Professor Snape have to talk about that was so private? While their relationship didnŐt seem nearly as full of animosity as it had been in years past, she couldnŐt picture either of them ever getting to the point of confiding anything private to one another. Whatever it was, it didnŐt last long. Harry exited the kitchen only a few minutes later carrying a small box, looking a bit perturbed. ŇWhatŐs that?Ó Hermione asked. ŇUh,Ó Harry said, letting out a little laugh. ŇTheyŐre presents from my housemates. Apparently the owls couldnŐt make it through the Fidelius.Ó HermioneŐs eyes widened. ŇAre youÉ are you saying Professor Snape made a special trip to bring you gifts?Ó she asked incredulously. Harry grinned. ŇNo. ThatŐd be the day hell freezes over.Ó He shook his head. ŇHe was coming here to talk to me anyway.Ó He then peered into the box. ŇMcGonagall stopped him on his way out, too, which IŐm convinced is the only reason he even bothered to bring themÉÓ he said, reaching in and pulling out a flat package. ŇThis one is for you.Ó He extended it towards Hermione. ŇWhat?Ó Hermione asked, taking the package from him. ŇIt was apparently in the Gryffindor common room yesterday.Ó Just then, Ron and the twins entered the dining room, followed by Mrs. Weasley. ŇWeŐre supposed to set the table for dinner,Ó George said. ŇApparently you two are excused from helping out since youŐre revising like good little students,Ó Fred added. ŇWhatŐs all that?Ó George asked, eyeing the box and the neatly wrapped gift in HermioneŐs hands. ŇUhÉ presents from my housemates,Ó Harry said quietly. ŇChristmas isnŐt for a few days,Ó Ron said, his tone a bit dark. Harry looked uncomfortable and awkward. ŇTheyŐre forÉ Yule, arenŐt they?Ó Ron continued, frowning. ŇYouŐre celebrating Yule now, Harry?Ó Mrs. Weasley asked. ŇUhÉÓ Harry said, his eyes widening. ŇMost of my housemates do.Ó ŇSo does Terry Boot!Ó Hermione said, suddenly feeling strangely awful about all the scrutiny Harry seemed to be under, despite the fact that sheŐd been quietly studying him for two days straight. ŇI asked him about it, and he said his family celebrates Yule instead of Christmas.Ó ŇThe Prewett family did as well when I was a child,Ó Mrs. Weasley said, and both Harry and Hermione looked at her in surprise. She smiled. ŇWe would light a Yule log and keep it burning throughout the night in order to keep the darkness away, and in the morning we would celebrate that the light was returning.Ó She looked somewhat nostalgic as she spoke. Ron was just opening his mouth to say something, but Hermione spotted it. ŇThatŐs really interesting!Ó she exclaimed. ŇI only recently started hearing about all these old traditions.Ó ŇWhy donŐt you celebrate it anymore?Ó Harry asked Mrs. Weasley. Mrs. Weasley sighed. ŇAfter my brothersÉÓ she paused, and an expression of grief flickered across her face. ŇWe started celebrating the Muggle holiday of Christmas when Arthur and I were married. We just kept up that tradition, instead.Ó She smiled wistfully and then swept into the kitchen. ŇSo whatŐd you get, Hermione?Ó Harry asked, reminding her of the gift she still held in her hands. Hermione looked down at the parcel, turning it over in her hands. ŇWaitÉ Hermione is getting presents from Slytherins, too?Ó George said with a laugh. ŇThereŐs a note,Ó Hermione said, unfolding the small bit of parchment taped to the parcel. ŇTo Miss Granger, Thank you for the loan of your Muggle books. In case you havenŐt bothered to speak to Boot yet, I thought you might find this interesting. Upon returning home for the holidays, I realized that my family had more than one copy. I figured you would get more use out of it than we would. Happy Yule to you. Your Possibly Evil Slytherin Acquaintance, Daphne GreengrassÓ Fred and George let out identical snorts. ŇI knew that Greengrass girl was all right,Ó George said. ŇAt least she has a sense of humor.Ó ŇBut isnŐt Harry HermioneŐs Slytherin acquaintance?Ó Fred added. ŇGreengrass said Ôpossibly evil,Ő dear brother,Ó George responded. ŇAnd we all know that Harry is actually evil.Ó Harry rolled his eyes, but grinned at the twins. Hermione was already unwrapping the package, and her eyes lit up when she saw that it was a copy of The Wheel of the Year. Greengrass was right that it was an old book, but it seemed to be in good condition. ŇIŐll have to write her and thank her,Ó Hermione said, smiling. ŇI have been rather curious about it.Ó Harry smiled and then took his box of gifts out of the room. Hermione sighed internally and wondered if sheŐd be able to catch Harry alone to find out what Snape wanted. Even if she did manage to pull him aside, she doubted that Harry would actually tell her anything at all. Chapter End Notes No worries: we'll be back to our regularly scheduled Harry next chapter... which is pretty much done, so it should be up REAL soon.
