Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes Judging from her expression and demeanor, Harry could tell that Hermione wanted to speak with him. Fortunately, Mrs. Weasley bustled around the kitchen as her children set the table, so he was given ample reason to avoid having any kind of private conversation with Hermione. Harry was at least grateful for that; although he knew heŐd be able to tell Hermione at least some of his conversation with Snape, there were pieces he would have to leave out. Despite his certainty of the path he was on, Harry was getting a little tired of having to dance around the truth. Dinner was an unusually silent affair, possibly because Sirius seemed to be in an absolutely rotten mood. HeŐd grown quite upset when he learned that Snape had come to speak with Harry while he slept. In retrospect, Harry found himself quite glad that Sirius hadnŐt been there; after their miniature confrontation at Solstice, he didnŐt want them anywhere in the vicinity of each other. After they finished cleaning the table, Sirius pulled Harry into his bedroom. ŇWhatŐs up?Ó Harry asked. ŇWhat did Snape want?Ó Sirius immediately asked. Harry sighed. ŇHeŐs going to be training me in Occlumency,Ó he said, Ňonce school starts up again.Ó A host of emotions flickered across SiriusŐs face. ŇYouŐre going to have to spend quite a bit of time alone with him,Ó he said, scowling. Harry nodded. Sirius let out a sigh. ŇI suppose there isnŐt really an option, though.Ó ŇThatŐs what Snape said, too,Ó Harry replied. Sirius muttered something under his breath and turned to his dresser, opening the top drawer and pulling out three small packages. ŇI was saving one of these for Christmas, but Yule seems more appropriate for you now,Ó he said. The first package was cylindrical, and Harry peered at it curiously before unwrapping it. He uncovered a black pillar candle that had obviously already been used at least once; the wick was charred and a divot laid in the top where it had been melted. He looked back up at Sirius in confusion. ŇYouŐll need that for your personal declaration rites,Ó Sirius explained. ŇMalfoy should be giving you one when you get back to school, provided he followed the Black rites correctly.Ó He handed over another package, one that was thin and flat. Harry pulled it out of the wrapping to discover a small black book that was obviously incredibly old; it had a hard leather cover and it had clearly been hand-stitched together at some point. There wasnŐt a title, but when he flipped it open he discovered what it was. ŇÔThe Declaration Rites of the Ancient and Most Noble House of Black,ŐÓ Harry read. The first page also had the Black crest, reading ÔToujours Pur.Ő ŇThat will explain why IŐm giving you a candle,Ó Sirius said. ŇThe candle is from when I declared.Ó He then gave a little scoffing noise and pointed at the crest. ŇThere are at least two parts of the rites that you may want to skip or alter because theyŐre all about pureblood superiority.Ó He smirked. ŇI just changed them to my liking and I still managed to complete my declaration.Ó Harry idly flipped through a few of the pages, and sure enough, there were a few lines that mentioned upholding the purity of wizarding blood. ŇHow will I know what to change them to?Ó ŇYou can ask me if youŐre not sure what youŐd like to do instead,Ó Sirius said. ŇThe rites arenŐt set in stone. As long as the basics are covered, the dark will consider them complete.Ó He turned the last package over in his hands before handing it to Harry. ŇAnd if you have questions - even though I canŐt be with you at Hogwarts - you can contact me whenever you want with this.Ó Harry pulled the wrapping off and uncovered a small hand mirror, which confused him even more than the candle. ŇItŐs a two-way mirror,Ó Sirius said. He reached back into his dresser and pulled out an identical piece. ŇJust look in the mirror and say my name, and weŐll be able to talk. It should prevent any of your house -Ó He paused. Ň-anyone from listening to our conversation, provided you just go somewhere private.Ó Harry ignored the near slight against his housemates. At least his godfather seemed to be trying. ŇThank you, Sirius,Ó he said quietly. ŇYou havenŐt chosen an easy path, Harry,Ó Sirius said, wrapping his arms around Harry. ŇBut I will be with you every step of the way.Ó *** After Harry snuck his gifts from Sirius into his room, Hermione finally managed to catch him in the hallway. ŇYou donŐt have to tell me if you donŐt want to,Ó she said. She didnŐt even need to explain what she was talking about. Harry let out a sigh and turned back around, opening the door to his bedroom. They entered and Harry closed the door behind him. ŇYou want to know what Snape wanted,Ó Harry said. Hermione nodded. ŇYou have to admit that itŐs a bit strange that Professor Snape stopped by to see you during the holidays,Ó she said. ŇAnd I doubt he only stopped by to give you yourÉ Yule gifts from your housemates.Ó Harry sighed and leaned against his bed, running a hand through his hair. ŇYeah, that was justÉÓ He paused. He hadnŐt even had a chance to open the gifts from his housemates yet. ŇHe came to tell me that heŐs going to be training me in Occlumency.Ó He glanced up at Hermione, wondering if sheŐd ever come across the term in all her reading. Snape had explained it and Harry still barely knew what it was. ŇIŐve never heard of it,Ó Hermione said. Harry nodded. ŇI guess itŐs a little obscure,Ó he replied. ŇBut itŐsÉ supposed to help guard my mind against mental attacks.Ó He gazed down at his feet as he spoke. HermioneŐs eyes widened. ŇSoÉ they really do think thatÉ Voldemort might be in your head?Ó she asked. Harry sighed. ŇSounds like it,Ó he said quietly, then he looked up to meet HermioneŐs gaze. ŇAnd you canŐt tell anyone,Ó he said. ŇIf it gets out that SnapeŐs training me, it might put his position as a spy at risk.Ó Hermione let out a breath. ŇThatŐs why he put the silencing spell up, isnŐt it?Ó Harry nodded, ignoring the flip-flop in his stomach as he did it. Of course, it wasnŐt actually the reason for the silencing spell, but that was exactly what he needed Hermione to believe. Snape had used the silencing spell to reveal that there were only two people in the school that were capable of training him in Occlumency - the other being Dumbledore. Snape had explained that the very nature of the training would allow him to see HarryŐs memories, and that Harry should be grateful that Dumbledore ordered Snape to do it rather than taking on the task himself. After all, whoever looked inside his mind would easily be able to see his recent involvement in the dark arts. To HarryŐs immense surprise, Snape truly did not want Dumbledore to find out about HarryŐs intention of declaring dark. Snape actually seemed incredibly determined to ensure that Harry completed the declaration rites, though he didnŐt explain why. Despite the unusual topic, Snape had been his usual bristly self through the entire conversation, and knowing their history it likely had nothing to do with Snape wanting to Ôlook outŐ for Harry. Like Snape would actually go out of his way to help me. Harry internally rolled his eyes. ŇSo when does this training start?Ó Hermione asked, pulling Harry out of his thoughts. ŇOnce the term starts back up,Ó Harry replied. ŇTwice a week.Ó He smirked. ŇHe requested that I tell everyone that I was getting remedial potions lessons, but I pointed out that nobody would believe that considering certain people have noticed that IŐve been doing much better in Potions this year.Ó He gave Hermione a pointed look with a slight smile. Hermione laughed. ŇSo what are you supposed to say instead?Ó Harry shrugged. ŇWe decided that it would just be regular Potions tutoring,Ó he said. ŇIt fits, since I genuinely do want to get an ÔOŐ on my Potions O.W.L. - I still kinda want to be an Auror, after all.Ó Then he let out a sigh, crossing his arms. ŇBut OcclumencyÉ Snape is actually going to be in my head. I hate knowing that.Ó ŇIs there anything you can do to prepare for thisÉ training?Ó ŇHe said something about Ôclearing my mindŐ before I went to sleep,Ó Harry responded, Ňbut I have absolutely no idea what that means.Ó HermioneŐs face screwed up in concentration, and Harry smiled at the sight. It was exactly how Hermione always looked when she was faced with a puzzle that sheŐd very much like to solve. ŇIŐll look in the library once we get back to Hogwarts,Ó she said. ŇThere has to be a book on the basics, right?Ó *** In the privacy and silence of his room that night, Harry finally opened his gifts from his housemates. When Harry opened BlaiseŐs gift, Harry was immediately glad that he hadnŐt chosen to unwrap anything in the company of others. His eyes widened as he saw that it was a book that described the origins of several dark arts spells and rituals, including the Unforgivables. He tucked the book into his hiding place with the candle and Black rites from Sirius - on a bookshelf, hidden behind a few other nondescript titles - and then reminded himself to punch Blaise in the arm when they were back at school. PansyŐs gift was exactly what he expected; after all, sheŐd be talking about it for weeks. He unveiled a bottle of hair potion. He smiled as he turned it over in his hands, but he had no intention in using it until Pansy showed him how. TheoŐs gift was simple - an incredibly soft scarf in Slytherin colors, but what touched him even more was the note of apology included with it. Tracey and Millicent had gone in together on their gift for him: it was another bottle of basic vision potion. He grinned, deciding to save it for the next Quidditch game in case Snape didnŐt give him another. If Snape did continue to supply it, Harry would find another use for it. Harry saved DracoŐs gift for last. It was the smallest package of the bunch. He unwrapped it and discovered a beautiful figurine carved from a light purple stone that fit perfectly into the palm of his hand. It was a lion that sat regally, and a snake was draped over its shoulders. Unlike most wizarding figures it wasnŐt animated, frozen in its pose. The snakeŐs head was raised, and it bared its fangs as if protecting the lion. Harry smiled at it before unfolding the note that Draco had included. Harry, IŐd already special-ordered this before you had that one awful Ôdream,Ő so IŐm sorry if this dredges up any bad feelings for you. However, I also thought it represented you quite well. You are the Slytherin with the heart of a Gryffindor, which is going to make you a very dangerous Slytherin. With each day, I grow more and more pleased that you joined our house. I trust that youŐve come to enjoy Slytherin, and I hope you eventually embrace it. Happy Yule, Draco Malfoy HarryŐs eyes widened. DracoŐs letter not only echoed what heŐd said to Harry the night before during the dedication rite, but it was also almost identical to what the Sorting Hat had told him at the beginning of the school year. Harry decided that while heŐd been feeling more comfortable being a Slytherin as the months passed he still wasnŐt exactly ready to ÔembraceŐ it. However, he was okay with being a combination of a snake and a lion, and he smiled down at the figure. He could probably embrace being a Slytherin with the heart of a Gryffindor. *** Christmas was just a few days later. To no oneŐs surprise, Hermione got both Harry and Ron homework planners, though Harry was infinitely more amused than Ron at how the planners shouted at them to do their work. HarryŐs shouted considerably less than RonŐs, considering most of his holiday assignments had already been completed. The gift that Harry found himself appreciating the most came from Mrs. Weasley. She, as usual, had made him a jumper as she had for all of her children, but his was the only one knitted in Slytherin colors. Harry jumped up from his spot on the floor to give her a hug of appreciation, and she seemed relieved that she hadnŐt misjudged the situation, stating that she hadnŐt been certain whether or not Harry was pleased with his new house placement. Harry proudly wore his jumper to St. MungoŐs to visit Mr. Weasley and he found himself in a good mood, at least up until they ran into Neville and his parents. Harry had heard what had happened to the Longbottoms - tortured to insanity by the Lestranges - but seeing them first hand made him see Neville in a new light. He laid awake in bed that night, strange conflicting emotions dancing across his mind. HarryŐs own parents had been killed, while NevilleŐs were still alive but barely a part of the world around them. He hated thinking it, but Harry honestly wasnŐt sure which of the two of them had it worse. That wasnŐt the only thing haunting him: Bellatrix Lestrange seemed to be the worst kind of dark witch, but Harry knew that she was far from alone. He wondered what could drive a witch or wizard to that kind of evil, and he remembered reading about how some dark witches and wizards would sometimes let themselves be controlled by dark magic. There was apparently a line somewhere, but Harry had no idea where that line was. And with how much had changed in Harry in the last few months, Harry wondered once again if he should be worried that the dark magic was controlling him. Even with the fear of Voldemort being in his mind, Harry had still chosen to start walking the dark path simply because it had Ôfelt right.Ő Draco had said the dark magic didnŐt take free will away, but that seemed almost contrary to what heŐd read. Harry knew he still had a lot to learn about the dark arts, and he had to find out more about what it meant when dark magic controlled a person. He knew he could ask Sirius, but he also found himself wishing that the holidays were already over because he really wanted to speak to Draco. Harry slept fitfully that night. *** Morning arrived and after a quiet breakfast, Harry again found himself revising with Hermione. She seemed delighted to actually have someone to discuss schoolwork with, and they worked companionably until they heard a crash at the door, followed by screeching. ŇFilth! Despicable half-breeds! Begone -Ó The screaming was quickly muffled as someone had obviously drawn the curtain over the painting of Mrs. Black. Hermione and Harry glanced at one another, wondering who had arrived. ŇHappy Boxing Day!Ó Tonks grinned as she entered the dining room. Barely more than her eyes and her blue hair could be seen, bundled up as she was. ŇIs there an Order meeting?Ó Harry wondered. Tonks shook her head. ŇNo. Just popping by for lunch,Ó she said. ŇBy request of Sirius.Ó She glanced over her shoulder. ŇThough IŐm not entirely sure why he wanted me to bring Mum,Ó she muttered. HarryŐs eyes widened as a woman with beautiful, almost royal features appeared behind Tonks. She had thick, shining brown hair and pale skin, and she seemed to carry herself just like Narcissa Malfoy - proud and proper. Her eyes almost immediately landed on Harry, and she smiled. ŇAndromeda?Ó Harry asked quietly, rising from his chair. ŇAndromeda Tonks?Ó ŇYou must be Harry,Ó she said. ŇItŐs nice to finally meet you in person.Ó Harry couldnŐt believe it. After months of writing back and forth with her, she was actually in front of him, in real life. AndromedaŐs smile widened, and then she extended her arms. Harry immediately took the invitation and crossed the dining room, and he found himself grinning as they embraced one another. HeŐd never even properly met her, but he felt like she was just as much a part of his life as Sirius. ŇIÉ IŐm confused,Ó Tonks said, scratching her head. ŇYou know each other?Ó ŇWe somewhat do, Nymphadora,Ó Andromeda said as she drew back from Harry. She cupped his face in her hands affectionately. ŇWeŐve been writing one another since Harry was sorted into my old house.Ó ŇAnd I canŐt begin to describe how much I appreciated your letters,Ó Harry said. ŇYou explained Slytherin much better than anyone else did.Ó Andromeda let out a melodic laugh. ŇWe Slytherins are difficult to explain,Ó she said, Ňeven when you are Slytherin yourself.Ó ŇIŐve been trying to understand my mother for years,Ó Tonks said dryly. Harry grinned. ŇHarry, Hermione?Ó Mrs. Weasley called from the kitchen. ŇCould you clear your books off the table and help set it for lunch?Ó ŇBe right there!Ó Harry called over his shoulder, never taking his eyes off Andromeda. ŇIŐm really glad youŐre here,Ó Harry said. ŇIŐd really like to talk to you.Ó Andromeda smirked. ŇWeŐll make time for a conversation, Harry,Ó she said. ŇGo give Molly a hand.Ó *** Lunch was enjoyable, although nearly everyone save Sirius and Harry didnŐt seem to know how to speak to Andromeda. SheŐd obviously been raised as a proper pureblood lady, and her manners seemed so impeccable that she seemed to shame the Weasley children simply by existing at the same table as them. She insisted on helping Mrs. Weasley clean up when they were done eating, and she and Mrs. Weasley chatted for quite some time, trading cleaning spells. Tonks kept rolling her eyes and making faces at the table. ŇShe always makes me feel like IŐm a giant disappointment whenever she gets into one of those conversations,Ó she said quietly to Harry and the twins. ŇI could never get a single one of her cleaning spells right.Ó ŇYour strengths clearly lie elsewhere, Nymphadora,Ó Andromeda called from the kitchen. ŇAnd her hearing is like MoodyŐs vision,Ó Tonks groaned, her head dropping down to the table with a Ôthunk.Ő ŇI swear, she mustŐve done some crazy ritual in order to have hearing like a bat.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened imperceptibly at that; fortunately, the others were distracted by Andromeda and Sirius coming back into the room. ŇOr you simply speak louder than you think you do,Ó Andromeda said. ŇYouŐve always been loud.Ó Tonks scowled, but then laughed and gave a shrug. ŇI guess youŐre not wrong.Ó ŇYouŐre like Sirius in that regard,Ó Andromeda said, glancing at her cousin. Sirius raised an eyebrow at that. ŇIŐm not sure if youŐre complimenting me or insulting your daughter.Ó ŇYouŐll just have to continue wondering,Ó Andromeda said, a slight smile on her lips. She then raised a hand and beckoned for Harry to join her, and he quickly got to his feet. ŇItŐs high time we spoke with one another, Mr. Potter. I have a few questions about some of the issues youŐve raised in your letters, and I daresay you might have some questions for me.Ó Harry nodded eagerly. ŇIs this a ÔSlytherins onlyŐ meeting?Ó Sirius said, a hint of sarcasm in his voice. ŇYes,Ó Andromeda replied, her tone making it obvious that she was not to be argued with. Harry laughed, and Andromeda draped her arm around HarryŐs shoulders and led him out of the room. They went into the parlor, and Harry let out a laugh. ŇI seem to be having a lot of private conversations in here lately.Ó ŇLetŐs make it more private than usual,Ó Andromeda said, drawing her wand. She waved it over their heads without a word, but it was obviously a silencing spell. They sat in opposite armchairs and Andromeda appeared to study Harry for a moment. ŇSirius dropped a hint to me that you may beÉ walking a certain path?Ó Andromeda said first. HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇUmÉ yeah,Ó he admitted. Andromeda smiled. ŇThatŐs wonderful to hear,Ó she said. ŇMost of us were born into it, and those like myself and Sirius seem to get driven out when we go against the grain. The dark needs new blood.Ó HarryŐs mouth dropped open. ŇYouŐreÉÓ He paused, unsure if he should mention that heŐd heard the exact same words from Narcissa Malfoy only a few days prior. The most he really knew about their relationship was that Mrs. Malfoy didnŐt even really mention her sister. Andromeda raised an eyebrow. ŇI thought Sirius would have told you that I am a dark witch,Ó she said. ŇHe did,Ó Harry responded. ŇDoesÉ does your family know?Ó he asked curiously. ŇThey do,Ó Andromeda said. ŇAlthough they are under the impression that I am a non.practicing dark witch, which isÉ mostly true.Ó She let out a sigh. ŇMy own daughter choosing to be an Auror has made me particularly conflicted, but I truthfully havenŐt observed the cyclical days in years, much like my cousin.Ó She smirked. ŇWhich is why I was all the more surprised when Sirius mentioned that heŐd attended YuleÉ with you.Ó ŇIÉÓ Harry paused, his eyes widening in shock. ŇI thought he wasnŐt able to tell anyone that I was there!Ó he exclaimed. AndromedaŐs smirk widened. ŇHe didnŐt tell me,Ó she said. ŇYou did.Ó Harry blinked. ŇYou need to be careful, Harry,Ó Andromeda said, shaking her head. ŇThe magic of Solstice may prevent anyone else from revealing that you were in attendance, and it may help prevent anyone from tearing it from your mind, but you can still reveal your own secrets simply by saying the wrong thing.Ó ŇYouÉÓ Harry trailed off. ŇDid you even know that I was actually...Ó Andromeda sighed. ŇSirius only told me that heŐd attended Yule, and I knew there had to be a reason why. I was able to piece together the rest from things youŐve said in your letters. You merely confirmed my suspicions.Ó Harry frowned. ŇI donŐt think IŐve mentioned dark arts in my lettersÉÓ ŇYou didnŐt,Ó Andromeda said. ŇBut I could tell there was something you werenŐt saying. I am quite capable of reading between the lines. You also mentionedÉ deceiving a friend.Ó She sighed. ŇI donŐt know you well, but you donŐt seem the type to be deceptive unless the situation truly calls for it.Ó She sighed. ŇFromÉ personal experience, I suspected that it may have been about the dark arts.Ó ŇYeah,Ó Harry admitted. ŇThatŐs exactly what it was about. I hate it.Ó He shook his head. ŇIt is a part of the burden we bear,Ó Andromeda said. ŇI lied to my Ted for over a year when we first started becoming friends.Ó She sighed. ŇI also feel it was with good reason. If heŐd found out the truth about me in the beginning stages of our friendship, I fear that we would have never gotten to where we are today.Ó Harry frowned. ŇIŐve already been friends with Hermione for years, though,Ó he said. ŇSince first year.Ó He sighed. ŇBut the thing with me and the dark artsÉ itŐs rather new.Ó He took in a shaky breath, looking down at his hands. ŇI also donŐt think I couldÉ do what youŐve done. I donŐt want to give up one for the other.Ó ŇOnce you are declared, you donŐt Ôgive upŐ being dark,Ó she said. ŇItŐs part of your identity. And just because I donŐt attend the cyclical celebrations anymore doesnŐt mean I donŐt occasionally sneak a little dark arts here and there.Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇLike a ritual to improve your hearing?Ó Andromeda just smirked in response. Harry grinned, and then sighed, remembering what they were actually discussing. ŇWith HermioneÉ sheŐs already implied that sheŐs suspicious of me, and it doesnŐt sound like sheŐs heard anything flattering about dark wizards or the dark arts.Ó ŇShe likely hasnŐt,Ó Andromeda said. ŇThere is an inherent prejudice against the dark. I suppose I can understand some of it; after all, it is often the dark arts that are used to cause extreme pain and suffering, and it requires someone with a darker soul to use such magic.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇWhat?Ó he asked. ŇIŐve never heard that.Ó Andromeda sighed. ŇItŐs mostly just an expression,Ó she said. ŇItŐs an old theory as to why some people have dark affinities and some do not.Ó ŇWhat does it mean to have a Ôdark soul?ŐÓ Harry asked. Andromeda shook her head. ŇI probably shouldnŐt have used that phrase,Ó she said, Ňbecause I honestly donŐt know.Ó ŇDo you have a theory?Ó Harry pressed. ŇEveryone does,Ó she responded. ŇSome believe itŐs hereditary. Others believe itŐs environmental, which could also explain why so many dark families produce dark children. Still others think that it could be exposure to the dark arts at a young age, which would also explain why it appears to be hereditary.Ó ŇWhat do you believe?Ó She let out a laugh. ŇI think the dark chooses its children based on what a person is willing to do.Ó Harry raised an eyebrow. ŇThat would explain so many dark witches and wizards being in Slytherin.Ó ŇÔAny means to achieve our ends,ŐÓ Andromeda said, smiling. ŇThatŐs precisely why I believe what I do.Ó Harry sat back, considering. ŇThat definitely sounds better than Ôhaving a dark soul.ŐÓ ŇWhy is that?Ó Andromeda asked. ŇBecause it makes it soundÉÓ ŇEvil?Ó Andromeda said lightly. Harry nodded. ŇYou seem to still have a prejudice against the dark yourself,Ó Andromeda said. ŇDark doesnŐt mean evil.Ó ŇI know that,Ó Harry said, sighing. ŇJustÉ when itŐs attached to a soul rather than justÉ magicÉ it soundsÉÓ He trailed off, unsure of what he wanted to say. ŇWell, you werenŐt raised in the dark community,Ó Andromeda said. ŇThe darkŐs notoriety, however false, is a difficult one to overcome.Ó She sighed. ŇThe reputation the dark has gained in the last century or so has made it difficult for us to simply exist.Ó She fixed a searching look on Harry, and he shifted uncomfortably. ŇWhat?Ó he asked. ŇHas it occurred to you thatÉÓ She paused, frowning. Ň... that you, of all people, being a dark wizard could help improve that unfortunate reputation?Ó Harry scowled. ŇItŐs beenÉ hinted at, yeah,Ó he said. ŇBut IŐm justÉ I donŐt want to beÉÓ ŇA figurehead?Ó Andromeda asked. ŇYou do realize that youŐve already been one for much of your life, right?Ó ŇYeah,Ó Harry responded. ŇAnd I hate it. I donŐt want it.Ó Andromeda made a humming sound and sat back in her chair, continuing to study Harry with curious eyes. ŇWell, then, my little SlytherinÉÓ she said, Ňwhat do you want?Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked. ŇSlytherins have goals. ItŐs part of being Slytherin,Ó Andromeda said. ŇHarry, what is it that you want more than anything else?Ó Harry sighed, slumping back in his own chair. ŇI want to see Voldemort defeated,Ó he said. He was fairly certain that he could already see where this conversation was leading. ŇThatŐs quite the goal,Ó Andromeda said lightly. Harry let out a cynical laugh. ŇI suppose itŐs occurred to you that your declaring dark could actually weaken You-Know.WhoŐs support?Ó Andromeda said. Harry nodded. ŇItŐs beenÉ pointed out to me,Ó he said. ŇMy housemates already said the same.Ó ŇAnd I agree with them wholeheartedly,Ó Andromeda said. ŇBeing the figurehead that you so despise could actually help you reach your goal.Ó Harry frowned, staring down at his hands. ŇSlytherins use any means to achieve our ends,Ó Andromeda said, Ňeven when we are not a fan of the means.Ó She suddenly reached out and took one of HarryŐs hands. ŇYouŐve already shown that you arenŐt afraid of using means youŐre not exactly happy about, and you were sorted into Slytherin for a reason.Ó HarryŐs scowl deepened. ŇThe reason being to Ôimprove the darkŐs reputation?ŐÓ he asked sarcastically. Andromeda shook her head. ŇIŐm not talking about anything so trite as fate, Harry. IŐm saying that you already have the ability to do this because you are Slytherin.Ó Harry sighed. HeŐd already thought about how he wanted to save his housemates from being forced into VoldemortŐs service, but heŐd been avoiding thinking about how to actually make that happen. ŇHow?Ó he asked cynically. ŇBy announcing to the world that IŐve been neck-deep in the dark arts?Ó ŇDonŐt do that - not until after youŐve declared,Ó Andromeda said sharply, and then her face softened. ŇYou have time to figure it out,Ó she continued. ŇYou also have the time to decide if you want to do it at all. Desires can change with time, after all. If you decide that thereŐs something you want more than seeing You-Know-Who endedÉÓ Harry let out a bitter laugh. ŇThatŐs not likely.Ó ŇYou wonŐt be alone, Harry,Ó Andromeda said, her voice firm. ŇI will stand with you. And IŐm certain others will do the same.Ó *** The rest of the holiday break passed mostly uneventfully. He rarely got a few moments to himself, but he did manage to sneak up to the Black Library twice. Due to his housemateŐs warning about the potential of his things being searched, he didnŐt want to bring anything back with him - or anything more than what he already had, rather. Even so, he was determined to research whatever he could about what happened when a dark wizard was controlled by dark magic. There wasnŐt much to be found, but from what little he did read, it seemed that being ÔcontrolledŐ by dark magic was much like a kind of addiction; those that gave in to it would find themselves with an inherent need to use the dark arts, and they could often exhibit signs of insanity. That made Harry feel a bit better; although he felt a need to learn more, he didnŐt feel like he needed to use the dark arts. He wanted to spend more time looking, but he kept getting pulled away by Mrs. Weasley or Hermione or the twins. He used his nights to read through the Black declaration rites, and he understood what TheoŐs father had meant about some people not being able to stomach them. Harry was fairly certain heŐd be able to handle it, but he could actually see why someone might be scared off. There was going to be blood and pain in the first rite, followed by a rite where he had to open himself completely to the dark. Based off of how heŐd already felt during his affinity rite and his rite of intent, Harry was unsure how much more intense it could get. That was the second rite, though, and Harry decided he was going to concentrate on getting through the first rite before worrying about the next. He wanted to complete all three before Beltane, and he wanted to make sure he did them correctly. He studied the first rite - the blood rite - over and over again until he could recite the entire thing from memory. That only occupied some of his time, and he spent much of the holidays bored out of his mind. The holidays seemed to drag on and on, and Harry felt more eager to get back to Hogwarts by the day. The last night of break he found himself nearly too excited to sleep, and the silence felt oppressive. He felt on edge, and it felt like there was somewhere he had to be. He kept figuring that he was just excited to finally return to Hogwarts, but he also felt like he was missing something. He lay in bed staring into his dim room when the dark magic of the house suddenly reached up to him again in a way it hadnŐt since heŐd first arrived at Grimmauld Place after his vision. He suddenly sat upright in his bed. He did have somewhere to be, and Draco had been right; now that he knew what he was sensing, he could tell that the dark magic was trying to guide him somewhere, just as it had led him to the Black Library over the summer. He sat on his bed for a few minutes, breathing in and out. He wanted to test DracoŐs words; he needed to make absolutely sure that the dark magic wasnŐt actually taking his free will away, so he simply sat and tried to understand what was actually happening. It wasnŐt like the Imperius, he decided, and it didnŐt feel as if he had an undying need to go wherever the magic was trying to take him. It was more like a nudge than a push, and he finally decided that Draco had been absolutely correct: he didnŐt have to do whatever the dark magic wanted him to do. If Harry wanted to, he could ignore it. Even so, his curiosity was definitely overriding the desire to resist it, and he finally swung his legs over the side of the bed and closed his eyes, letting the magic wash over him. Now that he knew what was happening, it was a strange sensation. The magic seemed to lick at him gently, and he smiled at the feeling. He let his feet guide him, and he left his room, turning the corner. He paused at the stairs and looked up. It was taking him back to the Black Library. Harry suddenly felt a pang of regret that he hadnŐt made more time to spend in the Library, but perhaps the dark magic would guide him to something important. It had before, after all. He silently scaled the stairs and opened the door to the familiar, comfortable room. He walked over to the shelves and looked up at them, then reached out with one hand and ran his fingers along the spines of the books. He found himself stopping at one book, and he pulled it down off the shelf. He had to tilt the cover to read the title in the dim candlelight. It was called Blood Magic: Rites, Rituals, Sacraments, and Sacrifices. He frowned, and suddenly realized that the dark magicŐs pull had stopped. It was apparently only this book that it wanted him to find. He flipped it open and skimmed through the table of contents, but nothing jumped out at him as being particularly important. Maybe the dark thought he might need it for his first declaration rite, but he didnŐt see anything that looked related. He shrugged, slamming the book shut again. Including the Black rites, the book Blaise had given him, and this new one on blood magic, he was sneaking only three books on dark arts back to Hogwarts. Perhaps heŐd stuff them in his bag rather than his trunk, and pass them to one of his housemates as soon as he arrived. Harry left the library and tucked the book back with the others once he reached his room. He crawled back into bed and was asleep almost as soon as his head hit the pillow. Chapter End Notes Next up: Back to Hogwarts! I know a lot of people are chomping at the bit for some Snape. Don't worry - he'll be around in the next chapter. :) And then things really start taking off... And now to break some news to y'all: I was recently offered a new job in another city. Yay for me, but prepping for the move is going to be taking up time I would otherwise spend writing. I'm hoping to get one more chapter out before I pick up my life and move, but I honestly don't know how it's going to go. My apologies in advance if there's more of a delay than I'm expecting. Chapter 18:The Laughter Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Thank you! See the end of the chapter for more notes After a stomach-lurching and somewhat awkward ride on the Knight Bus - where Harry had been forced to sit by Ron in painful silence due to a lack of seats - they finally arrived in Hogsmeade. Harry was relieved to be away from the prying eyes of the passengers, and he could barely suppress a grin as his eyes landed on Hogwarts in the distance. The walk up the icy path to the school seemed to take ages, as they were slowed even more than usual as they dragged their trunks through the slush. TheyŐd arrived just after the rest of the students that had come by train, and Harry quickly parted ways with the others when they reached the Great Hall, making his way over to the Slytherin table. ŇThere you are!Ó Pansy exclaimed, jumping up from her seat to give Harry a hug. ŇYou werenŐt on the train.Ó ŇWe were getting worried youŐd been expelled again,Ó Blaise joked. ŇSo I - ow!Ó he exclaimed as Harry swiftly punched him in the arm. ŇWhat was that for?Ó ŇIf IŐd been expelled, it would have been your fault,Ó Harry whispered furiously. ŇWhat were you thinking, sending me that book? IŐm lucky I opened it when no one was around.Ó BlaiseŐs eyes widened. ŇWith you almost certainly being under Fidelius again, I wasnŐt expecting you to actually get it until you got back to school,Ó he said sheepishly. ŇSomebody brought my gifts to me,Ó Harry said. ŇJust warn me next time, okay?Ó ŇIf you got your gifts, why does your hair still look like a Hippogriff molted on your head?Ó Pansy demanded. ŇÔCuz you have to show me how to use that hair potion,Ó Harry responded with a laugh. ŇOh, come on, Potter,Ó Draco said, rolling his eyes dramatically. ŇDidnŐt you read the directions?Ó ŇNo,Ó Harry said, grinning. ŇThatŐs what youŐre for.Ó ŇDo you have it with you?Ó Pansy asked. ŇOr did you leave it in your trunk?Ó Harry sighed and reached into his bag, but also felt proud of having been able to predict Pansy so well. Blaise peered over his shoulder and his eyes widened. ŇYou have dark arts book here?Ó he hissed quietly when he spotted the books. ŇIn the fuckinŐ Great Hall?Ó Harry elbowed Blaise in the side as he quickly shut his bag again. ŇI figured my person was less likely to get searched than my trunk,Ó he whispered back as he passed the bottle over to Pansy. Blaise pinched the bridge of his nose and sighed. ŇYouŐre probably right.Ó Pansy, meanwhile, immediately spun the cap off of the bottle. ŇBring me your head,Ó she said. ŇCanŐt this wait until after dinner?Ó Harry asked, his gut telling him he already knew what the answer would be. ŇBlaise, bring me HarryŐs head.Ó A twisted grin on his face, Blaise manhandled Harry around the Slytherin table, forcing him into the seat between Pansy and Draco. Pansy smoothed the cold liquid into HarryŐs hair, and her fingers worked their way all the way down to HarryŐs scalp. ŇDraco was right,Ó Harry said, closing his eyes. ŇYou do give good head massages.Ó A hand suddenly reached under his chin to turn his head, and HarryŐs eyes popped open to see Draco studying him. ŇItŐs an improvement,Ó Draco said, smirking. Pansy laughed, and Harry wondered what his head looked like. The spectacle appeared to entertain the Slytherins around them, and Harry finally noticed that quite a few of the older students seemed to be looking at Harry with a more appraising eye than they usually did. Considering heŐd seen most of the students that were staring at him at Solstice a few weeks earlier, Harry didnŐt think their scrutiny was due to his fashionable new hairstyle. *** After dinner, Harry trailed behind the rest of his house as he adjusted the straps on his bag, making sure that his books wouldnŐt be seen. Blaise turned around to wait for him, and Harry hurried to catch up. ŇI am sorry about sending you the book,Ó Blaise said when Harry reached his side. ŇI really didnŐt think it would actually make its way to you until we got back to school.Ó ŇItŐs fine,Ó Harry said, sighing. ŇNobody saw it.Ó ŇDid you check any of it out yet?Ó Blaise asked. ŇI got it for you because thereŐs some really interesting history in there - like how some spells that are used to attack these days have origins in dark healing.Ó ŇJust skimmed it,Ó Harry said. ŇI -Ó Without warning, Blaise suddenly shoved Harry to the ground, and Harry groaned as his elbow collided painfully with the stone floor. Over their heads, a curse flew through the air and blasted into the wall. HarryŐs eyes widened as Blaise raised his head to look behind them. ŇHey!Ó Blaise shouted. Ahead of them, Harry could see the others had stopped to look and see what had happened, and Draco was already running towards them. ŇDown!Ó Draco hissed, and Harry ducked his head again, thinking another curse was flying towards him. Instead, Draco leaped over his head, taking off down the hallway. Blaise quickly got to his feet and sprinted after him. Adrenaline pumping through HarryŐs veins, he also sprang to his feet and followed them down the hall. But when they turned the corner, it was empty. ŇYou stupid coward!Ó Draco shouted, his normally pale face more than a little flushed. ŇYou're going to find out why this school fears our house!Ó ŇWhat in the world are you shouting about, Mr. Malfoy?Ó Professor Sprout appeared behind them, looking more frazzled than usual. ŇSomeone tried to curse Harry - again,Ó Blaise snapped. ŇAnd it had to be one of your Hufflepuffs.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Sprout exclaimed. ŇWhat happened?Ó She looked over at Harry. ŇAre you all right, Potter?Ó ŇIŐm fine,Ó Harry said. ŇThey missed.Ó Even so, she proceeded to inspect Harry from head to toe. Blaise held his hand out towards Harry as she did so, and Harry looked at him in confusion. Blaise rolled his eyes and took HarryŐs bag off of his shoulder so Sprout could check him out thoroughly. HarryŐs eyes widened, and he nodded at Blaise in thanks. He doubted she would have spotted his books, but he certainly didn't want to chance it. ŇWell, I donŐt think you need to go to the hospital wing,Ó Sprout decided, apparently satisfied that Harry was unharmed. She turned to Blaise. ŇWhat makes you think it was someone from my house, Mr. Zabini?Ó ŇWe figured at the beginning of the year that whoever attacked Harry had to be Gryffindor or Hufflepuff,Ó Blaise explained. ŇAnd considering the Gryffindors and Ravenclaws go to the towers...Ó Sprout frowned. ŇAre you certain it couldnŐt have been another Slytherin?Ó she asked. ŇOr that someone from one of the other houses may have followed you down here?Ó ŇOf course you donŐt believe us,Ó Draco said nastily. ŇMind your tone, Mr. Malfoy,Ó Sprout said, frowning. ŇI do believe you that someone attempted to curse Mr. Potter, but IŐll need more than circumstantial evidence in order to figure out who did it.Ó She sighed. ŇRest assured that IŐll be reporting this and that the matter will be investigated. Run along to your common room, now.Ó She shooed them away. ŇWell, at least she didnŐt brush it off,Ó Blaise muttered. ŇBut itŐs not like theyŐve turned up anything from the earlier supposed Ôinvestigation.ŐÓ ŇSomeone is still after your head, Harry,Ó Draco said, an infuriated look on his face. ŇWhen we find out who, it will not be pleasant for them.Ó *** Draco tugged Harry up to their dorm as soon as they got back to the common room. ŇBecause IŐve been dreaming of it for months,Ó Harry said, watching SnapeŐs expression carefully. ŇAnd thatŐs where Mr. Weasley was attacked, wasnŐt it?Ó He narrowed his eyes. ŇWhat was he guarding?Ó ŇIt does not concern you,Ó Snape said, his voice cold. ŇIt does when I see it almost every single night!Ó Harry snapped. ŇPerhaps you need to work on not seeing it, then,Ó Snape replied. ŇSomethingŐs in there,Ó Harry insisted. ŇSomething that Voldemort wants -Ó ŇDo not speak his name!Ó Snape hissed. Harry fixed a hard glare on Snape, and his scar flared in pain. SnapeŐs expression seemed to mirror HarryŐs. Hatred seemed to ooze from both of them, and SnapeŐs lip curled in disgust. ŇWeŐre done for tonight,Ó Snape said. Harry finally sprang to his feet, snarling. ŇYou havenŐt answered a single question IŐve asked all night,Ó he spat. ŇYou wonŐt -Ó ŇPotter!Ó Snape spat out his name forcefully. ŇYou should not want the same knowledge that the Dark Lord desires!Ó Harry froze, a chill running down his spine, and his eyes widened in alarm. Did Snape mean that the reason Harry wanted to know what was behind that door was because Voldemort did? ŇI will take you back to Slytherin,Ó Snape said. ŇWe will continue on Wednesday.Ó Harry didnŐt respond. ŇYou are to clear your mind every night,Ó Snape continued. ŇYou must rid yourself of all emotion before going to sleep.Ó ŇRight,Ó Harry responded, even though he didnŐt understand at all. He absently rubbed at his scar, which still throbbed painfully. They walked back to Slytherin in silence. *** Harry felt like his brain had gone through a grater, and it seemed to become worse with every passing moment. He stared sightlessly down at his homework, not comprehending a single word in his text. ŇHarry, are you okay?Ó Pansy asked. He nodded, even though he felt sick to his stomach, as well as simultaneously flushed and freezing, as if he had a fever. He leaned forward and tried to concentrate, but that seemed to just make the pain in his scar even worse. He raised his hand and pressed it against his forehead, as if trying to hold the burning in. ŇThatŐs it,Ó Draco said, standing up. ŇYouŐre going to the hospital wing.Ó ŇNo,Ó Harry said, wincing at the sound of his own voice. ŇI just need to go to bed.Ó He stood up, as well, starting to gather his things. ŇIŐll work on this tomorrow.Ó Another slice of pain ran through his scar and he teetered forward, his eyes clamping shut, and two hands suddenly reached out to steady him. Harry opened his eyes to see that Draco was the one who had grabbed him. ŇTheo, can you bring HarryŐs things up to our room when you come up?Ó Draco asked quietly. ŇOf course,Ó Theo responded. Draco guided Harry to their room, his hands never leaving HarryŐs arms. He opened the door, and Harry could see Blaise across the room, looking up from his desk. Harry had barely taken two steps into the room when the pain in his scar increased a hundred.fold, as if a knife was being driven straight into his skull. He couldnŐt see in front of him, he could no longer tell where he was, and he briefly even forget his own name. He heard maniacal laughter, endless and ecstatic. He couldnŐt recall feeling that happy in years. Something wonderful had happened, something that was to be celebratedÉ ŇHarry!Ó someone said. ŇHarry!Ó It felt like something slapped him in the face. The gleeful laughter stuttered into a grunt, and then started right back up again. The happiness heŐd felt started to dissipate, but the laughter continued ringing out all around him. His eyes snapped open, and he was suddenly aware that the mad laughter was coming from his own mouth. His scar throbbed in time with his own heartbeat, rapid and forceful. He immediately stopped laughing, and his vision suddenly came into sharp focus. His back was flat on the floor and Blaise and Draco hovered over him, staring down at him. Blaise looked somewhat frightened, but Draco was full of nothing but concern. ŇHarry?Ó Blaise said quietly, as if he wasnŐt sure who he was speaking to. ŇWhat happened?Ó Draco asked. ŇHeŐsÉ really happy,Ó Harry said with a gasp. He forced himself to sit up to his elbows, and Draco helped him sit up all the way. ŇDo you mean theÉ the Dark Lord is happy?Ó Blaise asked, his voice shaking. ŇSomethingÉ something good happened,Ó Harry said, slowly getting to his feet with DracoŐs help. He didnŐt feel like the words coming out of his mouth were his own, but he knew they were true. Every single one of his muscles seemed to be trembling uncontrollably. ŇHeŐs been hoping for itÉÓ He trailed off and immediately turned to his bed, and Draco released him and Harry collapsed onto his pillows. ŇSomething good for -Ó Blaise cut himself off with a grunt, and Harry looked at Draco and Blaise through bleary eyes. ŇGo to sleep, Harry,Ó Draco said softly. Harry squeezed his eyes shut, and his stomach roiled unpleasantly as he tried to think of what could have possibly happened to make Voldemort the happiest heŐd been in over a decade. Chapter End Notes I usually try to present chapters with very little explanation, but there are a few things I really wanna say about this one. First, Snape is still Snape. There's definitely something he's not saying (a couple of things, actually), and he and Harry are already starting to have a different kind of relationship than they had in the books. That relationship will continue to evolve... but he's not going to turn into cuddly Snape. Second, I mentioned way back in the beginning that there are certain tropes I'd avoid, and one I'm avoiding is that Harry is suddenly an Occlumency prodigy. I've got a few of reasons for going this route, some of which have to do with my own interpretation of canon and some of which have to do with the story I'm writing. Third, the last scene here is a version of one of my FAVORITE scenes in the book, and I'm still disappointed to this day that they didn't include it in the movie. The mental image of Harry laying flat on his back with laughter that isn't his own coming out of his mouth... it makes me grin like a loon. ALL THAT SAID: This is definitely the last chapter I'll be able to get out before my big move. Unless a miracle happens, you won't be hearing from me for a couple of weeks (almost certainly not until the beginning of October). You may have also noticed that the total chapter count inched up again; that's because I was staring at my outline and realized that a handful of chapters were going to turn out to be monstrously long, so I rearranged a few things. Chapter 19: TheBlood Chapter Summary The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! Thank you SO very much for your patience! Settling in took a bit longer than I estimated. 11/18/2019: 2nd beta pass by guestuser28653. Thank you! Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes When Harry awoke the next morning, his clothes were unpleasantly damp, his muscles ached, and he felt as if something had crawled in his mouth and died overnight. He rolled over and let out a groan, placing a hand over his eyes. ŇHarry?Ó He raised his head at BlaiseŐs voice and almost immediately dropped it back onto the pillow, regretting the action as his skull seemed to throb in time with his heartbeat. ŇI feel like I got hit by a lorry,Ó Harry mumbled, pinching the bridge of his nose. ŇA what?Ó Harry absently waved a hand, and then regretfully rolled to his side in order to retrieve his glasses from his nightstand. He groped blindly for a moment; they werenŐt on the corner closest to his bed where he usually placed them each night. He finally found them near the photo of his parents, and he slowly sat up as he unfolded them. ŇDraco, heŐs awake,Ó Harry heard as he slipped his glasses onto his face. He squinted across the room, where he saw Blaise shaking DracoŐs shoulder. Draco was up and out of bed so quickly that Harry wasnŐt even sure if heŐd actually been asleep. He immediately crossed the room, staring at Harry with concern. ŇHow are you?Ó he asked quietly. ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, momentarily confused. And then he remembered what had happened the night before - the foreign feeling of elation and that terrifying, maniacal laughter falling from his own lips. His eyes widened as he realized just how disturbing it must have appeared to anyone looking on. ŇIŐmÉ better,Ó he said. ŇI think.Ó ŇYou think?Ó Blaise asked, sounding rather uncertain. ŇI kind of feel like crap,Ó Harry admitted. ŇButÉÓ ŇYouŐre not in the Dark LordŐs head anymore?Ó There was a hint of fear in BlaiseŐs voice. ŇRight,Ó Harry said in affirmation, but BlaiseŐs expression didnŐt change. Harry suppressed a sigh and took a quick glance around their dorm; Theo, Greg, and Vince were all still asleep. ŇDid youÉ were you up all night?Ó Harry asked, realization suddenly dawning on him. ŇWe each took a shift,Ó Draco said. ŇWhat happened last nightÉÓ ŇWe wanted to make sure you got help immediately if anything else happened,Ó Blaise continued. His voice trembled as he spoke, even if it was barely noticeable. A flash of guilt sparked inside Harry. ŇIŐm sorry.Ó ŇDonŐt be,Ó Draco said. Blaise nodded in agreement despite his almost palpable fear. Something sparked through the guilt, though; his friends had made sure someone was watching him all night. He supposed that Slytherin protection must come in many forms. ŇThank you,Ó he said quietly. Blaise grunted and walked back to his bed, leaving Harry and Draco staring at each other for a few long moments. Finally, Draco gave a single nod before turning away. Harry found his bearings and finally dragged himself out of bed in order to get a shower before breakfast. As he paced through the familiar routine his headache subsided, but he again found himself worrying over what Voldemort could have been so happy about the night before. *** They didnŐt have to wait long to find out. At breakfast Tracey let out a strangled gasp, and her copy of The Daily Prophet was quickly passed around for the other fifth years to read. Draco, who had foregone his usual seat in order to sit at HarryŐs side, reached out and spread the paper across the table in front of them so he, Harry, and Blaise could all see. The first thing that leapt out at Harry was a photo of a witch on the front page. His first thought was that it was Andromeda, but then tiny differences started to become apparent as he continued to study the photograph. The photographed witchŐs hair was darker, and there was an unpleasant, curled sneer drawn across her lips. The biggest difference was in her eyes, which looked more than a little mad. Bellatrix Lestrange, the caption underneath the photograph said, convicted of torture and permanent incapacitation of Frank and Alice Longbottom. HarryŐs breath caught in his throat, and for more than one reason. The woman was AndromedaŐs sister as well as DracoŐs aunt, and she was also the witch that had put NevilleŐs parents in St. MungoŐs. Nine other convicted Death Eaters had escaped from Azkaban alongside Bellatrix Lestrange, and a pit yawned open in HarryŐs stomach as he realized just how grave the situation was. ŇThis is almost certainly why the Dark Lord was so pleased last night, Harry,Ó Draco whispered. Harry nodded in agreement, and his eyes drifted up to the main headline. A chill ran through Harry as he read the words. ŇIs Fudge actually blamingÉÓ Draco shoved an elbow in his ribs and Harry snapped his mouth shut. Ten Death Eaters had escaped from Azkaban, and the Ministry was pinning the escape on Sirius. Harry fumed silently, shooting a glare at Draco. Draco seemed to know that the anger wasnŐt directed at him. ŇBe wary of who could be watching you, Harry,Ó Draco whispered, his expression unusually neutral. His eyes flicked up to the head table and then back to Harry. Harry risked a glance up towards the professors, and his gaze instantly fell on Umbridge. Some hunched together talking in low whispers, much like Harry and Draco had been, while others had the Prophet propped up in front of them, expressions of horror dawning on their faces as they read. Harry then glanced out into the hall, and quickly deduced that unlike the professors, hardly any of the students appeared to know what had happened. Most of them seemed calm and unafraid, as if it were a perfectly normal day. He supposed that made sense; not many students received The Daily Prophet. However, Harry definitely knew of one other who took the Prophet besides Tracey, and his eyes drifted towards the Gryffindor table. Almost as if she could sense him looking at her, Hermione raised her eyes to meet HarryŐs. She held the Prophet in front of her, and her eyebrows drew together in a combination of concentration, fear, and worry. Not knowing what else to do, Harry simply shook his head at her. ŇDonŐt show anything.Ó The pit in HarryŐs stomach seemed to widen at DracoŐs order, and he wasnŐt entirely sure why. Breakfast at the Slytherin table was the most subdued Harry had ever seen it, but he couldnŐt help but notice a few Slytherins in sixth and seventh year looking unusually pleased and smug. ŇAre you okay?Ó Hermione asked, not even bothering with a greeting. Harry shrugged. ŇI kind of think I have to be,Ó he said. HermioneŐs eyes widened. ŇAnd if you think sheŐs going to be watching you, in particular, youŐre going to need to be on your best behavior.Ó *** Hermione, as usual, was absolutely right. Umbridge was obviously in a horrid mood. ŇWhat did you need?Ó A brief flash of irritation showed on UmbridgeŐs face before her lips curled into that disgusting smile of hers. ŇI was simply wondering if youŐd given any more consideration about giving a statement to the Prophet.Ó It only took a moment for Harry to realize why she was asking; she was worried about the reaction once the news about the Azkaban break inevitably started spreading. ŇI daresay the Minister for Magic could -Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry spat out before he could stop himself. ŇThat happenedÉ quickly.Ó ŇI was sorted into Slytherin!Ó Harry snapped. ŇYouŐre the one who said I needed to figure out what that meant, and why, and -Ó ŇThat does not mean you should instantly begin trusting your housemates,Ó Snape hissed. Harry frowned. ŇWhat about the ÔSlytherin covenant?ŐÓ he asked, suddenly wondering if it had been around when Snape was a student. ŇOr is that somethingÉ new?Ó SnapeŐs eyes narrowed. ŇItŐs not Ônew,ŐÓ he replied, Ňbut for the Malfoys, familial loyalty trumps Slytherin loyalty. When it comes time to choose between you and his father, Draco will choose his father.Ó A chill ran down HarryŐs spine at that, but at the same time a fire seemed to ignite in HarryŐs gut. ŇI donŐt know if thatŐs true,Ó Harry said quietly. ŇDonŐt be ignorant, Potter,Ó Snape snapped. ŇYouŐve been a Slytherin for all of four months. Draco has been LuciusŐs son for his entire life.Ó HarryŐs eyes dropped to the floor, and he stared at his trainers with an unseeing gaze. ŇHowÉÓ He swallowed. ŇHow did you even find out that he knows?Ó he asked. Snape let out a frustrated sound. ŇDraco approached me yesterday and told me of theÉ episode you had on Monday night,Ó he said. ŇHe implied that our lesson had actually made the problem worse.Ó Harry looked up, startled. ŇHe didnŐt sound concerned for you, Potter,Ó Snape continued. ŇIt sounded far more like aÉ status report.Ó ŇThatŐs because he knows youŐre a Death Eater,Ó Harry responded before he could stop himself. ŇPrecisely,Ó Snape said. ŇNo matter how close you appear to have become with him, you should never lose sight of where DracoŐs loyalties truly lie.Ó ŇSo you think he only told you because he thinks youŐreÉ fattening me up for VoldemortŐs mental attacks?Ó ŇDo not say his name!Ó Snape spat. ŇWell, you should know,Ó Harry plowed on, Ňthat Draco also told me you were a Death Eater.Ó Snape seemed to freeze, and his eyes bore into Harry. ŇAnd what did you tell him in response?Ó Harry scowled. ŇIf youŐre asking if I told him youŐre a spy - I didnŐt tell him,Ó he said. ŇI havenŐt told any Slytherins about that. But I think it says a lot about DracoŐs loyalties if heŐs warning me about you.Ó Snape didnŐt respond, and Harry could have sworn that he actually looked shocked. ŇIs it possible that Draco came to you more as our mutual head-of-house?Ó Harry asked. ŇHeŐs also the one that sent for you after that vision I had before the holidays.Ó SnapeŐs lips curled unpleasantly. ŇYou sound as if youŐre searching for a different explanation for DracoŐs betrayal of you.Ó ŇHe didnŐt betray me,Ó Harry said. ŇHe betrayed you.Ó Even as he said it, something wriggled in the back of HarryŐs mind. It wasnŐt doubt about DracoŐs loyalties; theyŐd had enough conversations about Draco doubting VoldemortŐs cause for Harry to know exactly how conflicted Draco was about the impending war. Instead, it was the sheer shock that Harry was now defending DracoŐs loyalties. Their relationship had shifted, and Harry was astounded at how natural it felt, and how sure he was. Harry shook his head and sighed. ŇDraco has told me more than I think youŐd believe,Ó he said. ŇHe doesnŐt want to become a Death Eater, but he thinks his father is going to force him to.Ó SnapeŐs eyes widened at that. ŇAnd IŐm not stupid, despite whatever you think,Ó Harry said. ŇI havenŐt revealed any Order secrets to Draco or any other Slytherin.Ó ŇAside from these lessons,Ó Snape replied with a scowl. ŇThat wasÉÓ Harry sighed, his eyes dropping to the floor again. ŇHonestly, it was almost an accident. He gave me a piece of lepidolite for Yule, and -Ó ŇThat is rather... apropos,Ó Snape said quietly, and Harry glanced up in surprise at his tone of voice. ŇBoth for help in clearing your mind and for yourÉ journey on the dark path.Ó ŇDraco is interested in making sure I succeed in my declaration,Ó Harry said, and then he narrowed his eyes. ŇAnd so are you, but you havenŐt said why.Ó Once again, Snape took on that strange, searching expression, as if he were dissecting Harry with his eyes alone. ŇSo why are you?Ó Snape sneered. ŇAny sane dark wizard would welcome your declaration for the dark, because your declaration would be nothing but good for the dark.Ó HarryŐs gaze never wavered. ŇBut not good for the Ôdark lord.ŐÓ The words felt strange in HarryŐs mouth. SnapeŐs lips twitched before settling into his familiar scowl. ŇYou seem to be moreÉ aware of the possible outcome of your declaration than I expected you to be.Ó If Harry didnŐt know any better, he could have sworn that statement was something almost resembling a compliment. Perhaps HarryŐs relationship with Snape was shifting, as well. Even so, Snape immediately decided to actually begin the Occlumency lesson, which was just as terrible as the first. *** The days seemed to fly by faster than Harry could truly believe, and he suspected that it had something to do with the impending O.W.L.s. Blaise in particular seemed to grow more stressed by the day, even though the tests were still months away. Harry was beginning to feel the pressure, as well, as every free moment of his time seemed to be taken up by homework, Quidditch practice, and Occlumency lessons. Despite Harry and Snape somehow managing to have somewhat civil conversations - though not without their usual snipes and barbs - Occlumency was still HarryŐs least favorite part of his week. He still didnŐt understand what Snape meant by Ôclearing his mind,Ő and Harry was still dreaming of the corridors and the Department of Mysteries. The lessons didnŐt seem to be helping at all. His scar prickled more and more each day, and he continued to feel strange lurches of foreign emotion - smug satisfaction in the middle of Divination, anger after a particularly good Quidditch practice, and in one of the more bizarre occurrences, amusement during a particularly boring History of Magic class. Even as distracted as Harry was, he kept finding his thoughts returning to his place in the war. His housemates, Andromeda, and even Snape had all implied how HarryŐs declaration for the dark could potentially shift the outcome of the war, making it unfavorable for Voldemort. In other words, if Harry was known to be a dark wizard, he could present a new rallying point for dark witches and wizards. And on the other side of that, Hermione wanted him to make sure the public was fully aware of VoldemortŐs return; in other words, to rally all of the other witches and wizards. Harry was certain those other witches and wizards wouldnŐt be very receptive to him being a dark wizard. Blaise was right - Harry was forming another side in the war, whether he liked it or not. Every single action Harry had taken that year was leading towards it. But Harry didnŐt feel like he was making a new side; he felt like he was taking a side. And it was the side opposite of all of his old friends - Hermione, Neville, GinnyÉ Ron. Despite their friendship fracturing, Harry still didnŐt want to oppose Ron in a possible war. But he couldnŐt give up the dark. Harry knew that. He felt it down to his bones. He had an inherent need to walk the dark path, regardless of what he was going to leave behind. Two weeks into the term, Harry took the next step on the path. *** HarryŐs invisibility cloak offered no protection from the elements, and the air was bitterly cold. The wind stung his cheeks and hands before he finally remembered to cast a warming charm. He was certain Draco would be rolling his eyes at him if he knew, and Harry grinned wryly as he drew closer to the shore of the lake. He had chosen the location because it was where he had completed his affinity rite - where he and the dark had spoken, as PansyŐs mother said. Even though the ground was covered in snow and the wind swirled around him, it felt appropriate to perform his first personal rite in the same place as his first dark rite. He removed his bag from his shoulder and set it on the ground beside him. His stomach seemed to do a strange little flip, but not for the reason heŐd expected. HeŐd been certain that he was going to feel nervous - it was the first dark rite he would perform without company, after all - but the only thing he could really feel was excitement. He knelt down in the cold snow, reaching into his bag to remove the two black pillar candles, as well as his small dagger. Leaving the dagger in his lap, he set the candles on the ground in front of him, SiriusŐs to the left and DracoŐs to the right, and then brandished his wand. He reached over the candles and touched the tip of his wand to the ground, taking a deep breath in and out before he began. ŇI call down the dark to manifest. Protect me and guide me,Ó he whispered as he drew his wand around him, clockwise, drawing a faint circle in the snow. ŇUna cum nobis. I beg for its presence as I take this first step on the dark path.Ó He had to pass his wand from his right hand to his left behind his back, and he continued drawing the circle around him. ŇObsecro te venire. Mecum, tueri, dirige. Offero tibi sanguinem.Ó As his wand completed the circle, Harry felt something that now seemed familiar - dark magic falling into place, completely surrounding him. It even seemed to block the wind and the cold as it swirled around him, and he let out a little contented sigh. ŇA circle has no beginning and it never ends,Ó Harry continued. ŇI seek to know that magic. I seek to know that strength.Ó The dark magic around him seemed to pulse in answer, and Harry smiled before looking down at the candles in front of him. ŇTwo wizards guide me down this path,Ó Harry said, pointing his wand at the candle Sirius had given him. ŇThe mentor: Sirius Black.Ó He pictured Sirius in his mindŐs eye: tousled black hair, grey eyes, a haunting gauntness set into his cheeks. The candle let out a spark and then ignited, and Harry moved his wand to the second candle. ŇThe peer: Draco Malfoy.Ó Harry found it was easier to picture Draco in his mind: blond hair so pale it was nearly white, lips curled into an arrogant, playful smirk, and the same grey eyes as Sirius; it must have been a family trait. DracoŐs candle also flared to life, and the two candles cast a warm glow on HarryŐs tiny circle. Harry bit his lip. This was one of the parts that he had to improvise; he wasnŐt a Black, and he wasnŐt interested in explaining how devoted he was to blood purity. He knew Sirius had said that speaking words that meant something to him would work much better than following the Black family rites to the letter, anyway, but Harry was still worried that he could mess up the rite by saying the wrong thing. ŇSirius and Draco are both of Black blood,Ó Harry finally continued. ŇI am not a Black. IÉÓ He paused, taking a breath. ŇIŐm not even from a dark family. But the darkness still spoke to me, and these two dark wizards agreed to accept me for who I am and where I came from, as well as devote themselvesÉÓ HarryŐs voice unexpectedly caught in his throat at that, but he continued. Ň... as well as devote themselves to my journey. ŇAnd I devote myself to this journey,Ó Harry said. ŇI accept their guidance, as well as the guidance of other Blacks before me.Ó He smirked at that, thinking of Andromeda. ŇAnd I would accept the guidance of the dark, if it will have me.Ó The two candles suddenly flared up, the flames doubling in size before receding. Harry let out a relieved smile before picking up the dagger from his lap. He grasped it in his right hand and held out his left, palm up. ŇMy blood isnŐt pure,Ó Harry whispered. ŇIŐm the son of a pureblood and a Muggleborn, and my blood is just as red as any pureblood.Ó He paused and set his teeth, bracing himself as he set the blade against his palm. And then he froze. Blood of the enemy, forcibly takenÉ The graveyard flashed in front of his eyes, seemingly out of nowhere. Harry gasped at the force of the memory and he yanked the knife away from his hand. He found himself taking in breath after breath, suddenly feeling like he wasnŐt getting enough air. His blood had already been used in a dark ritual. Harry knew this -had known this - but the feel of the blade against his skin had suddenly made it all come rattling back. Why was it affecting him now? Harry had already used blood magic back in the beginning of the school year. It was even the same dagger. HeŐd been fine; he hadnŐt even thought about the ritual at the time. But his blood was the reason Voldemort was back. His blood had been the catalyst that brought about VoldemortŐs resurrection. Harry curled forward, hunching in on himself, and tried desperately to get his breathing under control. Suddenly, dark magic reached out and seemed to lick at his skin, gently comforting him. It wrapped around him and he found himself relaxing in its embrace. This time, itŐs not being taken, Harry found himself thinking. ItŐs being given. Harry breathed in and out a few times, steadying himself, before he sat back up and slowly placed the dagger back on his palm. ŇBlood is life,Ó he said, his voice determined and his jaw set. He dragged the blade down his palm diagonally and blood instantly began welling up. He held himself steady as he switched the dagger to his bleeding hand, repeating the action on his right hand. He placed the dagger on the ground and then held his hands out in front of him, squeezing them into fists. Blood dripped down in between the two candles, red slowly pooling in a stark contrast against the pristine white snow. ŇBlood is life, and life is magic,Ó he whispered. He unclenched his fists, his hands now completely coated in red, and picked up the candles. ŇI ask the dark to take my blood and to help it blossom.Ó He tilted the candles towards the center and black wax poured out on top of the tiny red pool. ŇDabo meum sanguinem forma.Ó The effect was almost instant - the black wax, his red blood, and the white snow swirled together, rising up from the ground. The colors twisted and coiled together, at first mixing together quite evenly, but then the white disappeared, followed by the black, leaving only red. The swirling mixture began to slow and it began to gain form, and then it finally settled into a cylindrical shape before it floated back down to the ground. A red pillar candle, identical in shape to the black candles in his hands, stood in front of him, and Harry let out a wild grin. HeŐd done it correctly. ŇThank you,Ó he said, and his smile widened. ŇI will return to you, when I come to dedicate my magic. I hope you will accept it and nurture it as you have with my blood.Ó He blew out SiriusŐs candle, then DracoŐs. ŇUntil we speak again.Ó Ignoring the fact that his hands were still dripping red, he set down the black candles and reached out to pick up the red one, feeling somewhat protective of it. The candle was something like a talisman; it would both accompany him and represent him as he walked the dark path. He glanced at the other two candles. The hollowed divots where the wax had melted still glistened in the moonlight. HarryŐs red candle would soon be black, just like SiriusŐs and DracoŐs. Harry felt an odd sense of pride as he began gathering his things, carefully stuffing them into his bag. He pulled out two handkerchiefs and wrapped them around his bleeding palms. Just like the woman heŐd seen at Solstice, who had performed the blood rite for fertility, he had to let the cuts on his hands heal naturally. Harry supposed it was likely a common part of blood magic; after all, if you instantly healed the injury, it wasnŐt truly a sacrifice. He finally got to his feet and scrubbed out the evidence of his circle with his foot. His footprints would still be all around, of course, but he left no evidence that a dark ritual had been performed. He threw his invisibility cloak over his head and began the trek back up to the castle. Harry quickly found that there was a new spring in his step, and it didnŐt take him long to realize why. It wasnŐt just pride and excitement he was feeling; he felt like he was more at peace than heŐd been in a very long time. The guilt over his inaction on Voldemort, the fear about his recent visions, his anger towards Dumbledore, along with everything else that had happened in the recent months - it all seemed muted. He still felt all of those things, of course, but he also had the odd sense that it was all going to be okay. He was on the right path. Chapter End Notes I'm baaaack. The move is over, and my new job is going all right. Due to the nature of the job (and because I started at the busiest time of the year) I will not be able to crank out a new chapter every other day like I was before, but I will still try to keep up a healthy pace. That said, the move is over!
