ŇItŐs Sunday, and we canŐt even enjoy the day off from classes.Ó ŇWe all agreed that weŐd spend the day in the library,Ó Tracey said. ŇNo going back on your word now.Ó ŇBesides, itŐs completely frightening outside,Ó Daphne added. ŇWhat could you possibly do with your free time if we werenŐt revising?Ó ŇIŐd go flying,Ó Harry said wistfully. ŇI love flying in the snow.Ó ŇThatŐs not snow,Ó Blaise quipped. ŇThatŐs a blizzard.Ó Harry peered at the ceiling of the hall, where the enchantment allowed them to see just how nasty the weather had turned overnight. The sky seemed to be completely white with how heavy the snow fell. A light kick to his shin tore HarryŐs attention away from the sky, and he shot Draco a playful glare. ŇWhat?Ó ŇYou and I wonŐt spend the whole day revising,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇYou still need to tell me how last night went.Ó Blaise suddenly perked up. ŇThatŐs right! I forgot you did the first rite yesterday.Ó Harry sighed. ŇYou guys were still up when I got back,Ó he said. ŇI told you it went fine.Ó ŇBut I want details,Ó Draco said with a whine. ŇAnd as your peer, I should be getting them.Ó ŇAnd I want details because IŐm nosy,Ó Blaise added, grinning. Harry rolled his eyes. ŇLater,Ó he said. ŇRight now IŐm mentally preparing myself for my brain to start leaking out of my ears from all the revising weŐre about to do today.Ó ŇYou paint such a lovely picture, Harry,Ó Pansy said, her nose wrinkling in disgust, and Harry laughed. Despite the bleak prospect of spending their entire free day in the library, Harry felt like he was in better spirits than he had been all term. The news of the Azkaban breakout had put a damper on his mood, especially after word had started spreading throughout the school. A handful of students had approached him to tell him that they now believed him after seeing the news about the escaped Death Eaters, but Harry had no idea what to say in response; he couldn't risk word getting back to Umbridge, but he also didnŐt want to deny the truth. He wound up saying nothing at all, and it left him feeling confused and guilty. He found himself second guessing his actions, wondering if he should have taken Hermione up on her offer of doing an interview - Umbridge be damned. But the completion of the first rite had put a bounce in his step that had been missing for weeks, despite his turmoil over everything else. In an odd kind of unspoken agreement, Harry and the other Slytherins dawdled over breakfast. They all knew they were procrastinating before finally heading up to the library for the day, but none of them would admit it. When it finally seemed like they wouldnŐt be able to drag their feet any longer, they reluctantly headed towards the doors of the hall. They made their way to the library, and Harry paused at one of the halls. ŇIŐve got to use the loo before we settle in,Ó he said. ŇYouŐre just delaying the inevitable, Harry!Ó Daphne said teasingly. ŇOr IŐve actually got to take a piss,Ó Harry replied with a roll of his eyes. ŇOh, IŐm sureÉÓ Blaise let out a laugh and waved the others on. ŇIŐll wait. We'll meet the rest of you there,Ó he said. ŇMake sure to get us a good table, preferably in the back.Ó Harry and Blaise turned the corner and headed for the bathroom only to come across Colin Creevey blocking the entrance. Harry hadnŐt spoken to Colin that school year - thankfully .and though Harry was a bit surprised that the kid had finally had a growth spurt, he still barely came up to HarryŐs shoulder. Colin had propped the door open with his foot, and his ever-present camera was glued to his face. It was pointed at a slightly downward angle, as if he was trying to take a picture of the floor. ŇMorning, Colin,Ó Harry said. ŇÔScuse us.Ó ŇHi, Harry,Ó Colin responded without glancing up from his camera. ŇHang on. ThereŐs a crack in the stone that looks like a lion, and I want toÉÓ He trailed off as he adjusted the lens. Blaise sneered and opened his mouth, but only let out a grunt as Harry elbowed him in the stomach. As annoying as he could be, Colin didnŐt deserve BlaiseŐs scathing insults. ŇColin,Ó Harry said in what he felt was a reasonable tone. ŇWeŐve got to use the loo.Ó ŇAlmost got it!Ó Colin didn't budge. A wave of irritation suddenly bubbled up inside Harry and before he even realized what he was doing, he reached out and snatched ColinŐs camera out of his hands before forcefully shoving Colin to the side of the doorway. ŇMove,Ó Harry snapped. Colin winced as the back of his head hit the doorjamb, and then he gawked at Harry with wide eyes. Harry felt just as shocked at his actions as Colin looked, and Colin continued staring at Harry in surprise and what seemed like a hint of fear. Blaise finally broke the awkward silence. ŇExcuse us,Ó he said, his voice dripping with disdain. The fear on ColinŐs face became more pronounced, and HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇUh,Ó Harry stuttered as Blaise pushed past them. ŇHere.Ó He held out the camera and after a beat of hesitation, Colin took it. Before Harry could apologize, Colin bolted down the hall. Harry stared after him, feeling guilty, but also incredibly alarmed at what he'd just done. It wasnŐt like he needed to go to the loo that badly, and he didn't think he had it in himself to shove people over something so trivial. And while Colin was a tad annoying, he didnŐt deserve to be physically pushed simply because heŐd been trying to take a photograph. ŇHas he always been like that?Ó Blaise asked snidely as Harry finally followed him into the bathroom. ŇUh, yeah,Ó Harry said quietly. Blaise turned his back as Harry relieved himself. ŇSo if heŐs always been like that,Ó Blaise said, Ňare you always like that with him?Ó Harry winced. It seemed Blaise knew him well enough to know that it hadnŐt been like him; he wouldnŐt be asking otherwise. Harry didnŐt respond, but his silence appeared to be enough of an answer for Blaise. ŇSo you were just channeling Draco?Ó Despite himself, Harry let out a laugh. ŇNo.Ó But Blaise was right - shoving people was more like Draco - or McLaggen - than Harry. Harry finished up, readjusted his cloak, and headed to the sink. After a moment of silence, Blaise continued. ŇThe Dark Lord, then?Ó Harry jerked his head up, his eyes meeting BlaiseŐs in the mirror over the sink. ŇIÉÓ Harry swallowed, then shook his head. ŇI donŐt think so.Ó ŇWhy donŐt you think so?Ó Harry had to give credit to Blaise for how steady he kept his voice, especially considering how much HarryŐs connection with Voldemort seemed to frighten Blaise. ŇMy scar didnŐt hurt,Ó Harry responded. ŇIt usually does whenÉÓ He trailed off. BlaiseŐs reflection smirked at him. ŇSo that was purely just your impressive temper.Ó Harry let out a noise of frustration. ŇMerlin, Blaise - stop!Ó he snapped, whipping away from the mirror to face Blaise directly, his lips curled into a snarl. Blaise blinked, and then his smirk widened. Harry sighed, turning back to the sink. He scooped up some of the cool water and splashed it on his suddenly warm face. ŇDonŐt get me wrong, Harry,Ó Blaise said. ŇI have no complaints about you shoving annoying little Gryffindors.Ó ŇBut thatŐs not like me,Ó Harry said quietly. ŇAre you sure?Ó ŇWhat?Ó ŇYou do have a temper,Ó Blaise said, crossing his arms. ŇIŐve seen you unleash it more times than I can count. You just usually make the walls your victims." ŇSo?Ó Harry said, frowning. Frustratingly, Blaise only smirked again. ŇIŐm just sayingÉ that mightŐve been all you.Ó Harry didnŐt want to dignify that with a response, and a sliver of worry began worming its way under HarryŐs skin. Even though Harry had been so sure of declaring dark, he still had fears about it. He knew that the possibility of his temper getting worse than it already had been was a distinct possibility, but he didnŐt think anything would actually change until he officially declared. Besides, Harry had been half-hoping that his recent short fuse had actually been VoldemortŐs influence, rather than Harry, himself. ŇAre you ready yet?Ó Blaise asked, interrupting HarryŐs thoughts. ŇOr are you still having a crisis?Ó Another sudden pop of irritation swept through Harry, and he whipped out his wand. Before Blaise could even blink, Harry had pointed his wand at the stream of water coming from the faucet and redirected it towards BlaiseŐs face. ŇHey!Ó Blaise spluttered, ducking away as the water hit him dead on. ŇWhat the bloody -Ó ŇQuit being annoying,Ó Harry snapped. ŇOr youŐre going to get more of my Ňimpressive temper.ŐÓ To his surprise, Blaise just laughed before turning to leave. Harry made to follow him but then immediately stopped in his tracks. Colin had been right. There was a crack in the stone that looked just like a lion. But just to the side of it was another series of fractures. It was uncannily shaped like a jaw ready to swallow the lion whole, fangs extended. The jaw was connected to another long, serpentine fissure that seemed to extend across the bathroom. It was long and curving, like a worm, orÉ Harry hurried after Blaise, deciding not to spare the cracks on the floor another thought. *** In a somewhat hilarious coincidence, Blaise and Harry found the other Slytherins in the library watching the spectacle Draco was creating as he scathingly insulted a few second-year Hufflepuffs, apparently in an effort to get the table Draco wanted. ŇYouŐre just now learning about aconite? ThatŐs a first-year topic, and a basic one at that,Ó Draco was saying, hovering over one of the little Hufflepuffs threateningly. ŇIf you havenŐt learned it by now, you shouldnŐt be at Hogwarts at all.Ó The prissy and posh accent was out to full effect. ŇYou should be spending your time more wisely - perhaps by bathing? You stink." Pansy laughed. Harry let out a quiet groan. It wasnŐt like it was the first time Harry had seen Draco pointlessly picking on kids, but seeing it right after Blaise had compared HarryŐs earlier behavior to this particular aspect of DracoŐs personality made it feel a little raw. ŇWhat do you want?Ó one of the little Hufflepuffs demanded. ŇDraco -Ó Harry spoke up, but Draco ignored him. ŇWe need this table,Ó Draco said haughtily. ŇWeŐre revising for O.W.L.s.Ó He sneered. "Move." ŇWe were here first!Ó the same boy shot back, and Harry had to give him credit for standing his ground. ŇWe can find another table, Draco,Ó Harry said, pushing through his housemates and grasping Draco by the arm. Draco shifted his glare from the Hufflepuffs to Harry. ŇWhy should pathetic excuses for students that are a year behind in their studies get to take the best table in the library?Ó Harry rolled his eyes. ŇItŐs not -Ó ŇWe were finishing up anyway!Ó another one of the little Hufflepuffs exclaimed, and she grabbed her friends by the arms. The three of them scampered, and Harry noticed that they didnŐt head in the direction of the door. Draco scowled at Harry, and Harry frowned right back at him. ŇThat was completely -Ó ŇSlytherins donŐt fight in public, Harry,Ó Pansy said, patting Harry on the shoulder as she took a seat at the now-vacated table. ŇI wasnŐt fighting,Ó Harry said. The two of them kept trying to outdo each other, and when the charm of the confection wore off they would wind up crashing violently to the ground. Harry had to admit it was nice to see the normally reserved Draco wildly flailing as he hit the floor. Harry still had a hard time stomaching it when it appeared that Draco was bullying someone, and he hated the thought that he was capable of the same. As Harry observed the others having their fun, his thoughts kept wandering back to what heŐd done. While it thankfully wasnŐt a huge crowd, there were still enough students to cause worry. ŇWe saw the whole thing.Ó ŇThen how do you explain Theo?Ó Blaise shot back at the Ravenclaw. Harry spun on his heel, BlaiseŐs words reminding him of their housemate. Theo lay prone on the ground, apparently having lost consciousness at some point. ŇIs he okay?Ó Harry asked, kneeling down beside Blaise. ŇI donŐt know,Ó Blaise said, shaking his head. ŇÔSelf-defenseŐ means defending against an active attack,Ó the Ravenclaw continued snootily. ŇHe said his skin was on fire,Ó Harry said, ignoring the RavenclawŐs words. Blaise reached out and placed a hand on TheoŐs forehead. ŇHeŐs not on fire,Ó Blaise said, his voice shaking. ŇHeŐs cold.Ó ŇHe...,Ó Harry swallowed nervously and then twisted around, looking straight at McGonagall. ŇProfessor! Theo needs help!Ó ŇI quite agree,Ó McGonagall said from where she was inspecting Applebee. ŇBoth of these students need to go to the hospital wing immediately.Ó She stood and with a wave of her wand, ApplebeeŐs body started floating beside her. ŇYou, Potter - with me.Ó Her expression was more stern than Harry had ever seen it, if that was even possible. ŇYour behavior was completely uncalled for, self-defense or not.Ó She waved her wand a second time to levitate Theo alongside Applebee. Harry got to his feet, and his anxiety over whatever was about to happen started to feel almost overwhelming. What would McGonagall do if she figured out what spell Harry had used? Shit. McGonagall continued with her lecture, even while she concentrated on keeping two unconscious students in the air. ŇYou are aware that there are far more acceptable ways of disarming an opponent-Ó ŇI will take care of Potter, Professor McGonagall,Ó said a familiar voice from behind Harry. That voice almost always sent his skin crawling, but for the first time in his life, Harry found himself relieved at hearing it. ŇAs his head of house, I believe PotterŐsÉ misbehavior is my responsibility.Ó Harry slowly turned to look at Snape. He couldnŐt believe his luck, and he meant that without a lick of sarcasm. Snape, of all freaking professors, was the best possible staff member to take him in the immediate aftermath of what had just happened. That was, quite possibly, the most bizarre, surreal feeling Harry had encountered all year. Snape was sure to still punish him for what Harry had done, but he could almost certainly find a way to prevent Harry from getting arrested for using dark arts on a fellow student. And since Snape seemed to be so invested in Harry declaring dark, Harry had a feeling that he was no longer looking for excuses to expel Harry. Snape would help him. Harry was sure of it. As Snape grabbed HarryŐs arm and began dragging him down the hallway, Harry mentally kicked himself for needing SnapeŐs help in the first place. ŇIŐm an idiot,Ó Harry said quietly. Snape didnŐt look at him, but the grip on HarryŐs arm tightened until it was like a vice. ŇWe are in agreement on that, Potter.Ó They made the rest of the long walk back to the dungeons in silence, which left Harry to his thoughts. HeŐd used two spells on Applebee. One was dark, and the other neutral. And now that he was safe in the hands of Snape - which was just so damn strange to be thinking - his anxiety was giving way to annoyance. The more Harry thought about the possible consequences of someone revealing his use of the dark arts, the more ridiculous the persecution of the dark arts seemed. After all, it was the neutral spell -Expulso - that had clearly caused actual harm. The dark spell heŐd used was, as Sirius had called it, a pantsing hex. Harry had risked going to Azkaban over pulling someoneŐs pants down. He really was a bloody idiot. And the fact that he could have gone to prison over that just didnŐt make sense to Harry. When they reached SnapeŐs office, Snape shoved Harry inside and slammed the door behind them. Harry braced himself for the beratement of a lifetime. Instead, Snape was silent for a moment, once again setting that odd, contemplative stare on Harry. ŇWhat spells did you use?Ó he finally asked. ŇCarinitus and Expulso,Ó Harry responded quietly. SnapeŐs eyes rolled upward and he muttered something that Harry couldnŐt quite make out. ŇDraw your wand,Ó Snape commanded. Harry blinked, then did as Snape ordered. ŇI am going to teach you a spell,Ó Snape said. ŇIt is called the Enlargement Charm, and itŐs one that I am quite certain youŐre unfamiliar with as it isnŐt taught until NEWT level Transfiguration.Ó He narrowed his eyes. ŇBut as McGonagall has beenÉ impressed with the recent improvement in your work, IŐm certain sheŐll believe that you somehow learned it on your own.Ó Harry was confused, but as Snape had essentially just rescued him from probable arrest, he wasnŐt sure asking questions would be wise. Snape placed a small jar on his desk and demonstrated the spell, which enlarged the small jar to the size of a pitcher. The spell, though NEWT level, was far easier than learning the Patronus charm in third year or the summoning charm from fourth year. It took Harry nearly ten minutes to cast it successfully. ŇCresceretus!Ó Harry grinned in satisfaction as the jar finally grew. Despite the circumstances, he always enjoyed getting a new spell to work. Snape, Harry realized, was far better at teaching spells than he was at teaching Potions. Harry wisely didnŐt say so. Snape, as expected, didnŐt offer any kind of praise for HarryŐs work. ŇNow, cast several spells that you use daily,Ó he said. ŇLumos, Calidium - and then perhaps throw in a few spells that youŐve been practicing for classes. Cast at least twenty.Ó Harry stared at Snape for a moment, and as SnapeŐs lips started curling in displeasure at not being immediately obeyed, Harry quickly began casting lighting spells and warming charms and multiplication charms. As he was casting, it suddenly occurred to Harry why Snape was having him do this. He needed to write a new spell history on his wand. ŇYou think someone will actually cast Priori Incantato on my wand?Ó Harry asked when he finished, leaning over and resting his hands on his knees. Even as simple as the spells were, he was unaccustomed to casting so many spells over such a short period of time. ŇNo,Ó Snape said. ŇBut it would not be wise to assume that someone would fail to do so.Ó He paused and his eyes narrowed. ŇAnd by ÔsomeoneŐ I mean Headmaster Dumbledore.Ó Harry nodded. ŇI know,Ó he said quietly. ŇWhile ÔCarinitusŐ isnŐt likely to be enough to send for aurors, it would still... alert the headmaster to your recent studies,Ó Snape said. Harry looked up in surprise. ŇSo even though itŐs dark, that spell isnŐtÉÓ ŇItŐs still technically illegal,Ó Snape said. ŇBut itŐs such a minor dark hex that the Ministry likely wouldnŐt bother with prosecuting it. Besides,Ó he continued, ŇitŐs also obscure enough that only wizards who have studied the dark arts would be familiar with it. ŇI didnŐt.Ó ŇThen why did you ask me what spells IŐd used?Ó Snape rolled his eyes once more. ŇIt is a question I ask all of my Slytherins in situations like these,Ó he said. ŇThey know to tell me the truth.Ó He paused, his upper lip curling. ŇThankfully, you have apparently become Slytherin enough to know that telling me the truth will always be the wisest course of action.Ó Harry stared at Snape in surprise. Being ÔSlytherin enoughŐ was damn near a compliment coming from Snape. And he supposed SnapeŐs other words made sense. He couldnŐt have been the only dark wizard in Slytherin to have let a dark spell loose without thinking. ŇYou need to cast two more spells,Ó Snape continued. ŇThe first is Cresceretus, once more,Ó he said, gesturing to the jar on his desk. ŇIf you are questioned by anyone besides me concerning the spells you used, you will state that you enlarged ApplebeeŐs pants, which is what caused them to fall down.Ó Harry wanted to laugh at that but he wisely did not, instead obediently casting the spell. ŇNow Expulso,Ó Snape said. ŇOn what?Ó Harry asked. He wasnŐt keen on destroying anything in SnapeŐs office. Snape summoned the cushion from his chair, levitating it in front of Harry. Harry sent the cushion flying back against the wall, and then looked back to Snape. They stared at each other for a few long, awkward moments. ŇDetention with me for three weeks, Potter,Ó Snape finally said. HarryŐs eyes widened. Snape had been so immensely helpful over the last half hour that heŐd nearly forgotten who he was actually dealing with. *** ŇThree weeks?Ó Draco said incredulously. ŇAre you serious?Ó ŇSnape does still hate me, in case you forgot,Ó Harry said. After all, no one could piss him off as much as Snape. Theo would be okay, theyŐd learned. Applebee had hit him with a basic frozen fire hex, which was easily countered. TheyŐd heard that Applebee had also woken up; Harry had been right that heŐd only been knocked out when Harry hit him with expulso. Harry picked at his lunch unhappily and once again wondered why his temper had been so volatile of late. First Colin, then Applebee. Harry desperately wanted to figure it out before he exploded on anyone else without thinking. ŇThere are all sorts of interesting rumors flying around about you now, Harry,Ó Pansy said, smirking. ŇYouŐre quite welcome, Mr. Potter.Ó *** The word of Tasmin ApplebeeŐs expulsion spread quickly. It was second year all over again, only this time, Harry thought, it mightŐve been worse. After all, heŐd at least had being in Gryffindor going for him at the time. Facing the rumors as a Slytherin felt completely different. Harry knowing he could actually be a danger to those around certainly didnŐt help him feel any better. Whispers followed Harry wherever he went. Fortunately for him, so did the Slytherins. *** Hermione finally managed to corner Harry on Friday afternoon by waiting just outside the Great Hall before dinner. ŇI think youŐd enjoy it.Ó *** When they broke the Imbolc circle the following night, the fifth-year Slytherins immediately began hurrying back to the castle, eager to get out of the cold night air. Harry hung towards the back of the group, finding that he wasnŐt really in the mood to join in. Draco soon sidled up to him, shaking powdery snow from his hair. ŇAre you okay?Ó Draco asked. Harry shrugged. ŇFor the most part, yeah,Ó he responded. ŇI might not have known you all that well in the past, but I feel like you likely wouldnŐt have done that before.Ó Harry tried to picture himself as a Gryffindor protecting the younger Slytherins, as he had protected Astoria, and he was surprised to find that he was having trouble even picturing himself as a Gryffindor. ŇMaybe I shouldnŐt have argued with the hat back in first year,Ó Harry said quietly. Draco let out a laugh and opened his mouth to respond. ŇWhat are you two dragging your feet for?Ó BlaiseŐs voice cut Draco off as he marched away from the other fifth years and towards the two of them. ŇItŐs freezing!Ó Blaise swooped in behind Harry and Draco and started shoving both of them towards the castle. ŇHave your heart to heart inside, for MerlinŐs sake.Ó This led to a three-way scuffle in the snow, where both Harry and Draco were at first trying to dump Blaise into a snowbank, but it soon became Ôall men for themselvesŐ when Draco hit Harry in the back of the head with a snowball. Their game lacked rules or goals, but they were all laughing by the time they reached the steps to the castle. A group of Ravenclaws traveled up the stairs in front of them, and Harry suddenly remembered his conversation with Hermione the previous day. ŇHermione wants to observe an Ostara celebration,Ó Harry said. ŇWhat?!Ó Both Blaise and Draco spoke at the same time. ŇIÉ why?Ó Blaise asked. ŇIs it because sheŐs suspicious of you?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇI donŐt think so. SheŐs not trying to come with us; she mentioned talking to Terry Boot.Ó ŇAÉ a Muggleborn wants to see wizarding tradition?Ó Draco asked skeptically. ŇWhy is that so surprising?Ó Harry asked. ŇMuggleborns generallyÉ donŐt,Ó Blaise said. ŇBut I suppose Granger has always been... curious.Ó ŇTheyŐre just... usually more interested in keeping hold of their Muggle traditions,Ó Draco said. ŇHas anyone ever tried inviting Muggleborns?Ó Harry asked, knowing full well that Blaise and Draco never had. ŇHow do you know theyŐre not interested?Ó He shook his head. ŇI mean, Hermione didnŐt even know about the cyclical days until this year. How would you know what Muggleborns are interested in if they donŐt even know about them?Ó Draco opened and then closed his mouth, scowling. ŇShe wants to know more,Ó Harry said. ŇShe said sheŐs really interestedÉÓ He trailed, blinking. ŇHuh.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Blaise asked. Harry had finally figured why HermioneŐs words the previous day sounded so familiar. ŇCould Hermione have a dark affinity?Ó he asked abruptly. Blaise and Draco glanced at each other. ŇWhat makes you ask that?Ó Blaise said slowly, as if he was choosing each word carefully. ŇSheÉ she sounds like I did when I first started studying the dark arts,Ó Harry said. ŇI mean, I know I learned about the dark arts first, and then the traditions, butÉ she genuinely seems really interested in it.Ó ŇLike I said earlier,Ó Blaise quipped, ŇGranger has always been curious.Ó ŇBut thereŐs never been a dark Muggleborn,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇThat you know of,Ó Harry said. ŇBesides, you also thought that only purebloods had dark affinitiesÉ up until me.Ó Draco and Blaise didnŐt say anything in response to that, but Harry didnŐt miss the thoughtful look that came over both of them. *** The following weekend was the Quidditch match against Ravenclaw. While Slytherin won, the score was far closer than it had been against Gryffindor. The score had been tied when Harry caught the snitch, but he didnŐt feel he had a choice; Chang was more than a capable seeker and he couldnŐt risk her getting to it first. Their homework, which had already been daunting, now seemed insurmountable. The closer they got to their O.W.L.s the more work the teachers assigned, and the more stressed Harry and his housemates became. Blaise, in particular, became peculiarly snippy with everyone, in stark contrast to his usual jovial demeanor. On the last night of HarryŐs detentions, Snape decided to throw in an extra Occlumency lesson, citing the fact that Dumbledore did seem suspicious of Harry, after all. HeŐd apparently questioned Snape quite thoroughly about what Harry had done, what Snape thought had set Harry off, and most alarmingly - what spells Harry had used. Harry should have been relieved that Dumbledore hadnŐt just asked Harry himself, but he found himself irritated, instead. It wasnŐt like he needed a reminder that the headmaster was avoiding him. And to make matters worse, HarryŐs Occlumency didnŐt seem to be improving at all. He felt completely terrible after every session with Snape, and he continued to dream of corridors and doors nearly every night. He wondered if it was possible that his odd connection with Voldemort circumvented Occlumency, or if he was just that truly terrible at it. Somehow, between Quidditch, homework, Occlumency, and dreams, Harry still managed to make time to prepare for his second rite. *** Unlike his first rite, where heŐd only felt the excitement of taking the next step on the dark path, Harry felt nervous, along with more than a hint of fear. The second rite was the rite of magic, where he had to completely open himself up to the dark. Considering how dark magic had made him feel during his affinity rite, and again during his rite of intent, Harry wasnŐt sure what would happen to him. Because of that, heŐd given Draco and Blaise explicit directions of where he was going to complete the rite. If he wasnŐt back in the Slytherin dorm by midnight, they would come looking for him. Of course, Harry wasnŐt sure what he was more worried about - losing consciousness to the intensity of dark magic, or losing himself so completely that he cursed the first person he saw. While it was still chilly, Harry was thankful that it wasnŐt as cold as it had been during his first rite, or even as it had been on Imbolc. Warming charms could only do so much, and HarryŐs warming charms kind of sucked, besides. Harry knelt on the ground and began emptying his bag and laying out its contents - two black candles, one red, as well as a small pouch of salt. He placed his red candle directly in front of him, with DracoŐs black candle to the right and SiriusŐs on the left. He placed the pouch in his lap and held out his wand in front of him. He braced himself for whatever was about to happen to him and then began. ŇI call down the dark to manifest. Protect me and guide me,Ó he said, drawing the circle around himself. ŇUna cum nobis. I beg for its presence as I take this next step on the dark path. Obsecro te venire. Mecum, tueri, dirige. Offero tibi veneficia.Ó The dark again fell into place around him, and despite his nervousness, he let out a little sigh of contentment. ŇA circle has no beginning and it never ends,Ó he continued. ŇI seek to know that magic. I seek to know that strength.Ó He then pointed his wand at SiriusŐs candle. ŇMy mentors have guided me this far, but I must take each step on my own.Ó He lit SiriusŐs candle, then DracoŐs. ŇMay they illuminate where my feet land.Ó He then lit his own red candle. He studied the three candles for a long moment, watching the flames dance in the light winter breeze. Then he closed his eyes, took a deep breath, and continued. ŇI only recently discovered the dark,Ó he said. ŇI wasnŐt raised in it, like most dark wizards. But every time the dark reaches out to me, I feel like I belong.Ó His voice caught at that. ŇI donŐt think IŐve ever felt like I truly belonged anywhere. ŇSomething has been drawing me to it, and I donŐt know what it is,Ó he continued. ŇIŐd be lying if I said I didnŐt want to find out eventually. IŐd like to know for sure why I have an affinity for the dark. IŐd like to know why it affects me soÉ deeply.Ó He paused. ŇBut IŐm alsoÉ I think IŐm okay with not knowing... because just being with the dark makes me feelÉÓ He trailed off, as the sensations of his affinity rite and his rite of intent came back to him. ŇItŐs comforting. ItŐs warm. It makes me feel welcome and safe and protected andÉ and loved.Ó He smiled. ŇI donŐt know if itŐs like that for everyone, but thatŐs why I trust the dark.Ó Harry opened his eyes. ŇIŐm willing to let the dark in completely because I trust it.Ó Dark magic seemed to brush his cheek in response, and he let out a shiver that was more pleasant than not. And the words were true. Harry might not have trusted himself, but he trusted the dark implicitly. He set down his wand, then picked up the pouch in his lap and tipped it until there was a tiny pile of salt resting in his hand. He took it and poured it on top of the circle heŐd drawn earlier. Little by little, the tiny granules of salt seemed to glow and then spark, growing in intensity until it seemed as if he were surrounded by small fireworks. The sparks gave way to a gentle purple glow, and Harry picked up and blew out SiriusŐs candle. He stuck his right index finger in the melted wax and quickly brought it to the back of his hand and drew a crescent - the symbol for the moon and the dark. He then picked up DracoŐs candle, blew it out, and used the wax to draw a triangle on the back of his hand - the symbol for fire, his chosen element. He rested his hands on his knees and then braced himself. He had no idea what was about to happen. He took another deep breath, then spoke. ŇIntrare.Ó Before his eyes, the symbols on his hands began to shift and change, forming new shapes, but they never seemed to settle on one form. He recognized some from astronomy class, such as Mercury, Ceres, and Vesta. He also knew that some were runes, having looked at HermioneŐs and BlaiseŐs homework enough times to recognize them, but he didnŐt know their meaning. Most of the symbols, though, were completely unfamiliar to him. The wax kept swirling and changing until Harry couldnŐt hope to keep up with them, and then it hit. He gasped as dark magic suddenly flooded into him in a way it hadnŐt before. It was nothing like his previous encounters with the dark. His vision went black, and his mind seemed to spiral into thoughts heŐd never had before. Seemingly out of nowhere, RonŐs face came to mind and a fury that Harry had never felt suddenly washed over him. It suddenly struck him how laughably easy it would be to make Ron pay for abandoning him and throwing away their friendship. HarryŐs lips curled into a devious smirk as he imagined using one of the spells heŐd learned over the summer; he could curse Ron to be a spider-magnet. And that wasnŐt the least of it. Harry could make Ron hurt even worse than Ron had hurt him in all sorts of creative ways. Dark magic coursed through his entire body, head to toe, fingertip to fingertip. Then DumbledoreŐs face replaced RonŐs in his mindŐs eye, and the rage seemed to grow tenfold. How could Dumbledore know what Harry had faced in the graveyard and then not bother to even speak to him? Harry let out a dark laugh as he realized how easy it would be to gain the headmasterŐs attention now. He wanted to march in DumbledoreŐs office and unleash all of the dark arts heŐd been learning just to spite him; after all, no one disapproved of the dark more than Dumbledore. He could feel the dark in his ears, his eyes, and he swore he could taste it on his tongue. Dumbledore suddenly began to morph and shift, twinkling blue eyes giving way to red. Voldemort laughed, and Harry snarled in response. How he wanted to show Voldemort what heŐd been learning. He wondered what it would feel like to hold Voldemort under a Cruciatus curse just like Voldemort had done to Harry in the graveyard. He wanted to make Voldemort scream in pain. He wanted to know what those screams sounded like. The dark magic was wrapped around every bone and every muscle and every organ, and he wasnŐt entirely sure where he ended and the magic began. And Voldemort wasnŐt the only one he wanted to make scream. The Dursleys appeared, and Harry started laughing again, almost maniacally this time. He had so many ideas for how he could make them pay for how they treated him over the years. He could break every single frail-looking bone in PetuniaŐs body. He could make Vernon light the stove and hold his hands over the flames. And he could make Dudley fall into starvation in the blink of an eye, his weight falling away until all of his skin hung off of his bones like a ragged old coat. He could do all of that and so much more. Maybe there was no separation between him and the dark. Maybe heŐd always been a part of it. His old house - the Gryffindors - would judge him for those desires, as well as the ability to make those desires a reality. They would think him evil just for being capable of those deeds. And Harry suddenly realized that he felt like he was superior to them for being able to access the dark while they couldnŐt, and for having knowledge of something they couldnŐt begin to understand. It was something that Harry didnŐt understand until that very moment. Just because he was capable of those awful things didnŐt mean he would actually commit those acts. He knew where the line was between right and wrong, and he would never cross it. Draco had been right, after all - Harry had free will. And Harry understood, with startling clarity, that he was changing. He wasnŐt who heŐd been the previous year, or even six months ago, and his metamorphosis would continue. It wouldnŐt stop when he completed his journey on the dark path, or when he finished his schooling at Hogwarts. It would be constant. Most importantly, he could steer that change; in fact, he was the only one who could. The only person who could control what he turned into was himself. Yes, heŐd lost control of his temper a few times in recent days, but that was all him. He couldnŐt control what was done to him, but he could figure out how to control himself. He would figure it out. The dark wasnŐt changing him. Or maybe it was; he and the dark were so closely connected that they could have been one and the same. Harry let out a breath of relief, and it was somewhat startling to realize that he could actually feel the air leave his lungs; heŐd been so consumed by dark magic that heŐd lost awareness of his own body. The magic was slowly leaving him, and he came back to himself little by little. When he finally had enough awareness to open his eyes, he stared up at the clear night sky, and he realized he must have fallen backward. He sat up with a gasp, wondering how long heŐd been out. Then he decided he didnŐt really care. The dark had brought out the absolute worst of him and made him face the fact that it was there. HeŐd felt hatred and rage and all sorts of terrible emotions, and heŐd come out the other side with answers to the questions heŐd been asking himself for weeks. He let out a laugh of delight, feeling giddy. *** Harry couldnŐt stop grinning. The second rite, as terrifying as it had been, had made everything so crystal clear - at least when it came to him and the dark. He might change as he walked the dark path. He might change after he declared. But as long as he kept a firm hold his morals, it simply didnŐt matter. After all, sometimes people changed. He actually let out a laugh at that, then quickly covered his mouth when he remembered that he was out after curfew. His chosen element may have been fire, but he was also a Slytherin. Slytherins were water .the element of change. And change was something to be welcomed, not feared. Harry felt good. He wished he could let out a cheer of delight, or whistle tunelessly, or even just skip through the hallways like he was a young child. He wanted to be able to share his joy with someone. Fortunately, he knew that Draco would be thrilled to hear of the epiphany that had come from HarryŐs second rite, and so Harry decided to traverse back to the Slytherin common room as fast as he could. He took off running down the hallway. Since he wore his invisibility cloak, no one had to know that he occasionally threw in a little skip as he went. Then he turned a corner and slammed into someone so hard that they both fell to the ground. Harry screwed his mouth shut to prevent himself from crying out, and a wave of adrenaline coursed through him at the thought that he was about to get caught. HeŐd somehow managed to land with his invisibility cloak still in place, and he quickly sat up, trying to figure out how he could get away. Then he noticed that the person heŐd run into was none other than Hermione Granger, and he couldnŐt help but let out a sigh of relief. ŇHarry?Ó Hermione asked quietly, looking around the hallway. ŇIs that you?Ó Harry yanked his cloak off. ŇThank Merlin itŐs you, Hermione,Ó he said, clambering to his feet and quickly helping Hermione to hers. He grinned wildly at her, and then he suddenly grasped her and spun her around in a strange kind of whirling dance. ŇIf you had been anyone elseÉÓ He laughed. He quickly realized that he mustŐve still been feeling some of the effects of the second rite because Hermione looked completely bewildered at his behavior. Harry released her and tried to wipe the smile off of his face. ŇWhat are you doing out?Ó she asked. ŇI needed to go for a walk,Ó Harry said without a missing a beat, bending over to gather up his cloak. ŇAre youÉ are you going to go all Prefect on me?Ó Hermione frowned. ŇI probably should, but no,Ó she said. ŇWhatŐs that on your hands?Ó she asked, reaching out and grasping one of HarryŐs hands with her own and raising it to inspect .much like sheŐd inspected his hands when they still bore the cuts from the first rite. Harry lowered his eyes to the hand that Hermione was studying and realized that there was still wax affixed to his skin and caked under his fingernails. ŇForgot to wash it off.Ó HermioneŐs gaze immediately flicked from HarryŐs hand to his face, and HarryŐs heart inexplicably sped up. ŇIŐm about to make you angry, and IŐm sorry.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, blinking. ŇAre you gonna turn me in after all?Ó Hermione shook her head. ŇNo. I just wantÉ I need to ask you something, and itŐs not going to make you very happy.Ó HarryŐs heart thundered in his chest. ŇOkay. Shoot.Ó Hermione took a deep breath, and a somber, concerned expression fell over her features. ŇAre youÉÓ She paused, swallowed, and then tried again. ŇAre you messing around with the dark arts?Ó ŇWh-what?Ó Harry asked, his eyes widening and a cold, icy feeling slamming into his chest. She knows. Harry stared at Hermione in very real horror, but then another emotion started creeping in among the panic and terror. Even though she was right, he felt angry. She was absolutely right, but Harry had been so damn careful. The only reason Hermione would ask him that were because of the rumors - not the facts. Even so, Harry still allowed himself to have a sliver of hope. After all, heŐd wondered if it was possible that Hermione could have a dark affinity, just like him. ŇAre youÉ are you seriously hopping on the whole... ÔHarryŐs the next dark lordŐ train, Hermione?Ó he asked, his voice quiet and a tad dangerous. He shook his head in disbelief. He knew it was hypocritical of him to ask that considering Hermione was right, but he just felt betrayed. ŇI can handle it coming from the rest of the school,Ó Harry continued, his voice wavering with barely contained emotion. ŇItŐs not like itŐs the first time IŐve dealt with it. But I canŐt handle it coming from you, Hermione.Ó He took a step away from her, shaking his head. ŇNot from you.Ó ŇItŐs not because of the stupid rumor, Harry!Ó Hermione shot back at him. ŇYouŐre pushing students - attacking students - and youŐve never done anything like that before. ŇAnd that spell you used on Applebee - I know I didnŐt hear ÔCresceretus!Ő ŇIf VoldemortŐs in your head, is heÉÓ she paused, swallowing. ŇIs he doing something to change you?Ó Harry stared at her for a moment, then shook his head. ŇI canŐt believe this,Ó he said. ŇYouŐve beenÉ doing nothing but accuse me of this or that since I was re-sorted to Slytherin,Ó he sneered. ŇI suppose IŐm grateful that you didnŐt completely turn on me like Ron did, but -Ó ŇIŐm just fucking worried about my friend!Ó Hermione shouted suddenly, and it took HarryŐs breath away. Never, in all the time heŐd known her, had he heard Hermione Granger swear. He paused and looked at her, and he could see unshed tears in her eyes, and all of his fury suddenly crashed to the ground. Hermione figured things out. It was what she did. It was one of the many reasons Harry loved her so much. So did he really have any right to be angry that she had figured him out? He didnŐt, he realized. He really, truly didnŐt. But even so, he couldnŐt let her know that she was right. There was too much at stake. He let out a sigh. ŇHermioneÉÓ he said, his voice quiet. ŇVoldemort doesnŐt control me. It doesnŐt work like that. And I donŐt like fighting about this.Ó ŇI donŐt like fighting at all,Ó Hermione said unhappily. ŇYeah,Ó Harry said in agreement. ŇI just wish you wouldÉ trust me.Ó He shook his head. ŇIt really hurts that you donŐt.Ó Even though sheŐs mostly right and IŐm lying about it, Harry thought, it still hurts. ŇHarry, you are one of my bestÉÓ She paused, then pursed her lips. ŇNo, you are my best friend.Ó And that, more than anything else, made HarryŐs heart feel like it was breaking. Because he had to keep lying to her. He couldnŐt risk her telling anyone about what he was doing because he couldnŐt risk someone stopping him from walking the dark path. ŇHarry, IÉÓ She sighed. ŇI donŐt mean toÉ" She paused, fixing Harry with a look that seemed to contain everything Harry loved about his friend. "But justÉÓ ŇYeah?Ó ŇJust promise me that youŐre not fooling around with the dark arts,Ó she said, her voice pleading. ŇPromise me.Ó Harry gave her what he hoped was a comforting smile. ŇHermioneÉÓ he said, enveloping her in a tight hug, ŇI promise that you have nothing to worry about.Ó Even though Harry told himself that his words werenŐt technically a lie, he still didnŐt feel any better about saying it. Chapter End Notes I feel obliged to mention this, especially since a few people have been pretty rough on Hermione in the comments: I absolutely do not condone what Harry is doing to Hermione here. He may feel he has good reason, but he's not doing their friendship any favors. THAT SAID. There is this... thing. This thing will unfold over the next two chapters. And as accurate as some of your wonderful comments have been, nobody has come close to mentioning this thing so I don't think y'all will see it coming. I am very excited to write this thing. Chapter 22:The Gain Chapter Notes See the end of the chapter for notes The year seemed to be flying by faster than it ever had before. Harry thought for certain it had to be the pressure of their upcoming O.W.L.s, but Freya explained to him that it was actually just a part of getting older, saying that her sixth year seemed to have gone twice as fast as fifth despite not having any year-end exams, and now that she was in seventh year she had no idea where the time had gone. Harry liked Freya. After the Solstice she made sure to find time to speak with Harry at least once a week, and Harry found her dry sense of humor refreshing. She was also open with Harry about her Death Eater father, and she also made it clear that she didnŐt share VoldemortŐs ideals. At the same time, though, FreyaŐs best friend was Cassius Warrington, and Freya confirmed HarryŐs suspicions that her friend was a staunch supporter of Voldemort, and that he hoped to join him when the school year was over. SheŐd spent so much time trying to convince him otherwise, but she knew it was hopeless. Harry appreciated the time he spent with her. She approached her entire family situation quite differently than Draco or Theo, often making crude jokes about how her father was nothing but a henchman and how he loved kissing VoldemortŐs feet. Harry soon figured out that it was simply how she dealt with the knowledge of what her father was. Harry was surprised to discover that he was capable of making friends in Slytherin outside of those in his year. He tolerated his Quidditch teammates - Warrington included - but he couldnŐt see himself becoming friends with them. Freya was different. He found himself spending most of the Ostara celebration with Freya. Per tradition, the fifth, sixth, and seventh years came together for one circle, but overall it seemed to be less of a big deal than Samhain or the Solstice. The two equinoxes, Harry remembered, were the cyclical days that were meant to celebrate all magic, but the Slytherins didnŐt seem all that interested in celebrating the light. ŇItŐs also because this is the vernal equinox,Ó Freya explained to Harry as they took a seat on the cool grass. ŇThis is when the light begins overtaking the dark. We tend to be a bit more jovial on Mabon in the fall.Ó ŇDo you think itŐs also because so many of the light traditions have been lost?Ó Harry asked. Freya considered his question. ŇIt could be. ThereŐs no balance anymore, so when it comes to celebrating all magic, it feels like us dark witches and wizards are justÉ going through the motions. Like weŐre just keeping the tradition for the sake of tradition.Ó Harry frowned, wanting to know why that was, but Freya continued before he could ask. ŇWhen are you planning on completing your final declaration?Ó she asked. ŇAre your personal rites going smoothly?Ó ŇBeltane,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd theyŐre going fine. IŐve already completed the first two.Ó ŇReally?Ó WarringtonŐs voice rang out from behind Harry. ŇYou actually are declaring dark, then?Ó Harry glanced over his shoulder, his eyes widening. Warrington took a seat on the grass by Freya and Harry and appeared to study Harry. ŇAnd what are you planning on doing after you declare?Ó he asked. ŇCass, donŐt,Ó Freya said with a slight bite to her tone. ŇI think itŐs a fair question,Ó Warrington said, never removing his eyes from Harry, Ňespecially considering who you are, Potter.Ó He smirked. ŇYou really think the light side will accept a dark wizard in their ranks?Ó Freya sat up, shooting Warrington a glare. ŇCass -Ó ŇIŐm not joining Voldemort, if thatŐs what youŐre asking,Ó Harry spat. ŇYouŐre going to be a dark wizard,Ó Warrington said, as if he was stating the most obvious fact in the world. ŇAnd he is the Dark Lord.Ó ŇCass, stop it,Ó Freya snapped. ŇYou must be joking. Voldemort killed my parents,Ó Harry said, scowling. ŇAnd I hate everything he stands for.Ó ŇExcept for the right to use the dark arts openly again,Ó Warrington said. ŇHeŐs the only one that will protect us dark wizards. If you two were able toÉ put aside your differences -Ó ŇThereŐs not a chance in hell, Warrington,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇI donŐt know why youŐre even bothering to ask.Ó ŇSo you think Dumbledore will protect you, then?Ó Warrington said, his voice dripping with disbelief. ŇWhat do you think will happen when he discovers that youŐve declared dark?Ó ŇI donŐt expect him to protect me,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd I donŐt want him to.Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇCass, go away before I curse you,Ó Freya said. Warrington smirked and got to his feet again. ŇJust think about it, Potter.Ó ŇOh, IŐll be sure to,Ó Harry said sarcastically. Freya glared at WarringtonŐs retreating back before shooting Harry an apologetic look. ŇIŐm sorry about him,Ó she said. ŇI should have expected that to happen eventually. I love him, but heŐs turning into a fanatic.Ó Harry sighed. ŇIf I have to be realistic, it probably wonŐt be the last time I get asked that question.Ó ŇAnd if you have to be realistic, he is also right about one thing,Ó Freya asked. ŇThe light side wonŐt take kindly to you being a dark wizard.Ó ŇI know.Ó ŇMaybe you can just stay neutral.Ó She smirked. ŇYou can join me in the Ôfuck everythingŐ club.Ó Harry shook his head. ŇThatŐs not an option,Ó he said before letting out a bitter laugh. ŇEven if I wanted it to be it wouldnŐt be an option.Ó ŇI figured,Ó Freya said. ŇBut I hope youŐre prepared for what will happen should the wizarding world figure you out.Ó *** ŇWhere are Draco and Pansy?Ó Harry wondered as he and Blaise wrapped up their Transfiguration essays. ŇI thought they were going to be working on these with us.Ó ŇWere they on Prefect duty tonight?Ó Blaise asked. ŇI donŐt think so,Ó Harry said. ŇBesides, they wouldnŐt be patrolling until after curfew.Ó They continued working in silence, and Harry passed his essay over to Blaise when he finally finished. Blaise dutifully began reading through it, making notes and crossing out entire lines. He seemed to be making far more edits than usual, and the more he scribbled, the more despondent Harry became. ŇIs it really that bad?Ó Harry asked, dismayed. ŇHonestly?Ó Blaise asked. ŇYes.Ó He held up HarryŐs essay, using the end of his quill to point at the parchment. ŇThis entire section needs re-written. It sounds like you misinterpreted GampŐs Law.Ó ŇUgh!Ó Harry cried out in frustration and slammed his text shut before pushing up from his seat at the table and launching himself onto a nearby sofa. ŇI hate O.W.L.s.Ó ŇSo youŐve said,Ó Blaise responded. The door to the common room opened and Harry glanced over to see Draco and Pansy step through, speaking hurriedly in hushed tones. ŇWhere have you two been?Ó Blaise asked. ŇI suppose I can understand extra Prefect duties, but -Ó ŇWe were hunting Gryffindors with Umbridge,Ó Draco said as they approached Harry and Blaise. He had a wicked smirk drawn across his face. ŇWhat?Ó Harry said, sitting up in alarm. To his surprise, DracoŐs lips curled unpleasantly and he fixed a nasty glare on Harry. ŇWhat did I lie about?Ó ŇYouŐve been leading us to believe that you werenŐt in DumbledoreŐs pocket anymore.Ó ŇIŐm not, though,Ó Harry responded, confused. ŇNo?Ó he said hesitatingly. ŇI donŐt think so, anyway.Ó ŇSo you knew nothing about the little army that your friend Granger put together, then?Ó DracoŐs voice was dripping with disdain that hadnŐt been directed towards Harry since before heŐd been re-sorted to Slytherin, and it startled Harry to hear it. ŇÔArmy?ŐÓ Harry asked in confusion. ŇIs Hermione okay?Ó he demanded, clambering to his feet. ŇPhysically, sheŐs fine,Ó Pansy said. That still didnŐt sound good. ŇWhat happened?Ó Harry asked, his eyes narrowing. ŇGranger apparently organized an army of students on DumbledoreŐs behalf,Ó Draco said. ŇWhat?!Ó HarryŐs voice rose. ŇWhat the hell do you mean?Ó ŇIt also seems that every single Gryffindor that you are still friendly with was a part of this army,Ó Pansy said, sneering. ŇAlthough it is a bit surprising that you werenŐt invited to be a part of it.Ó ŇDoes that mean they donŐt trust you anymore?Ó Blaise asked Harry. ŇYour friends, I mean? ŇAnd Umbridge wanted our help in catching them in the act.Ó He smirked, then appeared to force himself to wipe it off his face. ŇI have to inform you thatÉ I caught Granger.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó Harry asked. ŇMost of them scattered, but Draco tripped Granger so she got caught,Ó Pansy said. ŇI turned it into Umbridge.Ó HarryŐs upper lip curled in disgust. ŇYouŐre actually helping her now?Ó he asked. ŇI thought we hated her.Ó ŇOh, I do,Ó Pansy said. ŇBut sheŐs the High Inquisitor of Hogwarts, and she was asking us Prefects for help.Ó ŇWhat were we supposed to do?Ó Draco asked. ŇRefuse?Ó ŇI donŐt know,Ó Harry snapped. ŇMaybe.Ó ŇHarry, weŐre not Gryffindors,Ó Pansy said, sneering. ŇWeŐre not going to defy her if it doesnŐt gain us anything.Ó ŇPansy, hush,Ó Draco said, placing a hand on PansyŐs arm. ŇUmbridge took Granger up to DumbledoreŐs office,Ó he continued. ŇFudge was here, too - with aurors.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇAurors?Ó he asked. ŇIs HermioneÉ what happened to Hermione?Ó he demanded. Pansy and Draco shared a glance before Pansy spoke. ŇFrom what Umbridge said, Dumbledore admitted that heŐd asked Granger to recruit for him. ŇDumbledoreŐs gone?Ó he asked in disbelief. Pansy nodded. ŇDid you ever figure out exactly how Granger became suspicious of you?Ó Blaise suddenly asked. ŇAbout you and your recentÉ hobby, I mean?Ó Harry shook his head. Hermione hadnŐt asked him about the dark arts again aside from that one time. ŇWell, then,Ó he said. ŇI think this answers that.Ó ŇSnape did tell you that Dumbledore was almost certainly beginning to suspect you,Ó Draco said, nodding. ŇWhat do youÉÓ Harry paused, feeling a tad horrified. ŇGranger forms an army on DumbledoreŐs behalf and doesnŐt ask you to be a part of it. Based on what you said Snape told you, Dumbledore suspected that you were using dark arts. ŇSomething about all this sounds really... off.Ó Dumbledore wouldnŐt make an army of students. If Dumbledore truly was concerned about HarryŐs activities, Hermione would probably have been the person to turn to. ŇI need to talk to her,Ó Harry said, and he took off for the dorm without a second thought. He quickly grabbed his invisibility cloak out of his trunk and headed back to the common room, but his path out the door was blocked by Draco. ŇHarry -Ó ŇDonŐt!Ó Harry snapped. ŇI donŐt want to hear it. SheŐs my best friend. I need toÉÓ He trailed off. He needed to what? To make sure she was okay? To find out if Dumbledore had set off her suspicions about Harry? He shook his head, scowling. ŇIŐll be back,Ó Harry said, and he pushed past Draco, swirling his cloak over his shoulders as he headed out into the castle. *** The walk to Gryffindor tower was strange. Harry hadnŐt been to the tower since last year, and the path felt strange - intimately familiar, yet he knew he didnŐt belong anymore. Upon reaching the fat ladyŐs painting, he paused, feeling more nostalgic than heŐd expected. ŇBut can you see if Hermione Granger can come out to talk to me?Ó ŇHmpf.Ó The fat lady frowned and seemed to study him for a moment, then sighed. ŇThis is most unusual, but as you were once a Gryffindor, IŐll make an exception.Ó She disappeared from the frame, only to reappear moments later. ŇIŐm afraid sheŐs not in the common room. ŇEven Ron, although he probably wonŐt want to talk to meÉÓ ŇSit tight, Mr. Potter,Ó she said before disappearing from the frame once more. ŇIs Hermione okay?Ó GinnyŐs uncertainty gave way to surprise. ŇIŐm fine, Harry. So is Hermione. She went to bed. She hadÉ a bad night.Ó As she spoke, the uneasiness came back, as if she wasnŐt sure what she should actually say to him. ŇDraco and Pansy told me what -Ó ŇOh, did they?Ó Ginny cut him off, and in the blink of an eye, her hesitation turned to contempt. ŇBut I donŐt think they have the whole story,Ó Harry said, hoping he hadnŐt made a mistake in mentioning them. ŇCan you tell me what happened?Ó ŇWhy?Ó Ginny spat. ŇYour friends were there. They must have already told you.Ó ŇBut what they said didnŐt make any sense!Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇThey said you were all part of some kind of army that Dumbledore had Hermione put together!Ó GinnyŐs anger instantly dissolved as she burst out in laughter. Harry blinked. ŇAm IÉ missing something?Ó Ginny nodded. ŇYeah, you are,Ó she said. ŇIt wasnŐt an army - not really, anyway. We just called it ÔDumbledoreŐs ArmyŐ to spite Fudge. "She's the one who put it together, but Dumbledore took the blame." She swallowed, frowning. ŇHermione is understandably pretty upset. SheŐs blaming herself for Dumbledore being gone.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. Dumbledore had let himself be driven from the school for HermioneŐs sake. It seemed unbelievable but also sounded so completely like Dumbledore that it wasnŐt entirely surprising. ŇThenÉ if it wasnŐt an army, what was it really?Ó Ginny shrugged. ŇBasically a Defense club,Ó she said. ŇUmbridge wasnŐt teaching us the practical, so we were trying to teach ourselves.Ó She sighed. ŇWe just wanted toÉ we want to make sure weŐre not sitting ducks when the next war actually starts.Ó Harry felt simultaneously relieved that they hadnŐt formed an actual army, but he couldnŐt help but feel a strange sense of hurt that he hadnŐt even known about it. ŇEveryone tried to bring one thing to teach the others,Ó Ginny said. ŇHoly shit,Ó he said. ŇHermione did ask me about it! She asked if I would teach others...Ó Ginny nodded, then frowned. ŇShe said that you said Ôno,Ő though.Ó ŇI did,Ó Harry confirmed. Ginny seemed to study him for a moment before responding. ŇCan I ask why you refused?Ó she asked. ŇHermione didnŐt say.Ó ŇIÉ well...Ó Harry sighed and leaned against the wall, suddenly feeling quite unwilling to meet her eyes. ŇAnd she did that by trying to squash me.Ó ŇAnd it worked,Ó Ginny said. Her voice was soft, but her words packed a punch. Harry looked up in shock. ŇWhat?Ó he said, and then shook his head. ŇNo, I -Ó ŇUmbridge tried to silence you because you told everyone what happened last spring,Ó Ginny said, Ňand since then youŐve given the world nothing but silence.Ó ŇGinny -Ó ŇI know you were re-sorted to Slytherin, Harry,Ó she said, turning back to the fat ladyŐs portrait, Ňbut I didnŐt think that would turn you into a coward.Ó She whispered the password to the fat lady, apparently unwilling to let Harry hear it, and she disappeared into the tower. Harry was left having a staring match with the fat lady while GinnyŐs words seemed to hang in the air. *** ŇYou look upset,Ó Draco said as soon as Harry returned. The common room was mostly empty by then; he and Blaise were the only occupants. ŇWe were right, then,Ó Blaise surmised. ŇGranger was -Ó ŇNo, she wasnŐt,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇItŐsÉÓ He sighed. He didnŐt particularly want to talk about what Ginny had said with either of them. They hadnŐt been Gryffindors, and they certainly wouldnŐt understand why GinnyŐs words had upset him. Back in the beginning of the school year, Dumbledore had told the entire school that HarryŐs re-sorting didnŐt mean heŐd lost the bravery that made him a Gryffindor. Except he apparently had. ŇHarry?Ó Draco asked. ŇWhat happened?Ó ŇDid you find out why your friends didnŐt loop you in on DumbledoreŐs ragtag army that was made up of nothing but students?Ó Blaise asked snidely. Harry let out a derisive snort. ŇThatŐs just it, though,Ó he said. ŇI was looped in on it. Hermione asked me months ago if I wouldÉÓ He let out another sigh. ŇI justÉ forgot.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Draco said, his voice flat. ŇHow in the hell do you ÔforgetŐ being asked to join an army?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇIt wasnŐt an army at all,Ó he said. ŇThey were just practicing Defense Against the Dark Arts since our useless ÔprofessorŐ refuses to teach it properly. ŇThat is such a perfectly Gryffindor way of getting under FudgeŐs and UmbridgeŐs skin,Ó he said. Harry couldnŐt help but wince at his words. The entire reason why Harry had refused to teach it was because it had been too ÔGryffindor.Ő Ginny was right. ŇIf it turns out that you were invited, after all, why are you still upset?Ó Draco asked. Harry sighed and collapsed into the sofa by the fire. ŇIŐm justÉÓ He trailed off and stared sightlessly into the flames, frowning. ŇAre you going to finish that thought?Ó Blaise asked after a few long moments of silence. Harry closed his eyes, and his frown turned into a full-blown scowl. ŇMy friends managed to prepare for our O.W.L.s and trained to defend themselves in case all hell breaks loose. ŇAll thatŐs come out of it is that it makes me feel like shit!Ó ŇJust because you donŐt see results instantly or know what the exact outcome will be doesnŐt mean itŐs not worthwhile,Ó Blaise said. ŇBut the only reason I started doing it in the first place was to get myself out of trouble with her,Ó Harry said. ŇSo?Ó Draco said. ŇSo that makes me a coward!Ó Harry said. ŇWhat?Ó Blaise asked. ŇThatŐs not a bad thing. It means you finally have some survival instinct -Ó ŇBlaise, stop,Ó Draco said. His eyes remained on Harry, and his face was set in a contemplative expression. Harry leaned forward and buried his hands in his hair. ŇBeing called a coward is worse than when the wizarding world was calling me a liar,Ó he said quietly. ŇItŐs actually true, so itÉÓ He trailed off and then shook his head. ŇI donŐt think you guys would understand.Ó ŇYou feel like youŐre losing whatever made you a Gryffindor in the first place,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇI suppose I would feel odd if I thought I was losing what makes me a Slytherin.Ó Harry was beginning to despise how well Draco could read him. ŇUmbridge is terrorizing this school, and IŐve done nothing,Ó Harry said. The three of them were silent for a moment before Blaise finally spoke up again. ŇHarry, donŐt take this the wrong way, butÉ what would you actually be able to do besides antagonize her?Ó ŇAnd if you did antagonize her, what would it gain?Ó Draco added. Harry sighed. ŇI donŐt know.Ó *** The following morning, it was formerly announced that Dolores Umbridge had replaced Dumbledore as the new headmistress of Hogwarts. It was an odd, muted day. Most of the students seemed subdued - cowed, as Pansy put it - but the other houses were particularly nasty to all of Slytherin whenever they thought they could get away with it. More than one fight broke out in the hallways, and Daphne earned herself detention with Professor Sprout for cursing a student that tried to corner her little sister. Harry somehow managed to stay out of the line of fire. He caught Hermione at lunch but didnŐt have the time to do much of anything besides give her a hug and makes plans to meet up with her after dinner. He fought to hold his tongue during Defense Against the Dark Arts, and by the time he left the class his hands hurt from how hard heŐd clenched his fists. Umbridge seemed to be in an almost jubilant mood and her favoritism towards Slytherin shone more than it ever had before. Harry was fairly sure sheŐd awarded their class alone nearly one hundred points, most of them to Draco and Pansy. He tried his hardest to avoid Umbridge the rest of the day, but she, unfortunately, descended on the Slytherin table at the beginning of dinner. ŇMr. Malfoy and Miss Parkinson,Ó she said, giving them all a simpering smile. ŇI was wondering if you two would be able to join me in my office after dinner today.Ó Pansy nodded dutifully. ŇOf course, Professor,Ó she said in an unusually toady manner. ŇI meanÉ Headmistress.Ó Umbridge tittered before wishing them all a nice evening and departing for the head table. ŇKissass,Ó Harry hissed to Pansy, who gave him a swift kick in the shin under the table. ŇThereŐs nothing to gain by refusing, Harry,Ó Draco said. Harry violently smashed his fork into his potatoes, wishing he had something better to take his irritation out on. ŇI donŐt get the point in using Ôany meansŐ when you donŐt even know what the ÔendsŐ are gonna be,Ó he muttered. ŇWhat?Ó Pansy asked. Harry sighed, rolling his eyes. ŇNever mind.Ó He turned to Daphne. ŇWhen is your detention?Ó he asked. Daphne sighed. ŇTomorrow,Ó she said. ŇGreenhouse duty, naturally.Ó ŇWould you mind coming with me to the library after dinner tonight, then?Ó he asked. ŇIŐm meeting up with Hermione and Neville, and IŐm pretty sure youŐre the only other Slytherin theyŐll tolerate being around, all things considered.Ó ŇOf course.Ó *** Neville eyed Daphne a bit warily as she and Harry sat down but seemed to relax when Hermione greeted her cordially before immediately turning to Harry. ŇHarry, I hate to ask this, but I have to,Ó she said. Harry couldnŐt help but stiffen; the last time Hermione had something along those lines was when sheŐd asked him about the dark arts. Nevertheless, he nodded at her. ŇGo ahead.Ó Hermione didnŐt waste a moment. ŇDid you tell anyone that I asked you to teach Defense last fall?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇIf youŐre asking if I told Draco and Pansy, I didnŐt. They were only there because Umbridge asked for their assistance as Prefects,Ó he said. ŇI didnŐt tell anyone.Ó He let out a wry smile. ŇIŐll even admit that I spent a bit of last night being a little salty that you hadnŐt invited me. I just kinda forgot that you had.Ó ŇThatŐs what Ginny said,Ó Hermione replied. ŇAnd IŐm sorry I didnŐt tell you that I went ahead and did it. We decided to swear each other to secrecyÉÓ ŇWhich is why Marietta Edgecombe is walking around with ÔsneakŐ written across her face, isnŐt it?Ó Daphne asked, smirking. ŇI suppose I shouldnŐt be surprised that you went through with it anyway,Ó Harry said, smiling fondly at Hermione. ŇOnce youŐve got an idea, you donŐt ever let it go.Ó ŇBut you were right, though, Harry,Ó Hermione said. ŇIf you had said Ôyes,Ő you would almost certainly be expelled right now.Ó ŇWell, IŐm quite relieved about that,Ó Daphne said. ŇI wouldnŐt want Slytherin to lose Harry so soon after we gained him.Ó Harry rolled his eyes as Daphne continued. ŇBut for the record, Granger, I think you had a very good idea.Ó Hermione shook her head. ŇWe got caught, and now DumbledoreÉÓ She swallowed. ŇI just made everything worse.Ó ŇYou did what Umbridge is wilfully neglecting to do,Ó Daphne said. ŇAnd you made an effort to learn how to defend yourselves, and I think everyone at this table would agree thatŐs more important than getting in trouble.Ó Harry looked down at the table at DaphneŐs words, suddenly feeling ashamed of himself. ŇAlso, for the record,Ó Daphne continued, Ňthere would have been a few Slytherins that would have loved to take part in it.Ó ŇLike who?Ó Daphne smiled. ŇMe. AndÉÓ She paused, considering. ŇWell, most likely just me. But with how concerned Blaise is about O.W.L.s IŐm certain he would have loved to cover the practical piece, provided he could have gotten past his own stupidity.Ó Neville finally spoke up. ŇYou seem nice enough,Ó he said to Daphne, sounding nervous. ŇBut we voted on it, and it was unanimous to not invite any Slytherins, especially afterÉÓ His eyes seemed to dart over to Harry, who sighed. ŇAfter I said Ôno?ŐÓ Harry asked, and Neville nodded in response. ŇAnd it was smart of you to refuse, Harry,Ó Daphne said. ŇUmbridge wouldnŐt have let it go if youŐd been involved.Ó ŇI agree, Harry,Ó Hermione said, then she leaned forward and placed a hand over HarryŐs. ŇIt wouldnŐt have done you any good to risk expulsion.Ó Harry gave a half shrug. He certainly didnŐt agree, but he didnŐt feel up for convincing the others of his own cowardice. ŇIt would have been nice for you teach us the Patronus charm, though,Ó Hermione said. ŇWe did cover quite a bit and I think we gained a lot from what we all taught each other, but no one knew how to cast one.Ó Daphne shook her head a little too emphatically. ŇIt still wouldnŐt have been worth it,Ó she said. Harry offered Hermione a weak smile. ŇMaybe I can teach you later,Ó he said. ŇThereŐs nothing against the rules about regular gatherings of two, right?Ó *** The four of them made idle conversation for another hour or so before they decided to depart. They stood outside the library doors, finally allowing themselves to laugh out loud about UmbridgeŐs failed attempts in entering DumbledoreŐs office. Rumor had it that sheŐd stood outside the phoenix statue for much of the afternoon demanding entrance, but the statue had refused to budge. ŇHarry!Ó A familiar voice cried from down the hallway, and the four turned to see Draco and Blaise hurrying towards them. Hermione narrowed her eyes at Draco. ŇI suppose weŐll be leaving, then,Ó she said, scowling. ŇWe need to talk to you,Ó Draco said when he reached the four of them, panting. It appeared as if he and Blaise had run across the entire castle. ŇNow.Ó ŇGoodbye, Harry,Ó Hermione said, turning to leave. ŇWait, Granger,Ó Blaise said. Hermione stared at Blaise in shock, and to HarryŐs surprise, Neville suddenly stepped in front of Hermione, facing down Draco and Blaise. ŇYou need to hear this, too,Ó Draco said to Hermione, ignoring Neville. HermioneŐs shock grew to almost comical proportions at Draco Malfoy directly addressing her without an insult or a barb. ŇBut not here,Ó Draco continued, looking over his shoulder. ŇWe canŐt be seen.Ó Loathing replaced the shock on HermioneŐs face. ŇYou mean you canŐt be seen speaking with a Muggleborn?Ó she spat, scowling. ŇMore like I canŐt risk Umbridge seeing me speaking to anyone that was a member of ÔDumbledoreŐs Army,ŐÓ Draco sneered. ŇI was trying to do this as a favor to Harry since you are his friend, but if youŐd rather -Ó ŇPlease, Granger,Ó Blaise said, looking vaguely uncomfortable as he said it. ŇYou really do need to hear this. ItŐs about Umbridge.Ó Neville and Hermione shared a look, and Hermione finally sighed and nodded. ŇFine.Ó ŇThereŐs an empty classroom around the corner,Ó Daphne said. ŇCome on.Ó They quickly filed down the hallway and into the classroom. Blaise shut the door and promptly cast an Imperturbable Charm on it before circling back around to stand next to Draco, leaving Neville and Hermione nearest to the door. Harry raised an eyebrow, recognizing BlaiseŐs actions as being deliberate; he didnŐt want the two Gryffindors to feel cornered in an abandoned room full of Slytherins. Harry couldnŐt help but notice that Hermione and Neville both had their hands in their cloaks, obviously grasping their wands. He let out a silent sigh, supposing that he didnŐt blame Hermione and Neville at all, especially considering what had happened the night before. Two Gryffindors and four Slytherins in one room would usually be asking for disaster, even if one of the Slytherins was Harry. ŇWhat happened?Ó Harry said, wishing he knew of a way to ease the tension. Blaise and Draco glanced at one another. ŇWho should go first?Ó Blaise asked. ŇProbably you,Ó Draco said. Blaise rolled his eyes. ŇThatŐs just because you know youŐre going to piss Harry off.Ó ŇSo are you.Ó Daphne rolled her eyes. ŇWould you two just get on with it?Ó she said. ŇThe suspense is positively killing me.Ó Blaise sighed and leaned back against an old, worn-out desk, fixing his eyes on the ground. ŇIŐll just get this out of the way, first,Ó he said. ŇI borrowed your cloak tonight without asking you, Harry.Ó ŇMy invisibility cloak?Ó Harry asked. ŇWhy?Ó Out of the corner of his eye, he saw Hermione shoot him an alarmed look. Blaise glanced up with a raised eyebrow. ŇHuh. ŇI overheard Lee Jordan mentioning ÔtortureŐ when he was talking about having detention with Umbridge,Ó he said. ŇConsidering how much she favors us, I had a feeling that Umbridge handled Slytherin detentions differently than the rest of the school. ŇI went to her private quarters.Ó ŇOh, is it also a terrible palette of every shade of pink?Ó Daphne asked, and Blaise let out a short laugh. ŇIt is,Ó he said, and then his expression turned unusually serious. ŇBut that wasnŐt the worst part of it.Ó He shot a glance towards Hermione and then dropped his eyes to the ground again. ŇWhat is it?Ó Harry asked. ŇI found paperwork for a piece of legislation sheŐs working on,Ó Blaise said. ŇLegislation?Ó Harry asked. ŇA new law?Ó Blaise nodded. ŇAnd itŐsÉÓ He paused. He swallowed nervously. ŇGranger, you are not going to like this.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó Blaise opened his mouth, then shut it again, as if he couldnŐt decide what words to say. ŇItŐs apparently called the ÔMuggleborn Registration Commission,ŐÓ Draco said quietly. ŇThe claim is obviously false.Ó ŇThenÉÓ Hermione shook her head. ŇWhy would she make something like that up? WhatŐs her goal? ŇHavenŐt you been asking for exactly this for years?Ó Draco didnŐt respond, and Harry narrowed his eyes. ŇDraco?Ó he said, frowning. Draco took in a deep breath and let it out slowly before he spoke. ŇBut even if it doesnŐt, this does point at something else that could be even worse.Ó ŇWorse than rounding up Muggleborns?Ó Harry snapped. ŇWell, ÔworseŐ in terms of it possibly being a more immediate threat,Ó Blaise said. ŇWhatŐs that?Ó Hermione asked. ŇFudge is an idiot, but he isnŐt known for having any real anti-Muggle views,Ó Daphne said, her expression horrified. ŇButÉÓ Draco nodded. ŇUmbridgeŐs ideals appear to fall exactly in line with the Dark LordŐs.Ó He swallowed nervously. ŇAnd the more I think about it, the more it all seems to fit.Ó ŇYou think Umbridge is a Death Eater?Ó Harry said doubtfully. ŇNo,Ó Draco said, shaking his head. ŇI donŐt think sheŐs marked. ŇIt would certainly explain whyÉÓ He trailed off, suddenly shooting a wary look at Hermione and Neville. ŇExplain what?Ó Hermione asked. Draco scowled and shut his mouth, and Harry and BlaiseŐs eyes met. Harry instantly understood why Draco was clamming up - his father had known about Umbridge and why sheŐd been placed at Hogwarts. Explaining why that pointed towards Voldemort would involve Draco directly admitting that he knew his own father was a Death Eater, and that was something Draco almost certainly didnŐt want to admit to two Gryffindors. Harry wondered if he should tell Draco that Hermione and Neville already suspected his father. Blaise leaned over and began whispering something in DracoŐs ear. ŇBut in the meantime, doing this would accomplish far more than what youŐre accomplishing now.Ó ŇI think you should do it, Harry.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened as he pivoted towards Neville. ŇWhat?Ó he asked in disbelief. ŇHarry,Ó Neville said. ŇIf IŐm going to be questioned by Umbridge, IŐd rather you be there thanÉ another Slytherin. ŇThe more people that know about this, the less likely it is that sheŐll believe that youŐreÉ in her pocket.Ó ŇYouŐre talking like IŐve already agreed to this,Ó Harry said, annoyed. Even as he said it, though, Harry was starting to think that they might be right. Slytherins use any means to achieve our ends, even when we are not a fan of the means. As much as he hated the entire idea, he wanted Umbridge gone, and he couldnŐt think of anything else. ŇIŐll do it,Ó Harry finally said, closing his eyes. ŇHarry!Ó Hermione exclaimed. ŇYou donŐt have to, and I think this runs the risk of being counterproductive -Ó ŇAre you only disagreeing because you know what Harry will have to do in order to make Umbridge believe him?Ó Daphne said. ŇWhat do you mean?Ó Neville asked. Harry knew. He didnŐt want to say it, but he knew. ŇIf sheŐs hoping the Inquisitorial Squad is specifically going to crush the spirit of anyone who was a member of ÔDumbledoreŐs Army,Ő he wonŐt be able to be seen speaking with any of you,Ó Daphne said. ŇNot entirely,Ó Blaise said. ŇHe can speak to you, but he certainly canŐt be seen saying anything nice.Ó ŇAre you able to do that, Harry?Ó Draco asked. Harry knew what he was asking. According to Draco, Harry had been a very good Slytherin, but this would entail him needing to be a mean Slytherin. ŇI honestly have no idea,Ó he said quietly. ŇI suppose I canÉ try.Ó ŇIf youŐre truly insistent on trying this, are you certain we canŐt inform anyone else?Ó Hermione asked. ŇMaybe Fred and George?Ó ŇAnd Ginny,Ó Harry immediately said, shooting Draco a stern look. ŇThe whole list.Ó ŇThatŐs far too many,Ó Draco said, shaking his head. ŇI didnŐt even want these two to know.Ó He gestured at Neville and Hermione. ŇAre you sure you want to risk retaliation from the Weasley twins?Ó Daphne asked, smirking. ŇI have a feeling they can be quite vicious.Ó ŇSo can Ginny,Ó Neville said. ŇShe taught us all the Bat Bogey Hex, but hers is still the worst.Ó Draco let out a frustrated sound, throwing his hands up in the air. ŇFine.Ó ŇHarry, are you certain you want to do this?Ó Hermione asked. Harry shook his head. ŇNo,Ó he said. ŇBut right now, itŐs literally the only idea we have.Ó *** The following morning, Dolores Umbridge asked Harry to meet in her office before his first class. As expected, she invited him to become a member of the newly formed Inquisitorial Squad. Harry accepted. Chapter End Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people.
