I just needed a character for that slot, so there she is. Chapter 23: TheRole Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! I just want to sincerely thank everyone once again for all the lovely comments and kudos. I have had a very limited amount of time on a computer and I want to utilize most of that time to write, so I haven't been able to respond to all of them. But please know that I DO read each and every single one, and that I get a thrill of joy every time I get a notification that I've got one. Thank you all for sticking with me. <3 See the end of the chapter for more notes Six Slytherins formed a semi-circle around UmbridgeŐs desk, and Harry snuck a glance at the others. Vince and Greg stood to the right with their hands hanging at their side. Next to them was Draco, who had a pompous air to his entire stance, chin in the air and an arrogant smirk painted across his features. Pansy was next in line, and she clasped her hands in front of her with a simpering smile. Millicent seemed to be taking a militant approach, feet set apart and hands meeting at her back, as if she were standing at parade rest. They all looked comfortable in their skin while Harry felt like he was about to jump out of his. He shoved his hands in his pockets and just concentrated on appearing casual. Harry had never realized how difficult pretending to be at ease could actually be, especially when the situation he'd found himself in was anything but easy. Behind her desk, Umbridge folded her hands in front of her and gave them a toothy smile. ŇIŐm so pleased that youŐve all decided to take this opportunity,Ó she said. ŇThis is not only a chance to finally raise this school to the MinistryŐs standards, but it is also an excellent professional opportunity for each and every one of you.Ó She let out one of her disgusting, high-pitched laughs. ŇIn addition, you are the very first iteration of the Inquisitorial Squad, which I daresay might make your group somewhatÉ historical. IŐm certain that youŐll all be mentioned by name in a future edition of Hogwarts: A History.Ó Harry heard a few of the others shift, and Pansy let out a giggle. Harry didnŐt dare glance at them, but he imagined that they were likely smiling at her words. Even so, he couldnŐt bring himself to change anything in his expression and instead continued to stare at Umbridge flatly. Unfortunately, Umbridge looked directly at him right at that moment. ŇDoes this not please you, Mr. Potter?Ó she asked, a slight frown on her face. ŇYour fellow squad members all seem quite excited for this.Ó HarryŐs mind raced to come up with a response. ŇIŐmÉ already in a few wizarding history books, Headmistress, so IŐll admit that it isnŐt very important to me,Ó he said, before offering Umbridge what he hoped was a pleasant smile. ŇIŐm far more excited at being given a chance toÉÓ He paused, trying to find the right words. Ň... maintain order in this school.Ó Umbridge smiled terribly. ŇYou feel that the school has lacked a certain order, then?Ó Harry nodded. ŇI was attacked on the very first day of classes,Ó he said, Ňand while I was repeatedly reassured that the matter was Ôunder investigation,Ő nothing was actually done about it untilÉ you handled it.Ó He shook his head, hoping the movement didnŐt appear as stiff as it felt. ŇI donŐt want any other student to find themselves in that situation.Ó Once again, it appeared that he nailed the response he needed to give Umbridge because her smile widened to a disgusting proportion. Her eyes finally left Harry and they drifted across the line of students in front of her. She then slid her chair back from the desk before opening the top drawer, pulling out a small pouch. She uncinched it and tipped it into her hand, shaking out six tiny silver pins shaped like the letter ÔI.Ő ŇNow, then,Ó she said, standing up and strutting around her desk, Ňto make it officialÉÓ She approached Vince first and neatly pinned the badge to his robes, right above the Slytherin emblem on his chest. Vince smiled at her, looking a bit proud. She made her way down the line before finally coming to Harry. Harry felt his skin crawling as she attached the pin to his own robes, but he somehow managed to eke out a smile when she was done. ŇIŐve already spoken with each of you, but letŐs just summarize your responsibilities again, shall we?Ó she said, turning back to her desk. ŇYou have been given the authority to give and take points from all houses,Ó she continued as she grabbed a few pieces of parchment and turned back to them. ŇYou will be regularly patrolling the hallways of the school in order to get any wrong-doers back in line. If you donŐt feel that detracting points will get the message across, you may defer to myself or Mr. Filch to assign detention.Ó She smiled at each of them in turn, as if ensuring that they were all still following her. ŇYou will be monitoring student owl post coming to and from the school, and you will occasionally assist me in note-taking as I... question a few students that have proven themselves to be particularly troublesome.Ó She then passed out the pieces of parchment. When Harry looked down at it, the name Hermione Granger seemed to leap out at him, the letters stark across the very top of a list of names. He moved his eyes down the list, his heart sinking as he read each name. Ginny Weasley. Ron Weasley. Neville Longbottom. Terry Boot. Angelina Johnson. These were the members of DumbledoreŐs Army. The list was far longer than Harry had expected it to be; nearly thirty students had been involved, most of them Gryffindors. Hermione didnŐt do anything by halves, that was for sure. ŇThese are theÉ troublesome students that I just mentioned,Ó Umbridge said. ŇI ask that you all make sure to keep a very close eye on them. I expect you to tamp down any signs of trouble immediately, as well as to report any suspicious behavior that you see from any of them.Ó Her voice hardened ever so slightly. ŇIs that clear?Ó ŇYes, maŐam,Ó came the instant response, and Harry mentally kicked himself for not joining in. ŇMr. Potter,Ó Umbridge said, turning back to him. ŇWill you be able to remainÉ objective in this responsibility? I understand that you are a close friend of Miss GrangerŐs.Ó She was studying him very carefully to observe his reaction and his response, and Harry knew it. ŇIt wonŐt be a problem, maŐam,Ó he said as smoothly as he could manage, and it seemed to come out far better than heŐd expected it to. ŇIf IŐm to be completely honest, IŐm not very pleased with her right now.Ó ŇOh?Ó Umbridge asked curiously, her eyes flashing. Harry didnŐt feel like he could meet her eyes any longer, so he concentrated on neatly folding the parchment in half as he spoke. ŇFriends should trust one another,Ó he said, folding it again into quarters. ŇSheŐs proven that she doesnŐt trust me anymore, so IŐm not certain that weŐre still friends at all.Ó He carefully placed the parchment in his robes and finally steeled himself to look back up at her. Umbridge looked so damn pleased, and Harry hated it. ŇMarvelous to hear that, Harry,Ó she said, and an unpleasant chill went down his skin. ŇYou should all run along to class now,Ó she said. ŇI believe itŐs History of Magic, yes?Ó She shook her head. ŇLet me assure you that a ghost will not be teaching you that subject for much longer.Ó Harry barely remembered walking to class, and he couldnŐt remember a word of what Draco said to him on the way there. He sat in class in a haze as he prepared himself for what he knew he had to do. *** As they headed to the Great Hall for lunch, Harry felt like he was walking to his own funeral. His legs were jelly, his stomach was tied in knots, and his heart was on the verge of leaping out of his chest. ŇIf you donŐt think youŐll be able to pull this off, Harry, just donŐt say anything,Ó Pansy whispered. ŇJust stand back. You wearing the badge should be enough to anger them.Ó ŇAs fun as it'll be, I didn't think the point of all this was pissing off Gryffindors. Umbridge will almost certainly be watching,Ó Draco reminded them. ŇYou just canŐt -Ó ŇJust do me a favor,Ó Harry said, interrupting him. ŇBoth of you.Ó ŇWhat is it?Ó ŇDonŐt call Hermione a Mudblood.Ó Harry swallowed, once again glancing down at the silver ÔIŐ pinned just above the Slytherin crest on his robes. ŇThereŐs no way IŐll be able to do this if youÉ say that.Ó Pansy raised an eyebrow at him. ŇNow that I think about it, I donŐt think IŐve ever heard you sayÉ that word. HowÉ unusual that youŐre saying it now.Ó ŇI just wanted to get to the point fast,Ó Harry hissed. ŇYou two are both silver-tongued enough to insult her in other ways.Ó He narrowed his eyes. ŇJust donŐtÉ donŐt go after her blood.Ó Pansy let out a noise of frustration. ŇYou realize that destroys our easiest -Ó ŇI donŐt care!Ó Harry snapped, probably a bit too loudly. He hushed himself and shot Pansy a fiery glare. ŇI canŐt -Ó ŇWe wonŐt, Harry,Ó Draco whispered. TheyŐd reached the bottom of the stairs leading to the entrance hall, and Harry stared up in trepidation. Draco shot him a questioning look, and he swallowed, then nodded. They headed up the stairs, Pansy and Draco in the lead. Just as they were reaching the top of the stairs, HermioneŐs voice rang out clear as a bell. ŇI bet she fancied herself sitting in DumbledoreŐs office,Ó she was saying. ŇLording it over all of the other teachers, the stupid puffed-up, power-crazed -Ó ŇAre you quite sure you want to finish that sentence, Granger?Ó Draco said, his voice full of malice. Harry somehow managed to hide a wince. It sounded so bloody familiar. It had been directed at him for years, after all. Maybe I can do this, Harry thought desperately. Maybe I can just remember the way I used to go after Draco and use that. He peered around Pansy and Draco to spy Hermione standing with Neville, Ron, as well as Ernie Macmillan from Hufflepuff. Ernie, Harry remembered, had been on the list of names Umbridge had handed them all. ŇIŐm afraid IŐll have to dock some points from Gryffindor and Hufflepuff for this,Ó Draco continued, somehow sounding simultaneously lazy and gleeful. ŇWhat are you talking about, Malfoy?Ó Ernie asked, frowning. ŇOnly teachers can take away house points.Ó ŇWeŐre prefects, too, Malfoy,Ó Ron snarled. ŇYou canŐt just -Ó ŇOh, yes, we can,Ó Pansy sneered. ŇYou see, prefects arenŐt allowed to take points, but members of the Inquisitorial Squad can.Ó ŇThe what?Ó Hermione demanded. Her eyes darted to Harry and then back to Malfoy. Somehow, Harry hadnŐt been noticed by the others yet. HarryŐs eyes then drifted around the entrance hall until they landed on the figure heŐd been looking for; Umbridge was standing by the doors to the Great Hall, watching the scene with interest. ŇThe Inquisitorial Squad, Granger,Ó Draco sneered, pointing to the silver badge on his cloak. ŇAs we have proven our loyalty to the Ministry for Magic and the new headmistress, weŐve been hand-selected by said headmistress to whip this school back into shape.Ó ŇItŐs about time this school actually had standards,Ó Pansy added snidely. ŇSo, Granger,Ó Draco continued, ŇIŐll have five points from you for the disrespect towards the headmistress. Macmillan, five points for contradicting our authority. Your shirtŐs untucked, Weasel, so thatŐll be five points from you. And you, Longbottom -Ó ŇHarry?!Ó Ron suddenly exclaimed. Harry had to fight every muscle in his face to not show any emotion when Ron finally noticed him. As it was, he had to clench his teeth and his nostrils flared just so he wouldnŐt visibly grimace. He instantly realized that he wouldnŐt be able to do as Pansy asked by sitting back and saying nothing. It was time to see if he could actually pull this off. ŇWhat the bloody hell are you doing, Harry?Ó Ron demanded, staring at Harry in horror. His eyes seemed to be glued to the silver pin on Harry's robes. ŇThatŐs another five points for language, Weasel,Ó Pansy said, snickering. ŇHarryÉÓ Hermione said, her mouth parting. ŇYouŐre taking part in this... ÔInquisitorial Squad,Ő too?Ó She swallowed. ŇWhat are you doing?Ó Thank you, Hermione, Harry thought. TheyŐd planned to have a public argument, though they hadnŐt intended on it being so soon. But Hermione must have recognized that Harry needed a way to play the part, and having a pre-planned argument would surely be easier than directly leaping into the role with Ron, no matter how fractured their relationship was. Harry put on his best scowl and met HermioneŐs eyes. ŇWhat does it look like?Ó he sneered. ŇIŐd say it looks like you finally came to your senses, Harry,Ó Draco said, and Harry shot him a silent thanks, as well. Draco was trying to draw the conversation back to him; he obviously wasnŐt sure that Harry could actually do this, after all, and Harry quite agreed. But he had to try. He played a role with Umbridge. He could play this role, too. He had to. ŇHarryŐs just finally realized who his real friends are,Ó Draco continued, smirking. ŇSo you -Ó ŇThatŐs right,Ó Harry said. With how he felt at that moment, he didnŐt think he could pull off a smirk without looking like he was in pain, so he settled for a dark glare directed HermioneŐs way. ŇYou played at being friends, Hermione, but you showed me exactly what that friendship meant when you didnŐt say a word to me about this whole ÔarmyŐ you put together.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Ernie asked, glancing at Hermione, who appeared nervous. ŇYou told us that you asked him... and that he said he didnŐt want to!Ó ŇOh, did I?Ó Harry asked scornfully. ŇAnd when did I say that, Hermione?Ó ŇIÉÓ HermioneŐs expression was full of regret, and all she could do was shake her head. ŇYeah,Ó Harry said, sneering. ŇSounds like she lied, Macmillian.Ó TheyŐd planned this. They had to come up with a safeguard in case someone from DumbledoreŐs Army spilled to Umbridge that Harry had a hint of its existence, even if he had forgotten. It conveniently gave them the perfect excuse to have a very public argument right in front of Umbridge. ŇHarry,Ó Hermione said, her face full of regret, ŇIŐm sorry I didn't tell you, but -Ó ŇDonŐt botherÉ Granger,Ó Harry snarled. He pushed past Draco and Pansy, heading towards the Great Hall. ŇHarry, wait!Ó Ron said, stepping directly in front of him to block his path. ŇHermione might have made a mistake, but sheŐs still your friend. And what about Dumbledore?Ó Harry wasnŐt prepared for Ron. Ron wasnŐt part of the plan, and Harry felt like kicking himself for not being prepared for the inevitable. He thought he would have been able to work his way up to playing the part with anyone besides Hermione and Neville. He swallowed, hoping his nervousness wasnŐt terribly visible, and finally met RonŐs eyes. ŇWhat about him?Ó Harry asked, his voice quiet. Ron sputtered at that response. ŇYouŐre... I... heÉÓ He shook his head, apparently trying to regain his bearings. ŇDonŐt you have a shred of loyalty anymore?Ó ŇLoyalty?Ó Harry snapped before he could stop himself. Quite abruptly, Harry realized that the role would be far easier to play than he thought it would - at least at that exact moment. ŇYou mean loyalty to Dumbledore, who wonŐt even look me in the eye when heŐs speaking to me?Ó Harry snarled. ŇThat is - when he actually bothers to speak to me? Or when he tells my so-called ÔfriendsŐ to completely isolate me for the entire summer, and they actually do it? ŇOr do you mean loyalty to you, who dropped me like yesterdayŐs rubbish the moment I was re-sorted to Slytherin?Ó Harry continued, sneering. ŇYou really want to talk to me about loyalty, Weasley?Ó Harry finally allowed himself to smirk, knowing full well that heŐd have no problem pulling it off now. ŇI think IŐll take tenÉ no, make it twenty points,Ó he said slowly, his smirk growing, Ňfor being an arrogant, self-righteous prick.Ó RonŐs eyes were wide as he stared at Harry in complete and utter shock, and Harry felt a flash of satisfaction run down his spine. PansyŐs shriek of laughter brought Harry out of whatever spell heŐd been under, and Harry raked his eyes across the group. Ernie looked appalled, while Neville and Hermione were both staring at him in horror that looked incredibly real. Harry didnŐt doubt that it probably was incredibly real. ŇCome on, Harry,Ó Draco said, looping his arm into HarryŐs and guiding him towards the Great Hall. ŇLetŐs get out of this stench of DumbledoreŐs Army before I lose my appetite.Ó Harry wrenched his eyes away from Hermione and Neville and let himself be pulled towards the hall. Umbridge stood in front of them with a disgustingly satisfied smile on her face. She offered them a nod before she turned and damn near waltzed into the Great Hall. As they walked, HarryŐs horror at what heŐd just done kept growing. What heŐd said to Ron was all real. It wasnŐt supposed to be real. Draco dragged him towards the side of the table where he and Pansy usually sat. Harry couldnŐt help but be grateful for Draco's foresight as he took a seat with his back to the Gryffindors. Harry wasnŐt sure he could handle looking at them. More importantly, he wasnŐt sure he could handle them looking at him. ŇThat was absolutely brilliant, Harry,Ó Draco whispered in his ear. Harry knew heŐd played the performance well, but the pit yawning open in his stomach was even more unpleasant than it had been when Umbridge had pinned the silver ÔIŐ pin to his cloak. It wasnŐt supposed to be so real. And it wasnŐt supposed to be so easy. *** Harry somehow managed to go the rest of the day without having to say anything to anyone outside of his house. He settled for standing back and trying to look intimidating while Pansy and Draco went to work. He wasnŐt sure he was pulling it off all that well; he certainly didnŐt have Vince or GregŐs stature, and he wasnŐt all that imposing of a figure. But Pansy had been right in one respect: his mere presence with the other Inquisitorial Squad members seemed to rile up the rest of the school without him actually having to take action. A mix of shock, dark looks, and death glares were sent in his direction every time they were seen. Most of them didnŐt dare say anything directly to Harry aside but there were a couple of surprises, mostly from Susan Bones. ŇI wish my aunt had voted to have you expelled, after all, Potter,Ó she hissed at him, only to have Pansy promptly remove five points from Hufflepuff for wishing ill on Harry, another five for questioning the decision of the Wizengamot and therefore the Ministry of Magic, and then five more for not taking proper care of her hair. Angelina Johnson didnŐt address Harry with words, but she did flip two fingers in a ÔvŐ in his direction. Draco took ten points from Gryffindor for using an obscene gesture. By the time they were headed back to the common room after dinner, Harry was exhausted. He had no idea how he would be able to keep up the pretense for however long it took to unseat Umbridge, and he wanted nothing more than to curl up in his bed with a good dark arts book. Beltane was less than a month away, and Harry wanted to make sure he was prepared to complete his last personal rite within the next night or two. Unfortunately, as they approached the entrance to the common room, Snape came into view. He was apparently waiting for someone and judging by the way his eyes instantly locked on Harry, it was obvious who it was. ŇPotter, with me,Ó Snape said. Draco shot Harry a look that he couldnŐt quite interpret, and Harry could only let out a fatigued sigh before following Snape down the hall. They hadnŐt gone far before Snape drew his wand and almost absently waved it in the air, his eyes glued on HarryŐs face as he did it. Harry recognized the silencing spell, and he swallowed nervously as he stared up at Snape. ŇThat was anÉ interesting performance outside the Great Hall today, Potter,Ó Snape said. ŇProfessorÉ Headmistress Umbridge seemed particularly impressed.Ó Harry had no idea how to respond to that. ŇSir?Ó ŇExactly what plot are do you think you are hatching?Ó SnapeŐs lip curled upwards. ŇIÉÓ Harry quite abruptly realized that he had absolutely no clue as to where to start explaining the Ôplot.Ő ŇAnd are your housemates aware of whatever it is?Ó ŇIt was actually their idea,Ó Harry said quietly. ŇEither Draco or Blaise; IŐm not sure.Ó SnapeŐs eyes flashed. Whether it was in anger or surprise, Harry had no idea. Snape was silent for a moment, and when he finally spoke his voice was more of a hiss. ŇWhy?Ó ŇToÉ get close to her, I suppose,Ó Harry said. ŇTo try and find something on her we can use.Ó Snape didnŐt move a muscle, but something in his face shifted again and Harry suddenly felt as if his professor was looming over him. ŇHad you consulted with your head of house before enacting this foolish plan, I would have advised you not to,Ó he sneered. ŇBut seeing as how backing out now would put you in UmbridgeŐsÉ less-than-good graces again, youŐll have no choice but to see it through. ŇYou will immediately explain what you expect to accomplish, and why you and your housemates felt it was necessary to attempt such foolishness,Ó he continued. ŇOr else I will be forced to assign so much detention that you will have no choice but to resign from your newÉ position.Ó Harry swallowed. As much as he despised playing such a despicable role with Umbridge, he did not want to spend the rest of his life in detention with Snape. ŇSheŐs a threat,Ó Harry said. ŇI know you can see that. She already managed to drive Dumbledore away, and -Ó ŇDriving Dumbledore away at this stage is only helpful for you, Potter,Ó Snape pointed out. Harry narrowed his eyes. ŇHe was also the only thing standing between her and taking over the school,Ó he said. ŇSir,Ó he added, almost as an afterthought. ŇIŐm not that selfish.Ó ŇShe may be hampering the education ofÉ the student populace, but that still isnŐt severe enough to -Ó ŇSheŐs planning on introducing new legislation at the Ministry,Ó Harry said. ŇAgainst Muggleborns.Ó Snape seemed to freeze, and Harry used to opportunity to explain the Muggleborn Registration Commission: how Umbridge wanted Muggleborns hunted down, their wands stolen, and placed on trial. As he spoke, something astounding seemed to happen: SnapeŐs expression grew more and more severe. Harry swore that he looked absolutely furious, but a furious Snape was normally a very loud Snape. Instead, he said nothing. When Harry was done, though, Snape seemed to wipe any expression off of his face, and he gave Harry the most neutral look heŐd ever given Harry since theyŐd first met. Clear your mind, Harry thought absently, wishing he had SnapeŐs talent. It would certainly come in handy .Holy shit. If anyone knew anything about spying, it would be Snape. ŇSir?Ó Harry suddenly asked before he could stop himself. ŇHow do you pretend to be something youŐre not?Ó A flicker of surprise seemed to run across SnapeŐs face, but Harry thought he may have imagined it, especially when a sneer appeared on SnapeŐs lips. ŇAre you actually deigning to compare my position to yours?Ó ŇUhÉÓ Harry swallowed. "Of course not." ŇIn case youŐve forgotten, Potter,Ó Snape said, ŇI was once actually a Death Eater. What I do with the Dark Lord isnŐt so juvenile and asinine as pretending.Ó ŇNo, sir,Ó Harry said quickly. Snape paused, still fixing a displeased look on Harry. ŇYour performance today wasÉÓ He trailed off, and that, more than anything, startled Harry. Snape always finished his insults. Instead, Snape seemed to change track. ŇYou need to continue doing what you did today,Ó he finally said after a few moments of consideration, and HarryŐs eyes widened in surprise. ŇYou used grains of truth to perpetuate the role you are playing. Am I correct?Ó Harry sucked in a breath. ŇYes, sir,Ó he said, his voice quiet. ŇUsing the truth will always work better than Ôpretending,ŐÓ Snape said snidely. ŇChannel every bit of anger youŐve ever had at your old house. DonŐt try something so half-witted as Ôpretending.ŐÓ That didnŐt sound like something Harry wanted to do, but Snape was right: what heŐd unleashed on Ron earlier had been very real, and he knew he wouldnŐt be able to top that performance unless it was something else that was also very real. He found he couldnŐt respond vocally, so he simply nodded. ŇYou are still immensely stupid for attempting this.Ó Harry just nodded again. Snape rolled his eyes and escorted Harry back to the Slytherin common room. *** Harry trudged down the hallway the following morning, trailing behind Blaise with Theo not far behind. HeŐd once again dreamt of the hallway in the Department of Mysteries, and his inability to open the door left him frustrated and irritated upon waking. After the remnants of his dream had cleared, all of the anxiety about the Inquisitorial Squad came rushing back. Overall, he was in a miserable mood. ŇYou seem just about as awake as Theo today,Ó Blaise quipped. ŇYou all right, Harry?Ó Harry just grunted in response. ŇDidnŐt sleep well.Ó ŇYouŐll need to be more chipper than that if youŐre going to enter the Great Hall without another member of the illustrious Inquisitorial Squad,Ó Blaise said. ŇIŐd think Umbridge expects you to be alert and alive, ready to detract points from some poor unsuspecting Hufflepuff for breathing too loudly.Ó That just made Harry groan. ŇWhat the hell was I thinking, agreeing to this?Ó ŇIŐd love to be in your shoes, Harry,Ó Blaise said. ŇI, unfortunately, burnt all of my bridges with Umbridge earlier in the year. ŇIt would be nice to dish out half of whatŐs been done to us.Ó Harry sighed, knowing he wouldnŐt be able to convince Blaise that retaliation would be counterproductive. ŇAnd itŐs not just all the older students cornering us or attacking us behind our backs; itŐs the teachers, too,Ó Blaise continued. ŇYou remember our very first year at Hogwarts - back when you were a Gryffindor - the house cup was stolen from us.Ó Blaise scowled. ŇI know you mustŐve felt swell and recognized for doing such a Ôgood deed,Ő but imagine how we felt. ŇI didnŐt ask him to do that.Ó ŇI know you didnŐt,Ó Blaise said. ŇBut even as wretched of a person Umbridge is, itŐs nice for the odds to be in our favor, for once.Ó ŇSo you want me to dish out punishment for you,Ó Harry said dryly. ŇAny requests?Ó ŇPlenty,Ó Blaise said. ŇEnos Silverling, seventh year Ravenclaw. Zacharias Smith - heŐs spoiled rotten - possibly more than Draco -and heŐs on your little list. Linnea Fourpetal, sixth year Hufflepuff. Cormac McLaggen, and I think you would agree that he needs more punishment, as glorious as that head enlargement was earlier this year.Ó He snickered. ŇI still wish IŐd been there to see that.Ó Harry let out another sigh but silently conceded that it likely would be easy to remove points from McLaggen, especially when he remembered Astoria being shoved forcefully into a wall. Using real anger would work better than manufactured anger, after all. ŇWhat is Granger doing down here?Ó Blaise suddenly asked, and Harry jerked his head up. Hermione was down the hallway, standing to the side, ignoring the dirty looks and snide comments from other Slytherins as they passed. ŇWe need to talk,Ó she said to Harry as he approached, her voice stern, Ňright now.Ó Harry glanced around the hallway. A few Slytherins were heading to breakfast, and aside from Blaise and Theo, none of them were aware that Harry was only playing a part. He didnŐt want to appear too agreeable, but it was obvious that Hermione wouldnŐt give up. To her credit, Hermione apparently caught on to HarryŐs hesitation. ŇPlease?Ó she asked, her voice softer. ŇI know that youŐre angry with me, butÉÓ ŇHe dropped you, Granger,Ó Montague said snidely as he walked by them. ŇAbout time, too.Ó ŇItŐs far too early for this,Ó Harry bit out, forcing a scowl onto his face. ŇWhat do you want?Ó Warrington was right behind Montague, and he shot Harry a disbelieving look. ŇDonŐt tell me youŐre actually going to give her another chance,Ó he said, sneering. ŇThat anger was all real.Ó Harry hated the way she was looking at him, but he finally nodded. ŇIt makes sense that you have to use the truth in order to make people believe a lieÉ especially since youŐre such an honest person." That was the worst possible thing Hermione could have said. With a swift, sharp intake of breath, his stomach twisted unpleasantly and a chill ran through his entire body. Harry had been lying to Hermione for months. His horror must have been obvious in his expression, but Hermione thankfully didnŐt know what it meant because she merely stepped forward to wrap him in a tight hug. He wanted her embrace to ground him, but it only served to make him feel worse. He wasnŐt honest at all. HeŐd been manipulative, deceitful, and altogether unworthy of HermioneŐs trust in him. It was only when she stepped back to stare at him fondly that he was able to get his bearings again. ŇI know. ŇYou know I will, Harry.Ó *** ŇObsecro te venire. Mecum, tueri, dirige. Offero tibi sui.Ó He welcomed the comfort the dark offered as it gathered around him. ŇA circle has no beginning, and it never ends. I seek to know that magic. I seek to know that strength.Ó He certainly would need the strength of the dark to face everything that was culminating in his life. He had to convince as many Slytherins as he could that Voldemort wasnŐt the only path for them. He had to convince the rest of the world that Voldemort was back, and in order to do that, he had to contribute to the rumor that he would actually consider joining the person who had murdered his parents. He had to convince Umbridge that he was genuinely working for her cause because he not only had to find out what she was doing to the other students; he had to protect the other students from whatever she was doing. He had to do all of that on top of studying for his O.W.L.s, trying and failing to learn Occlumency, and continuing to hide his journey on the dark path from his closest friend. He had no idea how he was going to manage it all. ŇI need to know that strength,Ó Harry whispered. ŇPlease help me. I know I havenŐt finished declaring, but IÉÓ He let his eyes drift up into the night sky. ŇI need help.Ó Dark magic brushed his cheek in answer, and it seemed to whisper wordlessly as him, much like it had done on the Solstice. YouŐre safe. YouŐre where you belong, it seemed to convey. You already have that strength. Harry took in a shaking breath, nodding. He drew his wand and lit SiriusŐs candle, then DracoŐs. ŇThis is the last step I have to take before my final declaration,Ó he said. ŇI have dedicated my blood and my magic, and tonight I dedicate myself.Ó He lit his own candle, and as it flared to life a thrill went down his spine. He didnŐt waste a moment; he reached into his bag and pulled out a feather. It was long, though not terribly, and it seemed to glisten black and purple in the flickering candlelight. It was the tail feather of a blackbird, and it was a feather unique to the Black dedication rites. Harry supposed it made sense; it was a black bird, after all. But Draco had explained some of the stories behind the blackbird, as well as why their feathers were so appropriate to use in a rite of self - especially for Harry. The blackbird tended to sing at dawn and at dusk, making it known as a herald of change. It sang as the dark became light as well as when the light became dark, marking the boundaries between the two but never keeping one away from the other. If one heard a blackbirdŐs song outside of dusk or dawn, it was supposedly a sign that something in the life of the person who heard it would change. Harry smiled at the feather and placed it in his palm, holding it upright in front of him. ŇI offer the dark my body and self,Ó Harry said, his voice cracking with emotion. ŇPlease allow me to become your child.Ó His vision immediately went black. He seemed to be standing in a void; he couldnŐt tell which way was up or down, or what he was even standing on. Nothing had form and pure darkness surrounded him. His own breathing seemed far too loud, as if it was interrupting the silence. He pivoted on his feet and looked all around him, and it was just black as far as he could see. ŇWhat is this?Ó he whispered. He turned around again, more slowly this time, and another being had appeared behind him. It was himself. It was like looking in a mirror - though a proper wizarding mirror, not a Muggle one, as the other him didnŐt mimic his every movement. He was dressed in his Hogwarts uniform, Slytherin tie, crest, and colors, although Harry found himself a bit grateful that the other him didnŐt wear a silver ÔIŐ pin on his robes. Overall, they appeared completely identical. Understanding suddenly ran through him like a lightning bolt. This was him - but it was the him he would become. This was him as a dark wizard. Even after he finished his declaration, even after he became a child of the dark, even if he truly did change, he would still be the same person. He would still be Harry. Harry smiled at his other self, and he sent a silent word of gratitude to the dark for showing him. The other him suddenly smirked at him, and it couldnŐt have been longer than a fraction of a second - so fast that if Harry had blinked he would have missed it - but his eyes flashed red before returning to familiar green. And then he disappeared. ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked. He ran forward a few steps to where his other self had been standing, but he was alone in the void once more. He spun on his heel to see if he could find the other him again, only to find that the void behind him had completely disappeared. He was staring into what appeared to be a living room. He was in a house that he was certain heŐd never been in before, but it somehow felt incredibly familiar and he had no idea why. But as he explored the living room, crossing into a dining room, he seemed to know where all the furniture was, where every wall was, and he knew that if he turned the corner he would come to a set of stairs leading to a second floor. He peered up the stairs, wondering why he knew that at the top of the stairs and immediately to the right, a room would be there. He ascended, his hand gliding up the banister as he went. Immediately to the right at the top of the stairs was a door, and the door had a symbol painted with blood, just at eye level. It didnŐt really look like anything heŐd seen before, though it resembled the runes that Blaise and Hermione would study. There was a long line down the middle, with other lines sticking out at various angles. He reached out to trace the middle line, running his finger from top to bottom, and when he drew his hand away his index finger was red and glistening. He stared at the symbol for another long moment before reaching down and turning the knob, pushing the door open. His vision immediately filled with bright green light, and when he blinked against the glare, it was gone. He was back at Hogwarts, three candles burning away in front of him. He still held his hand in front of him, palm turned upwards, but the feather was gone. The red candle in the middle - his candle - started sparking and sputtering, and the flame seemed to grow beyond what the wick should have been able to maintain. The flames on the two black candles on either side seemed to lean inwards, towards the larger flame in the middle, before suddenly extinguishing themselves. Harry noticed it almost immediately; starting at the bottom of the red candle, black ink seemed to appear. It swirled up the sides of the pillar, spiraling and engulfing every inch of red. When the black reached the top of the candle, the flame gave one last flare before going out. HarryŐs breath caught in his throat as he picked up his candle. It was now completely black, identical to SiriusŐs and DracoŐs. He was one step closer to his final declaration, and more than anything else he felt like crying. He couldnŐt - the tears just wouldnŐt come - but he found himself wishing he could. He instead grasped his black candle in both hands, hunching over, and took in several gulping breaths. Seeing his candle turn black - actually seeing it transform - seemed to make his biggest worry all so much more real. He was going to declare dark in under a month. He would be a dark wizard, and he wouldnŐt be able to keep it hidden for long. And when the truth came out, he knew he would lose Hermione forever. Chapter End Notes Harry's got a lot going on, doesn't he? Chapter24: TheQuestion Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. ŇIs that why you were such a miserable date for my sister at the Yule Ball?Ó Harry blinked, confused. ŇWhat?Ó ŇMaybe you were already working for You-Know-Who then, and you just spent the whole time wishing you could have been there with some Death Eater,Ó Parvati snarled. ŇParkinson, maybe?Ó Padma added. Harry stared at them, partially dumbfounded and partially horrified, but Draco let out a loud laugh. ŇSounds like youŐre looking for excuses as to why he didnŐt find you all that thrilling, Patil.Ó Shaking off his stupor, Harry forced a smirk onto his face. ŇAnd itŐs no wonder why, given yourÉ charming personality. Or lack thereof, anyway.Ó Draco snickered. ŇFive more points from Ravenclaw, just to even it out,Ó Harry continued. ŇDid You-Know-Who come back or not?Ó This was exactly what Hermione had warned Harry about. ŇThe Dark Lord hasnŐt returned.Ó His words had the effect that Harry wanted - or needed, rather, as he certainly didnŐt want people to believe the rumor - because ErnieŐs eyes widened in horror. After all, Harry Potter, up until recently, was quite well-known for using VoldemortŐs actual name - never calling him ÔYou-Know-WhoŐ or ÔHe-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named.Ő Most of the witches and wizards who tended to call him the ÔDark LordŐ were sympathizers, at the least. Harry needed them to believe that he was the one who had changed, rather than letting them doubt the fact that Voldemort was back. Ernie had nothing to say in response; he just continued staring at Harry with betrayal written all over his face. Draco let out another laugh and they turned back towards the Great Hall, but HarryŐs steps faltered when he spotted Professor McGonagall watching them with a severe frown on her face. ŇCould I borrow you for a moment, Potter?Ó McGonagall asked. Harry swallowed. ŇOf course.Ó He gave Draco a nod. ŇIŐll meet you in there.Ó Draco nodded and gave McGonagall a nasty look as he passed her. McGonagall immediately pulled Harry to the side of the entrance hall. ŇIÉÓ Of course there was something he wanted to tell her. ŇMy door is still open, Potter,Ó she said, and she departed without another word. Harry wished he could let go of some of the tension that wound itself so tightly around his body, but he couldnŐt - not with Umbridge still watching him. Instead, he opted for rolling his eyes at McGonagallŐs retreating back and heading for the Great Hall. But just as Harry had nearly reached the entrance, Umbridge beckoned him over. Harry cursed internally. If he got any more tightly wound he was fairly sure that he was going to pop. He let out a silent sigh but obediently went to her. ŇYes, Headmistress?Ó he asked. ŇIŐd like you to come by my office after dinner tonight, Harry,Ó Umbridge said, and Harry had to suppress an unpleasant shiver at her using his name with such familiarity. ŇI need your assistance with something.Ó ŇOf course,Ó Harry said, nodding. Inside, though, his stomach seemed to turn over on itself. *** ŇWhat do you suppose she needs your help with?Ó Pansy asked at dinner that night. ŇYou, specifically, I mean?Ó ŇProbably something thatŐs gonna make Harry blast a hole straight through the castle,Ó Blaise said, snickering. Harry rolled his eyes and flicked a pea into BlaiseŐs temple. ŇJust be careful with it,Ó he said. ŇAnd let us know what you find out this time,Ó Draco added. ŇWhat are you looking for, anyway?Ó Harry said. Blaise just smirked. ŇIŐll let you know when I find it.Ó *** ŇThank you for being soÉ punctual, Harry,Ó Umbridge said as she closed the door behind him. ŇOf course, maŐam,Ó Harry said. Umbridge flooed the kitchens, instructing the house elves to bring a pot of tea to her office, then placed a chair directly in front of her desk. The tea arrived. Three teacups were lined up, and she carefully filled each one with tea. At least he wasnŐt continuing to worry about who Angelina could have told about his warning, or wondering what Umbridge had tried to put in her tea, or thinking of all the dirty looks heŐd been receiving from Gryffindor and the rest of the school all week, and how he would have to face it again each and every day until they could figure out how to topple Umbridge. At least heŐd be prepared for Charms. And he was. Flitwick was, surprisingly, exceedingly polite to Harry. Harry recited the rules of retraction flawlessly, and Flitwick applauded and awarded him fifty points. Unfortunately, only moments later Blaise lost them three hundred points for kicking over FlitwickŐs desk and proudly announcing that he was quitting Hogwarts to become a professional fan of the Holyhead Harpies. Draco shook his head as Blaise marched out of the room before leaning towards Harry. ŇIt wonŐt happen until thereŐs only one of you again,Ó he whispered in HarryŐs ear before gently pressing his lips to HarryŐs temple. They lingered there for a moment, then he drew back and gave Harry a sly smile before following Blaise out the door. Harry blinked, staring after them before quickly starting to gather up his things. He desperately needed to catch up with the others, but there seemed to be a hole in his bag. He kept shoving his Charms text into the top only for it to fall out the bottom. When Harry peered into his bag he couldnŐt see anything wrong with it, so Harry finally just scooped up his text and ran out into the hallway. The hallway was dark, and it looked almost sterile, completely unlike Hogwarts. In fact, it wasnŐt Hogwarts at all. Harry instantly realized he was back in the Department of Mysteries, and determination flooded into him. He wasnŐt letting anything stop him this time. He took off running down the hallway, knowing that the door he sought would be at the end. It finally loomed into his vision in the distance, and Harry pushed himself even harder, his legs pumping as fast as they could. He skidded to a halt in front of the door and placed his hand on the knob. Throwing his weight into the door, he wanted to crow in satisfaction as it finally started to open. Then his eyes flew open to the dim light of his dormitory just as the dying embers in the fireplace let out a loud Ôpop.Ő ŇFuck!Ó he swore in frustration. HeŐd been dreaming of that blasted door again. And heŐd almost had it this time... He rolled onto his back, frustrated, and dug a hand into his sweaty locks. And then a pillow again came flying out of the darkness and collided heavily with his face. Harry groaned and wondered why Blaise wasnŐt on the Quidditch team with an arm like he had. *** On Sunday, the Slytherins had decided to once again spend their day in the library. BlaiseŐs foul mood hadnŐt improved at all, and he kept snapping at everyone the entire morning. Daphne finally suggested that they switch to Transfiguration just so Blaise would have something to feel good about. It didnŐt work. ŇIf you donŐt know the difference between conjuration and summoning by now, why are you even here?Ó Blaise sniped at Greg at one point. ŇIf you donŐt cool down, Blaise,Ó Pansy said, ŇIŐm going to physically drag you out of this library myself so the rest of us can get some actual work done.Ó ŇAs if you could,Ó Blaise snapped back. ŇYouŐd be too afraid to break a nail.Ó Harry was getting a headache, and he promptly announced he was going to go find a book on conjuration just so he could get away from the table for a few minutes. As he walked through the stacks, he could see pockets of other fifth years that didnŐt seem to be in much better shape than the Slytherins. Terry Boot, Michael Corner, and Anthony Goldstein all appeared to be in a heated, whispered debate, and at one point Goldstein tore a book from BootŐs hands and tossed it on the floor. A few tables down he could see Hannah Abbott seemingly on the verge of tears as Susan Bones was tearing a piece of parchment to shreds with a furious expression on her face. If this was what everyone was like six weeks from their O.W.L.s, Harry would honestly be surprised if they made it through the school year without someone spontaneously combusting. ŇHello, dear Harry-kins.Ó Harry twisted around to spot Fred and George tucked down one of the aisles. Both had unusually stern expressions, a sharp contrast to their usual joviality. ŇHow fortunate it is that weŐve run into you,Ó Fred commented. ŇWeŐve been meaning to speak with you, you see,Ó George added. HarryŐs eyes widened and he quickly ducked into the aisle, then pulled his wand. ŇMuffliato.Ó Fred raised an eyebrow. ŇMuffling charm, Harry?Ó ŇQuite Slytherin of you,Ó George said. ŇAs is that very fashionable pin you have attached to your robes,Ó Fred added, scowling. ŇI canŐt be seen or heard talking to you two,Ó Harry said, keeping his voice low even with the muffling charm. ŇYou know that.Ó ŇYes, we heard from Hermione and Neville what you were trying to do,Ó George said. ŇBut we wanted to ask the question ourselves,Ó Fred added. ŇWe want to hear it from you,Ó George said. ŇYou are just pretending, right, Harry-kins?Ó The identical expressions on their faces suddenly made Harry very, very relieved that he had insisted on letting them know. The twins were honestly just a bit terrifying. ŇOf course,Ó Harry said. ŇThereŐs no way IŐd actually work for bloody Umbridge!Ó ŇAnd how about you suddenly calling You-Know-Who the ÔDark Lord?ŐÓ Fred asked. ŇYou havenŐt actually gone dark on us, right?Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry hissed, even as a nasty little voice whispered at him. But you have, it said. Just because youŐre not bowing to the Dark Lord doesnŐt mean youŐre not dark. ŇGood,Ó George said, nodding. ŇWe have to sayÉ youŐre a better actor than we thought you would be.Ó ŇHowever,Ó Fred continued, Ňwe also had an unusual report from Angelina last night.Ó ŇOh, no,Ó Harry said, his eyes widening. ŇHow many people did she tell?Ó ŇJust us, as far as we know,Ó Fred responded. ŇWe told her to hold off on mentioning it to anyone else.Ó ŇPlease tell her not to,Ó Harry said. ŇJustÉ spread the word to not drink anything Umbridge offers, okay? I saw her slip some kind of potion into the tea that she gave Angelina. I donŐt know what it is.Ó He let out a sigh. ŇAndÉ Angelina shouldnŐt know aboutÉ about me,Ó he added. ŇBut I had no idea how else to stop her from drinking.Ó He shook his head. ŇIŐm not very good at this,Ó he admitted. ŇWe disagree,Ó George said. ŇRonniekins is spending more time wondering what the hell is wrong with you than he is studying for O.W.L.s,Ó Fred added. ŇIt seems that heŐs quite worried about you.Ó Harry blinked. He hadnŐt expected that. He shook it off. ŇIŐll work on figuring out what the potion is,Ó Harry said. ŇMaybe I can evenÉ swap it, or somethingÉÓ He sighed. ŇAnd Blaise isÉÓ His eyes widened in realization. ŇÔBlaise?ŐÓ Fred asked. ŇYou mean Mr. Slytherin Zabini?Ó ŇYeah,Ó Harry said. ŇListen, can you talk to Lee about working with Blaise? He tried to speak with him last night, but -Ó ŇLee mentioned getting cornered by yourÉ friend,Ó George said. ŇHe wasnŐt pleased.Ó Harry rolled his eyes. ŇAnd Blaise wasnŐt pleased about him not being pleased,Ó he said. ŇJustÉ convince him, okay? Blaise is a little prickly sometimes, but he really is all right.Ó George sighed. ŇWeŐll try.Ó ŇAnd I hope this plan of yours is worth it, Harry.Ó *** After dinner, Harry and Draco retreated to the dorm. Draco wanted to finish his essay for Ancient Runes, but Harry decided that heŐd had enough school work for the day. Allowing himself to feel just a bit excited, he went to a little wood panel on the wall by BlaiseŐs bed. He and the others had modified it after it was decided that they shouldnŐt keep their dark arts materials in their trunks any longer, and it was quite the miniature library. Harry pried it open and dug out his books, flipping through the titles until he found what he was looking for. Harry was grateful to finally get a chance to look through the book heŐd taken from Grimmauld Place over the holiday break; he still didnŐt know why the dark had led him to it. As Draco got to work on his essay, Harry settled on his bed with Blood Magic: Rites, Rituals, Sacraments, and Sacrifices. He was planning on reading it from cover to cover later, but after the full day of revising for O.W.L.s, Harry felt he owed his brain a break. Besides, he wasnŐt sure heŐd actually really retain anything if he tried to do anything but skim through the pages. There were several interesting rites that heŐd be able to perform once he completed his declaration. While at the beginning of the year heŐd been tossing the idea aside of ever being able to complete any because he wasnŐt a dark wizard, he could now bookmark rites that interested him and weigh the idea of actually performing them. Harry only had a few more weeks before his declaration and he felt more and more excited by the day. He finished flipping through the rites and rituals and moved on to sacraments. It mainly seemed to cover oaths and dedications - promises that would be otherwise symbolic, but the added layer of blood magic would bring real consequences to anyone who broke the oath. He turned the page to the section on blood sacrifices and was pleased to see the same blood ward heŐd used at the beginning of the year - meant to keep anyone not of his blood out of his belongings - along with a few other wards and spells that focused on blood relatives. He flipped the page again and he froze, his eyes widening. There, at the top of the page, was a familiar symbol: a long line going up and down, with a few other lines sticking out from the center at a variety of angles. It wasnŐt like a tree or a rake; it looked like several runes stacked on top of each other. It was the exact same symbol heŐd seen painted in blood in his vision; it had been on the door at the top of the stairs of the oddly familiar house. Harry sat upright and eagerly began reading, his heart racing. Primum Cor, or ÔFirst Heart,Ő is another sacrifice designed for blood relatives. Its name comes from the idea that the first love one would experience is that of their family, as in the love between a parent and child or between siblings. The Primum Cor sacrifice is not well known or well documented. The sacrifice is both literal and permanent; the caster must die in order for Primum Cor to work as described. Due to this ultimate end, the casters do not live to tell of their success. The exact circumstances necessary in order to make Primum Cor successful are also incredibly rare, which lends to another reason why the Primum Cor is performed so infrequently. In order for a successful Primum Cor, all of the following circumstances must be true: * Both the caster and beneficiary must be blood relatives. * Both the caster and beneficiary must be facing imminent death. * The caster must have enough time to perform the Primum Cor, using their own blood to paint the glyph. * The caster must be killed prior to the beneficiary in order for the Primum Cor to take effect. * As blood magic this powerful almost certainly requires having an affinity for the dark, it can be assumed that the caster must have a strong dark affinity, though it is not known if the caster must have completed the necessary declaration rites. Upon the casterŐs death, a nearly undefeatable protection will be cast on the beneficiary. This protection is supposedly so strong that it will not only protect the beneficiary from certain death but may also be so powerful that it could drive an attacker back, if not outright destroying the attacker. Due to the rarity of all of these circumstances aligning, along with the caster needing the knowledge of the Primum Cor along with having the willingness to face death, the Primum Cor is the rarest blood sacrifice on record. In fact, according to written history, the Primum Cor has only been documented as being performed successfully two times: once in 1247 when Walter Selwyn sacrificed his life for his brother, Thomas, and one other time in 1681, when Rima Shafiq gave her life for her daughter, Sara. The exact circumstances of both events are unknown, though rumor and conjecture have led to the belief that both Rima and Sara were on the verge of being devoured by a Peruvian Vipertooth. Rima performed the Primum Cor, and upon RimaŐs death to the dragon, an immeasurably strong protection was laid over Sara. When the dragon attempted to consume Sara, RimaŐs sacrificial protection supposedly wound up destroying the Vipertooth. There is no proof of these events outside of hearsay. HarryŐs breath was caught in his throat, and he kept reading the entry over and over again. It couldnŐt be. ŇHarry?Ó Draco asked. His voice sounded faint, as if it was coming through a tube. But Harry couldnŐt tear his eyes away from the book. He finally lowered the book, placing it in his lap. He placed his index finger on the middle line of the symbol at the top of the page and traced it. He half expected his fingertip to be coated in blood when he pulled it away. ŇHarry!Ó A hand was suddenly placed over the book, snapping Harry out of his trance. ŇWhatŐs wrong?Ó For a moment, Harry could only stare at Draco, dumbfounded. Draco craned his neck to see what Harry was looking at. ŇWhat is it?Ó he asked. Harry swallowed, and it was hard to do so; a lump seemed to have formed in his throat. ŇIÉ I saw this symbol during my last personal rite,Ó he said, his voice sounding hoarse. ŇIt was in the vision.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Draco said, his eyebrows shooting upwards. He took the book from HarryŐs hands and turned it around to read through the passage. Harry could only watch him, still trying to wrap his head around the information. Draco quickly finished, and he looked back up at Harry. ŇWhy do you think the dark showed you this symbol?Ó he asked quietly. Harry didnŐt answer at first, but Draco persisted. ŇTell me what you saw, exactly.Ó Harry took a breath, closing his eyes to picture it. ŇItÉ I was in a house that seemed really familiar. I knew where all the furniture was, where all the rooms wereÉÓ His voice was shaking. ŇI didnŐt know what it was at the time, butÉÓ ŇBut what?Ó ŇThat house was GodricŐs Hollow,Ó Harry said. ŇI know that now.Ó He opened his eyes and looked down at the book once more, and then pointed at the symbol. ŇThisÉ this was on the door toÉ my room.Ó He raised an unsteady hand to run it through his hair, swallowing again. ŇI opened the door and there was a flash of green light.Ó He let out a breath, and when he continued to speak his voice was barely a whisper. ŇThe only thing I could remember about... that night... was a green light, andÉ my mother...Ó Draco let HarryŐs words sink in, but Harry could tell that he hadnŐt connected all the dots yet. ŇWhat do you think the symbol means, then?Ó ŇMy mother gave up her life for mine,Ó Harry said, his voice cracking. ŇAnd she used this.Ó The meaning seemed to have finally hit Draco because his eyes widened and his jaw fell open. ŇYour mother used sacrificial blood magic?Ó he asked. Harry could only nod, and he found himself reaching for DracoŐs hand without even realizing it. DracoŐs fingers wound together with his own, and the touch grounded Harry. ŇHarryÉÓ Draco swallowed, looking down at the book again. ŇThis is immensely powerful magic, and it says right here - the caster has to have a dark affinity, and thatŐsÉÓ He trailed off, appearing to consider his own words. ŇMaybe,Ó he continued quietly, Ňthe rumor about there never being a dark Muggleborn isnŐt true, either.Ó Finally allowing himself to smile, Harry nodded. ŇLooks like.Ó ŇNot only that,Ó Draco said, his eyes widening even further, Ňbut that means a Muggleborn defeated the Dark Lord using the dark artsÉ Harry, if this is true, this is huge.Ó ŇItŐs definitely true,Ó Harry said. ŇI know it is. It all fits. It exactly fits, and I have no idea why else the dark would show me that symbol in that house.Ó ŇButÉ where would she have learned it? Dark arts arenŐt available in the Hogwarts library, and dark arts arenŐt exactly common in Gryffindor,Ó Draco said. ŇYour godfather, maybe?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇNo,Ó he responded. ŇHe would have said something to me if that wereÉÓ He paused in thought, thinking over what little he knew about his mother, and his eyes widened. ŇHolyÉÓ ŇWhat?Ó ŇSnape!Ó Harry exclaimed. DracoŐs brow furrowed in confusion. ŇProfessor Snape? What about him?Ó ŇHe and my mother were friends for a while,Ó Harry said hurriedly. Another expression of shock appeared on DracoŐs face. ŇThey were?Ó he asked in disbelief. Harry nodded. ŇMcGonagall told me,Ó he said. ŇBut Snape is a dark wizard, and if they were friends, then he could haveÉ taught her. Or loaned her books - maybe a book that outlined how to perform a Primum Cor.Ó ŇYou need to ask him,Ó Draco said. ŇIf itŐs true that a Muggleborn can have a dark affinity, after all, then thatŐsÉÓ He shook his head. ŇThat puts some serious dents intoÉ into the darkŐs prejudice against them.Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇBut you know what else this means, Draco?Ó he said. ŇWhat?Ó ŇIt means,Ó Harry said, Ňthat my affinity isnŐt dark because of Voldemort, or because of my father.Ó He could tell that moment that Draco understood what Harry was about to say, and his fingers tightened around HarryŐs in a little comforting squeeze. HarryŐs smile broadened to a full-fledged grin. ŇIt means I got it from my mother.Ó Chapter End Notes I've been leading up to that reveal for months, and now that it's finally out there I'm simultaneously relieved and paranoid. >_> And because I keep getting asked about it: I do not have a posting schedule. I suggest subscribing to the story or my author page in order to get notifications of updates. I'm sorry for the inconvenience, but I will never have a schedule because they make me panic. You CAN expect a chapter every 1-3 weeks. The only hiatus this story has experienced was a little over a month, and that was when I moved three hours to the south. I'm not moving again any time soon. ;) Chapter25:The Shift Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! Snape appeared more than a tad startled when Harry not only arrived early for his career discussion, but also seemed to be laced with barely contained excitement. He actually grinned and he hardly waited for the door to SnapeŐs office to open before ducking inside, feeling almost elated that he was finally getting a chance to talk to Snape. He could barely believe it himself - heŐd been looking forward to this meeting since the previous night. HeŐd already been struck by how immensely odd that was, and Snape peered at him as if he'd grown a second head. Harry supposed he could understand SnapeŐs reaction; after all, he was never excited about needing to talk to Snape. Traditionally, they both tended to dread every single time they were forced to interact. One of the few things they agreed on was that they despised being in the same room together, especially alone. ŇSit down, Potter,Ó Snape said dryly. Harry sat on the very edge of the chair in front of SnapeŐs desk, one of his knees bouncing up and down uncontrollably. Snape gave him a suspicious glance as he walked to his desk, which was littered with a variety of pamphlets and brochures. ŇNot justÉ whatever it is you are doing now with Umbridge?Ó ŇIÉÓ Harry paused. He honestly hadnŐt thought of that. HeŐd known it, of course, but he hadnŐt really made that connection. ŇThe electives you chose to take have not left you much room for -Ó ŇItŐs about my mother!Ó Harry blurted out, jumping to his feet. Snape froze and stared at Harry, his hands stilling as they leafed through the pamphlets. Dead silence fell in the office. For a moment, neither of them even seemed to breathe. For once, Harry could actually read the emotion on SnapeŐs face, and Snape seemed to be just as uneasy as Harry felt. Harry was sure that heŐd almost certainly overstepped with Snape - as he usually did - but he couldnŐt find it in himself to care. ŇWhy would you ask me aboutÉ your mother?Ó Snape finally said, his expression unchanging. ŇBecause I was told that you knew her,Ó Harry said, holding SnapeŐs gaze unflinchingly. ŇThat you were friends.Ó ŇWho told you that?Ó ŇProfessor McGonagall.Ó SnapeŐs eyes rolled upward and his lip curled in distaste. ŇMinervaÉÓ he muttered. ŇI just need to know -Ó ŇIŐm not interested in reminiscing, Potter,Ó Snape snapped. He looked infuriated. Harry was not about to give up. He didnŐt care if Snape gave him a hundred detentions. ŇYou are the only one who would probably know this, though,Ó he said insistently. ŇPotter -Ó ŇSir, please!Ó Desperation clawed its way into HarryŐs throat, and it must have been evident in his voice because Snape looked more startled at that than anything else. But Snape didnŐt say anything. Harry gulped in a breath. ŇDid youÉÓ His voice was so hoarse that it could barely be heard, so he swallowed harder and tried again. ŇDid you ever loan her any books?Ó he asked. Snape studied Harry for a moment with that irritating, contemplative expression that had become steadily familiar over the past year. ŇA few,Ó he said, sounding quite wary. Harry clenched a fist. He wasnŐt sure why he was so nervous; he already knew the truth, but somehow, it seemed as if hearing any kind of confirmation out loud would make it even more real. ŇAnything onÉ blood magic?Ó SnapeŐs eyes widened. ŇWhy are you asking this?Ó His words came out slow, as if he wasnŐt sure that he actually wanted to know the reason. Harry suddenly felt as if he couldnŐt meet SnapeŐs gaze any longer, and he turned away, finding himself pacing back and forth behind the chair. ŇPotter!Ó Harry stopped in his tracks, closing his eyes. He took a deep breath and let it out. ŇMy mother used a blood sacrifice to save my life,Ó he finally said. ŇSheŐs the one whoÉ defeated Voldemort.Ó Surprisingly, Snape didnŐt react to VoldemortŐs name, at least not audibly. ŇShe gave her life for mine, and she used the dark arts to do it.Ó Snape still didnŐt offer a response, and after what seemed like a lifetime, Harry chanced a look at him. He appeared impossibly paler than usual, and his eyes didnŐt even really seem to be seeing Harry any longer. ŇShe... never returned that book,Ó Snape finally said. HarryŐs breath caught. ŇWhat spell was it?Ó ŇPrimum Cor,Ó Harry said, his voice barely above a whisper. ŇItŐs rare, but -Ó ŇIŐm familiar with it, Potter,Ó Snape snarled. ŇHow did you find this out?Ó Harry swallowed. ŇThe vision in my last personal rite,Ó he said. He quickly explained how heŐd been led to the book on blood magic over the winter holidays, what heŐd been shown in GodricŐs Hollow, and how heŐd only put two and two together the previous night. HeŐd never spoken so openly about his interactions with the dark - at least not with Snape .and that odd, speculative look continued to shine through SnapeŐs usually dour expression. ŇSirÉÓ Harry said, clenching his fist. ŇMy mother was dark, wasnŐt she?Ó Snape just kept staring, and Harry was beginning to grow irritated. It figured. All of his life, everyone only had stories to share about his father, and the only person who had information about his mother had to be Snape, of all people. And Snape wasnŐt exactly the sharing type. Harry let out a huff of annoyance and turned away from Snape, shaking his head. ŇShe almost certainly had an affinity for the dark,Ó Snape finally said, and Harry spun on his heel, surprise blossoming on his face. ŇShe was quite capable with the dark arts.Ó Snape paused. ŇBut she never declared.Ó Harry nodded, unsure what else he should do. HeŐd suspected as much - heŐd known, really .but hearing confirmation out loud from someone that had actually known her only cemented his relief. He was his motherŐs son, not an accident of VoldemortŐs. And then, to his surprise, Snape kept talking. ŇAs far as I knew, though, she stopped using the dark arts entirely when she was in her fifth year.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, blinking. ŇWhy?Ó ŇShe believed yourÉ the Gryffindors about how the dark arts were evil,Ó Snape said, his lip curling. Harry fully understood that SnapeŐs Ôslip of the tongueŐ wasnŐt accidental, but Harry couldnŐt find himself harboring any anger towards his father - at least not for that. It wasnŐt like James Potter was the only wizard to believe that the dark arts were evil, and he did eventually come to accept Sirius enough to have named him HarryŐs godfather. ŇShe started telling herself that her affinity for the dark wasÉ temptation,Ó Snape continued. ŇShe once embraced it, but she grew ashamed of it.Ó Something twisted in HarryŐs gut at SnapeŐs words. ŇSo sheŐd be ashamed of me, too,Ó Harry said without thinking, then abruptly shut his mouth. He wasnŐt exactly prepared to bare his soul to Snape; he just wanted information about his mother. And he wanted nothing more than for Snape to forget that heŐd said that, and he remembered that McGonagall had mentioned that the friendship between Snape and his mother seemed to have fallen out when they were around HarryŐs age. ŇNo, it isnŐt,Ó Snape said. ŇIt is your motherŐs past. And although I have come to realize that you are more like her than your insufferable fatherÉÓ He paused, sneering. ŇYou are not your mother, and her past is her own.Ó ŇBut she is the reason why I have a dark affinity,Ó Harry said, insistent. ŇAnd the dissolution of our friendship has nothing to do with your affinity,Ó Snape said, his tone making it clear that the subject was closed. Harry shut his mouth, fuming. ŇIf you truly want to work for the Ministry as an Auror, you will need to achieve an ÔOŐ on your Potions O.W.L.,Ó Snape said. ŇI suggest you start studying, as Mr. Malfoy will not be there to hold your hand. Now leave.Ó ŇI have another question,Ó Harry bit out. ŇIf I brought you a potion, would you be able to tell what it was?Ó He wanted nothing more than to leave the office, but he had to admit that dealing with the more immediate threat of Umbridge was probably more important than storming out. Snape raised an eyebrow. ŇWhat potion?Ó ŇItŐs clear, and I saw... someone slip it into some tea,Ó Harry said. Snape rolled his eyes. ŇUmbridge, I presume?Ó ŇHowÉ how did you know?Ó ŇItŐs Veritaserum,Ó Snape said. ŇI provided it to her at her request.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry snapped, infuriated. ŇHow could you do that?Ó ŇProviding Veritaserum is childŐs play compared to my position with the Dark Lord,Ó Snape said. ŇNo way -Ó ŇShe will suspect somethingŐs wrong if it doesnŐt work,Ó Snape said. ŇYou need to allow her to use it.Ó ŇI canŐt do that!Ó Harry insisted. ŇEspionage often requires an action - or a lack of action - that winds up helping the enemy. If you are unable to accept that, you will not succeed.Ó ŇBut -Ó ŇPotter, I am done,Ó Snape said. ŇI have career meetings with two more of your classmates before my next class. Cease wasting my time, and get out of my office.Ó *** When Umbridge questioned Katie Bell, Harry did nothing. ŇLetŐs go.Ó *** ŇAs the wizard among us who has been declared the longest, I open this circle.Ó HarryŐs body thrummed with anticipation as he and the others drew the circle. ŇThe darkness has protected and guided me, as I have protected and guided the dark.Ó He paused, taking a breath, and everything that had happened during his personal rites suddenly seemed to flash before him. He saw his blood dripping down like rain. He saw salt return to the earth. He saw the feather of a blackbird whisked away by the wind. And he saw the fire of his candle, flaring and transforming. All that was left was one last step. ŇI have dedicated my blood, my magic, and myself, and now hope that the dark will accept me as I become its child.Ó He tilted his head back and his gaze followed the point of his wand up to the stars, and he looked into the darkness of the night sky. He took one more breath, bracing himself for whatever was about to happen to him. ŇTenebris, ego sum tuus.Ó Unlike his affinity rite, where the dark magic had looked like a lightning bolt, this looked like a twister arching towards him. Dark, beautiful purple light swirled and churned, and his eyes widened in fascination and wonder as it grew closer and closer before it finally hit. It flooded into him. It seemed as if each and every interaction heŐd had with dark magic seemed like it was more than the last, and this was by far beyond anything heŐd felt. He was worried that the intensity of it all would make him lose consciousness, but instead he was more aware and awake than ever. And then something inside of him seemed to shift. ŇA circle has no beginning, and it never ends. This is our magic, and this is our strength,Ó he said, and his eyes seemed to keep darting back to Harry. ŇDonŐt trust me until youŐve seen it.Ó The warmth that had been flowing through Harry turned ice cold and he stared at Draco in horror, but Draco kept going. ŇDonŐt trust me. He felt Draco bury his face into his shoulder, his breath staggered and hot, and hands drifted up to grasp the cloth at the small of HarryŐs back. ŇI promise.Ó ŇIŐm going to have to choose between you and my father,Ó Draco said, Ňand itŐs going to be the hardest thing IŐve ever done.Ó It hurt to hear Draco say that, though Harry would never admit it. He obviously wanted Draco to choose him. ŇI know.Ó Harry dropped his head to DracoŐs shoulder, tightening his arms around him ever so slightly. He didnŐt want to let Draco go. ŇDraco, I promise IŐll look at your arm next fall,Ó he said, Ňbut you need to promise me something, too.Ó ŇWhat?Ó ŇWhenever you do make your choiceÉÓ Harry took in a deep breath. Ň... you make that choice for yourself. Not for your father, and not for me.Ó Draco was still for a moment before he nodded. And then he laughed, startling Harry. Draco lifted his head. ŇI guess thereŐs always the possibility that the Dark Lord would refuse to mark me,Ó he said. They talked about everything and nothing, wrapped together the entire time. Eventually they separated just enough to lay down, knees touching and fingers twined together. Soon, their whispers became drowsy and nonsensical and they began drifting off. Harry had nearly fallen asleep when Blaise came in, and he jerked awake, startled, while Draco slumbered beside him. Blaise merely took one look at them, put a finger to his lips, and drew the curtains around them. Smiling into the dark, Harry rested his head on DracoŐs hair, and let sleep retake him. For the first time in months, the endless hallways and the enticing door at the end were completely absent from his dreams. Chapter 26: TheRetribution Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! See the end of the chapter for more notes He found himself pleasantly curled around something that was warm and smelled good. He was only half-awake - if even that - and he basked in the relaxed serenity without really caring what it was. He fought consciousness for as long as he could until whatever he was curled around shifted. He fully woke and quickly realized his face was buried into a shoulder that was somehow simultaneously bony and comfortable. He blinked a few times to clear the fog from his eyes, then lifted his head only to wind up with a faceful of fine, white-blond hair that smelled faintly of citrus. Draco. Harry froze when he realized that heŐd fallen asleep in DracoŐs bed, but quickly let out a breath when he remembered that Draco didnŐt seem to have minded, and HarryŐs vague, sleep-addled memory of Blaise seemed to indicate that they likely werenŐt going to be teased about it by their other roommates. He quietly unwrapped himself from DracoŐs sleeping form, then rolled off the bed as gently as he could, grabbing his glasses from the nightstand. The unintentional sleeping arrangements probably should have felt odder than they had, but waking up closer to Draco than theyŐd been when they fell asleep somehow felt natural. Besides, he hadnŐt slept that well in months. Harry dawdled through his morning routine, showering, brushing his teeth, and dressing. Hufflepuff somehow managed to produce even more fouls than Slytherin, and it had only gotten worse as the game progressed. The crowd egged Hufflepuff on, cheering wildly every time a Slytherin was the victim of blatching, blurting, and especially cobbing. The loudest cheers came from Hufflepuff, but a fair number of students from Gryffindor and Ravenclaw joined in every time a Slytherin gained another bruise or was knocked off course. Harry was especially black and blue by the end of the game, which Slytherin won handily. The victory likely stemmed more from the number of penalties Slytherin had been awarded over decent flying. Hufflepuff was evidently more interested in physical violence over a win, especially since they were out of the running for the cup. Harry supposed it wasnŐt just the fact that he - the accused source of the HufflepuffsŐ misery .was on the team; the whole school had grown especially weary of the Inquisitorial SquadŐs run of the school, and Hufflepuff had been given the opportunity for some retribution. Angry Hufflepuffs, Harry had learned, were more than a little frightening. ŇRavenclaw is the only team that has a chance of catching us for the Quidditch Cup, and thatŐs only if they completely cream Gryffindor in the last match,Ó Montague said as he and Harry limped up to the hospital wing. ŇI need a favor, Harry. A big one,Ó he whispered. ŇMy cloak again?Ó ŇNo,Ó Blaise said. ŇHow many questioning sessions do you have left with the Gryffindors?Ó ŇUh, just one,Ó Harry said. ŇNeville.Ó Blaise nodded. ŇThatŐs better than I could have hoped. ItŐll probably be better if itŐs someone who knows, just in case they seeÉÓ ŇSees what?Ó Harry asked. ŇWhat are you asking for, Blaise?Ó ŇI need you to get the Vertitaserum from Umbridge.Ó *** The plan was all BlaiseŐs, but Harry was the one who had to pull it off. ŇLooks like the stinky Slytherins arenŐt very happy with our work.Ó ŇTime to move to another hall, then,Ó George replied, gathering up his catapult and what looked like several black orbs. ŇCome on,Ó Harry said, stepping onto the ice. I'm cackling. Chapter 27: TheSign Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. I discovered a plot hole that wasn't evident in my outline but became glaringly obvious once it was actually written. I thought I would have to rewrite a huge swath of this chapter and after moaning about it for days I realized I could fix it with one sentence. Naturally, Draco was gloating that heŐd almost certainly earned an ÔO.Ő They heard nothing from the Aurors, nor did they hear a peep from the recipients of the other two letters. On Tuesday came Care of Magical Creatures, and Harry, in turn, was able to gloat that heŐd certainly done better than Draco; Harry had witnessed Draco having to dodge out of the way of a gnarl when Draco had subjected it to his usual crass manner of interacting with magical creatures. There was again no word from the Aurors.
