Having studied both wizarding and Muggle Astronomy texts, she walked out feeling that sheŐd answered every question perfectly while Harry was certain that heŐd forgotten a few of JupiterŐs moons. After Astronomy, they parted ways so Harry could take his Divination O.W.L., which turned out to be the first O.W.L. where he was completely confident in what his results would be; heŐd almost certainly failed it horribly. When he exited the exam room with nothing but a sense of relief that he could finally drop the horrid class, he was surprised to find McGonagall waiting for him. ŇProfessor?Ó he asked. ŇIŐd like you to join me in my office, Potter,Ó she said briskly, turning on her heel and gesturing for him to follow. ŇImmediately.Ó Was he in trouble? As he followed McGonagall through the halls, he found himself becoming more and more anxious the closer they drew to her office. His anxiety turned to confusion when McGonagall waved her wand at the door after theyŐd entered. ŇImperturbable charm,Ó she explained when she saw his expression. ŇThis is a private conversation.Ó ŇUhÉ okay,Ó Harry said hesitantly. McGonagallŐs eyes seemed to be drawn to the silver ÔIŐ pinned to his cloak before her gaze settled on his face. ŇPotter, have you attempted to contact any Aurors lately?Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. Had the messages and the parcel been intercepted? Was Umbridge aware? When McGonagallŐs question was met with silence, she asked him another. ŇDoes the name ÔAsclepiusŐ mean anything to you?Ó It certainly did. Harry had no idea what the name meant, but it was the pseudonym that Blaise had chosen for his letters. When Harry again didnŐt offer McGonagall an answer, she said, ŇPotter?Ó Harry swallowed nervously and reminded himself that the door was imperturbed. ŇWhy are you asking me these things?Ó he said quietly. McGonagallŐs eyes narrowed and she frowned, seeming to consider HarryŐs words. ŇDo you already know why IŐm asking you these things?Ó Unexpectedly, Harry was hit with the urge to laugh. They were obviously both aware of the parcel that had been sent to the Aurors, but McGonagall didnŐt want to admit it until she knew that Harry also knew. ŇI do know,Ó Harry admitted. ŇA couple of Aurors received a package recently.Ó He paused. ŇI hope they did, anyway.Ó McGonagall let out a relieved sigh, and the tension that Harry hadnŐt realized was winding her so tight seemed to roll off of her in waves. ŇOh, thank Merlin,Ó she muttered before shaking her head and fixing a stern glare on Harry. ŇWeŐve been immensely worried about your recent behavior, Mr. Potter.Ó ŇIŐm sorry.Ó ŇThe best-case scenario any of us could come up with was that it was a ruse,Ó McGonagall continued. ŇSeeing you treat your friends so callously and bowing to UmbridgeŐs wishes was alarming enough, but then hearing you call You-Know-Who the ÔDark LordŐ has beenÉÓ She trailed off, pursing her lips. ŇI know,Ó he said. ŇButÉ I had to.Ó He paused, his eyes widening. ŇHow did you know to ask me about the package?Ó he asked, partially curious and partially alarmed. McGonagall frowned. ŇI assumed the pseudonym youŐd chosen was a sign.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Harry asked, confused. Had Blaise deliberately chosen a name that led them straight to Harry? ŇÔAsclepiusŐ - the mythical god of healing...Ó McGonagall paused. ŇHis most famous symbol is the serpent coiled around a staff.Ó ŇIŐmÉ not that well-read on myths,Ó he admitted, and then realization dawned on him. ŇYou thought that meant it had to be a Slytherin.Ó ŇIndeed,Ó McGonagall said with a nod. ŇWho else could it have been aside from you?Ó Harry frowned at the unsaid implication. Hagrid, Hermione, McGonagall, countless others .they all seemed to believe that Slytherins were only destined to accomplish terrible deeds and that only Harry could be capable of anything else. ŇIŐm not the only Slytherin, though,Ó he finally said. ŇAnd I donŐt know anything about ÔAsclepius.ŐÓ He shook his head. ŇI sent the package, Professor, and I might have helped get a piece of evidence,Ó he said, Ňbut I didnŐt get all of it, and I didnŐt write the letter.Ó McGonagallŐs eyes widened, but she appeared to school her expression, and Harry let out a silent sigh in response. ŇDid you think I was the only Slytherin that...Ó He sighed. ŇIŐm not the exception to a whole house, Professor.Ó ŇWhat do you mean?Ó Harry set his jaw and looked McGonagall straight in the eye. ŇTheyŐre all capable of good things.Ó McGonagall stared back at him with a contemplative expression. ŇI amÉ pleasantly surprised at hearing that, Mr. Potter,Ó she said. ŇAnd I apologize for making you feel the need to defend yourÉ housemates.Ó She then offered him a wry smile. ŇI will not apologize for assuming it was you, though. AlthoughÉ if it isnŐt you, who is Asclepius?Ó Harry shook his head. ŇIŐm not telling,Ó he said. ŇThereŐs a reason whyÉ they used a fake name.Ó Blaise had explained that he didnŐt want word getting back to his mother that he had actively tried to stop someone from introducing anti-Muggleborn legislation at the Ministry, and Harry wanted to respect his wishes. McGonagall appeared to accept that answer, nodding. ŇI see,Ó she said. ŇWell, thenÉ I feel the need to inform you of a few things. You may pass the information on toÉ Asclepius as you see fit.Ó ŇOkay.Ó ŇI have been called to the Ministry to give testimony to the Aurors that are conducting this investigation,Ó she said. ŇAlthough they feel that the evidence they received is enough, they would like to hear of some of my own interactions with Dolores Umbridge.Ó Harry nodded, unable to suppress the grin that was spreading across his lips. ŇI will therefore not be on the schoolŐs property from lunchtime tomorrow until late evening,Ó she continued. ŇYou should also be aware that Hagrid has left the property.Ó Harry gave her another nod, glad to hear that Hagrid had evidently taken his advice. ŇThis means that should anything happen that requires theÉ watchful eye of a Professor that is also a member of the Order,Ó she said, Ňyou should immediately go to Professor Snape.Ó She paused, raising an eyebrow. ŇI hope your relationship with him hasÉ improved somewhat since being moved into his house?Ó Harry let out a laugh. ŇNotÉ especially,Ó he replied, even as he knew that wasnŐt true. But he couldnŐt exactly say that Snape had been helping him cover up his use of the dark arts, as well as trying to protect him from Dumbledore, could he? ŇBarring any unforeseen circumstances,Ó McGonagall continued, Ňthe Aurors should arrive at Hogwarts early Friday morning to arrest the sitting Headmistress.Ó HarryŐs face lit up at hearing that. ŇReally?Ó he exclaimed. Then he tilted his head to the side in consideration. ŇWhatŐs taking so long?Ó he asked. ŇI meanÉ sheŐs been torturing students and using UnforgivablesÉÓ ŇUmbridge is a Ministry official, so the Aurors must tread carefully in order to obtain the necessary paperwork,Ó McGonagall said, obviously displeased. ŇIf the wrong person was to get wind of an impending warrantÉÓ Harry nodded. ŇAt leastÉ at least itŐs still happening,Ó he said. ŇIndeed.Ó McGonagall then offered Harry the most brilliant smile heŐd ever seen on the stern professorŐs face. ŇThank you, Mr. Potter.Ó ŇIt wasnŐt -Ó McGonagall waved her hand. ŇThank Asclepius for us, as well,Ó she said. ŇI hope that they will reveal their identity eventually so that we might thank them in person.Ó Harry returned McGonagallŐs smile. ŇIŐll let them know.Ó *** The miniature celebration in the Slytherin common room was short-lived, as they had their Astronomy practical that night. Even though the History of Magic O.W.L. wasnŐt even scheduled until the afternoon, Harry still wondered whoŐs idea it was to have the History exam the day after Astronomy. Despite getting to sleep in, Harry couldnŐt stop yawning, and he even saw Daphne nearly nod off after a grueling hour of answering questions that he didnŐt know the answers to. He sat up straighter in his desk, determined to not fall asleep as Daphne was threatening to. It was his last exam, and despite the fact that he barely knew a quarter of the answers he was determined to at least finish it. But as he stared down at the parchment the words began to swirl. He raised his head again and stared at the hourglass showing how much time was left for him to finish, and he found himself quite unable to look away from the trickle of sand falling into the bottom chamber. He walked through a dark corridor with confidence, the door at the end looming in his vision. He broke into a run without hesitation, and he nearly crashed through the door with his determination to get inside. He found himself in a strange room, full of numbered shelves of funny little glass orbs as far as he could see. He immediately started moving again, resolved to figure out what had been keeping him from this place for so long. He peered down each aisle with an air of curiosity and then paused when he saw a few figures near aisle ninety-seven. He quickly approached them, sneering. ŇYou will retrieve it for me,Ó he said in a cold voice to a figure that was half-collapsed on the ground. When the figure didnŐt move, he lifted his wand. ŇCrucio!Ó The power that burst from his wand gave him a sensation unlike any other, as if every bit of pain he caused translated into pleasure for him, and he laughed. The figure writhed on the ground in pain, letting out unholy screams. He released the spell and the figure trembled. Then the figure lifted his head. His face was bruised and blood-stained, and defiance shone in his eyes. ŇYouŐll have to kill me,Ó Sirius said. ŇOf that, I have no doubt,Ó he said. ŇYou will die here. But first, you will retrieve what I desire. No one will hear you scream down here, BlackÉÓ He began laughing again, but then the laughter turned into screams. Harry hit the floor painfully, but he paid it no mind as pain laced through his scar. He managed to get his screaming under control, but he still pressed his palm to his scar instinctively, as if he could push the throbbing pain back with the force of his hand. ŇMy word,Ó a voice said from above him. ŇAre you quite all right, Mr. Potter?Ó Harry managed to lower his hand, and his vision quickly focused on Professor Tofty, the examiner. ŇIŐm fine,Ó Harry gasped as the professor helped him to his feet and began escorting him out of the Great Hall. Harry tried to ignore the stares of the other students, but he desperately tried to catch the eyes of Hermione, Blaise, Draco, Ron -anyone who would possibly understand that he needed help immediately. ŇDo you need an escort to the Hospital Wing?Ó Tofty asked as the doors slipped shut behind them. ŇNo,Ó Harry said, still gasping. ŇI justÉ I fell asleep. I had a nightmare.Ó Tofty clucked his tongue. ŇThe pressure of examinations,Ó he said. ŇItŐs enough to -Ó ŇHarry!Ó The door nearly flew open and Draco, Blaise, and Pansy came pouring out. ŇAre you all right?Ó Draco said, immediately going to HarryŐs side. ŇWeŐll take him, Professor Tofty,Ó Blaise said. ŇWonderful,Ó Tofty replied, releasing Harry into DracoŐs care. ŇMake sure he has a good lie down, wonŐt you?Ó ŇWe will,Ó Pansy replied as Tofty disappeared back into the Great Hall, and Harry found himself surrounded as soon as the door shut. ŇWas thatÉ a vision?Ó Blaise asked quietly. ŇFrom the Dark Lord?Ó ŇYes,Ó Harry replied. ŇAndÉÓ He stopped, shaking his head. He didnŐt have time. Dumbledore was gone. McGonagall was gone. Hagrid was gone. Which leftÉ ŇCome on,Ó he said before taking off running, gesturing for the others to follow. ŇWhat -Ó Pansy cut herself off and Harry could hear their footsteps behind him. They struggled to keep up as he pushed himself as fast as he could go.. ŇWhatŐs going on?Ó Blaise shouted as they made their way through the halls. Harry knew he couldnŐt shout SiriusŐs name in the public corridors of Hogwarts, so he simply yelled, ŇJust follow me!Ó He made his way directly to the Slytherin common room. ŇFletus Hedera!Ó he hissed as soon as they reached the entrance. ŇNow will you tell us whatŐs happening?Ó Blaise asked, breathless, but Harry didnŐt stop; he continued straight into their dorm room. ŇSirius,Ó Harry said as he slammed through the door. ŇThe man who came with me to the Solstice celebration - Voldemort has him.Ó ŇHarry!Ó Draco hissed, anger evident in his voice. ŇWhy werenŐt youÉ you should have been Occluding!Ó ŇIf I had been, I wouldnŐt have known that Sirius is going to die!Ó Harry shot back as he threw his trunk open. He quickly dug through his belongings and without hesitation, pulled out one of the last secrets heŐd been keeping from his housemates: the MarauderŐs Map. ŇI solemnly swear that I am up to no good.Ó ŇWhat are you saying?Ó Blaise asked in disbelief, choking back a laugh. The familiar lines of Hogwarts began to appear before HarryŐs eyes, and he spun on his heel and dropped to his knees, then began spreading the map out on the floor. ŇHelp me find Snape.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Pansy asked as they surrounded the map. ŇWhatÉ what is this?Ó Draco said in amazement. ŇIs thatÉ is that us?Ó Pansy asked, pointing at the four dots with their names, huddled together in the Slytherin dormitories. ŇThis isÉ incredible!Ó Blaise exclaimed. ŇWhere did you get this?Ó ŇIŐll explain what it is later,Ó Harry said pleadingly. ŇJust find Snape!Ó The four of them scoured the map from top to bottom. ŇHeŐs not on here, Harry,Ó Draco said finally. ŇHeŐs supposed to be here!Ó Harry insisted, growing anxious and angry. ŇMcGonagall saidÉÓ He cut himself off, shaking his head. SnapeŐs secret still wasnŐt HarryŐs to tell, no matter how much heŐd come to rely on his Slytherin friends. ŇShit!Ó he swore, leaping to his feet. He paced away from the map, burying his hands in his hair. ŇHarryÉÓ DracoŐs voice was quiet. ŇAre you certain that what you sawÉ was even real?Ó ŇOf course it was!Ó Harry snapped. ŇHow do you know that?Ó Draco asked. ŇBecause my scar was on fire!Ó Harry said angrily. Draco had been so supportive and helpful all year; why wasnŐt he doing that now, when he needed DracoŐs support the most? ŇThe Dark Lord knows about your connection, though,Ó Draco said. ŇWhat if heÉ what if heŐs trying to trick you?Ó ŇIs there a way to get a hold of Black?Ó Blaise asked. HarryŐs eyes widened and he quickly went back to his trunk, his fingers wrapping around the small hand mirror. ŇSirius Black!Ó he nearly shouted at the mirror. Then he froze. If Sirius had the mirror with him while he was being held captive by Voldemort, he would be letting Voldemort know that someone was aware that Sirius had been captured. He swallowed, waiting and listening, but he heard nothing but silence from the mirror. Swearing, he tossed the mirror onto his bed. Then an idea struck him, and he ran to the fireplace. He reached up to the hearth and grabbed the container of floo powder. ŇHarryÉÓ Ignoring Draco, Harry scooped out a handful of the powder and threw it onto the fire. ŇNum-Ó His words choked in his throat and he quickly realized that the Fidelius was preventing him from saying the name of SiriusŐs home. Even so, it didnŐt seem to matter - the fireplace was violently spitting yellow sparks as soon as the powder hit the flames. ŇUmbridge blocked all floo calls except for the fireplace in her office,Ó Draco said. ŇShe told Pansy and me; I assumed sheŐd told you, as well.Ó ŇWell, she didnŐt!Ó Harry snapped. ŇHarry, please stop shouting at us,Ó Pansy said. ŇWe want to help you.Ó ŇSorry,Ó Harry spat. ŇIŐm a bit stressed right considering my godfather is being tortured and is about to be murdered!Ó Then he processed what Draco had told him and he immediately strode back over to the map, his eyes automatically being drawn to UmbridgeŐs office. Naturally, Umbridge was sitting right in front of the only working floo in the entire school. As a member of the Inquisitorial Squad, he would be welcome in there, but he needed her out of itÉ ŇCome on,Ó Harry said, quickly folding up the map. He slammed his trunk shut and immediately made for the door. ŇAre we running again?Ó Pansy asked unhappily, although she looked abashed when Harry shot her a nasty glare. They filed out of the Slytherin common room, Harry in the lead. ŇWhat is your plan, Harry?Ó Blaise asked as they jogged through the corridors. Harry didnŐt answer because he truly didnŐt know yet. He just knew he had to get into UmbridgeŐs office and to her fireplace. They turned the corner and nearly slammed straight into Hermione, Ron, and Neville. ŇHarry!Ó Hermione exclaimed. ŇAre you all right? What happened to you during the exam?Ó Harry was suddenly struck with inspiration as his eyes landed on Ron. ŇDo you have any more of those fireworks, Ron?Ó he asked. RonŐs mouth fell open and he seemed to draw away from Harry. ŇUhÉ of course not,Ó he said quietly, swallowing. Harry resisted the urge to smack a palm to his forehead. Ron obviously wouldnŐt admit that to him considering Harry was the one who had nearly turned him in for setting them off. ŇIŐm not trying toÉÓ he said, shaking his head. ŇLook, these two can vouch for me.Ó He pointed at Neville and Hermione. ŇI need a distraction that will get Umbridge out of her office.Ó ŇOf course I can vouch for you, but Harry, what happened?Ó Hermione said insistently. Harry quickly glanced all around them. He was satisfied that they were reasonably alone, but he still lowered his voice when he spoke. ŇVoldemort has Sirius.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Ron exclaimed. ŇHow do you know that?Ó Hermione asked. ŇI saw it,Ó Harry said. ŇWhen I fell asleep during the exam. HeŐs being tortured, and VoldemortŐs gonna kill him...Ó ŇHow?Ó Hermione asked, her face pale. ŇSirius isnŐt supposed to leaveÉÓ ŇI donŐt know how, but I know where,Ó Harry said. ŇTheyŐre at the Ministry, in the Department of Mysteries. He squeezed his eyes shut, but that only served to make the mental picture even more vivid. ŇSirius is gonna dieÉÓ Just then, a hand wrapped around his arm, and he looked up, startled. ŇHarry, we still donŐt know for sure if what you saw was even real,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇAs unlikely as this sounds, I agree with Malfoy,Ó Hermione said. ŇHave you tried getting a hold of Sirius?Ó Hermione asked. ŇHe already tried back in the Slytherin dungeons,Ó Pansy answered. ŇThatŐs why we need to get into UmbridgeŐs office,Ó Blaise said. ŇShe has the only working floo in the school.Ó Ron made a strangled noise and he stared at the Slytherins in disbelief. ŇYouŐre trying to trick your boss, then?Ó he asked. ŇDo you have anything that would be loud enough to get Umbridge out of her office?Ó Ron bit his lip. ŇIÉ I have a few whizbangs with me,Ó he said reluctantly. ŇOnly a few, though.Ó ŇTheyŐre not as loud as fireworks, but thatŐll have to be enough,Ó Pansy said. ŇI can tell Umbridge that we need her assistance, and then you set those off far enough down the hall in order to draw her away.Ó Ron scowled. And ZabiniÉÓ She trailed off and gave Blaise an appraising look. ŇHarryŐs not actually working for Umbridge, Ron,Ó Neville said. ŇHeŐs been faking it the whole time.Ó ŇWhat?Ó Ron exclaimed incredulously. ŇWhy?Ó Harry threw his hands up into the air, halfway wishing that heŐd only run into Hermione and Neville, not Ron. ŇWe donŐt have time to explain, Ron!Ó he snapped. ŇSirius is being tortured now.Ó How long had it been since Harry had left the exam? Twenty minutes? Longer? Sirius could already beÉ No. Harry wouldnŐt allow himself to think that. Pansy let out an exasperated sigh. ŇIf your Gryffindor friends wonŐt help, I suppose I can pull Umbridge out and lead her blindly across the schoolÉÓ she mused. ŇWeŐll do it,Ó Neville said. ŇRon, give me your whizbangs.Ó ŇNo, IŐll help,Ó Ron said, shaking his head. ŇLetŐs go.Ó The unusual troupe made their way up the stairs, pausing when they reached the hallway adjacent to UmbridgeŐs office. Harry pulled out the MarauderŐs Map, ignoring the disbelieving sound that came out of RonŐs mouth. ŇShould have figured you let the Slytherins know about -oof.Ó Harry glanced up and quickly determined that Hermione had elbowed Ron in the stomach, and then he looked back down at the map again. ŇSheŐs still in there,Ó he confirmed. They laid out the plan as quickly as they could, and Harry grew more impatient by the moment. The Gryffindors would move a few corridors away - close enough so Umbridge could still hear the whizbangs but far enough to give Harry time to use the floo. Pansy would pull Umbridge out of her office, while Harry snuck in with Draco and Blaise keeping watch outside. Pansy at first insisted that Draco come with her, serving as another Inquisitorial Squad member, but Draco adamantly refused. In fact, Draco had wanted to go into the office with Harry, but Harry explained that he couldnŐt be there due to the Fidelius. HermioneŐs eyes widened at his words, but she thankfully didnŐt say anything. ŇJust donŐt do anything stupid while youŐre alone in there,Ó Draco said, looking quite unhappy. They parted ways and the seconds seemed to drag on. HarryŐs scar prickled, but he oddly took comfort in that. Surely if Voldemort had killed Sirius he would have had another bout of blinding pain. He, Draco, and Blaise watched as Pansy knocked frantically on UmbridgeŐs door. Umbridge poked her head out, looking somewhat harried, and Pansy played it up, pointing emphatically down the hall in the other direction. On cue, they heard a whirring sound followed by a loud pop, and Umbridge and Pansy took off towards the noise. Harry sprinted down the hallway and burst into UmbridgeŐs office, immediately heading for the fireplace. He scooped out probably more floo powder than necessary and tossed it into the fire, a sense of relief coming over him when the flames turned green. ŇNumber twelve, Grimmauld Place!Ó he called, thrusting his head into the fire. ŇSirius!Ó he cried when the kitchen in Grimmauld Place swam into his vision, his knees still firmly planted on the floor of UmbridgeŐs office. The kitchen was empty and still, and a wave of dread passed through Harry. The whole house seemed quiet. ŇSirius!Ó he called once more, and then he was yanked back by his shoulders out of the flames. UmbridgeŐs office flared back into his vision, and Draco hovered over him with a pinched expression. ŇGet up and get your wand out,Ó Draco hissed. Blaise stood behind him looking frantic. Harry clambered to his feet, slipping his wand into his hand. ŇSirius wasnŐt -Ó ŇShut up,Ó Draco spat. ŇUmbridge is coming.Ó As soon as the words were out of his mouth, the door swung open and Umbridge marched inside with a look of smug satisfaction painted all over her unpleasant features. ŇHeadmistress!Ó Draco said, poking his wand into BlaiseŐs side. ŇHarry and I just caught Blaise breaking into your office.Ó Reluctantly, Harry pointed his wand at Blaise as well, and Umbridge gave them a sour, repulsive smile. ŇYour friends caught some wrong-doers, as well. Another wave of dread-filled adrenaline rushed through Harry when he saw Hermione, Neville, and Ron being marched into the office, being held at wandpoint by Pansy, and to his horror, the rest of the Inquisitorial Squad. Shit. Millicent, Vince, and Greg had no idea of the plan, and Pansy couldnŐt have possibly explained it with Umbridge present. ŇYou have been trouble all year long, Mr. Zabini,Ó Umbridge announced. ŇIŐll deal with you in a moment.Ó She turned to the Gryffindors. ŇBut first, I think itŐs time to expel these three, wouldnŐt you say?Ó Hermione gasped, her hands lifting to her mouth in horror, and Ron let out a shout. ŇYouÉ you rottenÉÓ He sputtered before shooting Harry a fiery glare. ŇYou did set us up, Potter!Ó ŇDid he, then?Ó Umbridge asked, not sounding as if she actually cared. ŇI think itŐs far more concerning that youŐve allowed yourselves to be manipulated by Dumbledore as you have.Ó ŇDumbledore didnŐt manipulate us!Ó Hermione said defiantly. They didnŐt have time for this. HarryŐs wand hand was shaking, and he saw Blaise shoot him an alarmed look. Sirius was getting tortured, and they were standing around playing pawn to Umbridge. ŇDumbledore may have his faults,Ó Harry said, his voice low and dangerous, Ňbut heŐs still a million times better than youŐll ever be.Ó A pin drop could have been heard in the silence that fell in the office. Umbridge slowly turned her head towards Harry. ŇExcuse me, Mr. Potter?Ó ŇYou heard me, you pathetic, simpering toad,Ó Harry snarled. Umbridge stared at Harry in utter disbelief before she pursed her lips in a disapproving frown. ŇI did have my doubts about you, Potter, and I see I was right to do so,Ó she said. ŇBut I think youŐll find that you are quite outnumbered.Ó HarryŐs only response was to move his wand from Blaise and towards UmbridgeŐs head instead. ŇYou couldnŐt have waited ten more hours, Harry?Ó Blaise said blandly. ŇI donŐt have ten hours, Blaise,Ó Harry replied. Sirius didnŐt have ten hours. Draco let out a long-suffering sigh before moving his wand, as well. One by one, the rest of the members of the Inquisitorial Squad moved their wands from their captives to Umbridge, even if Millicent, Vince, and Greg appeared to be a tad confused as they did so. UmbridgeŐs head swiveled, fear quickly clouding all of her features. ŇWhat is this?Ó she demanded. ŇWeŐve sent letters to The Daily Prophet as well as The Quibbler in case the Prophet didnŐt comply,Ó Blaise said. ŇLetters? Concerning what?Ó Umbridge asked. ŇYou,Ó Harry said simply. ŇUsing a quill infused with blood magic on students, dosing students with Veritaserum, using the Unforgiveables on students and then Obliviating themÉÓ Blaise ticked off her crimes on his fingers as he spoke. ŇEven if the warrant falls through, the press is going to have a field day with you.Ó Despite all of his worries about Sirius, Harry felt a flash of pride. Blaise had made that happen. ŇWarrant?Ó Umbridge repeated slowly. ŇYou should expect the Aurors to arrive by morning,Ó Blaise continued. ŇSorry,Ó Pansy said, not sounding the least bit apologetic. ŇI know that you probably wanted to do that, Harry, or that Blaise deserved to do it more, butÉ I really, really wanted to.Ó ŇWhatÉ what the hell?Ó Ron said, staring down at UmbridgeŐs crumpled form. ŇAll of you were in on this?Ó ŇDid you manage to get a hold of Black?Ó Draco asked Harry, ignoring Ron. Harry shook his head. ŇNo,Ó he said. ŇHe wasnŐt home. ŇOf all the stupidÉ Gryffindor ideasÉÓ Blaise said incredulously. ŇThat is precisely why I didnŐt want you at the floo alone!Ó Draco snarled. ŇYou donŐt even know for sure if the Dark Lord has Black -Ó ŇYes, I do!Ó Harry shouted back at them. ŇEither the Dark Lord has your godfather, or heŐs setting a trap for you!Ó Blaise spat. ŇEither way, youŐd be walking right into his hands, you idiotic Gryffindor!Ó ŇExcuse me?Ó Ron said. ŇHarry, I agree with them,Ó Hermione said, waving a hand to shush Ron. ItŐs whatŐs been happening all year -Ó ŇWe believe you saw what you saw, Harry,Ó Draco said, obviously making a concerted effort to calm his demeanor. ŇBut this is your complete lack of survival instinct rearing its ugly head again,Ó Blaise said, sneering. ŇI donŐt care!Ó Harry snapped. ŇPansy, shut up,Ó Draco said quietly. ŇI thoughtÉÓ ŇThis was just like the vision you had of the snake and my dad, wasnŐt it?Ó Ron asked. When Harry nodded, the resolve on RonŐs face became even more firm. ŇI quite agree,Ó Pansy said. ŇI donŐt care,Ó Harry shot back. Pansy stared over DracoŐs shoulder with a pinched expression, then sighed. ŇFine.Ó She pointed her wand at UmbridgeŐs prone form on the floor - Harry had nearly forgotten that she was there - and said, ŇIncarcerous!Ó A set of ropes wriggled their way out of PanyŐs wand and wrapped themselves around Umbridge, binding her tightly, and she then levitated Umbridge into the air. Draco shrugged, obviously trying to appear casual even as he swallowed nervously. ŇI already made it.Ó Warmth flooded through Harry at DracoŐs words, but it was immediately followed by fear. Once DracoŐs father found out, they had no idea what he would do. Even worse, it was entirely possible that his father would find out in a matter of minutes, depending on if he was at the Department of Mysteries or not. ŇI canŐtÉ I canŐt have you do this,Ó Harry said, shaking his head. ŇItŐs not up to you,Ó Draco said simply. ŇYou said it was my choice, right?Ó ŇWhat are you two even talking about?Ó Ron said, confused. ŇAnd why is Malfoy coming?Ó ŇThat makes me think even more that this is a trap, Harry,Ó Hermione said. ŇIŐll go with you, but Malfoy -Ó ŇI trust him,Ó Harry said. And that settled it. He trusted Draco, and he trusted DracoŐs choice. ŇAnd weŐre wasting time. LetŐs go.Ó ŇIf either of you wind up dead, IŐm killing you,Ó Pansy quipped as she began levitating UmbridgeŐs unconscious form out the door, quite deliberately knocking her head into the frame. ŇNoted,Ó Harry said before turning to the fireplace. He grabbed a handful of floo powder and with one last look at Draco, he took a breath before tossing it into the flames. ŇMinistry of Magic!Ó Chapter End Notes More video game references, and one Sandman reference. If anyone sees it... (nobody ever does). And I know from earlier comments that some of you might be unhappy that they're going to the DoM anyway, but all I have to say is... be patient. I was planning on saving this announcement, but enough people seem to be flipping out about it so I'll go ahead and make it now: YES, there will be a sequel to this story. I've actually been writing this fic with a 3-part series in mind, but seeing as how I'd never successfully tackled a project of this scale before, I didn't want to actually say that until I felt confident that I would at least finish the FIRST story. I'm still settling on a title, which is why 'Evitative' hasn't been added to a series - I didn't want to be that author that was all like 'Evitative, Part One of Untitled.' Regardless, I'll have a bit more information about the sequel at the end of the last chapter. Chapter 28: TheConfession Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! Of course I had to update on a palindrome day. :P I must again state that this is an AU of "Order of the Phoenix." Some pieces and dialogue will be very similar, though I have taken care to not copy large passages directly. It diverges at a certain point in the chapter, though. This chapter is also VERY self-referential. There are call-backs to most of the previous chapters in this story. Some are obvious. Some are not. Have fun. Update 2/6: Sharp reader chukimei caught a plot hole towards the end of this chapter that left me kicking myself. This is now fixed. (Only about three paragraphs changed, but it was all for the better.) Thank you so much for calling it out! <3 See the end of the chapter for more notes Harry barely spared a glance for the impressive golden statues surrounding the fountain, instead raking his eyes across the huge Ministry atrium. ŇIs it normally this empty?Ó Hermione asked quietly. ŇI know itŐs late, butÉÓ Harry shook his head, pointing at the empty security desk. ŇThere should be a guard,Ó he said. ŇSomethingŐs wrong.Ó The fact that the Ôsomething wrongŐ could very well be pointing towards Voldemort went unspoken. ŇCome on,Ó Harry said, already moving towards the rear of the atrium. ŇThe elevators are this way.Ó He hadnŐt even taken five steps when the fireplace behind them flared up again. He spun around, eyes wide, wand at the ready. A familiar figure appeared in the flames, looking a bit harassed as they stepped out of the fireplace. Harry gaped. ŇBlaise?Ó Brushing non-existent dirt off of his sweater before drawing his wand, Blaise merely offered them a nod. ŇSo youŐve come toÉ die horribly with us, then?Ó Hermione asked. Harry thought that if it wasnŐt for the dire situation theyŐd found themselves in, she would have sounded amused. Instead, it just sounded strained and uncertain. Blaise shrugged. ŇHarryŐs implied that IŐve already been channeling my inner Gryffindor this year - despite me not having an inner Gryffindor,Ó he said, sneering. ŇI may as well go all-in and die pointlessly like a Gryffindor, as well.Ó ŇHey!Ó Ron protested. ŇAnd when Pansy said sheŐd murder you if you died,Ó Blaise continued, Ňshe most likely would have just wound up murdering me.Ó He sighed before centering his gaze on Draco and Harry. ŇBesides, the thought of having to deal with a stressed Pansy all night while you two were off getting killedÉÓ He shook his head. Despite everything, Harry felt a wave a gratitude towards Blaise. Slytherins protect one another. ŇThank you, Blaise,Ó Harry said quietly. Blaise offered another shrug, clearly trying to appear nonchalant, but Harry could tell how tightly-wound he was. BlaiseŐs trepidation appeared to increase as he glanced around the atrium with wide eyes. ŇWhy is there no one here?Ó he asked shakily, unknowingly repeating HermioneŐs sentiment from earlier. ŇWeŐre... not sure,Ó Draco replied, his voice barely above a whisper. Harry couldnŐt help but wonder if his theory about Voldemort killing everyone on his way in was actually true, but he certainly didnŐt want to voice that thought out loud. Harry gripped his wand until his knuckles were white. ŇLetŐs go,Ó he said. They quickly made their way over to the elevators, and the six of them piled onto the nearest one. Harry stabbed the number nine with his finger and the metal gate closed with a clatter. The elevator groaned and clanked around them as it descended, punctuated by the sound of their nervous breathing. Harry hadnŐt realized how noisy the elevators were when heŐd come with Mr. Weasley on the day of his hearing, and he thought for sure that the sound would draw attention. Even so, when the elevator finally slowed to a stop, the door opened to an empty corridor, inhabited only by the flickering light of the torches. ŇDepartment of Mysteries,Ó a disembodied female voice announced. Harry ignored the chill that ran down his spine as he stepped off, the others quick to follow. ŇThis way,Ó Harry whispered. It sounded overly quiet after the jangle of the elevator. He moved to the dark wooden door - the one that had been haunting his dreams nearly every night for months. Harry pushed it open, and the group filed through. He glanced around and found that they were in a large, circular room. It was all black - black floors and black ceilings, black doors without handles that were evenly placed, each one divided by black candle branches. ŇShut the door,Ó Harry hissed, and as Neville did so he instantly regretted the request. The sliver of light that had come through the door had been much more helpful than the feeble light offered by the candles. As his eyes adjusted, he realized that there were dozens of doors to choose from, and Harry felt a spike of irritation. They couldn't possibly try every single one and still hope to get to Sirius on time. ŇWhich way, Harry?Ó Ron asked. Even though Harry knew that Ron didnŐt mean anything malicious with his question, it still magnified his ire tenfold. ŇFuck,Ó he whispered harshly, his face screwing up in anger. ŇWatch your temper, Harry,Ó Blaise bit out, only for Draco to shush him. A hand suddenly wound into his, cool and comforting, and Harry squeezed it in reflex. It was Draco. DracoŐs hand was trembling, and Harry quickly glanced at him. Draco appeared calm and collected, but Harry could tell that Draco was absolutely terrified, and he felt awful. Why had Draco come with him? Why had any of them come with him? ŇHarry?Ó Neville asked, voice laced with uncertainty. Shaking his head to clear it, Harry said, ŇWe need to find a room that kind ofÉ glitters.Ó He took a few steps forward, only for a deep rumbling to stop him in his tracks. The sparkling candles suddenly started moving, the flames swaying to the side. The room was rotating around them. ŇWhat was that all about?Ó Ron whispered fearfully. ŇI donŐt know, but now we canŐt tell which door we came in from,Ó Draco said, and Harry quickly realized that he was right. All of the doors looked the same, and although he knew the door theyŐd entered through was somewhere behind them and to their left, they had no way of knowing exactly which door it was. ŇHow are we going to get out of here?Ó Hermione asked, sounding fretful. ŇWe donŐt need to get out of here until weŐve found Sirius,Ó Harry shot back. He released DracoŐs hand and started marching forward. ŇLetŐs just start trying doors. IŐll know the right room when I see it.Ó ŇHarry, I donŐt think -Ó BlaiseŐs feeble protest was cut off by Harry wrenching open the first door he came to. He crossed the threshold and paused before shaking his head. ŇThis isnŐt it,Ó he said. The room was long and rectangular and much more well-lit than the circular room. A few desks were placed against the walls, and in the middle stood an enormous tank, full of strange, pale-white globs. ŇWhat are those?Ó Neville asked. ŇFish?Ó Blaise wondered. ŇNo,Ó Hermione breathed, approaching the tank with her usual curiosity. ŇI thinkÉ theyŐre brains.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened, and he quickly inspected the tank, as well. She was right - they were brains. He shook his head. ŇIt doesnŐt matter,Ó he said. ŇThis isnŐt the right spot. We should go back and try another.Ó ŇThereŐs more doors in here, though,Ó Ron said, pointing at the back of the room, and HarryŐs heart sank. How big was the Department of Mysteries, anyway? ŇThatŐs not it,Ó Harry said. He quickly turned the others around, marching back the way they came. He shut the door behind him and immediately turned towards another. ŇWait!Ó Hermione said, brandishing her wand at the door. ŇFlagrate!Ó A fiery X appeared on the door. ŇSmart,Ó Blaise commented, sounding impressed, and Hermione gave him a strange look in response. Harry ignored them and entered the next room. Again, he was instantly able to tell that it wasnŐt the right spot. It was even darker than the circular room; they couldnŐt even see the other side and had no way of knowing how big the room actually was. In the corner, though, there was a small spark of light beaming at them. Shaking his head, Harry turned and pushed through the others. ŇNot this one, either,Ó he said. Hermione again placed a fiery X on the door and they tried another. Frustratingly, the next one still wasnŐt the right room, but something made him pause. The room was large, dimly lit by an unknown source, and standing in the middle of what appeared to be an amphitheatre of some kind was a huge stone archway. Hanging from the arch was a tattered black veil, fluttering as if it was being captured by a breeze. Despite knowing that they still werenŐt in the right spot, Harry found himself approaching the archway, oddly feeling that there was someone just on the other side of it. ŇHarry, stop,Ó Draco said, reaching out and grabbing HarryŐs arm. ŇThereŐs noÉ glitter here. This isnŐt the right place.Ó ŇWhoŐs there?Ó Harry asked. ŇNo oneŐs there, Harry!Ó Ron said insistently. HarryŐs eyes were still glued to the veil, and Draco became even more persistent. ŇCome on, Harry,Ó he urged. ŇLetŐs go.Ó DracoŐs grip tightened, and Harry allowed himself to be pulled back out of the room. The room rotated around them again, and Blaise let out a groan. ŇIŐm starting to feel sick,Ó he muttered. ŇDelicate stomach?Ó Ron quipped, though the nervousness lacing through his voice covered up any true viciousness behind it. The room finally slowed to a stop and Harry desperately slammed through the closest door. ŇThis is it!Ó he said triumphantly. The light within sparkled in an eerily beautiful way, and as he quickly crossed the room he could see clocks of every shape and size, along with endless bookcases stacked so tightly together that it made the large room seem small. Harry sped up as he spied a door on the opposite side of the room. ŇWait,Ó Blaise hissed, and Harry turned to face him with a tinge of annoyance. ŇAre you saying that this is where you sawÉ your godfather?Ó He paused. ŇAnd the Dark Lord?Ó Harry nodded, and everyoneŐs expressions turned grim. Harry had the sense of mind to wait until everyone appeared ready - wands drawn, alert, and as prepared as they possibly could be. Taking in a deep breath, Harry pushed through the door. The ceiling was taller than any room theyŐd entered thus far; as high as a church, with row after row of shelves stretching from floor to the ceiling. Each shelf held seemingly hundreds of tiny glass orbs, every single one sitting above an old, yellowing label. Harry took a few steps forward and peered down a dimly lit aisle, feeling a bit disheartened when he realized he couldnŐt see the other end. ŇYou said it was row ninety-seven,Ó Hermione whispered. ŇRight,Ó Harry breathed, and he peered up at the shelf to spy a number sticking out at the side. ŇFifty-threeÉ fifty-fourÉÓ he read. ŇThis way!Ó Harry kept listening for the slightest sound of movement as they drew closer to row ninety-seven; surely he would be able to hear it if Voldemort still held Sirius captive. HeŐs not dead, Harry reminded himself desperately. I would know. They reached row ninety-seven, and Harry crept down it as quietly as possible. Sirius should be at the end of it, and they couldnŐt see that far downÉ ŇHeŐs not here, Harry,Ó Hermione whispered. ŇHeŐs got to be!Ó Harry said insistently. He felt sick to his stomach, and he didnŐt want to look at any of them. He didnŐt understand; Sirius should be there. He ran to the end of the aisle and looked up and down, but all he could see were endless rows of more shelves. ŇHarry?Ó Ron asked quietly. He didnŐt want to hear what Ron had to say; it was probably just another statement that Sirius wasnŐt there. ŇHarry!Ó Ron insisted. ŇDid you see this?Ó ŇSee what?Ó Harry asked scathingly. ŇThis oneÉ itŐs got your name on it,Ó Ron said. Harry turned around to see Ron peering at one of the tiny glass orbs. ŇMy name?Ó Harry asked in confusion, quickly drawing up to the shelf that Ron stared at. Ron was right. There, on the little yellow label below a tiny glass sphere, was a date from some sixteen years prior, and below that: S.P.T. to A.P.W.B.D Dark Lord And (?) Harry Potter ŇWhat is it?Ó Ron asked. ŇHarry,Ó Hermione said, sounding worried, ŇI donŐt think you should touch it.Ó ŇItŐs got my name on it, though,Ó Harry said, and he reached up to grasp the sphere. The instant his fingers touched the cold glass ball, something started flaring up inside of him. It felt familiar, and although it seemed more frantic than it ever had before it took him only a moment for him to realize what it was. The dark was screaming at him. Every hair on the back of his neck stood on end, and horror wrapped around him like a snake. ŇIt is a trap,Ó he whispered, realization dawning on him. He could hear a few gasps coming from behind him. He was so stupid. Sirius wasnŐt there. His friends had been right, after all - it was all a ploy to draw him to that exact place. The dark was screeching at him wordlessly, and his ears started ringing in fear. Daphne had given him the gift of succor - the ability to ask the dark for aid and assistance whenever he needed. Why hadnŐt he asked the dark for assistance earlier? He supposed heŐd never tried to use her gift before. It wasnŐt something he was used to having. But Harry was fairly sure that the answer was actually because he hadnŐt wanted to do anything having to do with the dark arts where Hermione, Ron, and Neville could see. Even if it wasnŐt something immediately visible, heŐd grown so used to keeping that part of him secret and separate from anyone not in Slytherin that the thought hadnŐt even occurred to him. Now, though, Harry unhesitantly answered the darkŐs silent howl. If this is a trap, we need help, he thought desperately. The dark answered, though he wasnŐt entirely happy with the response. All it told him was that they would get through it, but then it seemed to fling his senses out for him. As he still stood facing the shelf, orb in hand, he realized he could feel the presence of the two dark wizards at his side - Draco and Blaise. But they werenŐt alone. ŇThereŐs twelve of them,Ó Harry said without thinking. ŇTwelve what?Ó Ron asked desperately. ŇIŐm curious as to how you knew that, Potter,Ó came a familiar, drawling voice, and he heard DracoŐs breath catch in his throat. Harry slowly turned away from the shelf and spotted several dark figures clad in masks and black cloaks standing only a few meters away. ŇItŐs unimportant, though,Ó Lucius Malfoy continued, his voice giving him away even as his face was covered by a mask. ŇWhatŐs imperative for you now is that you give me that orb.Ó Blaise immediately stepped in front of Draco, who had not yet turned to face his father, and Harry moved next to Blaise, blocking Draco from LuciusŐs sight. ŇTo me, Potter,Ó Lucius repeated. Harry snuck a glance around them and quickly realized that they were surrounded. He felt Ron shift beside him, and he hissed out, ŇDonŐt do anything. Not yet.Ó A peal of laughter erupted from a woman standing to their left. ŇDo you hear him? He speaks as though he actually thinks of fighting us!Ó ŇYou donŐt know Potter as I do, Bellatrix,Ó Lucius responded. ŇHe has a weakness for heroics. The Dark Lord understands this about him; itŐs how he was able to draw him here in the first place.Ó ŇIf he wants to fight,Ó Bellatrix Lestrange said, Ňwe should let him!Ó She lifted her wand and pointed it directly at Harry. A bolt of adrenaline rushed through Harry as she fired a spell at him, and Harry raised a Protego just in the nick of time. The spell ricocheted off of HarryŐs shield and collided with one of the shelves, smashing several of the orbs. Eerie, ghost-like figures seemed to emerge, and haunting voices rose to join them. Ň... at the Solstice will come anewÉÓ said a bearded man. ŇBellatrix!Ó Lucius bellowed. ŇHe has the prophecy, and it cannot be damaged!Ó Prophecy? Without warning, the orb was snatched out of HarryŐs hand, and Draco stepped forward, holding the prophecy up like a Quaffle. ŇIŐll smash it,Ó Draco said in a low voice. ŇIŐll smash it unless you allow us to leave.Ó Lucius seemed to freeze at the sight of his son. ŇDraco?Ó he asked. DracoŐs eyes narrowed. ŇIf you let us leave, then IŐll hand it over.Ó Harry heard Ron let out a low hiss. ŇI knew itÉÓ Unhappy as he was at hearing DracoŐs words, Harry still knew that Draco wasnŐt betraying them; it was simply the survival instinct that had been ingrained into Draco for years. Besides, it wasnŐt as if Draco was throwing it directly to his father as Lucius was asking. ŇThey canŐt have it, Malfoy,Ó Hermione whispered furiously. ŇYou canŐt let them!Ó ŇDraco, what are you doing here?Ó Lucius demanded. ŇI realize that Potter is in your house now, but surely -Ó ŇThis is Draco?Ó Bellatrix asked, and a maniacal grin appeared on her face. ŇCome to Auntie, little DracoÉÓ ŇNo, thank you,Ó Draco responded, his voice wavering. It was then that Bellatrix seemed to realize something - Harry was no longer holding the prophecy. She quickly fired a spell at him and Harry again deflected it into the shelves, setting off more of the orbs. Ň... all shall fallÉÓ Ň... as it has scorched the earthÉÓ Ň... bring death as a giftÉÓ Harry heard Hermione use the noise to cover her frantic whisper. ŇThrow them into the prophecies,Ó she hissed. ŇAs many as possibleÉÓ ŇBellatrix!Ó Lucius bellowed. ŇI told you NO!Ó ŇHeŐs not holding the prophecy anymoreÉÓ ŇOn the count of three,Ó Harry whispered. ŇOneÉ twoÉÓ On three, the six of them launched as many spells as they could -Stupefy, Flipendo, anything that would send the Death Eaters back. Harry threw Expulso at two Death Eaters, satisfied to see them connect, throwing them into the prophecy shelves. So many orbs were now going off that the voices were blending together; none were discernible above the rest. A Death Eater they hadnŐt managed to strike dove at Draco, who tossed the orb back to Harry just as they landed on the floor in a pile of limbs. Harry shot another Expulso at the Death Eater, sending him skidding across the marble floor, and then dragged Draco to his feet. ŇRun!Ó Harry shouted, taking off for the head of the aisle. He rounded the corner and headed back for the door. Determined to put as much distance as they could between themselves and the Death Eaters while they were regrouping, Harry pushed himself as hard as he could, but he quickly realized that he and Ron were the fastest; the others were falling behind. Harry glanced over his shoulder just in time to see a wave of water crashing through their group. Harry and Ron watched in horror as their friends were swept away from them before the water seemed to dissipate. ŇWatch them!Ó Lucius commanded, pointing at the others while never taking his eyes off Harry. Bellatrix appeared at his side with a snarl on her face while three other Death Eaters surrounded Draco, Blaise, Hermione, and Neville. ŇYou will give me the prophecy, Potter,Ó Lucius sneered. ŇAnd then you will explain how you convinced my son to -Ó He was cut off by Ron throwing a stunner. He and Bellatrix both dodged, and Bellatrix faced them with a maniacal grin. ŇYou want to play, little baby?Ó she said, letting out a laugh. ŇSo do I!" She flicked her wand and a hex flew towards Ron, who threw up a Protego. He grunted as the curse smacked into the shield, and Bellatrix started laughing harder as she threw one hex after another. Harry, in turn, began throwing stunners back at her. Bellatrix darted out of the way out of each of his spells and seemingly ignored them as she continued to pummel Ron. Harry kept stealing glances at Ron out of the corner of his eye, impressed. Ron seemed to have all of the right instincts, and it was obvious heŐd been practicing. He supposed DumbledoreŐs Army had done exactly what Hermione had meant it to - train students for situations exactly like the one they found themselves in now. But he also knew that RonŐs shield wouldnŐt hold; he could see RonŐs exertion and Bellatrix wasnŐt letting up. RonŐs shield visibly wavered, and when the next curse crashed into it he was sent to the ground and the shield went down. Then Lucius also raised his wand and threw a curse towards them, as well. Harry knew he had to be aiming for Ron; Harry still had the prophecy, after all. Harry only had a split-second to react, and he acted purely on instinct. He planted his feet and shouted, ŇMuros!Ó He slashed his wand at the floor and then thrust his hands upwards, and a shimmering wall appeared in front of himself and Ron just in time for the curse to crash into it, followed by another thrown by Bellatrix. Lucius paused. ŇThatŐsÉ interesting spellwork, Potter,Ó he mused, his eyes narrowing. Harry swallowed at those words but didnŐt drop the wall, and he instinctively winced when another cutting curse from Bellatrix hit. It didnŐt even seem to affect HarryŐs wall. HeŐd thrown it up without really thinking, but it had worked. In fact, it seemed to be working even better than RonŐs Protego had. Harry felt no strain as yet another curse slammed into it, and the wall still seemed to be holding strong. And Harry suddenly realized how stupid it was that he wasnŐt using everything that heŐd been learning that year. After all, the reason heŐd initially given himself for first digging into the dark arts all those months ago was so he could actually fight back. Bellatrix shot hex after curse at he and Ron, while Lucius merely seemed to be studying whatever magic Harry had used. He glanced back at Ron, who was staring open-mouthed at HarryŐs wall as it absorbed each and every single one of BellatrixŐs curses. ŇCan you take the orb?Ó Harry whispered. Ron turned wide eyes towards Harry. ŇWhat is that, Harry?Ó he asked, pointing at the wall. ŇDonŐt worry about it,Ó Harry said. ŇCan you take the orb?Ó ŇOkayÉÓ Ron said, shakily getting to his feet. Harry tossed the orb to him and immediately directed his attention back to Lucius and Bellatrix. Harry held his free hand out in front of him and pointed his wand at his palm. ŇIra Sphaera!Ó A dark red ball sparked in his hand, at first no bigger than a galleon, and then as Harry slowly pulled his wand back it grew in size until it was nearly the size of a football. Then he whipped his wand around and sent the red sphere flying towards Bellatrix and Lucius. It was slow - much slower than the curses that Bellatrix was still sending their way .and they had plenty of time to move out of its way. It didnŐt matter. When it neared the two Death Eaters, eerie red tendrils seemed to grow out of the sphere, lashing out and striking them with lightning speed. Lucius hissed as one struck him in the shoulder and then the leg, and Bellatrix screamed when one slashed her across the face. She fell to the ground, cupping a hand to her cheek. And then she started laughing. She pulled her hand away to reveal a thin laceration stretching across her cheek, and blood dripped down her face as she grinned. ŇDark arts? You are such a surprise, Potter!Ó she cried mockingly, cackling. The sphere wasnŐt gone yet. It ricocheted off of a shelf and it went careening towards the other Death Eaters, who scattered. Harry dissipated the sphere with a flourish of his wand, not wanting it to harm his friends. He knew they were staring at him. He ignored it, instead focusing his attention on Bellatrix once more, who was still laughing. Then she disappeared. Harry heard Hermione cry out, and he swiveled his head to spy Bellatrix on the other side of the room, grasping onto Neville with pure malice in her eyes. ŇCare to throw it again, you stupid little half-blood?Ó she hissed, her hand closing around NevilleŐs throat. ŇLet him go!Ó Harry shouted. ŇDo it, little Potter!Ó Bellatrix called right back. ŇShow us what youŐve got, itty bitty baby Potter!Ó ŇExpulso!Ó Light exploded from DracoŐs wand, striking Bellatrix in the back and sending her flying forward. She landed straight on her face, skidding across the floor, but she launched herself to her feet, snarling. Harry could see her teeth were coated with blood before she spun on her heel to face Draco. She disappeared again and rematerialized behind Draco, her wand stuck up into the underside of DracoŐs chin. She yanked on his shoulder, dragging him towards her. ŇBellatrix!Ó Lucius snarled. ŇThat is my son!Ó ŇYour son is a traitor!Ó Bellatrix shot back, never taking her eyes off of Harry. She shoved her wand harder into DracoŐs throat, and he let out a whimper. HarryŐs ears popped. Rage boiled over inside of him, and it was unlike anything heŐd ever felt before. It was worse than any time heŐd lost his temper, or even when heŐd felt VoldemortŐs anger. He almost felt as if he was made up of nothing but fury. Harry pointed his wand at the ground and hissed, ŇHange.Ó Flames sprang up from the floor, quickly forming the tiny, meter-high fire sprite heŐd conjured all those months ago in the Slytherin dorms. Bellatrix started laughing again. ŇHow adorable, Potter!Ó she cackled. ŇIt thinks itŐs bigger than it is - just like you!Ó Then Harry thrust his wand at the sprite, crying, ŇTitanus!Ó He raised both hands up into the air, and the sprite mirrored his movement, but as the flaming arms stretched upwards, it began to grow. And it kept growing. It went up and up until it was nearly touching the tall ceiling - nearly fifteen meters. The flames crackled dangerously, and there was a low-pitched roar emanating from the being. Or the roar might have been the rage in HarryŐs head. He couldnŐt tell. Bellatrix stared up at the fiery giant and her eyes grew almost comically wide. HarryŐs eyes narrowed in concentration, and then he thrust both of his hands out in front of him. The giantŐs arm narrowed and lengthened, stretching across the room, seemingly heading straight for Bellatrix and Draco. The tube of fire narrowed to only a few inches wide, and it flew straight past DracoŐs head and directly into BellatrixŐs face. She let out a scream, and she was swept off her feet, crashing back into the darkness of the Hall. Someone shouted at him. It might have been his name, but Harry paid it no mind. Instead, Harry swept his left arm down and across his body, and the giant again imitated his movement. The flaming arm crashed into half a dozen Death Eaters, sending them careening down the Hall of Prophecy. Some crashed into the shelves, again setting off a scream of eerie voices that all blended together. Harry flicked his wand arm down and then out, and the giantŐs other arm sent Lucius flying down the Hall and out of sight. The few remaining Death Eaters were quickly scattering. ŇHarry!Ó He still didnŐt know who was shouting at him. It sounded like it was miles away, or as if it was coming from the other end of a cave. He ignored it, and he quickly turned his attention to the remaining Death Eaters. He flicked his wand hand down and the large spriteŐs arm caught the last of them in a single sweep, sending them in the same direction Bellatrix had been thrown. Ň... in the fragile balance.Ó The last remaining smashed prophecy orb finally fell silent, and Harry let out a breath he hadnŐt realized heŐd been holding. The only remaining sound was the crackle of the fire and Harry quickly brought his wand down, dispelling the giant. He heard footsteps, and he saw Draco and the others running towards him. Beside him, Ron was shakily getting to his feet. ŇHarry, that was insane,Ó Ron was saying, but Harry ignored him, only having eyes for one person. ŇAre you all right?Ó Harry immediately asked Draco, unconsciously reaching out for him. ŇIÉ I think you singed my hair,Ó Draco said, his voice shaking as Harry grasped his arm. ŇBut thank you.Ó He shook his head, and when he spoke again his voice was steadier. ŇAre you all right?Ó ŇThat was an enormous amount of magic,Ó Blaise said, sounding awed. Harry shook his head. ŇIt really wasnŐt,Ó he said. ŇThey just werenŐt expecting it. The sprite is slow. It wonŐt work again.Ó He turned his head to look into the darkness of the Hall. ŇWe need to move,Ó he said, Ňnow.Ó Adrenaline pumping furiously, he slid his hand down DracoŐs arm to grasp his fingers and started running back towards the entrance, dragging Draco behind him. A quick glance over his shoulder told him that the others were quick to follow. ŇThey wonŐt be gone for long,Ó Harry panted as they reached the door. He let go of Draco to shove it open, ushering the others through it. He slammed the door as soon as Neville bounded through it, breathless. He stared at the door for a moment in consideration. ŇWhat are you stopping for?Ó Ron cried, already a distance away from the rest. ŇTheyŐre gonna know we came this way!Ó ŇI wish IŐd brought my knife,Ó Harry muttered. ŇWhat?Ó Hermione asked, confused. ŇYou donŐt happen to have one, do you?Ó Harry asked Blaise. ŇWhat... do you need... a knife for?Ó Neville asked, completely out of breath. He leaned over and put his hands on his knees, panting. ŇYouŐreÉÓ Draco paused, narrowing his eyes. ŇI think I know what youŐre trying to do.Ó He turned on his heel, his eyes raking over the glittering clocks and chimes. Harry followed every movement as Draco raised his wand and pointed it at a grandfather clock. ŇReducto!Ó The curse flew into the glass covering the clockŐs pendulum and the pieces shattered, skittering across the floor like diamonds. ŇWhat the hell are you doing, Malfoy?Ó Ron demanded. Draco bent over to retrieve a long shard of glass, carefully holding it between two fingers, and then held it out to Harry. ŇThis should work.Ó Harry quickly stuck his wand in his pocket and took the glass, turning back to face the door again. Without even bothering to brace himself, he slashed the tip of his index finger, letting out a hiss at the sting. He dropped the glass, letting it shatter on the floor, then traced the now-familiar symbol on the palm of his other hand. ŇFootot sanguinis.Ó He slammed his palm into the door, shouting, ŇLacquera!Ó His palm seemed to send a wave of faint purple light rippling outward, covering the door from top to bottom. ŇThat wonŐt stop them for long,Ó Blaise said. ŇItŐs just a basic ward.Ó ŇI know,Ó Harry said, taking his wand out again and scrubbing his other hand down his face. ŇBut it might slow them down. ŇIs thatÉ thatŐs blood magic.Ó ŇAre youÉ why are you messing with that, Harry?Ó Ron asked, swallowing. Neville merely looked ill, though Harry didnŐt know if it was due to the physical exertion or watching Harry use dark spell after dark spell. After seeing what dark magic had done to his parents, Harry wasnŐt sure if he could blame him if it turned out to be the latter. ŇAnd what Bellatrix Lestrange said was true, wasnŐt it?Ó Hermione said. ŇThose spells you were using in there - that was dark arts.Ó Harry let out a sigh. After what they'd seen and heard, there was no point in denying it. "Yes." NevilleŐs eyes widened and RonŐs sharp intake of breath sent a different type of adrenaline rushing through Harry. ŇYou promised, Harry!Ó Hermione cried, her face falling. ŇYou promised me you werenŐt fooling around with the dark arts!Ó ŇNo, I didnŐt,Ó Harry said simply. ŇI promised you that there wasnŐt anything to worry about.Ó Blaise let out a snort. ŇIs that what you told her?Ó he asked, smirking. ŇShut up, Blaise,Ó Harry said tiredly. ŇHermione, I promised that there wasnŐt anything to worry about because there isnŐt.Ó ŇSo I was right, then!Ó Ron suddenly exploded, closing the distance between himself and Blaise. ŇYouÉ you and the other Slytherins - you did something to Harry -Ó ŇNo, they didnŐt!Ó Harry snapped, moving into the small gap between Blaise and Ron. ŇHarry just saved all of our lives by using the dark arts,Ó Draco said, his voice cold, the snootiness turned on full force. ŇYou should be thanking him.Ó Hermione shot him one of the nastiest glares Harry had ever seen on her face before she eyed Harry again with a ferocious scowl. ŇYou deliberately misled me.Ó ŇI did,Ó Harry said, beginning to grow impatient. ŇAnd trust me, I feel more badly about that than anything else - including the spells I just used.Ó He shook his head. ŇBut either way, is now really the best time to be discussing this?Ó Hermione shut her mouth, but her eyes burned furiously. ŇLetŐs justÉ get out of this alive, and then we can have it out,Ó Harry said, exasperated. ŇUh... Ň Blaise said, his voice wavering. ŇI feel like we may not get out of this alive.Ó Harry turned around to see Blaise pointing at the door. The smear of HarryŐs blood on the door was glowing. It let out a spark, and a flame appeared. Harry swore, backing up a few steps. ŇWe need to go now.Ó He pointed his wand at the ground directly in front of the door, hissing, ŇFulminos Samhla!Ó A glyph suddenly sparked into life, crackling with lightning, then sank into the floor and disappeared. He spun on his heel, taking off again. ŇGo!Ó he urged the others, wanting to make sure he took up the rear. ŇStraight ahead!Ó Having learned their lesson the first time, they sprinted as fast as the slowest among them would allow. The trip across the room seemed to take forever. They kept running and running, and just as Harry had the feeling that they should have reached the other side, Hermione spoke up, her voice strained and winded. ŇSomethingŐs wrong.Ó ŇGrangerŐs right,Ó Blaise said, slowing down. ŇWe should be there.Ó ŇDid the room get longer?Ó Draco wondered as they all drew to a stop. ŇWhatŐs that?Ó Ron asked, pointing to their left. ŇIs that it?Ó They all turned to see where Ron was gesturing and spied a door, nondescript and looking somewhat out of place. ŇLucius is using their names,Ó Blaise explained, and then he raised an eyebrow at Draco. DracoŐs eyes widened. ŇThatŐsÉ very stupid of him,Ó he said. ŇAnd convenient.Ó ŇRight,Ó Blaise agreed. ŇWhat are the names?Ó Blaise ran through the list again without skipping a beat, and Harry marveled at BlaiseŐs memory. ŇWhat do you care what their names are?Ó Ron asked furiously. ŇWhat are you thinking?Ó ŇI know a blood spell,Ó Draco said. Blaise sighed. ŇMore blood magic,Ó he said unhappily. ŇMore dark arts!Ó Ron exclaimed. ŇAll of you!Ó He glared at all of them, feeling hot and furious. ŇWe canŐt afford to fight each other right now. If heŐd been at Hogwarts heŐd have thrown his magic into the wall, but he didnŐt want to chance that in a room that was full ofÉ Scales? Harry hadnŐt yet given himself a chance to study the room theyŐd entered, and he was just noticing that it seemed to be full of every kind of scale imaginable. Ron passed the prophecy orb back to Harry before joining Hermione, Neville, and Blaise against the wall near the door. Harry looked at Draco. He was tucked into the corner of the room, transfigured blade in hand, and the top few buttons of his shirt undone - just enough for Draco to reach his heart. Harry swallowed nervously, then nodded. Draco took in a deep breath and Harry could seem a tremor in his hand as he lifted the blade.
