Draco was no Gryffindor, but he still showed perseverance even through his fear. Wetting his lips and closing his eyes, Draco raised the point of the blade to his temple. ŇFrigidus sanguis qui nocet proprio,Ó he said, slowly dragging the tip down to just beside his eye. Harry heard Hermione let out a gasp as blood began welling up. ŇFieri cloiche. Fieri cloiche.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened as blood dripped down the side of DracoŐs face. That was far more than just a scratch. He clenched his fist, but let Draco continue. It was deeper than heŐd expected, but it wasnŐt fatal. Draco then moved the blade to his palm. ŇFrigidus sanguisÉÓ He repeated the same words, slowly dragging the blade across his hand. Again, it was more blood than Harry wanted to see, but it wasnŐt any more than what heŐd given during his own blood rite. ŇFieri cloiche. Fieri cloiche.Ó A few drops of blood hit the floor, and Draco then nodded at Harry. Forcing himself to tear his eyes away from Draco, Harry steeled himself before wrenching the door open, wand in one hand and orb in the other. His eyes immediately landed on Bellatrix, who stood within spitting distance. One cheek had drying blood caked to it, and the other had a line of blisters from where the fire had struck her in the face. Harry gasped and instinctively raised his wand, taking a step backwards. He gripped the orb hard, his eyes raking the darkness for Lucius. A maniacal grin spread across BellatrixŐs lips. ŇFound you,Ó she said in a sing-song. ŇWeŐve spotted Potter!Ó Harry heard another voice say - he assumed it had to be Rookwood or Macnair. ŇOver here!Ó said another. Shit. While drawing the Death Eaters right to them had definitely been the plan, Harry hadnŐt counted on them being right outside the door. Stupid. Harry quickly backpedaled into the room and hoped against hope that Lucius wouldnŐt be far behind Bellatrix. One of the other Death Eaters - Rookwood, he thought - crossed the threshold first. ŇFlipendo!Ó Hermione hissed, and Rookwood was flung to the side. Something came flying at Harry through the door, and he barely had time to throw up a Muros before it struck him. He blinked, and stuck in his shimmering wall was a hatchet. It looked bizarre, seemingly hovering in mid-air, and the handle vibrated with the force of the impact. Another Death Eater entered, and then another and another. Ron and Blaise quickly began throwing their own hexes, jinxes, and curses, Ron belting out a bizarre kind of battle cry. Hermione followed up with a Bat Bogey Hex that wasnŐt quite as impressive as GinnyŐs but still packed a punch. After a stunned moment, Neville threw a tripping jinx and Harry was pleasantly surprised to see it connect. Some spells hit their targets while others did not. Harry kept one eye on the door and one eye on his friends, hoping that none of the Death Eaters had noticed Draco yet. When Bellatrix entered, chaos followed her. Grinning, she deflected a hex from Hermione almost effortlessly before turning to face her attackers. With a sweep of her wand, all four of them were sent flying back, skidding across the floor. HarryŐs breath caught for a moment, but as soon as Ron launched himself back to his feet Harry turned his attention back to Bellatrix. ŇStill want to play, little dark wizard?Ó she crowed at Harry. She cackled, swaying as she stepped towards him. ŇGreen darkÉ dark green,Ó she chanted, her eyes crazed. ŇSecani!Ó Harry shot a dark cutting curse at her, aiming for her leg. She narrowly avoided it, still laughing. ŇHe does want to play!Ó she shrieked, sounding delighted, and then she shot something right back at Harry. HarryŐs Muros was still up, but he instinctively threw himself aside and he instantly found himself glad he did. Her spell hit and the wall shattered like glass. He conjured his fire sprite despite his doubts that it would work again, and Bellatrix immediately drowned it in a wave of water. Adrenaline pumping, Harry threw the cutting curse towards her again while she volleyed another spell at him, and he quickly realized that he was completely outmatched. She had years of experience with the dark arts, while he only had a few scant months. The only reason heŐd overpowered them before was because they hadnŐt been expecting him to use anything but the basic spells that were taught at Hogwarts. He needed to be more creative. ŇEngorgio Skullus!Ó he hissed, mostly as a distraction, and when Bellatrix dodged he immediately followed it up with, ŇTimcheall!Ó A faint green dome appeared around Bellatrix, and he was momentarily satisfied to see her eyes widen in surprise. The dome shrank around her, and then collapsed, sending her crumbling into the ground with a shriek. She stumbled back to her feet, narrowly avoiding another curse Harry threw her way. ŇYou arrogant little brat!Ó she screamed, her eyes seeming to spark with madness. ŇI am the Dark LordŐs most loyal, and he is the one who trained me in the dark arts!Ó She raised her wand, her lips curled into a snarl. ŇYou think your little dark tricks will stand a chance against me?Ó The tip of her wand sparked. ŇBellatrix, stop!Ó Lucius MalfoyŐs voice rang out from the doorway. ŇHe has the prophecy, you idiot!Ó Just then, the flailing body of a Death Eater went flying through the air between Bellatrix and Harry. Gasping, Harry spared a glance to his left and saw Blaise standing tall, a furious expression on his face and wand pointed straight in front of him. When Harry looked back, though, a chill ran through him. The Death Eater had landed near Draco, and BellatrixŐs attention was now solely focused on the blade in DracoŐs hand. ŇLittle Draco is plotting a secret!Ó she snarled, immediately launching herself towards him. Lucius, in turn, leapt at Bellatrix, and he collided with her heavily. ŇBellatrix, stop -Ó ŇFieri cloiche!Ó Draco shouted, slicing the blade across his chest. Harry faintly heard Hermione letting out a strangled cry. It was eerie how quickly Bellatrix and Lucius completely ceased all movement. They were caught in mid-struggle, Bellatrix appearing that she was about to fall, with Lucius reaching for her wand. It had worked. Harry quickly looked around their frozen forms at Draco, and his heart skipped a beat. Maybe Draco had lied about how much blood was needed or maybe heŐd just accidentally gone too deep when heŐd seen Bellatrix coming for him, but the cut was far deeper than it should have been. Blood poured from the wound, and Draco pressed his hand over it in an effort to slow it down. Harry immediately started crossing the distance between them only for something to slam into his side, sending him tumbling head over feet through the air. The prophecy orb went flying out of his hand; he didnŐt have a hope of knowing where it went. He landed hard, grunting as his knee struck the ground with force. He tried to get his bearings as fast as possible, clambering to his feet. His knee throbbed painfully, but it thankfully didnŐt seem seriously hurt. A lanky, gangly Death Eater stood in front of him. Avery, HarryŐs mind supplied. He was supposedly not on BellatrixŐs level, but was easily still a force to be reckoned with. Harry dodged a hex and returned fire, and soon he was soon locked in another exchange of spells. He needed to get to Draco. He started volleying a mix of every spell he could think of, dark arts and not, dodging and side-stepping as Avery returned fire. Harry heard a strangled, deep scream, and his attention instantly went to Draco. Mask off, hair wild, and rage painted across his face, Rasmus Nott had a firm grasp of Draco, though there was something sticking out of his shoulder. Harry quickly realized that Draco must have stabbed him with the transfigured blade. In his distraction he didnŐt see AveryŐs next spell coming for him, and something wrapped around his ankles, pulling his feet out from under him. Harry landed hard on his back, and he gasped as the wind was knocked out of him. Behind Avery, he could see Nott dragging a struggling Draco towards the door, and Harry desperately tried to get his breath back. He let out a strangled, desperate sound as they disappeared through the doorway. ŇNoÉÓ Harry tried to say, his breath still labored. He couldnŐt even get a spell out, and Avery was raising his wand once more. ŇPetrificus Totalus!Ó AveryŐs body stiffened, his legs snapping together, and he fell to the side. Harry shakily got to his feet as Hermione appeared, her wand pointing down at Avery. ŇIncarcerous!Ó Ropes snaked out of her wand, binding Avery. ŇVery nice form, Miss Granger!Ó a familiar voice rang out. Kingsley appeared at HermioneŐs side, looking somewhat impressed. Harry could also hear Moody yelling NevilleŐs last name from across the room. The Order of the Phoenix had arrived. ŇAre you okay, Harry?Ó Hermione asked. Harry couldnŐt even nod, his sight still glued to the open door. ŇDracoÉÓ he managed to get out, his breath slowly returning to him. He took a few steps towards the door, stumbling at first before he managed to get his feet under him and he took off at a full run. ŇHarry!Ó Hermione cried out after him. He slid to a stop just outside of the room and looked around frantically. The room was full of shadows, none of them showing any movement. There was no sign of Nott or Draco. Then he looked down, and he almost felt as if his breath had been taken away again. There was so much blood. Harry wouldnŐt allow himself to think if it was DracoŐs or NottŐs - or both. It was a trail, and he lit his wand with a quick Lumos. The red was glistening in the light, and Harry quickly took off running again. Someone called after him, but he ignored it. He had to get to Draco. The trail of blood led him to a wide-open door, and when Harry unhesitatingly leapt through it he realized it was the large circular room theyŐd been looking for. The blood stretched across the room, but he still didnŐt see Draco or Nott. He kept following the blood, and it turned a corner right outside of the Department of Mysteries, leading to stairs he hadnŐt noticed before. He took the steps two at a time, his heartbeat echoing in his ears. Towards the top, he finally heard a voice. ŇThe Dark Lord will punish you, little MalfoyÉÓ Harry sprinted forward, quickly realizing that he was back in the main atrium of the Ministry. Nott was dragging a now-unresisting Draco towards the fireplaces, and a bolt of rage and fear flared through Harry. He wanted to hurt Nott. Fury boiling in every inch of him, Harry raised his wand and pointed it at NottŐs back. ŇReditus Dolorit!Ó he hissed. The magic that leapt out of HarryŐs wand was black, and when it struck Rasmus Nott it seemed to wrap around him and then seep into his body. Nott released Draco and fell to the ground. And then he started screaming. They were the kind of screams that would normally set HarryŐs teeth on edge, but it only gave Harry a strange kind of raw satisfaction. The screams echoed in the large atrium, making them seem even louder. Draco stared down at Nott before looking up at Harry with wide eyes. He took two steps towards Harry, his hand still pressed hard against his bleeding chest. Suddenly, HarryŐs scar blazed in blinding pain. Grating, high-pitched laughter rang out across the room, blending with the sound of NottŐs screams. ŇYouŐve been studying, Potter.Ó It was that laughter and that voice that had been haunting HarryŐs dreams, and he gasped and spun around at the words. Hood drawn around the snake-like face and eyes glittering like rubies, Voldemort stood only meters away, his wand pointed directly at Harry. Fear sliced through Harry, and his scar continued to pulse furiously. ŇWhere is the prophecy, Potter?Ó Voldemort asked. Harry found himself quite unable to speak. HeŐd already figured out that the dark arts heŐd learned wouldnŐt be enough to save him. He knew heŐd made leaps and bounds with the dark arts at an astounding pace, but heŐd still only been studying them for less than a year. He could barely stand up to Bellatrix; there was no way he could stand against the wizard who had taught her. VoldemortŐs terrible features screwed up into something like fury. ŇYou wouldnŐt bother telling me even if you knew, would you?Ó he said, sneering. ŇIŐll find it without you, Potter. YouŐve troubled me long enough. Avada Kedavra!Ó Green light came flying towards him, and Harry found himself rooted in place, his scar still burning angrily. Harry heard Draco let out a wordless yell, ringing out above the sound of NottŐs screams, and then the golden statue of the witch from the fountain suddenly flew in front of him. The curse ricocheted noisily off of the statue, and Harry gaped. ŇWhatÉÓ Voldemort said, lips peeling back from his teeth in a snarl. ŇDumbledore!Ó Harry spun his head, letting out a gasp when he spotted the headmaster, relief flooding through him. Harry didnŐt have a hope of standing up against Voldemort, but Dumbledore certainly could. ŇAurors are coming, Tom,Ó Dumbledore said, his voice eerily calm. ŇIt was foolish of you to come here.Ó He waved his hand at Harry, and Harry found himself being pushed to the side of the atrium and behind a suit of armor. He then moved his hand towards Draco, who was pushed behind the fountain. ŇIŐll be long gone before they arrive,Ó Voldemort snapped. ŇAnd you will be dead!Ó He threw another killing curse towards Dumbledore. It missed and hit a desk, setting it aflame. Dumbledore flicked his wand, and HarryŐs hair stood on end as the spell flew past him. Voldemort conjured a shimmering silver shield, and when the spell struck it rang out with a chilling, reverberant sound. The two traded terrifying spells, none of them connecting with the other, but the sounds and the power swum around the atrium like a storm. HarryŐs eyes sought Draco out, and he was relieved to see Draco staring back at him, clearly scared witless but still awake and aware, even as blood continued to pour from his wounds. Harry took two steps towards him before being forcefully shoved back behind the suit of armor. Dumbledore clearly wanted him to stay put. HarryŐs scar pounded as his attention was split between the impressive duel and Draco. He snuck a glance at the armor, wondering if he could use it to get to Draco, and then a bright flash of fire drew his full attention back to the fight. Fawkes had appeared, and the phoenix dove down to swallow what looked like a killing curse. Harry let out a cry as it burst into flames and fell to the floor, small and wrinkled. Dumbledore then raised his wand high and the water from the fountain seemed to pour into the air and completely surrounded Voldemort, wrapping him in a shimmering cocoon. For a moment, Voldemort was only a shadow, his red eyes glinting as he struggled. Then he disappeared. Near silence fell in the atrium, except for one last chilling sound that had been there from the beginning: Nott was still screaming. Harry surged from behind the suit of armor again, feeling positive that Voldemort had fled. ŇStay where you are, Harry!Ó DumbledoreŐs voice bellowed, and in the back of his mind, Harry realized that for the first time, Dumbledore sounded frightened. Harry didnŐt care; he just needed to get to Draco. And then his scar exploded in pain, far beyond anything heŐd ever felt from him. Harry felt sure that he was going to die, or else he was already dead. His body was no longer his own. Something else was inside him, seeping into every nook and crevice. His jaw creaked open and he felt laughter that wasnŐt his pouring from his lips. And then it used him to speak. ŇDo you know what your boy has been up to, Dumbledore?Ó He felt black mirth running through him; whatever had merged with him was so amused that Harry had been dancing in his playground. The dark was his; the stupid boy had no place in it. But deep within him, something sparked. Love and home and protection seemed to roar out of his gut, pushing at the presence that had invaded his body. As it shoved at the presence, Harry slowly began to become aware of himself again, and he could feel exactly what it was that was fighting back. It was Pansy and Blaise. It was Theo and Millicent. It was Draco. It was the dark. The dark, fueled by the protection that his friends had given him, was driving the presence out. Where they were still connected, he could feel the presenceŐs fury at the dark magic swirling inside him as it worked to pluck his grip from HarryŐs being one stranglehold at a time, becoming weaker and weaker with every pulse. With a cry, Harry helped the dark give the presence one final push, and HarryŐs body was his own once again. Harry came back to himself and realized he was shivering uncontrollably, face-down on the cool floor. He could again hear Nott screaming, the voice becoming hoarse and sickly sounding. He opened his eyes and quickly realized his glasses were laying in front of him. His hand shaking, he grabbed them and sat up as he placed them back on his nose. His vision swam into focus and he saw Dumbledore inches away from him, staring at Harry in concern. ŇAre you all right, Harry?Ó ŇHarry!Ó Draco fell to his knees beside Harry, bloodied hands reaching out for him. Harry grasped at them, relief flooding through him. ŇIŐm fine,Ó Harry whispered. ŇIt fought for me. It pushed him out.Ó DumbledoreŐs expression screwed up in contemplation. ŇWhat do you mean, Harry?Ó Movement behind Dumbledore caught HarryŐs attention. People were flooding into the atrium, wands drawn. Fudge was among them, looking stricken and dumb-founded. ŇHe was there!Ó a wizard was shouting. ŇI swear, Minister - You-Know-Who was standing right there!Ó ŇI saw him, too!Ó Fudge fretted. ŇHow - it was him, right here - in the Ministry!Ó He continued blubbering as a few aurors appeared, Kingsley Shacklebolt among them. The fight on the lower level must have been under control. ŇWeŐve arrested Bellatrix Lestrange and Lucius Malfoy downstairs in the Department of Mysteries, sir,Ó Kingsley said. ŇThere are also a few other escaped Death Eaters that are now incapacitated that weŐre in the process of arresting.Ó ŇAnd the one screaming on the floor over there is Rasmus Nott,Ó another auror said, one that Harry didnŐt recognize. ŇWhatŐs wrong with him?Ó ŇSomething odd about Lestrange and Malfoy,Ó another auror was saying. ŇItŐs as if theyŐre under a permanent Petrificus Totalus.Ó Kingsley's eyebrows shot upwards. "Whatever struck them must have also struck Auror Tonks," he said. "We've got a Healer coming in to look at her now." ŇReally?Ó a different auror remarked. ŇPretty widespread, because that must be what got Sirius Black, too. See?Ó He turned and pointed behind him, where three aurors were dragging Sirius through the door, chains and shackles completely surrounding him. Harry let out a gasp as they threw Sirius to the floor. He knew the vision of Sirius had been false, but he must have arrived with the rest of the Order. Sirius didnŐt move a muscle. His face seemed to be frozen in mid-yell, lips parted and eyes wide. DracoŐs fingers tightened around HarryŐs, and Harry instantly realized that Draco was sending him a silent apology. DracoŐs Cloiche had trapped Sirius, as well. ŇWe canŐt Finite whatever it is,Ó an auror was saying. ŇWell, letŐs not look a gift-horse in the mouth,Ó another auror said. ŇWeŐve been looking for him for years now -Ó ŇBut heŐs innocent!Ó Harry burst out. ŇI tried telling Fudge back then and he didnŐt believe me!Ó He turned furious eyes on Fudge, and the Minister actually took a step back when Harry shakily clambered to his feet. ŇYou didnŐt believe me about Sirius or Voldemort, and guess who was right about Voldemort?Ó Harry approached Fudge with ferocity, suddenly finding himself wanting to curse the man. Fudge seemed to try to shake off his stupor, looking affronted at a teenager taking him to task. Even so, his voice still wavered as he spoke. ŇPotter, that man betrayed your parents -Ó ŇNo, he didnŐt!Ó Harry roared. ŇYou stupid, idiotic -Ó Suddenly, Sirius let out a cry. ŇTonks, watch out!Ó Then he gasped, staring down at the chains surrounding him. ŇWhat the -Ó Harry stared, then turned on his heel to see Draco and Dumbledore caught in a staring match. DumbledoreŐs wand was drawn, and though his shirt was still soaked with blood, DracoŐs wounds had closed. Harry quickly figured out that Dumbledore must have healed Draco, ending the spell. Several witches and wizards looked from Draco to Sirius, and Fudge suddenly exploded. ŇBlood magic!Ó he cried. ŇThatŐs clearly blood magic!Ó Draco was shaking his head, wheeling back with his hands up. ŇNo, I -Ó ŇHe had clearly self-inflicted wounds,Ó an auror was saying, Ňand as soon as they were healedÉ this has every sign of blood magic!Ó ŇIŐm inclined to agree,Ó Kingsley said, drawing his wand. ŇDark arts rumors have surrounded the Malfoy family for years, and his father was just found with the escaped Death Eaters,Ó another one of the aurors said. ŇItŐs no surprise, really -Ó "And isn't this boy related to both Black and Auror Tonks?" ŇIŐll bet that whateverŐs wrong with the one over there is more dark arts!Ó another auror said, gesturing towards the still screaming Nott. ŇFinite isnŐt working on him, either!Ó ŇArrest him!Ó Fudge said, pointing at Draco. ŇWe can figure out exactly what he used later -Ó ŇNo!Ó Harry moved in front of Draco, one hand moving as if to block Kingsley, the other grasping his wand tightly. ŇDonŐt touch him!Ó ŇThe dark arts are illegal, Harry,Ó Kingsley said, shaking his head. ŇIŐve got no choice.Ó A hand landed on HarryŐs back, and he heard Draco hiss at him, ŇHarry, donŐt.Ó Harry ignored Draco and pointed his wand at Nott. ŇFin Thiel.Ó Nott finally stopped screaming, and just when Harry felt as if a vice had been taken off his head from the lack of the horrible sound, Nott started sobbing. Kingsley stared at Nott, dumbfounded, before turning a horrified look HarryŐs way. ŇHarry?Ó Dumbledore said carefully. ŇWhat did you just do?Ó ŇIŐm the one who cursed Nott,Ó Harry explained. ŇAnd IŐve used much more dark arts than Draco did tonight.Ó FudgeŐs eyes seemed to bug out of his skull as he stared at Harry, mouth agape. ŇYou?!Ó Harry set his jaw, staring at the aurors as if he was daring them to come near him. ŇIf you arrest Draco, youŐll have to arrest me, too.Ó Sirius was staring at him in horror. ŇNo,Ó he bit out desperately, struggling against the chains that held him, ŇHarry!Ó Harry saw two of the aurors whispering furiously and he felt DracoŐs fist clenched at the back of HarryŐs shirt. ŇI - you -Ó Fudge was sputtering. An auror started towards Harry and Draco, and Harry braced himself to be placed in shackles like Sirius, but then Dumbledore stepped in between them and the auror. He bent over to pick up a piece of debris from the battle - part of the golden statue, it appeared - and pointed his wand at it. ŇPortus.Ó He held it out towards Harry and Draco. ŇBoth of you - hold tight, now,Ó he said. Draco unhesitatingly reached around Harry and grasped the golden portkey before nudging Harry to do the same. Just before the familiar hook wrapped around his navel, HarryŐs eyes met DumbledoreŐs. The last thing Harry thought before the atrium disappeared was that heŐd never seen the headmaster look so disappointed. Chapter End Notes This is a beast of a chapter, ain't it? And I know there are some unanswered questions about some things that happened in the battle, but I promise they'll be answered in the next - and last - chapter. Hope it was worth the wait. And for those of you that were screaming about Sirius - I am someone who takes the archive warnings very seriously. Just saying.) Chapter 29: TheBeginning Chapter Notes The Trevor Project and Trans Lifeline are two wonderful organizations that provide great services for trans people. Please consider giving them a visit to see how you can get involved! Last chapter. Longest chapter. Just as a reminder, this is not a bashing fic. It has never been a bashing fic and it never will be. See the end of the chapter for more notes The odd silver tools and stained glass windows were familiar, and Harry immediately recognized that theyŐd been sent to DumbledoreŐs office. The faint light of dawn was just beginning to stream through the windows, casting odd shapes upon the floor. HarryŐs hand found DracoŐs almost by instinct, and Draco squeezed back just as hard as Harry held him. Harry turned and looked around, confirming his suspicions - he and Draco had been the only ones sent back to Hogwarts. ŇI hope the others are okay,Ó Harry said quietly, his breath catching. HeŐd left them in that room when heŐd taken off after Draco. The Order had been there, so that was a comfort, but he had no idea what happened to them. ŇDumbledore - heŐsÉ preventing us from being arrested?Ó Draco asked, sounding somewhere between disbelieving and amazed. ŇAt least for now,Ó Harry said. ŇI donŐt know what heŐll do later, but for nowÉÓ ŇHe protected us,Ó Draco said, sounding astounded. Harry nodded. ŇI got my father arrested,Ó Draco said quietly, his voice shaking, and his grip on HarryŐs hand suddenly turned vice-like. ŇAnd your godfather, too. IŐm sorry.Ó Harry could only hope that Fudge was too distracted by the revelation that Voldemort had actually returned before he remembered that heŐd promised to give Sirius Black the dementorŐs kiss on sight. He had to tell himself that Dumbledore was there, along with Kingsley. Surely they could prevent anything truly awful from happening to Sirius. ŇHow am I going to tell Theo that I got his father arrested, too?Ó Harry asked, instead, trying to distract himself from the fact that Sirius was likely headed back to Azkaban. ŇOr that IÉ used that curse on him?Ó ŇWhat was that, anyway?Ó Draco asked curiously. ŇIŐve never seen anything like it before.Ó Harry sighed. ŇItŐs something I learned from one of the books in theÉ room,Ó he answered. ŇBack in the summer. IŐd never gotten a chance to actually try it onÉ a real person.Ó He shook his head. ŇÔReturn of Suffering.Ő ItŐs supposed to make someone feel every bit of pain theyŐve ever caused someone else.Ó ŇThat seemsÉ like it would be quite effective against Death Eaters, then,Ó Draco said, and there was something odd in his voice. Abruptly, Harry realized what Draco was actually doing. He was trying to distract himself - and Harry - from the fact that they could both be facing Azkaban. ŇIf they try to take us, we can run,Ó Harry said. ŇIf you want to, I mean.Ó Draco froze and stared at Harry for a moment before shaking his head. ŇWhy did you throw yourself under the bus, Harry?Ó he asked. ŇYou and your stupid lack of survival instinctÉ they still didnŐt have proof of what IŐd actually done, and if thereŐs one thing IŐve learned from my father, itŐs how to get away with things.Ó He let out a huff. "Malfoys are slippery. In fact, I believe we're known for it." Harry let out a quiet laugh. ŇThatŐs definitely true,Ó he said before sighing deeply. ŇAndÉ maybe itŐs the last of the Gryffindor in me,Ó he said. ŇI justÉ I couldnŐt let them take you away.Ó He stared down at their linked hands and realized how true those words were. The threat of losing Draco seemed to have driven a good deal of HarryŐs actions that night .attacking Bellatrix and Lucius, cursing Nott, and admitting that heŐd used the dark arts - it had all been for Draco. ŇYouÉ you just keep telling me that I should use my name,Ó Harry continued. ŇSo... I did. I figuredÉ maybe they wouldnŐt arrest me since they finally know that Voldemort is back...Ó ŇOr now weŐre both going to be arrested for it,Ó Draco said, shaking his head. ŇMaybe theyŐll take pity and put us in the cell between my father and Black.Ó Harry wasnŐt sure what to say to that. He wanted to comfort Draco, but he knew that it was incredibly likely that they would face some heavy repercussions from his actions. ŇHarryÉÓ Draco said. ŇI have -Ó He was cut off as the fireplace flared to life. Harry instinctively drew his wand from his pocket; he still felt geared up and on high alert. But it was Dumbledore that stepped through. He paused, staring at the two students with a frown. For the first time that Harry could remember, Dumbledore looked every bit of his age. He seemed exhausted, worn down, and sorrowful. ŇWhere are the others? And whatŐs happened to Sirius?Ó Harry immediately asked. ŇIs he okay? Are they?Ó A light seemed to appear in DumbledoreŐs eyes at the words. ŇTheyŐre all fine, Harry,Ó he replied. ŇIŐve managed to convince the Minister to keep your godfather in a holding cell within the Ministry rather than being sent to Azkaban - at least for now.Ó Harry let out a sigh of relief, nodding. ŇAnd what about us?Ó Draco asked, his voice tight, and when Harry glanced back at him, his expression was pinched. ŇSir?Ó he added, almost as an afterthought. Dumbledore peered at Draco over his glasses. ŇWhat do you mean, Mr. Malfoy?Ó Draco sneered. ŇShould we expect a holding cell?Ó he asked. ŇOr Azkaban?Ó The light that had appeared before dulled and Dumbledore shook his head. ŇThe two of you will not be arrested,Ó he said. ŇI used the dark arts, though,Ó Draco said. ŇWe both did,Ó Harry quickly added. ŇAnd during the last war, some dark arts were permitted as a form of self-defense,Ó Dumbledore replied. ŇSince the Ministry can no longer deny that Voldemort has returned, it is safe to say that we are, once again, at war.Ó Despite his relief at hearing that Aurors wouldnŐt be coming to arrest them, a chill ran down HarryŐs spine at his words. ŇMr. Malfoy, I hate to ask this of you considering what youŐve both just been through,Ó Dumbledore said, Ňbut would you mind giving Harry and myself a moment alone? ŇDraco stays.Ó Dumbledore stared at him, his beard twitching just slightly, and Harry didnŐt have a hope of reading his expression. ŇHarry, I do think it would be best -Ó ŇIf youÉÓ Harry paused, trying to choose his words carefully. ŇIf you think you canŐt trust Draco, do I have to remind you that he just helped me?Ó he said. ŇHe directly defied his father - and Voldemort -Ó ŇHarryÉÓ Draco murmured. But Dumbledore was shaking his head. ŇI am sorry, Harry. That isnŐt at all what I was trying to imply,Ó he said. ŇI simply mean that what we have to discuss may beÉ upsetting, and I wanted to offer you privacy.Ó Harry blinked, and he felt Draco give his hand a comforting squeeze. ŇAre the others back yet?Ó Draco asked. ŇBlaise and the others?Ó Dumbledore nodded. ŇYou should find them in the entrance hall,Ó he replied. Draco put an arm on his shoulder before looking at Dumbledore. ŇWere Mr. Crabbe and Mr. Goyle also arrested?Ó Draco asked. Dumbledore paused, glancing between Draco and Harry for a moment before responding. ŇThey were.Ó HarryŐs eyes widened. ŇYou can tell me whatever you want to later.Ó Harry buried his face into DracoŐs shoulder and nodded. ŇOkay.Ó Draco released Harry, shot a look at Dumbledore, and then left. The clip of the door shutting behind him sounded ominous, and Harry slowly turned back to face Dumbledore with trepidation. The exhausted demeanor to Dumbledore seemed to grow a hundredfold, and the old wizard sighed as he strode around his desk and pulled out the chair. ŇHarry, I despise using words such as these, butÉÓ He sat down, clasping his hands in front of him. Ň... I never thought I would find myself so disappointed in you.Ó He waved his hand and a chair appeared in front of his desk, a clear invitation. Harry scowled and crossed his arms. He took a few steps forward to stand behind the offered chair but did not sit down. ŇIs that what you didnŐt want Draco to hear?Ó he asked, his tone biting. ŇIt is not, but it is nonetheless something that we must speak about,Ó Dumbledore replied. ŇDo you realize how dangerous meddling in the dark arts is?Ó Harry resisted the urge to roll his eyes, and he somehow also managed to bite back a scathing comment that was on the tip of his tongue. Dumbledore apparently wanted a response, though, because he said, ŇHarry?Ó He wanted to get Harry to stop, Harry realized, but he was too late. Snape had told him, Andromeda had told him - there was nothing that Dumbledore could do about it now, and so Harry saw no reason to not tell him the truth. ŇIŐve done more than meddle,Ó he said, Ňsir.Ó DumbledoreŐs eyes widened almost imperceptibly, and Harry held his gaze unflinchingly. ŇWhat do you mean?Ó ŇIŐve declared for the dark,Ó Harry said simply. The expression that came over DumbledoreŐs face was unmistakably sad. ŇOh, Harry,Ó he said, his voice quiet. ŇWhat have you done?Ó ŇI just told you,Ó Harry said without thinking, his tone a bit sardonic. ŇIŐve become a dark wizard.Ó It was strange but Dumbledore looked like he was grieving, and despite HarryŐs recent irritation with him, a pang went through his chest. ŇI am so sorry I wasnŐt there for you this year. I should have been -Ó ŇYeah, you should have,Ó Harry bit out. ŇI was already looking into it before I was even expelled.Ó ŇI thought you would have missed your old house, though?Ó ŇI did. I do,Ó Harry said. ŇI recall you standing in this very office years ago, fretting over how the hat had wanted you in Slytherin at all.Ó ŇPeople change,Ó Harry bit out. ŇAh, yes. We all do,Ó Dumbledore said, nodding. ŇI certainly am not the same person I was when I was in my youth.Ó ŇBut youŐre still the same person,Ó Harry said. ŇYour attitude might change, or the way you view the world might changeÉ but youŐll always still be the same person youŐve always been.Ó ŇThat can be a very bleak outlook, Harry,Ó Dumbledore replied. ŇThat sounds like weŐre incapable of becoming better people.Ó ŇThat isnŐt true, though. ŇNo matter how many times a snake sheds his skin, heŐs still a snake.Ó He couldnŐt help but smirk at that. ŇBut shedding skin gets rid of the bad and lets the snake grow.Ó Dumbledore looked startled at HarryŐs words before he offered Harry a smile. But when he spoke, it was nothing that Harry expected. ŇIt is not your fault, Harry,Ó Dumbledore said. ŇThatŐs really -Ó ŇHarry, I know far more about dark wizards and the dark arts than you might have realized,Ó Dumbledore said. ŇItŐs blood magic.Ó ŇYour motherÉ wasnŐt a dark witch, Harry,Ó Dumbledore said quietly. ŇShe wasnŐt,Ó Harry said. ŇBut she had an affinity for the dark, just like I do.Ó He paused. ŇYou said you know about dark wizards and witches, right? You know about affinities?Ó Dumbledore gave him a short nod. ŇI do,Ó he confirmed. ŇIt was explained to me that there are light affinities and dark affinities, but since I had never heard of anyone confirming a light affinityÉ I must admit that I suspected it wasÉ an excuse that dark wizards used to explain why they turned to the dark arts in the first place.Ó ŇTrust me when I say itŐs not an excuse,Ó Harry snapped, even as he wondered about if what Dumbledore said about light affinities was really true. ŇI just worry about what the dark arts will do to you -Ó ŇIt wonŐt do anything,Ó Harry said. ŇI donŐt think youŐd understand.Ó Dumbledore was quiet, and he studied Harry with a calculating look. ŇPerhaps you can educate me, then,Ó he finally said. Harry looked at him in surprise. Did that mean that Dumbledore was actually willing to listen? Dumbledore continued. ŇWhat he didÉ what he becameÉÓ Harry wondered who the friend had been, but judging from the haunted expression on DumbledoreŐs face, he decided it would be in bad taste to ask. ŇThe orbs contained in the Hall of Prophecy can only be touched by those who are subjects of their individual prophecy,Ó he said, Ňmeaning the only people who could touch the orb that the Death Eaters desired were -Ó ŇMe and Voldemort,Ó Harry finished, remembering the names he'd seen on the old, yellowing label. ŇThe date on the prophecyÉ was from before I was born.Ó Dumbledore nodded sadly. ŇIt was.Ó ŇYouŐveÉ youŐve known there was a prophecy about me... for years?Ó he asked incredulously. ŇI regret that I must sayÉ I did.Ó He rose from his desk, looking tired and stepped over to a cabinet. He pulled out his pensieve - the one that Harry had fallen into the year prior. ŇI was interviewing Sybil Trelawney for a possible teaching job at Hogwarts. ŇIt was quite disappointing. But when she spoke, it wasnŐt the airy, spacey voice that she tended to use in class; it was the same voice Harry had heard at the end of his third year. It was harsh and hoarse and barely sounded human. ŇThe one with the power to vanquish the Dark Lord approachesÉ Born to those who have thrice defied him, born as the seventh month diesÉ and the dark lord will mark him as his equal, but he will have power the Dark Lord knows notÉ and either must die at the hand of the other for neither can live while the other survivesÉ the one with the power to vanquish the Dark Lord will be born as the seventh month diesÉÓ Professor Trelawney sank back into the pensieve, and the silence that fell over the office felt almost oppressive. ŇWhat does that mean?Ó Harry whispered, unable to tear his eyes away from the pensieve. ŇIt means,Ó Dumbledore said, Ňthat the person who had a chance of defeating Lord Voldemort would be born at the end of July, nearly sixteen years ago, to parents who had already defied Voldemort three times.Ó Harry wrenched his gaze towards Dumbledore, feeling almost light-headed. He shook his head. ŇYouŐreÉÓ Harry had felt on edge and angry since Dumbledore had first appeared, and he realized that he was now absolutely furious. ŇYouŐve known about this stupid prophecy since before I was born. Why didnŐt you tell me before?Ó Dumbledore let out a great sigh and shook his head. ŇIŐm afraidÉ I didnŐt want to burden you with this knowledge, Harry. My great follyÉÓ ŇYeah, that is pretty great,Ó Harry spat. He glared at Dumbledore, fury racing through him, and sneered, ŇYouŐre a coward.Ó Dumbledore didnŐt protest HarryŐs words. Instead, he said, ŇI am truly sorry, Harry.Ó ŇYou should be.Ó It was then that a bolt of clarity ran through Harry. HeŐd spent much of the year being irritated with Dumbledore, but every single time heŐd felt pure, unadulterated anger towards the headmaster, that anger had generally been VoldemortŐs. Now, however, the anger was all his own. ŇI do not deserve your forgiveness, Harry,Ó Dumbledore said quietly. ŇThe truth is that I had hoped to keep this burden from you for a little longer, but I waited for far too long.Ó Harry had no idea what to say to that. ŇI had hoped that you would be able to remain a child for a little while longer,Ó Dumbledore continued. The person who stands before me is a young man - one who stands by his convictions with all his heart.Ó Harry let out a huff and turned away from the headmaster, shaking his head. ŇHave I damaged our friendship beyond repair, Harry?Ó Despite his anger and irritation, there was the tiniest sliver of guilt that began lacing through his heart. It was almost entirely self-serving .which was exactly what Hermione had said about Slytherins. ŇHarry?Ó ŇI donŐt know, sir,Ó Harry replied. ŇIŐll have to think about it.Ó Regardless of his guilt, Harry knew he was still too angry to make a decision on whether or not he could forgive Dumbledore. ŇCan we have that talk now?Ó Hermione said quietly. ŇNow that weŐveÉ made it out alive?Ó *** They quickly crossed the castle in silence, making their way to the abandoned classroom near the library. Harry saw what was coming and braced himself for the inevitable explosion. ŇHow could you, Harry?Ó Hermione asked. ŇYouŐre smarter than that - I know you are! ŇTell me about your year.Ó And Harry did. It wasnŐt easy. He at first tried to explain it chronologically, but then kept realizing something heŐd forgotten to mention earlier. He settled on trying to explain one thing at a time, pausing to clarify something whenever Hermione had a question. He told her about the dark arts and dark affinities, and how he had a strong, almost irresistible affinity for the dark. With some difficulty, he admitted that his interest in the dark arts had likely led him to unintentionally making the decision to go to Slytherin instead of Gryffindor. He told her about his fear that his own affinity had come from Voldemort, then smiling a little when he relayed that Draco was the one who pointed out that no matter where it had come from, it was still his affinity now. He went on to describe the feeling of hope heŐd had when he first thought it might have come from his father, the disappointment when he found out it hadnŐt, and then the wonder at the foundation-shattering revelation that it had actually been his mother whoŐd had the talent for the dark arts. He explained how he decided to become a dark wizard and how the dark was the first place heŐd ever felt like he belonged, even though the dark wasnŐt exactly a place. He tried to explain how it felt to have the dark wrap around him and comfort him, and how he seemed to know exactly what it meant even when it didnŐt utter a single actual word. He tried to make her understand that although some things about him had changed - his already-foul temper was even worse than it had been before, the Patronus charm felt like something terrible was wriggling through his body, and that he now understood that he was capable of causing immense pain and misery - he was still, at the core, the same Harry heŐd always been. He still loved the same people, and he still wanted to help everyone he could. The difference was that now he wanted to help dark wizards in addition to the rest of the wizarding world. He explained about the unfair persecution of the dark and those that were aligned with the dark, and he was satisfied to hear Hermione let out a tiny gasp when he told her about the one-sided view that was available in the Hogwarts library. He revealed that although many dark wizards went to Voldemort because they truly believed in his flawed ideology, several went to him because they felt he was the only one that would protect them from persecution. With reluctance, he explained that some of them felt that he could show them that Voldemort wasnŐt the only choice. ŇI donŐt think my being dark would have stayed hidden for much longer,Ó he explained. ŇAnd itŐs not how I would have wanted it to be revealed, but -Ó ŇThe Minister ordered everyone present to keep it quiet,Ó Hermione said. Harry blinked at her, incredulous. ŇWhat?Ó Hermione shook her head. ŇI didnŐt get a chance to tell you earlier,Ó she said. ŇBut we caught the tail-end of what he was telling the Aurors.Ó ŇWhy would he do that?Ó Harry asked. ŇHe already knows that the wizarding world is going to blame him for not confirming that Voldemort is back,Ó she said. ŇI think he believes that he would get blamed for youÉ turning dark, as well. ŇHe knows itŐll leak sooner rather than later, but I think he believes that so muchÉ bad news at once wouldnŐt be good for public morale.Ó ŇItŐs notÉ bad news,Ó Harry said, even though he knew very well that it would be exactly how much of the wizarding world would take it. ŇYou felt dark magic even though it wasnŐt even directed at you?Ó There had been so much going on at the time that he wasnŐt even sure if he had felt the magic from DracoŐs spell. ŇIt wasnŐtÉ wrapping around me like a cocoon or comforting or anything like you were saying earlier.Ó ŇBut the fact you felt it at allÉÓ Harry trailed off, again painfully aware that he didnŐt know nearly enough about the dark arts. ŇHermioneÉ over the summerÉÓ He fixed her with a contemplative look. ŇDid you try to get into that room in Grimmauld Place? ŇI have another question,Ó he said. ŇA few months ago, when you first asked me if I was messing around with the dark arts, what set your suspicions off?Ó ŇIÉÓ Hermione paused, her confusion seeming to grow. ŇThose injuries on your hands, I suppose, plus that spell you used on Applebee. There were also all the rumors flying around Gryffindor, but it was mostlyÉ just a gut feeling that there was something I was missing,Ó she said. ŇI felt like I had to ask you about it.Ó In the back of his mind, he knew he was hoping against hope, probably because he wanted to find a reason for Hermione to remain his friend. But Hermione had felt the magic of a dark spell - Harry hadnŐt even felt that spell. The library hadn't opened for her as it had for him, but what was it that Sirius had said? That library only opens itself to those that it trusts. The library, like the dark, was almost sentient, and either Hermione didnŐt have the right affinity to get in, or she did and the library didnŐt trust her for one reason or another. After all, Hermione had been taught to think that the dark arts were evil, but it wasnŐt as if she was the first Muggleborn to think that. ŇHermioneÉÓ Harry said slowly. ŇIf we go back to Grimmauld Place again this summer, I think you should try that door again.Ó ŇWhy?Ó Hermione asked curiously. Then she offered him a tentative smile. ŇActually, I was going to sayÉ about all thisÉÓ ŇÔAll this?ŐÓ Harry asked. ŇWhat, you mean me and the dark arts?Ó ŇYes,Ó Hermione said. ŇThat is to sayÉ I am willing, provided you can lend me the books that prove what youŐre saying - the ones you said were missing from the Hogwarts library.Ó Harry stared at her for a moment, then burst out laughing. "It kind of figures that you'll believe a book more than you'll believe me," he said, feeling just a bit hysterical. He supposed it was probably understandable - he was bordering on complete exhaustion, after all. Scowling, Hermione picked up one of her cushions and threw it at Harry, and his laughter just grew louder. After a moment, she began laughing with him. It felt a bit like they were laughing just to laugh, but it also felt good. Harry was painfully aware of the fact that his friendship with Hermione could still be in danger, but she was still willing to look into it herself, and that meant the world to him. Getting a chance to laugh with her -really laugh - felt long overdue and wonderfully familiar. They eventually managed to calm down, Hermione actually wiping her eyes as Harry held a stitch that had formed in his side. ŇItŐs not like we actually get anything for it.Ó ŇEasy for you to say,Ó Pansy shot back at him. ŇHow many times was it just handed to you while you were in Gryffindor?Ó ŇAt least Gryffindor isnŐt winning it this time, either,Ó Blaise pointed out. ŇTheyŐre in last place. They were in second after you restored the Inquisitorial points. I guess the professors got fed up with them all using the last of the Weasley products after Umbridge wasnŐt here to use them on anymore.Ó ŇI guess if it canŐt be us, better Ravenclaw than Gryffindor or Hufflepuff,Ó Draco said. As if on cue, Dumbledore got to his feet, and the Hall fell silent. ŇWe again find ourselves at the end of the year,Ó he said, his voice easily carrying across the large room. ŇI daresay that this has been a year like none other, and I think most of you would agree. ŇWhile the news of VoldemortŐs return has likely put a damper on your spirits, we still have the matter of the House Cup. Considering the events of the last week or so, I do, as usual, have some last-minute points to award.Ó ŇHere we go again,Ó Pansy whispered. ŇThe totals are as follows: in fourth place, Gryffindor, with three hundred and twenty-eight points; In third, Slytherin, with three hundred and forty-seven points; Hufflepuff in second, with three hundred and fifty-two points; and finally, Ravenclaw is in first, with three hundred and fifty-eight points.Ó ŇDumbledoreŐs about to pull the rug out from Ravenclaw, isnŐt he?Ó Blaise whispered. ŇFirstly, to Marietta Edgecombe of Ravenclaw, for reliving and giving a memory of something painful for the aide of the school, I award you ten points.Ó ŇHuh,Ó Draco said. ŇThatŐs unexpected.Ó ŇNext, for Terry Boot and Anthony Goldstein of Ravenclaw, I award ten points each for unraveling an Obliviated memory with all the intellect that your house represents,Ó Dumbledore continued. ŇWhy is he bothering with this when Ravenclaw is already winning?Ó Blaise asked. ŇJust drive the knife in even deeper, why donŐt you?Ó ŇFor Hermione Granger of Gryffindor, for assisting the Ravenclaws in uncovering the Obliviate laced in that memory, I award you ten points.Ó ŇAnd there it is,Ó Pansy hissed. ŇThe start of the waterfall of points heŐs going to shower on Gryffindor...Ó ŇFor Lee Jordan of Gryffindor, for realizing when it was time for house rivalries to come to a pause for the good of the school, I award you ten points.Ó Pansy continued sniping, but Harry found himself hiding a smile. He knew Dumbledore well enough to tell exactly where all these points were pointing. ŇFor Angelina Johnson of Gryffindor, I award ten points for not only heeding a warning, but for knowing when to be discreet about where that warning came from.Ó ŇGryffindor is now in second place,Ó Blaise muttered. ŇLet me guess, a thousand points to Longbottom for being a bumbling, plant-obsessed nerd?Ó Harry threw another elbow into BlaiseŐs gut. ŇFor Harry Potter of Slytherin, for growing into his new house with all of the cunning that Salazar Slytherin himself took pride in, and for finding a way to warn the school of imminent danger as well as finding a way to remove that danger, I award you ten points.Ó ŇTen points?Ó Pansy said, rolling her eyes. ŇHe was much more generous with you when you were in -Ó ŇFor Blaise Zabini of Slytherin, for orchestrating the arrest of a person who was a threat to each and every student in this school, and for reaching out to students from nearly every other house in order to put that threat to rest, I award youÉ thirty points.Ó Pansy gasped and Blaise gaped. ŇThatÉ that puts us just one point behind RavenclawÉÓ Blaise said. ŇNext time, Mr. Zabini, work a Hufflepuff into your mix,Ó Dumbledore said, his eyes twinkling. ŇIs heÉ is he serious?Ó Pansy hissed. ŇHeŐs going to get us so close and then not actually do it because Blaise didnŐt -Ó ŇLastly, to Pansy Parkinson of Slytherin, for choosing to do the right thing even when it hinders your own ambitions, I award you ten points.Ó Harry let out a broad grin. The other fifth years sat in stunned silence for a moment, while Harry heard the other Slytherins erupting all around him. Then Pansy let out a shriek and she jumped to her feet before clambering onto the bench and then up onto the table. HarryŐs eyes widened as she knocked a goblet onto BlaiseŐs plate before leaping directly onto both him and Blaise with a peal of high-pitched laughter. The force of PansyŐs high-flying tackle sent all three of them pitching back over the rear of the bench, and HarryŐs foot kicked the table noisily on the way down. ŇBloody - MerlinŐs - fucking -Ó Blaise spat, while Harry just groaned in pain as they hit the floor. *** It was different, Harry realized, riding with the Slytherins on the train. They didnŐt play Exploding Snap or Gobstones, and there wasnŐt much laughter during the journey. Of course, that could have been because the ride was unusually somber, their joviality over winning the House Cup having already worn off. Theo hadnŐt heard a peep from the Ministry, so he was going home with Pansy and her family. PansyŐs mother was his aunt, after all, and it would be easier to fight the MinistryŐs wishes should they decide to try to take him in. Vince and Greg sat and listened to Tracey and Millicent as the two came up with ideas for making sure their mothers would be okay. Draco, to HarryŐs surprise, wouldnŐt be staying at Malfoy Manor. Mrs. Malfoy had informed him that though they would stop there for a few hours to put a few things in order, she didnŐt feel the house was safe. After all, Draco had made himself an enemy of the Dark Lord, and Mrs. Malfoy had no idea what Lucius had done to the building. They couldnŐt be sure if Voldemort would show up there at any moment, and sheŐd rather not risk it. After stopping at the Manor, they were going to be headed for BlaiseŐs house. Blaise had written to his mother on a whim after hearing what Mrs. Malfoy had sent to Draco, and to his surprise, she had agreed to allow them to stay, at least for that summer. ŇBetter for me,Ó Blaise said. ŇIf she hears what Umbridge was planning and that I helped put a stop to it, sheŐll be much less likely to murder me if thereŐs company about.Ó Harry merely stared out the window, sightlessly watching the countryside pass them by. Ridiculously, he found himself a bit jealous of Draco. Despite the terrible circumstances, Draco was going to a different home, and Harry found that he craved that opportunity. Instead, he, as usual, was headed back to the Dursleys.Ő DracoŐs fingers tightened around his. ŇDo you really have to go to the Muggles?Ó he asked quietly. ŇYou could come with me and MotherÉÓ Harry shook his head. ŇIÉ I want to, but I shouldnŐt.Ó Despite his anger at Dumbledore, he didnŐt want to go completely out of DumbledoreŐs reach. He knew that it was likely that he would need Dumbledore in order to find a way to defeat Voldemort. Any means, Harry reminded himself unhappily. ŇBut -Ó ŇIŐm not planning on staying there for long,Ó Harry replied, and it was true. HeŐd already written to Andromeda, wondering if the offer Tonks had made was genuine, and Andromeda had replied with a confirmation. Harry was welcome at her house. To his relief, she was apparently also speaking to a solicitor about Sirius. She wanted to ensure her cousin stayed in the MinistryŐs holding cell rather than being transferred to Azkaban, but she also thought that HarryŐs testimony could help Sirius out. But more than anything, he wondered what kind of books Andromeda might have on the dark arts. She obviously still practiced them despite attempting to hide them from her daughter and husband. HeŐd exhausted the small collection heŐd taken from Grimmauld Place, and he wanted to start digging into new books as soon as he possibly could. He had to find some kind of magic that Voldemort didnŐt know about, and he didnŐt feel like he could waste a moment of time before getting started. ŇIf - or when - you wind up at my Aunt AndromedaŐs, write to me,Ó Draco said. ŇAnd you took out another subscription to the Prophet, right? YouŐll need to know when the news about what you did at the Ministry finally dropsÉÓ ŇI did,Ó Harry said. ŇAnd I will.Ó He hoped Andromeda would be able to guide him through what would happen when HarryŐs darker inclinations were revealed to the wizarding world. As the train rumbled on, HarryŐs thoughts again turned to what he needed to learn, and he worried that Andromeda might not have what he was looking for. He supposed that even if she didnŐt have any decently dark books, it would still be better than staying at the Dursleys.Ő And if it turned out that she had nothing, Harry decided, he could always try to head back to Grimmauld Place. After all, he already knew there was a well-stocked library there, behind a door on the top floor, at the end of a dimly lit hall. Chapter End Notes So... that's that. I hope it was satisfactory. I don't even know what to say now that it's done. First, thank you all so much for your lovely response to this fic. When I started writing this on a whim, I never expected that it would get such a positive reception. I'd also like to thank all of Godspeed You!Black Emperor's albums for being my soundtrack as I wrote. (That band is phenomenal. I can only write with instrumental music playing, and they set the mood perfectly all the time.) Next, some notes about what's coming: While the sequel is going to be a part of this series (now called 'Mutatum'), it will be a whole new story and a few things are going to change. The story I need to tell dictates a slight stylistic change. And, while it will still be an AU of Half-Blood Prince, the world is going to diverge more and more the farther we get into it. Some of the other changes may not make everyone happy, such as the tone becoming much darker. This fic was an AU of Order of the Phoenix, and it definitely had a lighter tone than the original material. The same will not be true for the next story. I don't want to get into too many details lest anyone reading not want to get spoiled. Therefore, I will be taking questions about the next story at my Tumblr. I'm not on there very much, but it's probably the most convenient platform. While I have the general storylines planned out, I have yet to write a firm outline. The outline I had for this story was nearly 20k words, so I have some work ahead of me. (I'll admit that there's also a few plot points that I need to fine-tune.) In other words, there is no exact timeline on when the next story is going to start being posted, and it will likely be a few months. (Yes, months.) I hope to see you all there when it does happen, though. Thank you for sticking with me, and I hope you've enjoyed your time spent with my Slytherin Harry. Works inspired by this one Evitative Chapter 21.5 by dmhello The Adventures of Ronald Weasley by turin411 [Podfic] Evitative by Vichan by Metropolyka (Yarikao) [Restricted Work] by sarcasticbrain To Us and More by DeviantHufflepuff Evitative [podfic] by Vichan by SaySoul The Young Lord by Lord_David_the_gay On Second Thought by Lullaby1717177 [Restricted Work] by WanSue Domum in Tenebris by GeorgeandJulian [Restricted Work] by orphan_account Through The Ghosts Of Our Past by robyna [Restricted Work] by Maybeitstimetoearnmybluebead the declaration by foxyjeonginnie (jettsintheheavens) The different Magic Families by Anonymous Whispers of the Serpent: Unveiling Slytherin's Mystical Secrets and Eternal Enchantments by orphan_account Recondite by LadyStardust_24 A New Dawn by echos_void (orphan_account) [Restricted Work] by Dijn Snake In A Lions Den by reakenvirge Between good and bad by Watercolor_sky Cover Art for Evitative by SliceOfLuck In The Blink Of A Crinkling Eye (Everything Has Changed) by Halfbloodprincess003 Please drop by the Archive and comment to let the creator know if you enjoyed their work!
